review_text:
OK... so.. My first experience with Han was getting a take-out order of Dan Dan noodles in haste at the Old City location years ago.  I couldn't get with it.  The spice totally overwhelmed me.  BUT.. my next experiences were lunch with co workers at the Han Dynasty on Main Street.  It was there that I learned the awesomeness of the Dan Dan noodles, along with a host of other things I now have on rotation when I do frequent the Main Street location (scallion pancakes, kung -pao chicken, garlic shrimp).  Yes, I may be a bit of a 'punk' on the spice level.(pay attention to the spice levels listed in the menu.. they'll even cater (and change) to your spice tolerance on dishes too). I'm more adventurous in a group setting... I exercised my spiciness chops at a couple b-day dinners I've attended.. including my own that I had at the Han Dynasty in Uni City.  I'd totally recommend going in a large party.. the pricing is right, there's so much food, the chef caters the menu to your group's taste and it's just great to taste all the various wonderful dishes on their menu. Everything is made to order, served hot (and sometimes under flames) to your table. It's just good.. SPICY and good.  And notice how I listed three locations and have yet to really determine a difference.  These locations are all equally good in my book.
If spicy is your thing.. and even if it's not and you want to try a little something a bit daring, give Han a try!

Just relocated to FL, and with hesitation and encouragement I tried PDQ. 

Just found out this is a chain, but you can't tell from the restaurant. 

The decor suggests it, but the tone does not. The staff seem to enjoy themselves, the place is very clean and the food is great. 

No red meat, but many choices anyway. 

I never give a five but if I could do over four I would. 

Highly recommend to anyone to try it, I will be back.

Two words: food poisoning.

After a long day traveling to Philadelphia with little else to eat, I was excited to get some food and ordered the slice of the day, a caesar salad and a beer.

The next twelve hours were filled with gut wrenching pain as I laid on the floor of the hotel bathroom attempting to expel the vile refuse from my system.

The "cookie jar" dessert I ordered to-go still sits on the counter, untouched and mocking me. I think it's best to throw it out.

I certainly found one place I will never return to on my travels to Philly.

Awesome food and service. They have any type of food that you can imagine from typical diner food to seafood and spaghetti and meatballs! I got the country fried steak with eggs and their potatoes which were interesting. The food was great! The service even better! I'll definitely be back to try something else on the menu because everything that was coming out of the kitchen looked amazing!

I love this place. They always go above and beyond to make sure everything is good. Very attentive, entertaining and food is delicious. My kids love it here even though we've seen the hibachi show 20 times. A birthday dinner favorite!!

I went here last night with my family.  We decided to share multiple dishes, and ordered many of the appetizers.  Most of the food was fine, some was really good, but for the most part I left feeling like I had drank a few cups of oil. Everything was completely saturated in it!  Even the vegetable tapas (DON'T GET THIS) tasted more unhealthy than the potato chicken dish I ordered because all of the vegetables were absolutely drenched.  Also on the tapas dish three of the choices were fried! I'm assuming that most people ordering a huge dish of veggies aren't hoping they're fried and battered - because then they'd just order the french fries.

I won't be back for main courses, but the environment was nice and our waiter was great, so I'd come back for some drinks and fries.

Food was good but expect small portions & its pricey for the stuff. 
I like their concept being similar to chipotle.

Went to Kanella for dinner last night. On a tip from pro Yelper Shannon P, we ordered her 3 favorite starters: grilled octopus, lamb dumplings (Manti), and tuna salad. All three were excellent, but my favorite was definitely the lamb dumplings - they were extremely flavorful and I especially enjoyed dipping my bread in the leftover yogurt sauce (I can't help it, I love bread).

We also ordered the dips of the day. One was a hummus, one I forget what it was, and the other was something with anchovies. I hated the anchovy dip, but loved the other two. The pita bread served with the dips was also delicious and perfectly warm.

Next up were the fattoush salad which was very tasty - pomegranate, tomatoes, cucumber, pita chips (interesting) and a bunch of other fun flavors. The only thing I didn't love was that there were a TON of onions, I think too much, but they were easy enough to push some off to the side and still enjoy the salad.

We also had the braised oxtail with cilantro dumplings. I only had a bite, as my boyfriend devoured it, and it was an extremely tasty bite.

I should also add that the service was great here. Our waiter seemed to have a great personality and was very friendly and attentive. My boyfriend got there first and was sat inside, but it was a beautiful evening so after a bit of hesitation and not wanting to be a pain (our wine had already been poured), I asked if we could move outside and there was no issue at all. The hostess and the waiters were very friendly about it.

On my next visit, I would probably order a little less food (we were hungry) and make sure to get some dessert as well (it looked really good at the table next to us).

Oh right and most importantly - its a BYOB! Gotta love BYOB's.

Before I get deep into this review, I want to say that sometimes the Yelp star rating throws me off a bit.  I tend to judge an establishment more on whether or not I could see myself coming back again in the future.  To me, that's really the deciding factor.  That being said, yes, I would come back here again!  While Luke Restaurant may not be a pioneer in the culinary world, the kitchen does deliver sound dishes that cannot be ignored.

Luke Restaurant is located on St. Charles Ave. in the CBD, directly across the street from One Shell Square.  It shares the adjacent space with the Hilton Hotel, which, if anyone remembers pre-Katrina, used to be the boutique Hotel Monaco.  Luke Restaurant used to be The Cobalt, a hip and modern lounge/restaurant owned by Susan Spicer, where great cocktails, as well as amazing eats could be found.  While the restaurant has changed owner, chef and style, the quality of the dishes has not diminished in the slightest.  Luke Restaurant advertises itself as a fine dining Bistro and I would say this description is accurate.  I've been frequenting this restaurant off and on since it's opening in 2007 and I've never been given a subpar product.

I would like to draw special attention to the detail of the interior design inside Luke Restaurant.  It reminds me of a social steakhouse ripped straight out of New York City in the 1920s.  Heavy and very ornately carved wood cabinets, doors and bar surround the dining area.  The floor near the bar is completely tiled in black & white while the dining area is situated upon what resembles vintage wood flooring.  To the front and left side of the restaurant, there is a large wooden board containing various newspapers for businessmen to peruse.  Above dining guests are slow-moving ceiling fans which are operated via a pulley; a system no longer common these days.  The restaurant sports a very vintage feel and I believe this works in Luke's favor.  The only other steakhouse resembling Luke Restaurant in terms of interior design and dish offering that comes to mind would be Anthony Bourdain's home base, Brasserie Les Halles, located on Park Ave. in New York.

The last two occasions that I dined in Luke Restaurant were over lunch with colleagues.  I am somewhat a slave to routine so I placed my order for the Luke Burger, a monster of a burger that attempts to contain tomatoes, Allen Benton's bacon, caramelized onions and Emmenthaler cheese within the confines of the bun.  It also comes with housemade fries.  Now, believe me when I say that while this burger is steeply priced at $17, I dare you to eat it all in one sitting.  I typically cut the burger in half and take the rest home, as it easily can feed a single person twice!  It is delivered to the customer on a cutting board and you are given pocket sized bottles of Ketchup, Mustard and Mayonnaise.  This is one heck of a huge burger but it's worth every penny!

Before diving into the burger, I also placed a starter of the gumbo that, when paired with the fresh rolls, is out-of-this-world!  The rest of my table ordered various dishes and starters but all who had selected the gumbo also stated it was terrific!

My burger later arrived and it looked amazing.  The flavor was breathtaking.  The large cuts of bacon, the delishus swirl of caramelized onions and cheese created a sweet aroma so thick, one might say you could swim in it!  The meat was perfectly seasoned and cooked and the flavors came together in a manner that literally created an orgiastic feast of the senses.  In the nature of a true wine connoisseur who takes most pleasure from a vintage's distinct scent, I thoroughly enjoy breathing in the fresh smells from a dish produced by a kitchen.  At such times, one could wish for the olfactory powers of the canine.  It's often occurred to me that pulling a dog away from a scent is akin to seizing a scholar at the museum by the scruff of his neck and dragging him away from his studies.

As I've said before, Luke Restaurant may not be groundbreaking but what it does deliver is exceptional quality and great service.  This is a wonderful place to pop in by yourself or with a small group for lunch or dinner.  If you find yourself hungry and within proximity, do yourself a favor and visit Luke.  Your tummy will thank you!
This is our favorite buffet! We live in Oregon and anytime we stop in Reno we make sure to stop by. The food is always on point and staff is super attentive and friendly!

What a hidden gem of a restaurant.  While everyone else go to Taco Bus and stands in long lines I'll come here to Taco Son and get just as good of food without the lines. Everything here is made from scratch and you can taste the freshness of the ingredients.  The portions are big so you get your monies worth. Oh and their breakfast burritos are killer grab you one after a long night.

So I have been meaning to try this place since it opened, but I finally got there yesterday. Something that surprised me was the amount of workers they had behind the counter. They had four guys! They were squished in the back, but they made the line go very quickly, and once you were in it, your food came quickly.

Onto the food - your taco salads and bowls come in a fried shell, which always gets people going. They're all $8.33 for tacos, salad, bowl, etc., and that's for every kind of meat (chicken, shrimp, etc.). I got shrimp, and it was grilled fresh! You choose your toppings and then boom, you're done. They have Jamaica and Horchata for like $2.75, but the Jamaica wasn't as sweet as I'm used to.

The only thing I would change would be to have actual plates for people dining in instead of plastic clamshells that are also used for take out. This would be more environmentally friendly and make the dining in experience nicer! Can't wait to go back!

Just had dinner at Carisma with my wife. The food was fresh and delicious. The portions were generous. We were very pleased with the service. We will surely return there. Very pleasant experience. Highly recommend stopping there.

A had my share of cheese steak at all the talk about places in Philly . I order a American wit . Bread was light and toasty . Steak was a bit dry , but very flavorful ! So I rank this place middle of the road . 1 cheese steak and a milk shake $11 .

Top quality stuff here. We had breakfast and enjoyed the biscuits & gravy and breakfast salad. Both were top notch. The menu items are not your normal breakfast fare (where else have you seen "breakfast salad?"). Coffee and iced tea were very good.

We will be back!

We were sick of eating buffet and thinking why not try out some local restaurants. We ordered a fried rice, chow mein, pork chop over rice and popcorn chicken. Overall, the food were pretty good but the portion were kinda small.

I actually eat here at least once a month . The food is good and the service compliments it. You will fall in love with there zucchini muffins. I always leave with some stashed in my purse, you know for later. The prices are cheap and they have a fantastic frozen yogurt bar . with unlimited toppings. Have fun !! yum yum

Hate to start off with all negatives, so I'll say this.  We were given a table promptly at 5:10 on a Monday night (we were dining with our daughter, granddaughter and son-in-law . . . the little one requiring dinner NOW).  We were seated by a pleasant server and we were looking forward to a nice dinner.  Drinks game about 20 minutes after we ordered them and our meals were served 55+ minutes after we placed our orders.  Four of the five orders were served with the remaining one served 10 MINUTES later.  I ordered a London Burger (basic hamburger) medium.  When I looked at it I was shocked.  WELL done and dry as a bone.  No juice whatsoever.  My wife ordered duck spring rolls . . .  DRY AS A BONE.  My daughter ordered a vegetable platter . . . none of which she could eat.  Granddaughter (not a picky eater)  took one taste of her grilled cheese sandwich and fries and asked to go home to eat.  I tasted her dinner and agreed that we should leave.  Daughter and son-in-law had been to the London Grill before and thought it was okay, but now none of us will return.  SLOW service and poor food on a slow night.
This was exactly the type of place our little sleepy town of Tarpon Springs needs.  Not only was the food delicious, it was also healthy.   I'll definitely be back again and again.
It is a decent place. The food is great and the service is nice. I was there twice during my stay in town. I ordered their daily specials and was very satisfied.

Terrible.  Terrible.  Worst Thai food I have ever eaten.  Very small quantities for the money.  But looking back on it - that is the best part of the experience.  Frozen veggies, greasy sauce and cardboard-like meat.  Could not have told the difference between the beef and chicken.  Same color, same taste, same texture.

Terrible.
Good Chinese food, not the best, but Better than a few that are in that area and service is awesome. I love going in there and the fact that they deliver is good too.
Always friendly and great service. Steak is always prepared accurately since I enjoy a good rare steak.

I wasn't expecting the food to be THAT good, but it was. It came out super fast so I'm not confident about that. I got the gnocchi pasta and it had a good amount a flavor and went well the bread of the table. I wish they had more menu options, but the bread pudding made up for the variety. One of the best bread puddings I have ever tried in my life. Get it!
Somehow this carmalitas is not as good as the one up the street on east bay. Not quite as fresh and just seems dark in there.

this is a nice place for breakfast/lunch.. my wife and I go here at least once a week. sometimes more. the food is excellent and the service is friendly and quick and there's a good selection on the menu...

This place is quite good even though my quesadilla came without the chicken I ordered. I'm thinking it had to do with the language barrier. The chips, salsa, and cheese dip were fantastic. My mixture of sweet and unsweetened tea was just right. Also, my co-worker gave his chimichanga two thumbs-up. It's not the best Mexican ever, but better than 2/3 out there.

Always great food. Remodel is nice. Great breakfast, great pancakes, bacon cooked just right, and BUTTER IN A SQUEEZE BOTTLE! 
Their club sandwich is very good, and a favorite late night snack...meal. 
Wonderful milk shakes, friendly service and to-go orders are common. Also, waitr is available to deliver your order to Ocshner & you can skip the cafeteria food for a meal.

This place has really stepped up their game. Their food has always been excellent, but they have also added cute decor and a fast lunch menu. They change up the menu fairly often to add fresh, in-season menu items, which I love, but it appears they keep some favorites on there as well. My husband and I frequent this restaurant for dinner and brunch on the weekends. Most recently, we went for dinner and ordered the asparagus appetizer- so crispy and fresh! I had the Cubano sandwich that had a touch of sweetness, some tangy from the mustard, and the pork was well-seasoned and tender. He ordered the STL Masterpiece, which was a burger cooked to perfection and piled high with cheeses, BBQ sauce, and spicy mayo- SO tasty. We are worried because the restaurant never seems to be that busy, but it's really awesome!

We were brought for lunch last Friday to this place before I left for my return flight to New York and the claim was they have excellent sushi. And while the place looks unassuming and at first glance, dark and not so impressive, when it came to the food, yes, they lived up to that expectation.

I tried Godzilla and it was incredibly good. If you love salmon with avocado roll with some level of spicy, topped with crab meat, then you will truly enjoy this.

I tried their Beef Teriyaki too and it was one of the best Teriyakis that I've had. The meat was tender and while deliciously savory, was not overwhelmingly so. I think they also mentioned somewhere that they do not have MSG in their food. 

If you're craving for some good Japanese in your area, this is your best bet. The place was pretty packed for lunch and that says something!

P.S
Get the fried ice cream for dessert! One of the good ones that I've had this lifetime!
It was on the pricier side for us (~$30 per person), but food was delicious along with great service

Stopped in after flight during rush hour traffic. Service is good and the entries were less desirable.
Chicken Noodle Soup had noodles that were overcooked and mushy. Broth was weak and lacked flavor. Soup lacked seasoning.
Linguini with Maranara sauce with Chicken. Noodles cooked correctly, chicken very dry, and marinara sauce was a cross between BBQ and water.
Wife's coleslaw was not edible - it was spoiled.
Not coming back.

I am local to this area and have visit this restaurant many times. 
Each dishes they making very fresh. 
All dishes have tried here has satisfied me with test. Must visit this place specially for south indian dishes.

LOVED it!  I don't know how they cook steak like they do!!  This is a great for me to go eat at, because it's something I definitely can't say, "I could have made this at home", because there is just no way!  The carne asada simply melted in your mouth!  I think I could eat it everyday, and I'm really not a steak eater!!  We went on a Monday night, it was empty, and told us that we had the place to ourselves!  The chef came out to ask how everything was - I love when that happens!  It just shows that they really care, and want to see the delight of their customers!  I had the french onion soup - very good - full of flavor, and the brioche on top was perfect - it didn't have too much cheese like you see in a lot of french onion soups.  I also tried the prickly pear glazed albondigas appetizer which was decent too, and a chocolate, orange blossom beer!  Thank you Bianca for recommending that I mix the two!  It was a perfect combo!  Tip - If you ask for more chips, it will be and extra $1.75.  Also, it doesn't seem they quite know how to process the Meet Me at Maynard's 10% discount yet, I asked for that - my friend got it, and I didn't, and it showed up as a military discount. I can't wait to bring my husband!!  Oh!!  Chips and Salsa - AWESOME!!  They came warm and with a roasted salsa and a green!  Thus the second bowl!!

Let me start by saying the food was "Amaaazing" we had the Duck Duck Rice, and the shrimp and Grits, corn and crab soup, shrimp and crab stuffed oysters, French 75 cocktail, apple fall dumpling, and a chocolate tort.

Luke has a great atmosphere the ambiance was very nice. Our server Gary was on point, and was excellent with all of his recommendations and conversation and the same goes for the manager.

 I believe her Name was Elizabeth though we had dinner there on 12/29/17, so it's been a few days....so sorry if I didn't get her name right. Gary and Elizabeth made the experience very enjoyable for us and our little service dog. We will definitely go back, everything on the menu looked appetizing.

We were looking for a place to eat off route 30 on our way to NJ...were in the mood for Mexican, so picked El Jalapeño/El Limon based on the Yelp reviews/ratings. And we were not disappointed!!
We tried the tortilla chips with guacamole and pico di gallo, Carnitas and chorizo tacos, chorizo and chicken quesadillas and veggie fajitas. All the non-veg items were superb...fresh and bursting with flavor! Pico Di Gallo and Guacamole were really fresh and delicious!! Compared to the non-veg items, the vegetarian fajitas were not as flavorful, but still pretty good. Overall, we were very happy to have found this eatery, especially after seeing the (small) bill LOL
We are all about flavor when eating out, and that is present in the food at this tiny, unassuming little hole-in-the-wall to the fullest, so this would have been our go-to place for Mexican had we been local...
But if you value ambience while eating out, this place is not for you.
Wonderful, fresh food. Only complaint is not always having the ingredients to make what you ask for, but understandable in winter.
Such a disappointment. Food seemed to have way too much sodium as I felt so sick afterwards.  Price too high for the lame quality of food.

First time visit with friends. It's a small little pizza place with a few tables inside. It wasn't horribly crowded which was nice. I had the spinach calzone. The calzone was huge and very delicious. My husband had chicken Caesar salad. It was average but not horrible. Fries are very yummy! I will definitely be back to try their pizza. Overall review would be it's an average Italian place.

First visit to 39 West and we will definitely return! 

Pros:  BYOB, small menu with great variety. Most important, our meals were superb!  The tuna, tempura veggies, New England clam chowder, stuffed goat cheese chicken, salmon and steak frites were delicious and cooked to perfection!

Cons:  VERY small tables throughout entire restaurant; we had 3 in our party and struggled for space with appetizers and main course. 

Chairs are small, hard and the design makes you slide awkwardly forward. Not great for the elderly or folks with bad backs. 

Service was frequent, polite and helpful. However, they took our appetizers away without asking if we were finished and rushed dinner upon us before we could finish chewing appetizers.  

Not a great place to bring kids or large groups due to small table size and no kids menu. 

Overall, best food we have tasted across all of West Chester in years! We will be back!

We have been here many times, we always get the house special pizza with no onions (it's basically a supreme pizza) a mini Greek salad and baked spaghetti with sausage. Certain days they off 4.99 baked spaghetti and some days they have pizza specials. The house made salad dressing they have for the Greek is so amazing! I even use it to dip my pizza crust in. The baked spaghetti is always on point with tons of sausage. Parking is great and they have a little arcade for kids. One tip is call your order in ahead of time and then you won't have to wait as along because the pizza is cooked fresh to order
First time here and the service and food was great. I enjoyed every bite and will be bringing my friends next time

Omg! This place is fantastic! Great service great food great price great environment great show! I am so happy that we picked this restaurant when we traveled in Philadelphia! A must go restaurant!!!!! Highly recommend!!

This is actually a great place for very very small groups (4 people MAX). Their burritos are decent (though mostly overrated), their fried pickle chips are admittedly amazing, and their margarita pitchers are the real reason I ever used to go there. Try to get there for happy hour or after 10pm because the pitchers are half off.  It's also very vegan/vegetarian friendly. 

They're a lot more accommodating when they have their outdoor seating open, otherwise you could be stuck there for hours just waiting to be seated. 

I used to love this place... until this weekend, when some friends and I came here. There were 10 of us (which is understandably a lot), and they looked at us like we were from another planet when we asked for a table. It was a beautiful night, and all of their outdoor benches were lined up outside, but for whatever reason they refused to seat us.  I get that it's popular and gets packed, but they could lose the attitude.  

We walked across the street to Gunner's Run, got seated immediately, had an amazing dinner, and dropped about $400 that El Camino lost out on. 


I work at a cafe in old city, so I get that places have their 'off' days, but places like El Camino in the nolibs area... need to seriously calm down.

I made my first visit to Blake's yesterday. I give it a 3.5. It's a great addition to the fast food scene here. A different, more hip vibe and a decent burger.  I love that their ketchup packets actually have a useful amount of ketchup in them. Free wifi is a plus, I used it and it worked great. The staff were friendly, and it seemed sincere; they seem happy to be working there.  The Lotaburger came with a substantial meat patty, that had good flavor and texture.  Two complaints.  The chocolate milkshake was bad.  It's the dreaded 'ice milk' from my childhood that the family would buy when we were on hard times financially.  A huge yuk, didn't even finish it.  Also, the Hatch green chiles were too hot.  I love hot, as evidenced by my high ratings for Poco and Mom's here in Tucson and for various NM food venues.  But this was ridiculous, just too much, which takes away from enjoying not only the chile but the hamburger.  I guess if frat boys want to get into a competition to survive high heat in their mouth, this is the place for them.  But the rest of us will have to make a more palatable green chile burger at home.  If I go again, and I might, I'll have a burger sans green chile and stick with a soda rather than the shake.

I just ordered delivery from the garden district location. The salad was bitter and smelled like urine! The sandwich "uptowner" I've had many times in the past, was on a different type of bread than usual and the cheese was not even melted. I threw the salad out, couldn't finish the sandwich and we'll never order from reginellis, again! Stay away, really gone down, down, down

If you looking for a quick in and out breakfast...this is not it but def worth the wait!!! We got lucky and snagged a outside table pretty quick(it was hot and no one wanted to be outside).

Breakfast is MY FAVORITE so i was very overwhelmed looking at this menu! With the help of our waiter(and reading tons of reviews)I ordered the Breakfast Quesadilla & my husband ordered the Huevos Rancheros. Very thing was so fresh and delish. I would love to go back and try more. 

FYI- you can BYOB....wish we would have known:)

A bunch of my Iraqi friends mentioned that this was the best place to go for Iraqi food. So I went there with my friend and OMG I could not stop eating! We had the lamb kabob with rice and the falafel dish served with hummus and bread. This restaurant recently got new management and it is seriously the most authentic place to have Middle Eastern cuisine. You know it is an excellent place when you're a minority and there's Arabic subtitles on the TV. I will definitely be returning!
Shakes, burgers and chicken sandwiches.  It ain't fancy but it is usually damn tasty.  Don't expect fine dining here folks, but what they do.. they do ok.

In the day - when they first opened, it was great.  The quality of food and service have faltered greatly in past few months though.  The bar? - great...drinks are always spot on.  The food?  meh - not worth the prices.
Great breakfast, will be back! Very friendly staff and owner very welcoming. Lots of variety on the menu as well.

I love the surf and turfs ($5.69/regular, $8.09/large)  here! It's always so delicious. A little greasy, but who doesn't expect their philly's to be a little greasy?! I get them dressed and always add chopped jalapeños for a little kick. I've also tried a "healthier option" by getting shrimp and chicken instead of steak. Also amazing. Idk what they use to season their meats, but it's super flavorful. 

Their cheese fries ($2.79) are made to order, so the fries are always crispy and hot. They're also seasoned.

Any and every time I stop by Esplanade Mall, I have to make a pit stop here before leaving. Do yourself a favor and eat here!

Best pizza around. You could order anything and it will come out perfect. Everything is made fresh, and the pizza is the best. The calamari is one of our favorite sides, as well as ordering the white pizza. I reccommend this to anyone looking for a delicious meal to eat!
The red fish was amazing.  Romantic setting, great service.  A keeper!

The food is very good and cheap! However, no matter what time or day you go, the service will always be slow. It's usually 45 min wait for food no matter what you get to eat. Also, it's very small inside so trying to self seat on a Friday night may not be possible. I'd love to come here more often if the service was a little quicker.

Amazing, amazing food and what is even better is that you can buy it in larger sizes to take home. I lived off yats for the entirety of last winter. I treated it like soup somtimes and didn't even make rice for it to go with.
Yats pretty much saves lives. This may be a slight exaggeration because my friends and I are college students who are living on a budget, too tired to cook, or both. 
Pro Tip: EXTRA BREAD IS A MUST

Also, I'm pretty sure they accept cards now....yes?

This place is good, really good. Happy hour runs from 3 - 6, half price cocktails and 50 cent oysters...you can't buy oysters at the market for 50 cents. Half price drinks, I recommend the Sazarac, the official drink of New Orleans, the best deal in the city at $4.50. Andre, one of the oyster shuckers, is one of the most personable people I have ever met. He is extremely passionate about what he does and is very good at it. We are in New Orleans for a week and thank God Luke is only a block from our hotel, we have been there 4 times during our stay and will be going back tomorrow. The rabbit and duck liver pate is one of the best pate's I have ever had, served with country bread, sweet and sour pickles, pepper jam, pickled watermelon rind and country mustard, all house made of course.

Amazing seafood, service, and authentic Cajun cuisine. A bit pricey but worth it for the fresh quality of the food. Right next to the hilton French quarter and they have an amazing happy hour most afternoons from 4-6pm .

Food is good but not service. They are extremely slow and had to remind them to bring water several times. We went there at lunch time on a weekday and we had to wait more than 1 hour to get the food. Several others just walked out as they were waiting for the same amount of time as we were. We finally got up to walk out and that's when the waitress came to us and said food was ready and offered to pack it for take out. We paid for it, took the food only to find later that it was not what we ordered. Extremely disappointed.

The name of the restaurant is true. The Indian fry bread is fantastic! In my opinion the Indian taco with green chili was very weak. There was hardly any chili and it was not spicy at all. Quite disappointing. Next time we go I will just pickup takeout bread and prepare my own chili.

The setting allows you to ponder how this place came about.

Very trendy place.

8/10 food quality for breakfast. 7.5/10 food quality for dinner.

Groups of 6 or more should not attend on weekends during busy hours of operations.

I would have given it a 2.5 but did not like it enough to give it a 3.

Don't get me wrong, we had a fairly serviceable meal here. But only because we ordered something that is not on the menu...and we really only enjoyed part of that.

We stopped in on a Saturday after being wallet molested at the nursery behind it. It's a simple layout with plastic covered tables set up in really odd directions. 

The staff seem nice enough and our waitress was quick to bring us a menu.

There is where my first problem was. The menu was disgusting and the prices were the same as French Quarter prices. Clean yo menus!

We looked and looked and finally decided to risk just doing a couple of appetizers. 

As we waited for our waitress to come back, we saw someone get the apps we were going to order. Both guys tried them and both guys made gross-out faces. One mentioned that it was 7.50 for something the size and flavor of a freezer section taquito. So we needed to find something else to order.

As we waited some more, the waitress brought a giant platter of boiled seafood to the table in front of us. I saw crab legs!

We asked the waitress about that. It's not on the menu but it was about 40 bucks. We ordered one to share.

This platter came with 3lbs of crawfish, a cluster of snowcrab legs, a cluster of dungeness crab and a pound of shrimp.

We dug into the crab. Both sets of legs were tasty and the butter dipping sauce was spot on. 
Once we demolished those we moved onto the crawfish and shrimp. That's where things went back down hill.

She shrimp were over-cooked and tough to peel. Not much flavor to them.

The crawfish were some of the most bland, boring crawfish I'd ever eaten.  Just horrible. I asked the waitress about this and she said they had a lot of older folks in who didn't like the spice. Ok..But why NO flavor at all? I swear they tasted like they had been boiled in unsalted water.

Needless to say, we didn't get to finish the platter but the crab-legs were delicious. I just wish they knew how to boil the other seafood.

It ended up costing us 50 bucks for 2 sets of crab legs, 4 shrimp and 3 crawfish, before tip. Not a great bargain, really.

I would recommend this place to people with spice allergies and extreme crab leg lovers.

My daughter and I went to this litte GEM and it was great.   I had the Catfish Nuggets and it was some of the best I've ever had.   My daughter had a burger ( which I ate half off) and Wow.  It was the best 3.99 burger ever!  Looking forward to my next visit!

Anyone who would give this place fewer than four stars should consider having their heads and sense of taste examined. I had the lamb kabob, which was the special. Except for what, to me, was kind of a small portion (only reason it gets 4 instead of 5 stars) it was absolutely fantastic!

After a couple of visits, I think I can safely say this is a consistently good sushi spot.  There is nothing spectacular about the sushi, but the price has my coming in as a regular now. 

The hibachi is good, the BBQ squid is delicious, and add mentioned before, all the sushi I've tried has been good. The service every time I've visited had been stellar.  The dining area is nice and neat; it's a very comfortable atmosphere.  This place is casual, but romantic enough for a date night, and even good for a family outing. 

They are not reinventing sushi here.  Everything is pretty standard, with a few unique Tsunami twists here and there.  I'm giving 4 stars because it's a well run restaurant, with good food and service, and great prices.  I haven't had a bad experience yet, and I can't think of anything to complain about.

A short review/explanation aimed at the grossed-out, tasteless non-St. Louisans:

Instead of mozzarella - universally and un-originally used in every pizza from coast-to-coast, including your grocer's freezer - Imo's uses Provel, which is a delicious, 100% processed cheese blend presented by God as a gift to His Favored City, St. Louis. Read up:

http://en.wikipedia.or...

No other pizza in the world - and I've eaten them from coast-to-coast and on three continents - compares to the thin little square of Heaven that is Imo's!

The trick is to make sure you have all the condiments on hand, which includes a Cardinals or Blues game on the tube.

I will start with the Guacamole plain tasteless no flavor what so ever. Molida is a two out of five. Tostada I ordered had to be eaten with a fork due to the beans being too runny. Cheese is Queso Fresca. Again very bland tasteless food. Ordered Chicken Fajitas. No flavor chicken was rubbery. Very few pieces of chicken mostly a big plate of bell peppers few onions. To be honest the bell peppers were fresh. Salsa tasted like plain tomatoes. Again no flavor here at all.

Service and cleanliness 5 stars but the food was just ok. We are huge fans of Ryaki but they do not have indoor dining right now and the takeout isn't up to par, so we gave Kokoro a shot. Really wanted to love it but the noodles felt more like spaghetti than the chewy wavy textures were accustom to. Lemon water was a nice touch. Pork buns were toasted which seemed odd but still good. Presentation was was well executed. Hope to give it another shot.

Ahead of Bob Evans and Cracker Barrel, I think I've eaten at The Original Pancake House (W 86th Indianapolis, E 116th Fishers and W 146th Carmel locations) the most over the years. The food and service have impressed me, especially at this W 86th Street location, which I visit most.

Today I ordered something different than usual. The 49ers Pancakes. These 3 cakes are made from a different batter, almost crepe-like. I dressed them up with some banana slices (sold separately) and powdered sugar (provided with the bananas, along with cream). Maple syrup and whipped butter accompanied the cakes, but I found the bananas sufficed for my sweet taste.

Aside from their "original" pancake selections, "The House" is famous for their special coffee blend, along with their thick sliced bacon. (Note: I have yet to find another breakfast restaurant claiming "thick sliced bacon" to actually serve bacon as THICK as bacon served at The Original Pancake House's!) Today, however, I went with the Turkey sausage (a lame dietary attempt on my part to ease my guilt of poor eating choices over the last few days! lol) instead. I did have the coffee!

Margaret, the morning host and server, greeted me very pleasantly and invited me to sit anywhere I wanted (I got here just before 7:00am and seating was still plentiful). I sat in a booth next to the window so I could enjoy the dark outdoors and drizzly rain as I started my morning with a hot cup of coffee that Juan served me. He's got a great personality and very accommodating. He kept my coffee hot with refills, too!

Candy the working/serving General Manager, pleasant as ever stopped by to say "Hello" as she attended her tables.

If you've never tried eating breakfast here, you should try this place at least once. Are they perfect? Of course not! But name me ANY restaurant that hasn't made a mistake on an order at least once. What makes this place VERY SPECIAL is NOT just their great food... It's the QUALITY of people they employ and how they do their best to make your dining experience a pleasant and fulfilling one that you'll want to return for, again and again.

Highly recommend you give them a try!

I had a carnitas taco, pork tamale, and a Revuelta pupusa. The carnitas taco was amazing! My other dishes were also really good, but I can't get the taco off my mind. This place is very close to my work, so I will be back often. It's a great place to stop for a quick bite to eat. Next time I may get a plate of tacos, but I want to try it all!

Our visit began with a 20 minute wait for our table, despite a reservation and no apology. Server took 15 minutes to come to the table, another 20 to get drinks and order dinner. Half the drinks had to be send back, replacing took another 15 minutes. At this point, we are just plain annoyed. Had the food been amazing the visit may have been redeemed, but it was very mediocre (with the one notable exception of the onion tarte). Servers gave up and just ignored us, they said they would be buying us dessert, then put it on the bill. Skip it.

So we've always gone to this Benihana, Plymouth Meeting Mall, location. Never disappointed, not in years. We ordered takeout. After ordering online, I was emailed a pickup time, 8:40pm. I showed up at 8:50 and the place was busy, in the weeds really. The manager did not know if my meal had been prepared, as they CUT the online takeout. What? I'm standing there wondering if my PAID receipt meant anything. Manager slips away, talking inaudibly behind his mask. I waited for a few, then eventually, at 9:04 pm, I confirmed that our order was in fact coming at some point. The hostess could not confirm when my order would arrive. I told her I'd return in 10 min. When I did, the order was there, at 9:15, 35 minutes late. 

When we arrived home, we found the servings were very small. The two 24oz hibachi dinners were not 24oz and the California Rolls were quite dinky. They forgot the Hot Sauce, $5 and I gave a $10 tip in advance. Total price for this birthday dinner for four, over $100.  And the icing on the cake, not once did anyone at Benihana say, sorry for the delay and ridiculous misunderstanding or thank you for your patience and tip. I was treated like they were just doing me a favor. It was a sad ending to a decade of enjoying this restaurant.

I love the sandwiches but the owner of this location might be the laziest person  on earth. I just tried to go here a short while ago while they are supposed to be open yet they were not. This happens on a regular basis. If you want primo go to another location to get it

Decided to Try this recycled restaurant location for breakfast today. Honestly, I have been rooting for this location since it's start as a Denny's. I have tried every new restaurant that has opened here in this location. The set up is still basic and clean. There is a fresh coat of paint on the walls and minimal decor. We were sat near the restroom in a pretty much open restaurant which irked me. I started to get flashbacks of our last horrible breakfast experience here when it was a different restaurant and got nervous but powered through. We decided to try their specialty items instead of our regular breakfast staples. Steak tips and eggs and skillet ala diabla. We added a side of gravy to have with our biscuit. The country gravy was my least favorite thing. It was basic and lacking in flavor. The biscuits were fantastic, fluffy and piping hot. We were told they make most items from scratch. I loved the ala diabla skillet. It was loaded with flavor. The steak tips were a little tough but well seasoned and it was a massive portion. The country potatoes were delicious. Overall a good breakfast! Fair priced for portion size and very fresh. 
The star of this review is really the service provided. Fernando was our outstanding server. He was energetic, and positive. He showed excitement over the menu and was honest when he wasn't sure about something. If he was asked for something, he would hustle to get it and was all smiles 100% of the time. Not to knock anyone else there, because everyone was nice and accommodating but He just had an energy about him that really made the environment and meal more enjoyable. Great job to that young man. Definitely model service and behavior that is missing in customer service today. We will be back and hopefully seated in his section!!
The hoagie was decent but for the price they should spend a bit more for the quality of their meat's.

Dined with my wife on Saturday night.

I started with the chicken liver parfait.  It was a generous portion of chicken liver pate with 2 slices of toasted multigrain bread.  The bread was a bit undertoasted.  Soft pate with soft bread isn't the best texture combination.  The chicken liver pate itself was tasty and rich.

Second course was the beef wellington.  I expected medium rare or rare, but got well done.  WAY overcooked.  The beef tasted steamed/boiled it was so overcooked.  The turnip puree was A-OK, and the pickled beets were nice. 

The saving grace of the meal was the sticky toffee pudding.  It was big enough to share for 2 people and was excellent.

The service was quite good -- attentive to drinks.
I've been to quite a few hibachi restaurants but this place was awesome. Plenty of food and the chef made all the tricks his own. Plus the steak was the best I've ever had in years.
My favorite mexican spot. Great service. Fast delivery. Delicious food.

The food was ok. It did not wow me that I need to return anytime soon. The temperatures seemed consistent with regulatory standards and the restrooms were very clean. The atmosphere is very nice and not your typical UNOs that I had been to in the past.  The fire place was burning nicely and provide a warm feeling on a cold overcast afternoon. Sadly the aesthetically pleasing environment of the restaurant and taste of the food could not begin to balance the customer service issue.   The waitress made a comment reviewing the drink menu and said these are our fruity drinks but you don't look like the fruity type- which could be construed in a number of ways by the wrong person when communicating with the public. Then we patiently waited over 30 minutes for our appetizer of nachos to arrive. Getting a refill on my beverage was a task, and the waitress wanted to bring the check and hurry us out before we were done. The restaurant was not even 1/2 full. I was disappointed with the service. There was not even an apology for the food being so late- despite indirectly finding out she failed to put it in the computer until 30 minutes after we arrived.  No ownership or accountability for the service just an attempt to rush us out the door. I will not be returning anytime soon!

This place was awesome! All of the dishes are color coded to match the price! They have a large variety of sushi, noodles, salads and soups. I got the chicken noodles and the seafood soup. They were both delish, especially the seafood soup. My only complaint is that they do not have shrimp sauce (which I love with my sushi). Overall, I'd rate this place a 4.5!

There was very little that went well with this visit. 
Let's start with the post-order spectacle that ended it. 

As I sat in the JIB lot with my order (because of a closed dining room) I watched an employee dispute spill outside and girls briefly grapple with each other before retreating back inside. Shortly after, one girl exited to yell and curse into her phone. Realizing she'd been locked out of the restaurant she began screaming, cursing, and banging on the door, demanding to be let in. 

Moments later her male friend drove up to offer his help and perform the same activities at a window. Not surprisingly, local constables showed up shortly afterward to sort things out. I'm guessing this isn't the type of work activity owners expect of employees. 

Before all that there was the poor drive-thru service with no order confirmation. I tried to get an answer whether my order was received but received a demanding "Pull around, please."

At the window I had to repeat my order because the employee hadn't gotten it earlier. Almost 15 minutes later the order arrived: Fries upside down in the bag, wrong sandwich with a smear of mayo on the wrapper that transferred to my slacks, and a special request ignored.

What was right? I got my Coke Zero. I think. It tastes so much like Coke it's hard to tell.

Roast Pork Italiano and grilled chicken Cesar salad. Both excellent options. All ingredients fresh, roll for sandwich perfect and chicken could not have been grilled any better! I don't review a lot but it was that good!
Great food and service! KFC is delicious. Great variety and atmosphere. Would recommend!

A small Moroccan vibe tucked away in an alley. This is a must try if you are looking to try some Moroccan food. It's a prefixed menu which has a few options for us to choose from. The chicken is a must try! Overall the service, hospitality is good.
Try the outdoor seating for a breath of fresh air.

This was hands down the BEST pasta dish I have had since moving to Nashville. I got the gnocchi with the mushroom cream sauce and it was incredible. The portions were huge, prices were low, and the pasta was authentic and fresh. 

My friend and I will definitely be back when we start craving pasta!

I would rate my experience lower if I could! The worst experience I have had at ANY restaurant. When we first arrived we were so excited the hostess was very nice but thats the extent of positive feedback! The restaurant was so smokey you could not breathe. We had to ask the waitress to turn on the so called exhaust above the table (which gave no relief). The waitress took 20 mins to acknowledge the table. She brought only half the table their drinks, then the remaining had to wait 15 mins longer. Once we all finished our salads, it took 45 mins for the cook to come to the table. We had to ask the waitress (when we were able to find her) what was going on. Once the cook came the smoke was even worse. I could not eat my food because I simply could not breathe. Let's not forget 2 people from the table had to hunt down another waitress for drink refills. Once the cook finished the little girl sitting beside me jumped up because the table was leaking water or grease all over her lap. She had to run to the bathroom to clean herself. I asked for the manager who replied that no one was supposed to be seated there because they were aware of the leak.......really?!?!?! WTH.......the manager gave everyone a 15% discount because of the wait but really they should have paid us to even eat there. Word of advice fix the exhaust systems, train your staff, fix the leaky table, and deep clean the dirty tables......... Just saying over 3 hours in this place was hell!!!

Go here for great Mexican food, but only if you're going in person. DO NOT I repeat DO NOT order online. I love this places food, but if you order online you will more and likely be missing items. I've order from the four times within the past 3 months and every time I have been missing items. Then the only thing to do is request a refund for them (which is fine), but it sucks because now you're out a meal or a drink. When I called to see about getting my items they said I would personally have to come get it. Which in my opinion is disappointing because I not only paid a delivery fee, but now I'd be using my own gas to go fix the mistake made by the restaurant. Personally I'm not going to drive 15 min away just to get a drink or a quesadilla that was forgotten. It's just upsetting when you order food and everyone in your house gets to eat, but now you have to go eat leftovers because your food was forgotten. Just eat here in person and you'll be better off.

I have been to las cazuelas a few times and it is def one of the more authentic Mexican places! The inside is very small but cozy and they have outdoor seating which is always a plus.  The first time I came here I got the appetizer sampler and chile relleno and they were just ok.  It wasn't till the next time that I realized how good this place is! Their arroz con pollo is amazing and one of the best dishes I've ever had! Their mole enchiladas r pretty good too w some of the best mole I've ever had!  
I love that this place is byot and their marg mixes r good esp the classic!

I didn't give this place 5 stars cuz the service can be bad sometimes depending on which server u get and for the so- so food on my first visit.

Oh and by the way, check out groupon, they have deals for this place from time to time!

We were lucky enough to get reservations to Hearthside on their second night and I must say that it was a fantastic experience. Phenomenal food and service. Stunning room.  BYO of course. 

 We went with the carpaccio, the Brussel sprouts, the prawns, the bucattini, risotto and the scallops. While it seems like a lot of food, the plates were small and perfect. The highlight were the risotto, scallops and the carpaccio. Great apple fritter dessert too. I anticipate reservations will be harder to come by in the coming weeks, but I definitely plan on visiting again.

After reading reviews that Green Basil happily accommodates vegan and vegetarian diets, I ordered delivery specifying that everything must be vegan like this: (***** VEGAN PLEASE *****). Normally if a restaurant cannot accommodate this, they have the courtesy of contacting me to let me know. Green Basil did not contact me and delivered two curries. Satisfied with the meal, we attempted to dine in a few days later. When we asked about making our curry vegan again, we were informed that they do not make vegan curries because they use fish sauce in all their curries. That means that instead of contacting me to let me know they could not make my curry vegan, they just sent me a non-vegan dish. Luckily I am not allergic to fish, but had there been an allergy that would be a VERY DANGEROUS situation. I am extremely unhappy that I was not contacted before my food was delivered to let me know my request could not be completed. I was, however, informed that I needed to approve a +$1 update since i added tofu to my veggie dish. To me, this is inexcusable and I cannot dine here again. I feel obligated to warn others who may encounter the same issue.

April 22, 2019. Gave this place another try. Ordered 8 piece chicken. They asked if ok that they had no legs, which we wanted. Then they came back and said never mind. We pulled up to the window and inquired about the chicken legs. They said all is good. We get home, no chicken legs. They are just awful there. Rolls were hard as hockey pucks. 

Prior review. Stopped by to order an 8 piece original dark meat and they had no original chicken ready. Would be a 16 minute wait. Really?
Amazing food,Great staff ! We went for grand opening the line moved very fast, they were very organized!

We arrived and didn't have to wait too long, it's decoration of a swamp scene highlighted with  black light was the best thing.  The food was okay we had crab legs crawdads prawns and veggies   The server only asked the two men what they were drinking my friend made a point to ask and then she walked away and never asked me.  Then our food came but not mine and then when we ordered a second round she didn't bring mine.  I was not drinking prior so I'm sure she wasn't concerned for that  I think she just couldn't figure out there were four people at the table

Really good! Cool atmosphere. I got the brisket and the smokey taste was just right. Not greasy like some other brisket. None of the sides looked like they were from a can, all seemed homemade. I went at 2:15 on a Saturday and it wasn't crowded or busy at all. The sauce was really good too! The chips and salsa are the bomb if you like a "smokey" taste. My dad got a meat trio and he said the grilled chicken was his favorite.
Very good food and quick,friendly service....we always go here for Chinese takeout!

This is my favorite pizza place in Santa Barbara. It's always really good, they don't skimp on toppings, flavor, or cheese. I think they might slice the veggies with a mandolin which I love. The sausage tastes high quality as does the pepperoni. Taste amazing cold too!! Yum!!

Bab cafe is VERY affordable, healthy food in Reno, something we're in desperate need of.  Both the "Top Bab" and the "Classic Bab" are great, with the former being served with cauliflower, broccoli, and baby carrots (almost certainly pre-cut and delivered to them in bags, but whatever) and the latter containing a variety of pickled, shaved vegetables.  If you're looking to go easy on the salt, go for the Top Bab option.

The Bulgogi (beef) is of a pretty low quality, and it shows - the flavor is a bit overpowered by some sort of powdered spice blend, and the texture and flavor of the beef itself isn't all that pleasant.  Fortunately, the Dak-Galbi (teriyaki chicken) is tender, delicious, and much more mild in flavor.  I used to order the beef bowls just because the price is so good, and the bowls can often be stretched into two meals, but since I've tried the chicken I haven't ever gone back to the beef.

Is Bab Cafe "fast food?"  Yeah, pretty much.  If they're not busy, your bowl will be ready in about two minutes, because all they have to do back in the kitchen is assemble it, really.  But at under $12 per bowl, their meals are filling, and if you eat your veggies, they can be pretty healthy as well!  If you're looking to cut down on sodium, order the chicken, go for the "Top bab" option with the steamed veggies, and try it without the sauce before you decide to add any.  Personally, I think it's plenty flavorsome without it.  

I'll be a regular customer as long as they keep doing what they're doing!

This place is quick and convenient. Has many options from sushi to bento boxes to Chinese entrees. Friendly staff and great prices on everything. Best sushi in Indy. They're way better then 45 degrees!

As a young kid I always remember going to Ralph's for the best italian food in Philly. Well now that it is the oldest italian restaurant in America this must be the best in America. It has that home feel to it and the staff is wonderful. 

The place is always packed and loud which is what I love! Delicious bread and wine to start the meal with the best mussels - we couldn't get enough of the sauce!! 

For dinner my friends had veal parm, chicken parm and a penne in vodka sauce which were all delicious. I got linguine with clams - the clams are so amazing and the broth is to die for! Portions are extremely generous - always leftovers for tomorrows lunch! :) 

To end the meal make sure you get a piece of the cannoli cake. I love desserts and this has to be one of my absolute favorites! 

If you want an at home feel with simply delicious food this is the place to be. We always leave happy and very full! This place may be over a 100 years old but I have a feeling it will be around for at least 100 more!
Great food, great friends.  We celebrated my neighbor's birthday here.  We had the back room with our own private bathroom.  I highly recommend it if you go in with a group.

15% off for paying cash right now!!! The food was delicious. The service by Mary was excellent. The Crab Rangoon was different but really good. It was peppery and very crispy. The Pad Thai and Yakisoba were I think the best I have ever had. Both tasted so fresh and very flavorful. We will definitely be back!!

I was there with a group of about 15+ people. We were seated quickly and our wait staff was very attentive. As usual my ribeye was cooked to perfection. I believe everyone was pleased with their meal. I don't know what more could be said about Outback Steakhouse. This location is consistently busy, but the staff never gives you the feeling of being stressed. They work together and make sure everyone is served seamlessly. Large groups don't have to worry about their dining needs being met.

First time here and it exceeded my expectations! Did a pick up order, but couldn't figure out where to park to pick up. Spots close to the restaurant were taken, so wish they had a pick up spot.

The place is shower cute and very clean. Staff is nice and my order was at the pick up area on a shelf. 

I had their limeade which was refreshing and not too sweet. Ordered their Falafel wrap and it was delicious. Better than I expected.

I will say it was one of the best falafels I have had in the area. All the veggies were fresh and pita was fantastic. The dressing was so good but my fave were the crispy chickpeas! The portion was on the generous side. Definitely going back.

Here's a pic but my photo does no justice, because I needed to jump on a conference call and was hungry.

SAFE FOR GLUTEN FREE/CELIAC :) Great food! Good wine and beer list. Good service, good atmosphere, nice location, just an all around nice place to go in VC. Classy but not over-priced, quieter than most places in VC which was a nice change of pace. Food options for people with various dietary needs. Thanks for a great night!

A special meal. My wife and I went here for my birthday. The staff were very good hosts and the food was fantastic. First things first, there's no corkage fee, so bring a bottle and enjoy. We had the flamenkuche for an appetizer. It's a flat bread with caramelized onions, cheese, and the most flavorful, smoky bacon. The fennel seeds really cinch the dish. We then had crab and corn bisque, which had a nice kick of red pepper at the finish, and the beet salad, which is better than it sounds. It's beets, a mix of greens, blue cheese, and spicy pistachios. The only criticism I could offer is that the beets would be more appealing if julienned instead of sliced. For the main, we had the shrimp en cocotte, and that's definitely the dish to get your first time. [Side note: I've had a burger before, too, and it's one of the most under-rated in town. The price is high, but the meat mix is perfect and the result is great.] For sides, we added the roasted vegetables and, of course, the frites. You really can't go there and not have the frites. Dessert was steamed chocolate with salted caramel ice cream, which sounds a little strange but tastes great.

Our waiters were pros -- they anticipated needs/wants and met them. Besh, that old Marine, sets a high standard for his restaurants and Luke lives up to it.
First time here. Not many ppl fast service. Prices good, if you like a little spice the burgers are it.

Panera at this location is a good one.   Got soup and a sandwich and now I have a new favorite!   The squash soup with Indian flavors was absolutely tasty!   The aroma alone from the soup was an experience!   And the chicken chipotle sandwich!   Heavenly!   I went back the next day for exactly the same meal!    Yummmmmm tasted fresh and clean too.   The mac and cheese side however was literally 5 bites.  It covered the bottom of the small bowl and wasn't enough lunch for an adult even with the half sandwich.   So they need to work on the portions there.   Will I come back?   Ummm YesYesYes.   Just not for mac and cheese.....lol

Another delicious meal to celebrate our December anniversary. Loved everything about it.
We always start out with their toasted ravs, then the excellent Caesar salad. Next we tried the Chicken Soto entree which was fabulous. We ended with a slice of classic Tiramisu. Thank you for another great evening dining with you.

Not a whole lot of direction when we entered - had to ask a lot of questions to get an answer on where we could sit. 

Whipped feta as an app was delish. Figs in bacon were nothing special and fairly small for the price. 

Pork Belly Gnocchi was under-seasoned and didn't have any pork belly flavor

The trout entree was good, but not the correct fish to serve whole (because of all the bones). It made eating a chore and wasn't appetizer. 

Staff was friendly. 

Undecided if we'll return.
Food was good but selection was a bit small. The highlight for me was the Dim Sum and the chilled mussels. For the price, it is certainly worth it at around 14 per person

Came here for a fun and fancy date night, and it was AMAZING!!!! our server TJ was super knowledgeable and definitely recommended some delicious dishes. super great atmosphere, and a complimentary dessert because it was our anniversary  highly recommend!!

Not a great experience here. The store recently reopened and I was hoping the service had improved since the reopen. Unfortunately not. First I waited over 20 minutes to get my food. Not exactly fast food. So you would think if I wait long for my order I'd get it right. Wrong. My order was both incorrect and missing items. I'd recommend finding another Taco Bell location if you really want Taco Bell.

Been here 5 times and this place is beyond my exceptions at each experience. The food is delectable and the presentation is outstanding. The service is professional and knowledgeable. The owner and manager I have met on many occasions and the owner is super engaging. The manager even spoke to my friend in detail about gluten free options and bought us all deserts, as my friend was opening up a gluten free bakery location. He even gave us contacts regarding similar connections for her. The roasted chicken in exceptional, and love their hottest sauces for chips, which they still cannot out hot me. The beef tacos make it yourself portions are ridiculously tasty and super fun. I appricate cultural ties as there are direct ties to Mexico at Rostizado, which isn't always necessary, but that can make a huge difference. For example, Japanese in Edmonton at its more than 100 locations, is really reduced almost entirely to the handful of actual Japanese culturally run locations making a huge difference. Rostizado is all around one of the best restaurants in the city.

Excellent fare  and ingenious menu with great tastes. Everything was a taste treat. Service sucked tho and was very spotty
 Almost like they needed more servers. Not that they were not friendly but could not consistently serve our meal.
The food is always delicious and fresh. I've used them to cater my daughter's graduation party as well. I ordered the day off and my food was ready! I highly recommend them.

One of the most unique food trucks around, which is visually apparent when you walk past a slew of sandwich trucks to get to this brightly-colored vehicle. As popular as this place is, I've never had to wait more than 10 minutes for my food; big props to the staff for running an efficient and delicious operation! To Penn students - it is worth the hike!

My favorite order, and the most famous one, is the Capn' Crunch Tilapia Burrito, consisting of lettuce, tomato, avocado, and two big slabs of cereal-encrusted fish, all covered in sriracha mayo and bundled in a flour tortilla. As weird as it sounds, the breading only imparts a slightly sweet touch while still giving the fish a satisfying crunch. The mix of textures and flavors in this burrito is amazing!

Another classic order is their twist on chicken katsu, a sizeable breaded chicken cutlet (but not with Capn' Crunch) served with rice and salad. It comes topped with that yummy spicy mayo, and a more traditional brown savory sauce with sprinkles of dry seaweed. Once again, what a great combo of textures and flavors! 

I once tried their chicken satay platter, since I'm a fan of those skewers in most Southeast Asian restaurants. The meat was dry for my liking, but the peanut sauce was as good as any. I tried getting the peanut sauce on my katsu once for kicks - nice that they let you customize.

Last but not least, the Thai tea! Wonderfully sweet and milky while still having a deep tea flavor. It's cheaper than standard bubble tea places but comes with a lot of ice, so the value for quantity is probably comparable, but the quality of the drink makes it still worth it.

Amazing Food and the wait staff are awesome. They have many choices from typical comfort food to healthy choices and breakfast all day. 
I prefer the Cobb Salad when I go. The portions are amazing and the food is always fresh. Outdoor seating is available as well as typical booths, tables or bar seating.

There were more memorable items than not while eating at Spice Finch and as the rating suggests, I would come here again. We had 4 people total, so the food options were plentiful. 

The dishes that I'm still thinking about the day after: Charred Octopus, Baked Cheese, Lamb Ribs and Cauliflower I could eat those 4 items daily if it was gonna run me major $$ or be a minor health hazard.

The dishes that did not spark the same level of zeal: Chili Chicken Kebab, Shishito Peppers, Little Neck Clams, and an item that we ordered, never received--Bean Targine.

After reading reviews from others we decided to check this place out. It was Sunday morning and a little busy which was a good sign. 

My husband had the eggs Benedict which was pretty good and I had the Greek omelet which had a little different taste. The eggs were watered down and were tasteless but there was something else I couldn't put my finger on which was not good and after picking around at the omelette I set it aside. The hash browns tasted like they were cooked in used oil as well.  I had a quarter of the waffle we shared  and that was okay. 

Service was a little inattentive maybe because they were semi busy but my husband had to wait a while for his coffee and remind the waiter to get it. 

After my disappointing meal, while paying the check we were asked if everything was okay at which my husband informed them that I did not care for my meal.  I rarely complain about meals and chocked it off to somewhere I didn't care for but Instead of apologizing or offering to make it right the person said everything was made fresh and everyone's tastebuds are different. 

This same person cleared the table and it was obvious that I barely touched my breakfast. She didn't ask them if everything was okay. Since it was our first time I felt since they didn't know us she didn't care. Obvious also that they had "regulars" who they definitely took care of better than us. 

I guess we didn't fit their clientele profile well enough for them to care about providing good service or care about the food they serve. I was offended by that remark and will definitely not return. How rude.
I just had the food i was suprised It was really good ......especially the shrimp egg roll.
We ordered their signature drinks along with their catfish, jambalaya, gumbo, and more, and it was wonderful! Excellent service!

I visited this restaurant yesterday with my family. We were a party of 13 and were treated horribly by this place, especially the 2 waitresses and the busboy. First, they didn't greet us when we came in the restaurant. No instructions on where to seat so we just picked the biggest table for our party. They didn't ask for our drinks and just brought out a bunch of water after we already made the order. I personally had to get the waitress attention so that we can order our food. We ordered a total of 3 orders of bo 7 mon along with other dishes like pho, rice plates. The food was basic and the service was HORRIBLE. When we ordered a bowl of pho without green onion, cilantro, etc. for 3 little kids under the age of 5 to share. The waitress brought out 2 big bowls of pho instead and blamed that she heard 2 of us say we want to order pho. We didn't complaint and even gladly take them because didn't want to creat any fuss about it. The waitresses were not attentive, they barely checked on our food until we had to raise our hands and asked for more like vegetables, rice noodles, rice paper, etc. 
Also, we were being told 3 times by the bus boy to not let our 3 little kids play with their small bowls and to not put them back to the chopstick holder. First of all, he should not talk to customers in that rudely manner. He can kindly ask. Secondly, we know better than that and don't need to be schooled like a little child. 

This is an unbelievable experience!!! We spent over $200 at this place for these people to treat us so nasty. They need to have better customer service skills and listening skills. Never again we would come back to this place!!!

We had a work luncheon in the evening yesterday and I needed something easy to serve each individual. Bellacino's was a suggestion so I put together an order of 25 full size grinders. I called to explain and spoke to Hunter. He asked me to fax in the order. I also asked if they could write each persons name on the boxes for easy indentification. Everything went off without a hitch. The sandwiches arrived ahead of schedule, they were hot and delicious. Each employee stated hw good they were. So thank you Bellacino's for making this a success and thank you Hunter for making it a simple porocess.

This spot serves street tacos which you won't find often here. I had a taste of all their meat choices and wasn't disappointed. Their al pastor is also legit! They also have frozen mixed drinks which are perfect for the summer heat with the fun. The service was friendly and food came quick for takeout.

Well Florida.. we finally did it. We got decent Chinese food. It took, well forever, but it's finally here. 

Tried the General Tso's and Orange chicken, both pretty damn good.

Rice was on point. 

Egg rolls, while nuclear hot for a period of time that almost didn't add up scientifically, very good.

We'll be back, and you should too. If they don't stay open we have only each other to blame.
My food was delicious! The service was excellent. I had tried their new watermelon mint drink, it was really good and refreshing.

Hands down best Indian in town. I think they are one of few if not only Indian dinner buffet. I especially like their dosa themed Thursday night buffet where they will make you fresh to order dosa includes within your buffet price! We went today and experienced their Sunday bbq buffet where you can order vegetarian and non vegetarian kabobs also included in the buffet price! Buffet food is always fresh with a vegetarian and non vegetarian sections. Must try!
I wish my kids didn't love Taco Bell so much because I'd never go back.  Rudest employees EVER!!! They are ALWAYS slow and rude - ALWAYS!!! I hate giving them my business!!!

Solely for food, I would give 10 out of 5 seriously. But drinks are mehhh so I am settling for 3 stars. 

Guys, if you want a cheap Taco Tuesday deal and you want to stay in Metairie area, this is it. I mean, THIS IS IT. Los Jefes got music on, a cute little patio, a pretty decent size bar and an ample dining area. Please see one of the attached pictures but they have 5 tacos for $5 on Tuesdays. I have been to multiple Mexican Restaurants where they offer $1 tacos on Tuesdays but by far, this location beats everywhere else. Super generous portion! They have daily specials from Monday to Friday so I definitely will be back to check out other specials beside Tuesday. 

So here comes the Mehhh part. I am sorry but your margarita is not my fav :( it is made with super sugary sour mix and I feel like Tequila deserves higher companion than that! They had Avion two for one special going on but hmm... was not really feeling it. 

Anyway, definitely come check this place out. You might like their margarita, who knows. Oh by the way, free chips and salsa!
I've been eating here since I was a kid. It never disappoints. Everything is good which makes decisions on what to order difficult at times.

Great food. Plenty of choice for veggies which is a big deal when you're travelling in the US. Good service. Only downside was the music - bit intense - was thrash metal or some variant the night we were there. Lots of the customers seemed into it but for me it was just a bit much. Didn't stop me enjoying the food though. Would certainly go again.

I took a group of 10 there last night and it was fantastic!  The chips and salsa are very good, the margaritas are excellent, and the food was amazing with large portions at extremely reasonable prices!  The service is the best I've ever seen...they're always refilling the chips and salsa and checking up if you need anything.  I highly recommend this place to Mexican food lovers!  We'll be back!

I hate raw oysters. Haven't eaten them in ~20 years. But had to try the grilled oysters at Drago's, and I'm so glad I did. They are so tasty, and when they're all gone, you get a piece of French bread to sop up all of the smoky deliciousness of the drippings. Wow!

TERRIBLE FOOD! My party and I all ordered different pasta and ended up with the same Alfredo based pasta. No seafood in the pasta and not as advertised. Alfredo tasted like jar pasta, no mushrooms and Luke warm. The pasta also included scallops, there was ONE small small small almost like bay scallops. ONE!l SCALLOPS!!!!! It was so disappointing, the scallop was definitely from the freezer. It is a seafood spot, I would expect fresh seafood. The waiter was nice and trying to make it right but you can tell the kitchen was just not at their best. The pasta I ordered wasn't even Alfredo, it was an garlic oil pasta. What the hell. We sent it back and it still came back Alfredo based and not the right pasta. They added ONE more scallop and a couple mushrooms.To  me it was really half ass. Again, The  Waiter is not at fault but food is definitely not worth it. They ended up not charging one pasta but it still wasn't worth the money spent, I could have made better seafood pasta at home. The waiter told the manager the situation and not once did the manager come over and address us and apologize, terrible. DO NOT WASTE YOUR MONEY!!!!!!!!!

Total dive probably worth only 3.5 stars, but they had pretty solid tacos. It's just not the quality of seasoning or yumminess that I have come to expect from out of the way Mexican food trucks. It wasn't bad, and it might have been an off day, but I was definitely underwhelmed across the board.
Delicioso!

The food was fantastic , the broths delicious and savory. Service was great.

I have one main concern that will keep me from returning . The ventilation is terrible, and based on the space layout , there is not good air flow. As a result, your clothes and jacket and anything you bring in will absolutely reek of potent Mongolian spices for a very long time. Do not wear anything that can't be washed in a standard washing mine here .

Again food was great , but not worth the Hassle for me.

Best sushi in the suburbs. Better than Norristown's Bluefin, waaaay better than Minado sushi buffet. The sushi chef and young man who serves take pride in what they do. Extremely fresh and non of that supermarket/previously frozen taste.
The details: Across from the outback steakhouse look for a blue sign. We've never had a problem with parking. One of the biggest draws is the BYOB aspect. Small intimate dining room.
My only complaint is that there is no white tuna when I'm there!
Takeout orders are accepted and the take credit cards.
I'll probably be back next week, it's great when you find such a great restaurant so close by!

This was the first time I've been here for a long time and prices were higher than I expected. 

They have a pretty good kids menu. The Mac & cheese was not on it but I ordered it for my 2 year old anyway and shouldn't have. It's very peppery which is prob why it's not on the kids menu. They offered to make some without pepper but it would take 15 mins so I got him a grilled cheese instead. It still took a while to get it. I liked that the sandwich wasn't swimming in butter as I often get at restaurants. The fries were really good. 

I got a BLT with green beans. The beans were amazing with bacon and onions! Yum! 

The wait staff was really great. I was just not expecting a $21 bill for one adult and child at a diner.
