Ok i love waffle house the food is alway good. This place was packed. There was one table we sat at. The service was fast. We got our drinks in no time. My grandsons and I ordered our food and the food came out quick. The staff was friendly as well. I can't believe this pack was that packed and they were able to give us excellent service. The owner should be proud of this crew.
We ordered a huge pizza and Hoagie lunch yesterday for a work meeting and it was absolutely awesome! Thanks For the delivery with a smile and a fantastic lunch for our team.

Number 1 China is delicious. I only get dumplings and veggie-fried rice, but they are really good. The perfect amount of veggies in the rice, and with each entree they give you a little packet with a fortune cookie, fork, and sauces. They are extremely quick with delivery as well, so you're never waiting a long time for your order. My go-to Chinese place!
Service was acceptable and the pizza was tasty, but nothing to write home about. Usually, I rate a place on whether I would go back again, and I probably wouldn't go back there.

Currently one of my favorite restaurants in the city, much like its sister Bing Bing, it serves great food along with great service with a self-deprecating whimsy, a flair for the dramatic, from someone who knows they are dead on in delivering amazing flavors.  They don't need to overhype the experience, it's really good and they revel in sharing that experience with you.  Great prices considering the quality, and the cocktail program is very solid (omg Beachin).  Wish it was bigger and next to my house!

Had lunch with my wife and we were happy in so many ways. We ordered off the lunch menu and had many nice, inexpensive choices. I had a shredded beef taco with tamale (no sauce). Taco was packed with good tasting, tender meat. The tamale was much better than most restaurant tamales. Really enjoyed both. My wife's chicken burrito was very large and full of meat (no bean filler). Terrific! All for under $15!!! Service was outstanding! We'll be back.

Love this little spot on Girard Ave that's given me a big appreciation for Indian food. The food is everything you'd expect it to be: flavorful, colorful, and delicious. My favorite on the menu so far is the Chicken Tikka Masala and the garlic naan, but have also tried the mango chicken and mint chicken. Every meal was excellent; you can barely keep yourself from using up every bit of the sauces. 

The restaurant is very clean and tidy and the service is always fast and friendly. As others have mentioned, the menu may seem a bit overpriced; especially because the portions don't look large at first. But it is always filling and flavorful and ultimately, worth it.

One of the things I hate about traveling, is discovering sandwiches like "Da" Duck. I thought I knew a few great duck sandwiches, I was wrong. Also, our waitress had that great southern hospitality. I didn't love the fries, but the chicken gumbo was also delicious. Eat "Da" Duck, dammit!

I want to love Nora's but I'm not quite there.

Today we had the clam chowder. It was good, but nothing to write home about. Definitely had a super fishy odour! If I had some oyster crackers & hot sauce,  I could've made something happen.

I tried the Hangover. I really liked the bread & the thinly-sliced fresh turkey & salami. The sandwich was not hot though, instead it was warm on the outside, & cool in the middle. The grain mustard was nice but there was way too much & the flavour was way over-bearing. Upon dissecting my sandwich, to see if I could scrape some off, I discovered that the mustard was mixed with the bacon. So I let it be.

Husband had the cold turkey & seemed to enjoy himself. 

The one thing that was really disappointing was the patio salad. There was absolutely no love there. Super duper bland. It needed salt, pepper, onion, mustard, celery, something! 

I'll be back to try some of the other soups, beer cheese looks amazing.

Easily a top 10 memorable place for my GI ... yes, this place is that special and that good.

Artisan crafted pizza with fabulous options for The People ... come one, come all ... Gluten-Free, Vegan, Gluten-Free Vegan, Wishy-Washy Vegetarian, Carnivore.

The ambiance of Nobles is a relief to all your senses.  Pure respite from the bustling and noisy nearby Casinos.  Appreciated their cool, dark and modern decor ... thank you NPP!

Wonderfully consistent quality from one of the best seafood places in Boise. The seating is limited, however, so plan on arriving after 7:30, go on a holiday when many Boiseans are out of town (we went on July 5), or just be patient. The wait is worth it. I had Captain Alex's Seafood Platter this time, and every mouthful was succulent.

Husband and I went here for date night a few weeks ago. We got there at 6 and there was already a 55 minute wait so we opted to sit at the bar. The wine pour was definitely well worth it, but the Manhattan my husband ordered was served on the rocks vs standard straight up. Really no big deal, just not what he prefers (he should've probably specified). We had the hummus, guacamole sampler which came out quickly and was very, very good. He ordered the salmon and I had the scallops. For his first time ordering salmon in a restaurant (he's usually a steak guy) he was slightly disappointed when it came out a bit over cooked. The scallops were definitely perfectly seasoned and cooked to my liking. Service was excellent!
The absolute best Italian food in town for the money, great portions, and exceptional salads

Delish! It's 95 degrees outside but it's cool and delicious in here. My fruit bowl was fresh and plentiful and my friends pancakes were big and yummy! Everyone left full! Oh and my coffee was good (the only reason I didn't give it 5 stars was because there was a hair in it).

I'm not sure why there are so many low reviews. I've been here 5 times now with the family and it's always a great experience. The food is delicious and the servers are always friendly and responsive.

The bag o donuts is amazing too, if you have room at the end.
Love this place!  Have been four times; the food is consistently fresh and delicious. The staff is super friendly and vibe is very positive.

Came in on Saturday afternoon about 2pm. We were seated right away, waiter was nice. Appetizers came right away, surprising actually... And then it was all downhill... Half of our party didn't get food with the other half and when it came it was wrong?!? One order was wrong twice! Experience was so bad they had to comp the check. Will not likely return. Check the menu, prices are higher than I would have expected for this type of food...

I do not take this lightly when I say it, but wow - best crab cakes in Tampa! Seriously. They aren't even in the shape of a cake, because they have so much crab and so little filler in them. You are given two very large cakes for the entree. I ended up eating one for dinner, and taking the other one home for a sandwich for lunch the next day. We ended up getting there a bit early for our reservation, so we sat out on the patio and indulged in the happy hour. Amazing price on both the food and the appetizers. We got the ahi tuna wontons and they were so tasty. I could have eaten multiple plates of them. It is on the pricy side, but if you are looking for a good seafood restaurant, close to Tampa, located right on the water, Chart House is your spot!

HORRIBLE FOOD AND HORRIBLE SERVICE. 
My food arrived cold, I was charged a different total than what I was charged when I ordered online, and when I called to correct my stale, cold food, I was told that no manager was there and that I should call back in a few hours. I said that it was unacceptable to not resolve this issue, and even asked for them to call the manager. No owner, manager, or anyone to take care of the problem was on hand, and I ended up getting yelled out by a worker that didn't understand what I was saying. She said the owner or manager just left. Wouldn't tell me when they would be in next or anything. I have worked in the restaurant industry for over a decade, and I have never heard of a place leaving someone in charge without proper training on how to handle mishaps. I will NEVER go back!

This place gets 5 stars for a few reasons. It's something different. You won't find many places around Marlton where you can get take out Greek food at a very affordable price. I've been three or four times and every time I get the fresh made hummus and kabobs. The hummus is drizzled with olive oil, seasoned with paprika, and topped with kalamata olives. The kabob entrees come with homemade pita and Greek salad. I'd highly recommend this place for lunch or dinner any night.

The Greek Lady is a great lunch spot for students as well as individuals working in the area! I ordered the Lamb Gyro and for the price and size it was delicious! It definitely kept me full for hours!

Best spring rolls I've ever had, I just wish it was bigger. Had drunken noodles for the first time and it was good but there was not enough chicken (barely found 4 pieces). I would go there again to try Pad Thai. Service was great.

Met with long-time friends from out of state to catch up and show them a bit of the Boise area. It was fantastic! Service was excellent (even though I showed up awkwardly early) they were super friendly and took care of me while I waited. Food was excellent which really helped me to show-off the Boise area.

Was perfect lunch stop after the WWII museum. Gumbo was good, oyster po boy very good and craw fish quesadillas also yummy. Balsamic dressing is a bit too sweet if we had to lodge a criticism but this is made up by 40 taps, bottomless mimosa's and a great waitress,,, Sheri.
Don't expect white gloves and fine linens,,, just an honest good place to grab a comfortable meal. Enjoy.

Twice in one day.  Need I say more?   Hot Sausage the first time and Cheese Burger the second time.  Both incredibly delicious.  Service is very friendly.  We hope to make this a stop every time we come to town.

My new favorite hole in the wall place to eat in Santa Barbara. Sure there other places to eat, but this places keeps it puro. I had the pozole, tamal, taco bistec, and horchata. Yum. The line was long, but most places with good food is long. The best part is that they don't make the tortilla until you place an order, so it guaranteed fresh.
Went day 2 and were blown away with the food, service, ambiance & drinks! We will be regulars here. Loved it!

Ghinis is one of my favorite places for breakfast in town. Their biscuits and gravy are my favorite and they also have fantastic omelettes. The attached bakery is a favorite with perfect croissants (flaky, buttery, and soft) and out of this world eclairs (great chocolate and cream but not overly sweet). My only advice is to not head there in a hurry, it's often busy and even when it isn't there can be a bit of wait for food after you're seated. I don't mind because the food is good and worth the wait and the staff is often kind but if you're looking to eat and leave quickly that might not be possible on a weekend.

We went here specifically to get bubble tea and we literally waited 15 minutes while they did not make our teas. Several people got their to go orders and made orders after us. Then they weren't even very good. Extremely bland. Maybe I'm just spoiled on good bubble tea but ill never go back

A co-worker and I went to try this restaurant out for the first time during lunch last Friday.  It was busy, but not uncomfortable.  I had the Bangkok fried rice with chicken and got the Thai hot.  I expected it to be more spicy, but it was alright.  I enjoyed the Tom yum soup and the spring roll that comes with the lunch meal.  My co-worker ordered the Drunken Noodles and he said that he really enjoyed them.  I still like Thai 
Spice a little more near Greenwood, but we will be returning to Siam for more lunches in the near future.  Price was decent as well.

This place is the bee knees! Their "number 1" sandwich is seriously the best chicken sandwich I've had - EVER! Honestly everything I've tried here was insanely delicious. I find myself having to talk myself out of ordering from here numerous times a week. * yes, it's truly THAT good. * This place is a must try!

My GOD.

Okay, so Philly is known for the Philly Cheesesteak, and YES, I did try one, so you can't even knick me on that.. BUT. What it should be known for is literally the best grilled cheese I have ever had in my life.

It gets better. Let's discuss the details of this mighty sandwich, shall we? I got the "Red White and Green", which, in comparison to the details of the other sandwiches sounds plain, is in fact a trio of perfection. This sandwich had fresh mozzarella (That they spin daily. DAILY!), freshly grilled tomatoes (with garlic), and baby spinach. Repeat after me... PERFECTION.

Anyways, the sandwich is quick to be made, the ingredients are extremely fresh and have a wonderful taste, and the workers are friendly! What more can you ask for? 

C'est fantastique.
Overpriced cheap frozen food. Save the trip and buy the baged garbage the the grocery store and make it yourself. We desperately need the local back...

Best Mexican food I've had, everything was extremely fresh. We went when it was packed so the service was a little slow, but the waitress apologized and even gave us some delicious complimentary flan for desert. We'll definitely be back in the future.

One of my besties is a local in NOLA and when she told me this place had the best char grilled oysters, I couldn't not go. And it's true. Their oysters were delicious and so full of flavor! To be fair though, we didn't try oysters at any other spot during our trip to NOLA, so I didn't have anything to compare it to. We also tried the gumbo and that was pretty good, too. I'm not a huge gumbo fan, but I did not regret ordering some especially during an unusually cold day in NOLA. The atmosphere wasn't my favorite - very corporate-like if that makes any sense, but it's what I expected. Still, it wouldn't stop me from going back to get another couple dozen of their char grilled oysters the next time I'm in NOLA.

4 stars for the food, an extra star for value.  I got a 18" Los Olivos pizza, which was perfectly cooked with a great crust.  It was delicious and could have fed a family of four pretty easily.  At $9.25 for a well-made gourmet pizza with olives, artichokes, etc., it's probably the best value for pizza in Tucson.  They also have $5 pizzas.  

Not a place to come for a romantic Italian dinner, though; there are places to sit (including on the porch) but there is no ambiance.
Excellent NY pizza!!! Slices served pipping hot just shy of burn the roof of your mouth. Great deal on slices

Best Pizza in Delaware county.. Period !  The crust is perfect every time.  Call ahead & order & they'll have it ready for dining in when you get there.  It's not fancy by any means but it has character.  Friendly waitresses They also make a great house special cheese steak with tomato & ham !

Their chargrilled oysters are the best you'll ever have.  I have been there 3 times and have had no less and 2 dozen every time. The gumbo is also very good.  Sorry that I can't comment on the rest of the menu; maybe someday I'll get to it but probably not as long as they dont run out of oysters.   It's a big place with a few bars.  They have always been busy when I was there.  I like to sit at the bar in front of the grill where the oysters are made.
The food was delicious!  I had the chicken shawarma, my wife had the gyro sandwich. We both really liked it.  The owners were really nice too!  We will definitely come back.

My friend has been raving about this place since he was introduced so I had to try it out. I was apprehensive at first because we were going to a Thai restaurant for breakfast but I'm glad I gave it a try. The food was fresh, made to order, I believe all of it was made from scratch, at least the chorizo was made there. I ordered the Mexican breakfast with tortillas and other dishes ordered were ham and American cheese omelet, biscuits and gravy with a side of eggs and home fried potatoes, french toast and our appetizer was an order of spring rolls with the rice paper wrapping. 

First off, the coffee, in my opinion, was the best coffee I have ever had at a sit down diner ever. They use small thermal carafes so the coffee doesn't sit long and it's not sitting on a hot plate which makes the coffee bitter after a while. I asked if it was Colombian coffee, he said it was imported Thai coffee, which I have never had before but in my experience, every drop was smooth and good.  

I wanted to try the Thai eggs, but wasn't sure if it would be too skimpy for me as I was really hungry so I got the Mexican eggs that has chorizo because I had a chorizo burrito from NIco's yesterday and I wanted to make a comparison. The chorizo was fresh and apparently they make it there at the restaurant. They mix it with a lot of cheese, some jalapenos and then have their perfectly cooked home fried potatoes on the side. You get a choice of toast or tortillas, so I had the tortillas which also tasted like they were made fresh. I was worried when it first came out that it wouldn't fill me up, but I think because of the cheese it just stuck to my ribs and I was satisfied. The eggs were mixed in with the cheese and chorizo and weren't over cooked or under cooked. 

I was able to try their sausage gravy from my friends biscuits and gravy and I really liked it too. It was definitely home made and not too thin or starchy. It goes great with the biscuits or just as a side to your breakfast. The tofu spring rolls were also very fresh and delicious. The rice paper wrapper was not rubbery or mushy. The vegetables were really crisp and I believe ( I don't really know because I sucked it down so fast ) the tofu was firm. Anyhow, it was very satisfying at the least. 

My friends daughter ordered the French toast which was fluffy, sweet and had a gorgeous cinnamon crust that made it not too overly sweet and not too mushy. I'm not a big fan of sweets but once again, the French toast was fresh tasting and worth the try. 

If you're in a hurry, this is not the best place to go, because I think they make a lot of things from scratch and with a lot of care. Two of our dishes came out and then our friends didn't come out for another few minutes. I saw some people that were getting a little impatient, but I'm sure when they got their food, that all went away. I didn't mind the wait, especially because of the delicious smooth coffee that I had to sip on. 

I don't usually write reviews until I have gone to a restaurant more than once, but this was so good that I had to share. I will do a follow up when I go back for some Thai food, but until then I will sit back and enjoy this wonderful food high that I have received from my new favorite restaurant.

Loved their pad see u (no idea if I'm spelling that right). I got it with chicken, and asked for it extra spicy, and they delivered. It was delicious! My girlfriend got the pineapple fried rice with chicken, and loved hers too. We'll be back, and highly recommend you try it too!

Got the Meat Lovers Calzone ($9.99), which might have been a special because I ordered it off their chalkboard and didn't even look at the menu. Aside from it being really oily, I was really happy with the size of it and value! My picture isn't the best, doesn't do it justice to show all the meat that was inside..but I was satisfied with all the meat inside. I think it was Italian sausage, ham, and something else...to be honest, I wasn't paying attention because I was really hungry from walking around in 97 degree weather so I was just stuffing my face. :)  I also liked that the bread wasn't too thick of fluffy, filling me up and leaving less room for the meat. I wasn't able to finish it all by myself, and I think it's great to share.

Their seafood was excellent. Server was very kind. Great service. One issue. Their fryer was broken. This eliminated a ton of food options, especially for me, a person who lives off of fried foods. Otherwise 5/5.

Just enjoyed a wonderful meal at Main Street Pizzeria and Grille.  This is the 1st time that I was here and wanted to sample a few items and write this review.  The restaurant it tastefully decorated and clean.  Very appealing to the eye.  Sampled a few things with my friends.  Had the sweetie pie pizza.  AMAZING.  Loved it.

We enjoyed the calamari as an appetizer.  It was out of this world!  Cooked to perfection and served with sliced cherry peppers and a side of sauce.  Yummy.  The homemade chicken salad is the best I have had in such a long time.  It was so so so good.  The cheeseburgers were huge!  They were cooked to perfection and came with lettuce/tomato/onion/pickles and chips.

The staff members were friendly, polite and willing to assist with any needs.  This is a fantastic restaurant that I will frequent more often in the near future!

Get there early.... It gets really crowded!

I am usually at KOP on a sorta regular basis to eat at one of their food courts and I eat at Five Guys whenever I am extremely hungry.

The first time I ate here, I was a little hesitant since it was much more expensive than what I was used to (McD's, BK, etc).  The guy at the counter basically said to me, "Trust me, it's worth it." He was right.  That little bacon cheeseburger with grilled onions, mushrooms, and A1 sauce was amazing!  The fries were amazing as well although I felt that the small order was way too much.  I returned to the counter when I was done and said to him, "You were right" and he smiled in agreement.

I have eaten there a few more times over the past several months and the burgers and fries have always been amazing.  The staff has always been very nice to me, even offering me a free refill of my drink from time to time, since I usually sit close by.  That kind of service coupled with the amazing attention to detail with the food has shied me away from burger places like McD's and BK.  Five Guys has kind of raised my standards for what a burger should be like.  I just make sure to eat there only when I'm absolutely hungry as the portions are huge. 

I am unsure if they do this at this location, but I know that you can preorder online through their website and just stop over and pick up your order.  This is useful if you are in a hurry or if you have a really big order.  I have seen this done once at another location when a bunch of friends of mine were placing a big order for 8 of us.  They messed up our order but felt so bad for the mess-up, they gave us our corrected order and extra fries for free.  We were extremely full that night with 2 burgers and a huge amount of fries, but were so satisfied from the meal.  That was a classy move on their part, in my opinion, and would make me want to eat there more often should the craving arise.

First time at top donair today.  went in and thought i saw the donair meat that some donair shops must get from athena. (Not sure if that the company these shops get the bland meat from or the equipment.) but this shop was very clean very bright upon entering. The staff could be a little more friendly. i ordered a regular combo and thought i would be getting a bland donair.  i got my order in a fair amount of time.  headed home munch on the fries on the way and they were nicely seasoned.  The fries could have stayed in the fryer a little longer.  got home and unwrapped the donair. Very good nicely seasoned and had a nice spicy heat to it not to much just right.  i will be back again.

It took one hour for miso soup and edamame. Need I say more?!

If I need to say more, then I'll say service in that hour was friendly, and our drinks were refilled once, but a hour for soup.... really?!

We've been here twice. Service at the bar was kind of awful, but within the restaurant it was great.  Our bartender didn't even salt our edamame (and we've had it sitting inside - it should come with salt).  Their rolls are big, fish is fresh, and prices are good.  But service at the bar was inexcusable.

Ok...first....it's a Waffle House...so don't expect a gourmet meal.  This place has just opened, so I tried a few times before I wrote this.  My first visit was during the lunch hour(s), and there were so many "employees" it was almost impossible to get an order taken correctly.  There were at least 7 doing the cooking, and over 10 servers.   My order was done, albeit slow, and almost not correct.  The second time was at dinner time, and it was pouring rain.  The place was full, but everybody appeared to have had their order taken.  We sat down, someone came over to take our drink order, then we waited....for over 15 minutes...before someone else came over to see if we wanted to order.  Really?   Again, several people at the cooking station, but they were more interested in chatting with each other than getting the food out.  I also saw something I've never seen before at a Waffle House,  someone opening the door for me, then doing nothing until a table left, and they cleaned it, only to return to waiting by the door for the next table/customer.  Let's see how things progress, and I'll update this as needed, but for the time being, maybe wait a bit before trying it.

I've seen this place many times and just had no desire to eat here. Just didn't seem like a place I wanted to eat. Man have I been missing out. After a movie BF stated we were going to eat here and I didn't protest. Glad I didn't. It wasn't busy so we were seated rather quickly. Apps we ordered buffalo cauliflower and some peppers. Cauliflower excellent, peppers ehh. It was just a plate of spicy/salty peppers. We dipped our bread in the sauce and added peppers to our pizza later. Pizza was very good.  I had shrimp scampi, yum! He ordered a veggie with cauliflower crust and loved it. Son ordered five cheese with tomato and loved his as well. Oh and butter cake with ice cream for dessert. Another hit. So I'll return. I've been wanting shrimp scampi pizza since I finished my leftovers the day after.

Dry,dry,dry....and I'm not talking'bout their humor! Everything was dry/overcooked, except maybe the lettuce. Decor is very unappealing, no soap in restrooms...hummmm? Did you say Yum? Eat here only outta desperation....which one can often have when searching for food on this end of Dickerson Pike.
I don't do shakes so just commenting on the burgers... Just two stars as RENO has several other options for great hamburgers...like (in and out)....

I was a guest at Hilton and this restaurant was located onsite. First impressions: I was not asked what I wanted as I sat, not even water, just an empty glass at the table. I had to ask an employee cleaning a table to help me get the waiter. So not a great first impression. When I placed the order, it did arrive quick which was a plus. I had the crawfish etouffee. At first sight, I was disappointed at the quantity for the price. Was really small and I was done eating in like 2 minutes. Lol. However the taste was alright. I also had to ask about the dessert, waiter did not offer or ask if I was interested in dessert. However I loved the bread pudding. The cold Ice cream and warm pudding made for a nice melt in my mouth. If you like pecans, you'd love the crunch as well. I loved the ponchatoula punch. Well balanced and sweet. Rating 3 stars because I don't think I got a bang for my buck with the entree and the customer service was less than ideal.

Sushi was great here!  Special Scallop and hamachi was really fresh and stood out for me here.  The rolls were reasonably priced and super tasty as well.  I wouldn't order their yakitori, was not good at all.  Failed on meat and flavor.  Other than that, would definitely recommend this to a friend.

My girlfriend and I are currently on a mission to eat at every single restaraunt in Reno/Sparks, so naturally this place was on our list.  

First of all the service is great; our drinks were never empty, our waiter was on his game. My girlfriend ordered the macho Diablo burrito with shredded beef, it wad very good, and quite spicy as well.  I ordered a ground beef chimichanga, it was very fresh, the meat was great, and I even ate the rice and beans, which I usually don't do.  All in all both of us give this place a solid 5 stars, for great service great food and decent atmosphere, you should definitely go check it out, it isn't too expensive either.
.

I was very impressed with this bar. It's up-scale but not pretentious which I love. The decor is very trendy yet organic, which makes it inviting. The staff is very friendly and helpful. I asked our waitress to recommend a drink based off of my tastes, she customized a drink for me. This really impressed me. It's a perfect placed to have a drink at a table. It's a little loud and dark but that's what makes it trendy. It's a fantastic first date place because you can talk but you can distract yourself too;-)

Odi's is the best! My family and I have been going for years. Perfect for before the beach or just to stop in for lunch. The food is homemade and phenomenal. My favorites are spinach pie, vegetarian grape leaves, and pasta salad. Odi the owner is very friendly and welcoming. Prices are great as well and very reasonable. Don't forget to try the cheesecake baklava! They also have a great selection of Mediterranean groceries and alcohol. A must try

Best spring rolls I've ever had. I've never been let down by their vermicelli dishes and they have about 50 more items on their menu which look amazing. Good sized portions and they always have the Vietnamese version of American Idol playing on tv!

Some friends took me to Juan's while I was visiting in New Orleans because of their large, flexible, and accommodating nature towards vegetarians.  I was excited to go because of the overwhelmingly positive reviews on Yelp (I had bookmarked Juans before coming to New Orleans) and my review of their menu.  When we arrived, the waitress boasted that they could make anything vegetarian (except, probably, the flying burrito, which is chicken, beef, and shrimp).

I am surprised by the glowing reviews here.  The food was institutional tasting and mediocre.  We started with an order of chips, salsa, and guacamole.  The chips were terrible and had an awful aftertaste.  So did the salsa.  The guacamole seemed processed.  It's a pass.

The burrito itself was blah.  While I inquired about the more exotic vegetarian burrios on the menu -- such as the one vegetable burrito with tons of veggies -- I was directed towards a normal bean burrito and told to add three or four vegetables to it.  I got mushrooms, onions, jalapenos, and spinach.  It was nothing to write home about.  It was lacking flavor, and tasted as if it was marinated in margerine.

Wouldn't rush to go back.  I think people in New Orleans rave about this place because the options are so few and people are happy to have some Mexican place to go to that offers a variety of options for those of different dining persuasions.  I give Juan's credit for that.  I wish I could give them credit for better food.

I absolutely LOVED everything I tasted here and have been dreaming about it! In fact, I'm just recently back from traveling and it's the first meal thought about and craved!! 

We came for a large family lunch and had no problem reserving a table last minute in their somewhat odd fitting medieval feeling small dining room on a Sunday afternoon. (Watch out, they do close between lunch and dinner from 3-5pm!) I LOVED their exposed brick walls, nooks for their dripping white pillar candles, street art mural and chandelier! Good vibes!! 

I shared the tofu clay pot bibimbap with my daughter. It was tasty, piping hot and filled with veggies and crisped rice!! Delish! They give you your choice of sauce to mix in if you so choose, but I ate it plain and really enjoyed it! (It's vegan minus the egg to top.) My sister in law had the same thing only with beef and thoroughly enjoyed hers. (She's Korean, I think that holds some weight!) 

My youngest ordered her Korean favorite and a great vegetarian option, the japache, but here it's served with sweet potato noodles. It was so delish!! Perfectly cooked noodles and big vegetables in a warm, comforting toasted sesame oil! Yum! 

The table also ordered the Tteokbokki to share. It's a Chili rice cake gnocchi! I was so intrigued! There's both rice and fish cakes, so veg heads, examine closely if splitting this one. The sauce has a great kick! It's a traditional dish made modern with the addition of the mozzarella cheese. (The fusion of new and classic seems to be a theme with the entire menu, so keep your minds open if you're looking for a more traditional Korean meal here) 

My brother, who lived in Korea for years was happy to see "Army Stew", which is apparently somewhat rare in the states. 

Everyone was pleased and we immediately spoke about a return visit! 

Prices were reasonable and it's BYO!

Happy Hour is Tues, Wed, Thurs, Sun from 5-7. Half off apps with full entree purchase.

Not even close to the Pietro's on Walnut Street in Center City.  First of all, way too many little kids.  Screaming, running around inside and out, and typhoid like coughing.(Interesting side note:  There have been 8 cases of whooping cough identified at Radnor High School.)  Ewwwww.  The waitress was efficient, but a little curt.  The bread is good...toasted.  The atmosphere is nice, but if you like the Pietro's in center city, this place will be a huge disappointment.  But if you looking for a step up from a McDonalds with an indoor playground, this place may fit the bill.
I had the buffet and was surprisingly pleased by the quality of the food. Very delicious and many options to choose from. Highly recommend it
This restaurant is absolutely delicious. I will be back to try the entire menu. I had the turkey and dressing with the greens.
I'm not usually a fan of South St businesses, but I had a great lunch here. Nice decor, professional service, and good food. Slightly pricey for the location though.

The wonderful BBQ aroma hits you the second you walk through the front door. We love the food here from the salads, sandwiches, dinners to the salad bar.
And if you're reading this today, no band in bar this weekend. NCAA rules this weekend with all the TV's in the bar. 
Ask for the Habanero Sauce..it ROCKS!

First visit last night. We got there at 9 and it was a hr wait. They were out of a few of the meat plates.I ordered the strawberry black pepper soda, Mexican street corn and steak arrachera with chimichurri absolutely delicious the char on the steak was perfect the chimichurri was awesome, it just wasn't enough.the steak came with barley which had roasted onions in it. I've never had barley but it was really good also. While waiting my bf ordered the rye or die drink it was really good.  For his food he ordered mole-roasted bird,ranchero chicken skin, cream taco, the knackwurst(beef sausage) it came with onions and a really good mustard sauce,masa fritter,this came with butcher butter,assorted pickles this included pickled onions a red bbq sauce kinda weird and white bbq sauce was pretty good and a pineapple sage soda. What's cool about this place is you don't have to wait for your full order to be ready before they bring the food to you. They bring the food out as its ready. So your food doesn't sit under a heat lamp for 15min before you get it.  Everything was so good. I want to go back and try a few other small plates and brisket. The restaurant is VERY nice and the servers are awesome. I will definitely return.

The ladyfriend and I checked out this place a week ago as she needed her monthly burger fix.  Desi Vega's burgers is located in the same place that Phil's Grill used to occupy.  It looks exactly the same as it did before.  The menu even was basically the same.  Hey both were burger joints so it makes sense.

The burgers are somewhat thin, which for my money they should be.  The buns are smashable and the toppings are of your choosing as it's a build your burger type place.  Basically the burger was everything I wanted it to be.  I opted for the shoestring onion rings as a side which cost an extra buck.  They were marinated in tabasco prior to frying.  They were a tad too greasy but very tasty.  I had had these exact same onion rings at Andy's Bistro once before.  

The atmosphere is that of a strip mall which cheap table and chairs.  The music was some sort of weird Herbie Hancock dubstep crap that made me want to stab myself in the ear with my utensils.  4 stars for the food, 2 for the atmosphere.

I ordered the Steamboat Willie which is totally out of my norm, and a side salad. YUMMMMMMYYY. I am not a roast beef fan, but this sandwich was really really good. The side salad was HUGE and full of fresh ingredients. Romaine, salami, egg, onion, tomato, etc. Will def be back.

This restaurant has always been one of the few that serves great seafood dishes. The night that we went was a Wednesday and it was still packed full of people. The atmosphere was very lively and the food was excellent. We shared a seafood platter which was excellent. However, the amount of squid was overwhelming; I would recommend that the restaurant lessen that and throw in a carb or something (risotto). When the restaurant is very busy, our server became rather inattentive; this was one of the negative experiences. Overall, I would recommend this restaurant to anybody who likes seafood. 

Payment: Visa, AMEX, MasterCard, debit, cash

Parking: Street parking. 

Worst time to go: Nights are usually very busy. I highly recommend making a reservation in advance. 

Noise: Extremely lively when busy 

Food quality: Excellent seafood dishes. 

Value: This restaurant can be considered more expensive than Earls or Joeys. While the costs are expensive, the portions and quality are great. It's worth the money if you're willing to pay. 

Kid friendliness: Seems more like an adult oriented restaurant. No change tables stuck out at me. 

Bathroom cleanliness: Soap and paper is stocked properly. Restrooms are very clean. 

Restaurant size: Very big. They have a dedicated section (closed off) should you want more privacy with your party. 

Must have: Seafood dishes are a must have.

The food here was very umami. Which means delicious. The waitress was attentive and the eating area was clean. I would recommend this place if you are looking fir a nice hit bowl or either Udon it Ramen. Also, the fried chicken was the "BOMB"

My husband and I enjoyed our visit! What stood out the most was the excellent and friendly service. We felt as if we were regulars, and that is a great quality of NolaNica. We decided to start with the boiled yuca appetizer as well as a order of the yuca fritters. For our meal, we split the large platter and an order of the taquitos. The only thing we didn't care for was the yuca fritters. There is something in them that is a bit chalky, almost like uncooked flour or dry cheese. However the rest was great. We enjoyed the meats, plantains, rice and beans and yuca with slaw. I recommend them for sure and will go back to try other dishes!

I had a few slices and they were good. Crisp bacon. Also had their old style pizza which I recommend.

They have $3.99 cheesesteak specials. Excellent. They chop it up fine and the cheese and onion flavor infuses the whole steak. About a 6 inch roll. Fries added for 99 cents. Highly recommend that.

They have other options for the $3.99 deals.

I have been ordering from this store, for well over 30 years. Love the food. However the lady who currently takes orders need to be changed. She is unfriendly and has no customer service skills. If the wasn't good. I totally would never order again.5/20/16 11:09 order placed and I laughed because, she needs a hug. Rather than loose my religion.

The person who admitted making my food was wearing his mask down around his chin and when  I complained and he pulled it up it was so loose it fell right down, which means HE IS NOT COVERED WHILE PREPARING FOOD

The restaurant is really nice, the concept and  the design amazing however the food  for that price U expect something better, If u ever try a real paella with saffron and more than just mussels you will understand what I'm talking about, the drinks were very good.

I've been coming to Vietnam Restaurant for years, since long before it morphed into a slick upscale eatery. Back in the day, it was just another hole in the wall Vietnamese restaurant.

To tell the truth, I kind of miss the hole in the wall place. I really miss the hole in the wall prices. But one thing that has remained constant over the years is the fried spring rolls. They are the best in the world.

Seriously, I've been all over the country and I've spent a considerable amount of time in Vietnam and I feel confident that Vietnam Restaurant's spring rolls are the best. Not only that, but the vegetarian rolls are just as good as the meat filled ones.

Outside of the spring rolls, the menu can be hit or miss. But who cares? (The writing in Playboy can a little over the top, but I'm not buying the magazine for the articles, now am I?) Just get an order of spring rolls and an order of bun with spring rolls on top and wash it down with a Tiger Beer. Then make a mental note to go running the next day.

It's Qdoba. I like it a lot. I like a good smothered burrito. I wish they had tofu like Chipotle does, though. 

The wait at this location can be insanely long at lunchtime. Be sure to order online in advance.

While visiting Philadelphia, a group of 4 of us had an early dinner at Amada, choosing the chef's tasting menu ($65/person) and pitchers of the white sangria.

Amazing experience!

The plates just kept coming and coming and coming...I believe there were a total of 10 dishes, plus dessert, so don't even THINK about ordering anything else if you opt for the tasting menu.

Goat cheese starter, followed by a roasted beet salad, charcuterie selection, octopus (I'm usually not a fan, but the smoked paprika was fantastic), wagu beef, short ribs that fell off of the bone, a roasted eggplant dish, scallops (the only thing I didn't like, but I'm not a fan of scallops to begin with), a beef flatbread and I know I'm forgetting another item.

We finished with the olive oil cake, served with ice cream, lemon curd and whipped cream and seriously were wondering how we were ever going to roll ourselves out of the place at the end of the meal.

Our waiter was Michael, and he explained every dish to us and accommodated my aversion to lamb, making sure that none of the chef selections that made it to the table contained any. He was personable, attentive and served us at our own pace, so we didn't feel rushed at all.

When I return to Philadelphia, this is definitely on my list for a second visit.
We love this place. They deliver quickly and the food is delicious. Be aware though, the portions HUGE.
That chicken is the BOMB!! Do yourself a favor and get a little side of their ranch to go on it. Adds a sweet element to the spicy. Very good!

Great service by Desa and flexibility of the kitchen to split the two edamame appetizer options into one order so I could try both options. Awesome Poki - which is hard to find. Very good food and very reasonably priced. Would love to come back.
very disappointed! tri tip tasted 5 days old slapped on a bun. mac n cheese had no flavor. ribs were dry and tasteless. drove out of my way to try this place not worth it. Dogs got lucky today!!!

The people are friendly and accommodating. Food was savory and delicious. Great combination of chorizo and avocado on blue corn tortillas. One downside was where I was seated, but otherwise excellent service and food.

Good sandwich shopp. Everyone was very friendly. Good chat about college football.  Only accepts cash and its not noted on door. Hours are only 10-4.  Good LSU experience for the Tiger's fans.  WIll visit again if I am in NO.
best food very real vert authentic . service is good too . the atmosphere is homey . 10/10 would go again
One of the best hidden resterauntuts in south jersey great prices huge plates of food

I went here last night to grab some drinks and take advantage of the Yelp Drink specials. We ordered "La Manzana," which was hot apple cider with tequila. As someone that has horrible flashbacks to freshman year when tequila is mentioned, I was pleasantly surprised with how tasty this drink was. 
They seem to have a pretty solid happy hour in general: specially priced beer and wine, half priced apps. The beer selection is pretty darn good too. I think I saw Rogue, Victory, Dogfish. 
Supper also offers specially priced apps during happy hour, so of course we took advantage and ordered Crispy Squid, Duck Fat Fingerlings, and Smoked Chicken Wings. All were delicious, but my favorite was the Fingerlings, yum! The plates are super tiny (think: 3 chicken wings) but they were only $3 each, quite the deal!
The staff is amazingly attentive. The bartender never let our drinks empty without offering another round. Other employees (hostess, wait staff, not sure who they were?) checked in once or twice to see how everything was. 
I left here with an overall good feeling about this place. I'll be sure to add this to my list of happy hour locations.

A bit of a let down.  The porkbelly was fairly well done, but the butternut squash soup was one of the worst dishes I have ever had in a restaurant.   I am a huge butternut squash soup fan and almost always order it anywhere I go.  Perhaps the chef simply had a bad day, but the soup was so over poweringly sweet it was inedible.  It was sweeter than most deserts.  It tasted like a bowl of honey.  I have never had soup anywhere near that sweet.  A truly terrible dish.    

The charcuterie was mediocre.  

That said, my dining mates really enjoyed the duck and sea food entre.  

Overall a mixed experience, and I will not be returning.
Seriously disappointed. Service is terrible and way overpriced. Too many great restaurants in this town to waste money here.

Super good pizza for cheap. You dont have to pay extra for toppings. Same price for any amount of toppings you want. We got a make your own pizza with a ton of toppings, 4 dough knots (which are BOMB) and a fountain drink for $15! Fast and efficient customer service. Only thing they need to add is an option to add honey onto your pizza.

Man, oh man, I remember how fancy I found buffets as a kid back in the 90's! The sneeze guard, a grand salad bar, prime rib carving stations, Mongolian BBQ and that deliciously artificial soft serve ice cream used to make me do a happy dance...As I got older, I moved on from buffets as I found I never had enough room in my stomach for all that food.

While it's definitely still too much food for this Yelper, I must say that Toucan Charlie's is one of the best buffets in town. In addition to a grandiose salad bar, they also offer freshly prepared specialty salads, pho, and of course my beloved Mongolian BBQ. They have prime rib too, but I am a long time vegetarian so I cannot attest to that item. They also have all the classic stations: American comfort, Chinese, Mexican, and one heck of a dessert bar featuring delicious gelato in addition to my beloved soft serve (amongst a plethora of other delectables including fresh cotton candy if you have a serious sweet tooth). 

Naturally, buffet food cannot really hold a candle to individually prepared dishes (especially at Atlantis where they have so many incredible restaurants). I would give Toucan Charlie's more of a 3.5 for food, but I'm rounding my score up for having more made-to-order items than your typical buffet as well as for having excellent service. Our server was very pleasant and professional, always filling our iced teas in a timely manner and clearing dirty dishes from our table. Don't forget to tip your buffet waitstaff, they work hard to make your experience a great one, even if they don't have to take your order or directly serve your food. 

I may not be a frequent flyer at Toucan Charlie's, but I will definitely be back the next time I want to go into a good, old fashioned, American Food Coma(TM)!

I had a couple of sandwiches here, and they were excellent. Then I tried the Duck Dip. I think this is my favorite sandwich in Boise, it is amazing!!!! Just expect cold super tender smoked duck with an awesome tomato jus

I came all the way from New York, I ordered the Thursday special before my other two friends. My side order was asparagus and broccoli. My other two friends also had broccoli as a side order. The dark skinned guy come tell me outside while we're waiting for our food I have bad news for you I don't have broccoli for you, I have it for the other two girls. Mind you he took my order first. The tall dark skinned guy tells me it's the way the kitchen guy prepared the food which made no sense. Horrible customer service if someone orders the same thing before you their supposed to get it first. I was feening to go to the spot and try out their food I was very disappointed. The place was dirty and also their seafood had a fresh taste like it just came out the ocean. My friends also didn't enjoy the food. My friend shrimp was over cooked and salty they fried it too much. I wouldn't recommend this place to no one who is going to New Orleans. I honestly wouldn't even give this place a rating I have no choice that's the only way I can post my review.

The secret is out, this place is legit! Move over Casa Garcia.. Don Jose is the new kid on the block!

Everything I've eating is good. If I could give some advise, I'd say to wait and order until your ready because the food comes out really fast

My daughter and I stumbled upon this place while we were in Westchase. They had pho on the menu and that's what got us in for lunch! I ordered the chicken pho, which was so flavorful and delicious. I have to say this had to be the BEST chicken pho in Tampa Bay! My daughter had the Miso Ramen and she really enjoyed it as well. The staff was great and Pho is beyond my expectation, tastes like homemade cooking Pho!

I went to Fogo with an old flame who was in town. Didn't really think about how much I'd be spending on a girl who isn't going to eat much anyway! Outside of that, what a meal! It's all you can eat, so that's awesome! The lamb is amazing! they also offer like 16 different types of meat that's a wide range. The real catch about the place is it's a bit pricey, which... totally worth it for a guy who's gonna eat... most of the time I was pondering why she wasn't eating more! That is the ONLY bad thing I could say about it. So, skip the girl and take your dad/son/brother/business associate out to celebrate! Save the girl for some French food or Tapas

Yesterday, my roommate and I were brainstorming ideas on places we might want to eat - and I told him I want an underground place, a real hole in the wall that when I post it on Snapchat everyone is gonna be like where is that!? We looked up pizza and wings in the Tucson area, and were about to go to one location, when out of nowhere we discovered this place and it's glowing reviews. We got a 18" pepperoni pizza as well as a box of hot wings! The wings were so gas, very meaty and the hot sauce was delicious. And the pizza was amazing. The first bite into it I knew we discovered our new pizza place. This place is exactly what we were hoping for! And even had several people replying to my snap chat asking where I had just gone for food! Great spot! Super nice staff! Great food!

ahh mazinggggggggggggg! the freakin best. better than kooma. better than any sushi place in west chester. i got a vegan dragon and a veg out burrito...so delicious, more expensive than other sushi but worth it for what you get! The place itself is super tiny, but cute. A nice place to grab lunch!
Chef Lil John and our server Tuyen were the best! They made sure the kids food was cut up and cooled off before serving them which was so thoughtful. Food was delicious!!! We will be back again soon!

Go for the pot stickers.
Stay and return for the insanely polite and friendly service.

Today I had literally the worst take out service I have ever received from a restaurant . I ordered 5 chicken fajita meals at 7pm , was told on the phone they are a little backed up and it would be ready in an hour to and hour and 15 minutes . Well at 8:45 they come out and say we are sorry we are out of fajita chicken . And they only have steak . So I suck it up and deal with it . Finally at 9:10 ( ten minutes after they close my order comes out ) . The entire time I'm there other people are getting their orders . And people are being sat for dine in . This is normally my go to for Mexican cuisine but after this experience with not even so much as an apology or a offered a glass of water while I wait . I will never return .

There was no line when we went. Probably because it want lunch time or dinner time. Ordered a steak taco, carnitas taco, chile relleno thingy..., and a cheese fondue thing.. Lol everything was great! I usually like steak tacos better than chicken or carnitas but I actually liked the carnitas better. The flavoring was great! Will definitely be back if ever in town!
I ordered number 1 beef pho and it was super yummy. Will definitely go back when I'm in Reno.

One thing drew me in here - strolling by and on the other side of the street, I caught a glimpse inside through the large front opening and spied a Hammond B3 organ, the real deal.  The place was full, and we had no reservation.  Darn.  Luckily, two seats opened at the bar, all the way in the back, and I had the corner seat which turned out to be the best seat in the house to watch the keyboard player work the organ.  We saw the BB King All Starts deliver a lively set of R&B. Nothing like a B3 singing and screaming through a Leslie.

We stopped in for a bite to eat before we hit up a night out. Most of us ordered the California Burritos which were huge. I ate the whole things and my buddy tried his darndest because he was really enjoying it. He had the tongue and I had the shrimp. The chips were excellent and weren't salted in the basket (which I prefer). Our waitress was friendly and everything came out timely. Its not incredible, but it was good. If you're in the area pop in and I think you'll enjoy it.

Amazing Vegetarian Food, From a Non-Vegetarian!!!

1. The Food: So far everything on their menu has been phenomenal. The "meats" taste like meat. I honestly think some of "meats" taste better than the actual meat that they are trying to imitate. The menu changes daily, but if it's​ still there get the Cuban Tacos - Oh my! These are a MUST.  You also get free coffee and soup with your order. 
2. The Ambiance/ Service: Very cute and quaint, I love the vibe here as it is very vibrant in colors and uplifting.  Upbeat and Spanish influenced ambiance. They sometimes have live music too! All indoor seating and casual. You pay up front, get a number and sit where ever. 
3. Recommendation - This is hands down one of the best vegetarian food I have ever had!

This has got to be the best waterfront dining in the area. Beautiful view from most seats. The decor is really cool. Captains seats surround the bar. I sat inside but there's deck seating outside on the water too. I tried the salmon salad an was definitely impressed! It was perfectly prepared. There's a kids menu too, so bring the family. Their cocktail menu offers what you'd expect at a waterfront restaurant. The punch bucket was great! 

Came back with my guy and it was another great experience! We each had a Caribbean Punch bucket which was on point again. I had the stuffed flounder. Huge portion and exceeded my expectations. You get two sides with it. I'd recommend veggies and potatoes. The rice is not very good. He had a burger which he said was awesome and the fries were good. Can't wait to come back with dad!
We got the toad in a hole and a brisket sandwhich.  Both of these items were pretty good.  We loved the variety of cheese they offer here as well.  Delicious!
Amazing food! Great service! Not too far from the KOP mall. Butter chicken and garlic chilli roti/naan is my fav!!!

So this isn't  a place I would sit down and do a full meal at because it's tapas. But I would come here and get a few plate and try a few things each time. The interior is so beautiful it's perfect for a date night. Perfect for a pre going out spot. The drinks here are amazing I can say that. The food here is pretty good too. Everything is vegan and everything was delicious. The brussle sprout dish was super. I will be back in the future to try more.

As a sushi fanatic, this place is absolutely horrendous. If I could burn the entire sushi bar down, I would do it. The fact that a sushi place can serve bad quality fish is beyond me. Yet, tons of people rave about the food here. I don't get it. Anyways, I'll back up my rant with the food I ordered and my experience with it. 

I've only been here once, and it will stay that way. I ordered a yellowtail roll, and eel avocado roll, and a spicy tuna roll. Every roll was terrible. The consistency of the spicy tuna was unlike any place I've had it at, and trust me, that's a lot of places. The eel avocado roll was more of an avocado eel roll. Come on, you're seriously going to skimp? The yellowtail was not edible. So why am I even providing 2 stars versus 1? Well, some of the food was tolerable.

Decent dishes: the poke salad. Huge portion and the sushi was masked by a creamy mayo sauce, which was actually tasty. The gyoza was also edible, so there are your stars origami. 

STAY AWAY.
This spot was so good weve been looking for good italian food! Dj was our server and he was amazing and very attentive! We will definitely come back!
Fantastic, authentic French cuisine. The chef is amazing, and very knowledgeable about there wine options. Enjoy a romantic evening with your Sweetheart. Great food and great Wine :-)

I LOVE their pizza but they have the WORST customer service ever. We ordered pizza to be delivered online from their site but weren't told how long it would take. After about an hour we called to get an update and they said it would be 3 hours. THREE HOURS!!! We tried to see if we could pick it up but they said we'd have to cancel our order and reorder and we'd be at the back of the line. Despite my love for their pizza I will not order from them again.
Went in on Saturday and had best AYCE sushi! Fresh, not too much rice and quaint surroundings. Like it better than Sushi Pier mid town. Service a little slow but food makes it worth the wait.

Our group reserved the back room of the restaurant. It's a fairly large room. We had four, long tables. The waitstaff was polite and attentive. With white tablecloths and full table settings, it was a special meal indeed. The best part was the delicious food! It may be the best I've had in the French Quarter. Bread was served - fabulous, Course 1 was Gumbo - so savory, Course 2 was Barbecue Shrimp and Grits - the BEST - we used the bread to catch the last of the gravy, Course 3 was Banana Bread Pudding - very delicious! I would definitely come back!!

Wish I could give 5 stars because the flavor is admittedly great, however this is the 3rd time now we have been overcharged. Not even just a little, by 7 dollars this time. I understand that good food costs a little more, but this is a bit ridiculous. We could almost could have gotten another dish with that price disparity! One burrito, an order of tacos, and a side of pico de gallo should not cost 30 dollars especially when  based on  their menu it should have been 23, and that's including tax. Ive gotten great pico de gallo for 3 dollars many other places. If you want to eat here I reccomend having a menu on hand to be able to prove the correct price.
This is my all time favorite place to eat at when in town. The garlic knots are to die for, the Italian Stromboli is even better. Nice people, great food!

Cute place. But it's more cute than tasty. I did not care for the menu options. I ordered biscuits and gravy and the biscuit was rock hard (see video). The server earned their money making several trips. We were missing a set of silverware, needed refills... soo many things. He was kind enough to trade out the "biscuit" for a replacement ball of friend dough and side of gravy.

I've only eaten here once and the food was not that great.  Indian buffet food is never your best bet, but the quality here is not up to par.  Everything on the buffet was bland and tasteless.  Not horrible, mind you - but not even mid-level for quality and taste.  I expect that's why so many Americans have rated it so highly, eg it is very very bland compared to what it ought to be.

However I don't rate Indian food based on American expectations, I rate it based on what it is - Indian food.  I even give credit for good Americanized Indian food.  This fails to make the bar for either type.

This review is based on the quality of the buffet.  I don't expect as much from a buffet as I do from an a la carte menu, but if you can't feed me adequately at lunch from the buffet, I'm not going to bother to come back and check your a la carte menu.  If you can't meet my lowered expectations for your buffet, I'm not even remotely interested in seeing what you do with the a la carte menu, for which my expectations are going to be a lot higher.

The space it is in is absolutely cavernous which detracts significantly from the ambience.  There is no way they will ever fill that space with anything but a catered wedding - and no Indian family would want their wedding catered by these guys.  

Anybody who thinks this is "the best Indian food in Reno" is sadly lacking in experience with actual Indian food.  Both Thali and Bawarachi - relative newcomers to the Indian food scene in Reno - are FAR far better.  Neither serve a buffet.  Thali is only open for dinner F, Sa, Su and M last I checked.  Bawarachi is open for lunch as well as dinner, a la carte only.  Bawarachi makes a really good masala dosa - but be sure to tell them to spice it up if you like your food hot.  They tend towards less hotness to cater to American taste buds.  Dosa are not on the lunch menu so you will have to ask.  Not all their cooks know how to make them so its a bit of the luck of the draw if you can get it at lunch time.

2 stars is exactly what it deserves - meh.  I've had better.  Its not going to make you throw up.  It's not going to taste awful.  It just doesn't taste of much of anything at all.  For Indian food, that's a damning statement indeed.

I would like to note, some other reviewers make the claim that this is in a "sketchy" neighborhood.  I surmise those folks must be the paranoid sort that lives in gated communities lest the riff-raff get a sniff of the extra-special air that hovers over their extra-special properties.  

There is NOTHING "shady" or scary about the neighborhood it is in.  It's an upper middle class area full of upper middle class homes.  If you think this area is scary or shady, I assure you, there is medication for that.  Get some.

After work on a friday i was finally able to stop and give El Merkury a try. This kitchen does not disappoint. Ordering is done via a touch screen which i like because it lessens the chance of any mistakes with your order. Swipe your card right st the screen and you're done. I'm not sure if they take cash , but who carries cash anymore now a days. If the system was set up to take NFC via apple pay or android wallet that would be nice but not a big deal. 

I ordered the chicken taquitos and they were delicious. Came out very crispy and hot with some great accompanying accruements. Spicer than i expected but a pleasant surprise. 

Also finished out the meal with a tres leche churro. It came out hot and crispy as well. The tres leche was also good.

Stopped by moon dog grill on Saturday! I always get my favs, a Chicago dog and chili and cheese dog! Can't go wrong with either of those. I have been a customer since the cherry hill location and will continue to support this place and their great dogs. :-)
The only reason I'm giving two stars is because the food is decent. Employees here are extremely rude! The only nice worker is the manager

Everything I've tried here tasted really good. The employees aren't jerks. There's an amazing soda machine from the future there. The ambience isn't stellar, but the place is always clean. No major complaints.
They serve different Japanese and Korean foods. My favorites are the mini bowls. This is 5 minutes away from me and definitely my go-to- place! Will order some more!

When Harvest first opened they offered an interesting farm to table concept of casual fine dining, an eclectic seasonal menu, craft cocktails and an extensive wine list.  I think it was owned by the same folks as Hacienda del Sol, and it was fantastic.  

That was many years ago and things have slowly gone down hill.  My wife and I ate dinner at Harvest last night, and it was mediocre at best.  The service was not bad, but not exemplary.  The wine list is utterly unimpressive.  No craft cocktails.  The dinner menu offers an extensive selection of sandwiches, which pretty much sums up the devolution of Harvest into a joint that does not live up to its heritage.

Not terrible, but definitely not great, and definitely not worth the $100+ we spent on our meal.  I don't think we'll go back, which is unfortunate.  Harvest has always been our go-to for a special dining experience in Oro Valley.  No more. :(

Sushi night!! I thought this restaurant was just a sushi restaurant, surprise!, it has a pretty complete menu.
We ordered the assortment plate of sushi, the mussels and the seafood linguine. The sushi was ok, not the freshest magaro I've had, the sushi roll diameter was a little small in my opinion, but I'm not a huge fan of sushi rolls so no big deal, I am however a sashmi fan and the sushi was below my expectations. The mussels were pretty phenomenal, very nice broth, since this is not my first rodeo for mussels, my only comment is these were the smallest mussels I have ever had! I guess I'm just spoiled by much larger mussels in the past, but that would not hinder me from ordering this again.
The seafood linguine is a must order. The only thing I normally ask for is to have the chef scramble an egg in the sauce to thicken up the sauce. The seafood linguine had nice chunks of salmon, bass and I think halibut or cod. A very nice dish to order.

This is our go to pizza spot when we're in town from NYC to visit family. A nice mom and pop establishment with charm. The food is great -especially the pizza... something about the crust really hits the spot! Everything is made fresh to order and you can really taste the difference in quality. Looking forward to trying their cheesesteaks and pasta dishes as well. 

Only 4 stars because we've only had them deliver but will have to dine in on our next go around. Bob, the super friendly delivery guy did let us know that they are BYOB! Sweet!
Delicious food.  Service is slow but we like not being rushed.  My only complaint would be the lack of choices for both specialty burgers and drinks.
I ordered a pizza and wings special. Pepperoni pizza was decent, and wings were great. Both were piping hot when delivered which was a major plus. Will definitely order from them again.

The best local Mexican restaurant in Tucson is Leo's. The restaurant is family owned and run. You feel like you are eating at a family member's home, not a public restaurant. Very relaxing and charming setting. Perfect after getting off of a day at work! 

The menu options are diverse and all of the food we have ordered so far has been excellent. Delicious margaritas on the rocks as well as a large selection of cervezas. Salsa has a nice kick to it. We've had the carne seca enchiladas, WOW are they good!! The fajitas are excellent as are the tacos. Fresh vegetables, guacamole, sour cream, fresh beans and rice accompany most meals. 

A lot of times when you go to the larger Mexican restaurants, you get your main entree with a side of "slop" - runny beans, bland rice, etc. Leo's is top quality, fresh ingredients, and tastes delicious. You should try it soon! It's a hidden gem!

My husband ordered a tonkotsu ramen from them with extra egg and the egg they gave us was *sour* and past its due. Had to throw the entire ramen away because the disgusting egg had contaminated the rest of the broth. Never coming back here again.

Great quick service food - the salad bar is the best.  Non-smoking restaurant with larger (and cleaner) service area than the location on Dorsett.

Fish, chicken, burgers.  Steak fries and a bakery.  Yum.

hln
Sign says "We open at 8AM...usually". Monday morning at 8:15AM: not open. I'll revise if I ever get to visit. #fail
Over priced, and the food was honestly not worth eating. Sad because, they have potential

Fantastic sandwich! As others have said, there may be some cheaper banh mis around town but you can truly sense the quality here. The vegetables are very fresh and crunch when you bite in. Perhaps most importantly to me, the French bread is perfectly crispy, but doesn't cut up your gums when you dig in. 

I ordered takeout which was very organized and efficient. They had a stand for pickup orders and mine was ready right on time. It was an agonizing car ride home smelling the delicious pork!

Great steak and Tenderloin........Try the Cream Corn Bruele'.  I've had some really good Waygu and this is good quality.  We showed a up a few minutes before 5PM (had tickets to the symphony at 7 PM) and they got us through.  Don't plan on getting a drink from the bar if you show up early....I assume the liquor license. We were asking about a "odd" Vodka bottle and the Manager took the time to make me a drink to test it out!  I'm not a Martini drinker, it was a "Pear" martini with a "Pear olive" (a piece of pear shaped like an olive)......which was delicious!  A bit pricey some said?  I didn't think it was a bad value.  Wholesale Waygu is $20+ dollars a Lbs depending on quality.....and everything was cooked perfectly.  Wait staff was excellent.  Only complaint was after being asked to wait to be seated we felt "rushed" to our table by the hostess;  it wasn't rude...just my only quibble on the night.   If you like steak.....it's worth a try!

Service / ordering seems a little wonky however the food was delicious. We dropped in on Saturday night and place was pretty empty even though it was around 8pm  Service staff were young and appeared disjointed. We had the Cuban and Reuben style sandwiches... highly recommend both.. Get the service figured out we'll give it higher rating next time.
Great food!  I ordered the Arroz con Camerones, very good dish! Food is always delicious .

I forgot how good yummy house was. We had the dumplings, salt and pepper calamari, Hong king wonton soup, house fried rice, and Mongolian beef. Nothing bad on the table. Even my picky nephew had more than one favorites. Will try to go back soon as the family style try a bunch for not too much is one of my favorite aspects of the place. Nice staff and pretty Devore at this location.

We stopped by since this spot is so well known but we were super disappointed with the sandwiches we tried.  

Mushroom Cheesesteak: Enjoyed this more than the Roast Pork Sandwich but it was not anything special compared to other cheesesteaks we've tried. 

Roast Pork Sandwich with Spinach: The bread was soggy, which was an unpleasant surprise. The meat also tasted only like salt and pepper.

Good, but the seasonings on the chicken and the dipping sauces were not as good as what I've experienced in DC and Baltimore.  The dipping sauces were pre-packaged and not fresh. However, the service was good, and they offered authentic drinks.

The sauce for the claws was amazing and that garlic bread was just soooo good with it. I would completely recommend that appetizer. The oysters royal left a ton to be desired and were lukewarm when served. Overall for the price, it was just ok.

Stopped in here for a late lunch cause of the good reviews, and had a good time. Service was friendly, food was good. Not much to complain about. 
I had the Hawaiian roll and salmon nigiri, the nigiri was great, I didn't really care for the Hawaiian roll but I think that's just my own personal tastes and not the quality of the food. 

Good little place.

We ordered sandwiches to go. The chicken sandwich was good but not very flavorful. The gyro sandwich had better flavor and it was a good size. The white sauce is not tatziki sauce which is a bummer. The fries were delicious. Usually carry out fries get mushy but not these fries!

This was our second visit, and if it had been the first, it would have been our last. Our server was really bad this time. The place was very busy at 7:00 on a Saturday night that could be part of it, but. The sushi has been great both visits and it comes very quickly. I ordered the coconut shrimp entree and it came as an appetizer size, just four pieces of shrimp and nothing else. I got my soup first but we had to ask three times for my husband's soup. It came after a long wait after his entree. I ordered another entree that was supposed to come with rice, and it didn't. My husband ordered another entree and it took waaaaay too long and was partially cold. Oh, and we also were served three items that we did not order, they belonged to the table behind us. Then we got our check and those items were listed on our tab, and my husband's soup was not. So the waiter was mixing up the orders. It will be a long time until we return and I sure hope it is better than this visit. If not, we will not return. Hope they get their act together and be consistent.

I have been here numerous times for dinner. Today I came for lunch, and I was not satisfied. The staff for the day time makes this seem like a whole other Wahoo's. After I ordered a carnitas taco, I decided to get churro chips to go since they are one of my favorite wahoo homemade items. 

I observed the unsanitary habits of the workers while I was waiting for my chips. Not only were there 2 female servers hovering around the counter on their phones, but they then proceeded to handle food without washing their hands. I also saw another worker handle money and then food, also without washing her hands. Phones and money are two of the most germ-infested items. It makes me sick to think about all the bacteria and germs that transferred from their phone to the food. 

Also, regarding the phones, they were distracted on their phones and failed to notice the food that was ready to be delivered. I saw my to go box on the counter, just sitting there for 5 minutes. While they scrambled to catch up on the serving that they missed because they were on their phones. I understand that there are other customers, food needs to be served, plates need to be cleaned, but why you on your phones? That is just unprofessional and unsanitary. 

I witnessed my chips sitting on the counter for 5 minutes, during which 3 different servers approached the box, opened it, looked at it, and closed it again. If I could, I would just walk 5 feet to grab my box and go. By the time I finally got my chips, they were soggy since they were enclosed in the box. The heat had no place to escape while it was sitting on the counter. I have ordered these plenty of times, so i know how they should be. 

Bottomline, avoid coming here during the day time. The night staff is so much cleaner and on top of their job.

Go somewhere else if you want real fresh authentic Chinese. Went here late night for the first time in years when there were no options. I usually prefer Jade Harbor or Thai Lake for legit Cantonese food in Chinatown. This place seems to cater towards Non-Asians and also attracts undesirables late night. If you order something authentic like Crab Meat Fish Maw Soup or Seafood Pan Fried Noodles, it has no taste and seems like it is flash frozen and microwaved. They just push out their Salt and Pepper Wings, General Tso, and Lo Mein, the typical American Chinese food you can get at your local Chinese spot, which is probably fresher. Also, I would not recommend anyone ordering seafood here. Mai Lei Wa is disgusting, overcrowded, rude staff, and overpriced. Spend your money elsewhere.
Don't go here. It took us an hour to get our food and then the chicken was so spicy yet no taste which I didn't think was possible. Stay away.

I ordered chicken parm tonight and had to pay $6 extra because I did not want sauce on my side of pasta, I wanted garlic and oil.  I have never paid one cent extra at any other restaurant for this request.  This was outrageous.  I will never go back to this restaurant and I will never recommend to anyone.

The food has a really unique flavour. We went there on a food crawl of Edmonton's China Town. It's a cute and clean little place. We sampled the Gui Lin style soup with the thicker rice noodle, tomato and the most distinct flavour, the fermented vegetables. The meat noodles and broth were awesome, but the veggies I was torn on. I don't know if I liked them or not! The base comes out seperate from the soup broth, and comes in Gui Lin, which is darker and spicier, and a more mild broth that I can't remember the name of. I think I would have liked it better if I would have left the both on the side and dipped the noodles which is one of the styles of eating they mentioned, different than just dumping the broth in. I liked the flavour initially but found the fermented flavour got really into the broth,  so the flavour was less a subtle bite and took over the subtle heat of the gui lin broth. I'll be back to try a different technique and to sample some of the other food. The gui lin style mac and cheese interestes me!

This place just gets it right.  Quaint, eclectic, lots of cool art to look at,  good music,  delicious and inspired food.   I have been coming here on and off for years and have never been disappointed.  I also do not hesitate to take guests here.   Over the years,  I've had steak,  mahi mahi, chicken,  the shroom, saganaki, etc here and it's allll good!  I remember one time in particular where they gave us all soup samplers...free!  Of course we ordered the soup after we tried it,  so objective met (o:  Just delicious,  definitely give it a try!

We were looking for top rated Italian restaurants in the area and Alfano's was at the top. Was concerned with a number of very low ratings, and there were also good ones- so a mixed bag, but tried it last night. There was a sparse attendance; about a third of the tables were occupied. Ordered wedding soup, which was pretty good and quick. No wait for our seats, but a 40 minute wait for the two entrees - veal Milanese and chicken Marsala. Several apologies from the wait staff that they were very busy. The veal was good; the chicken tasteless and the pasta sauce tasted odd. So a very mixed meal opinion.....just like the reviews. If you go, I hope you can write a good review, but beware. One star for the meal issues vs the price.

When I am craving Pho- this place hits the spot!  Everyone knows that Blue Bell and surrounding areas are the pits when it comes to lunch options.  You can score just about anything for lunch that includes a drive-thru and an entire week of regret but when it comes to quality lunch options that are fast and tasty... they are hard to come by.
