Pho Thai Nam is the Blue Bell exception.  Food is quality and they have some of the best lunch specials in the area.  Pho or noodles, with a drink and an appetizer for $12.00. You've won my heart! Each time visiting Pho Thai Nam I've tried different combos of the lunch deal but the standouts are definitely the spring rolls and dumplings.  Solid top notch starters.  Of course their pho is in the major leagues, in my opinion. The shrimp pho which is my favorite.  Items that are must orders: the pineapple fried rice and drunken noodles.  I put these dishes in "things I could eat for  the rest of my life" category. Lastly, the Vietnamese coffee is kick-you-in-the-pants fantastic.  Creamy delicious strong and satisfying. 

The place is ALWAYS packed whenever I've dined there which is a sign that fellow working/living Blue Bell'ians know what the good word is.  Service is top notch too. I love coming here!

I try to never go to chain restaurants, but from time to time I have to.  I came here Friday for a post graduation meal.  I do have to say that I have been to several Red Lobster's and I will have to admit, that I have never had a bad meal at any of them.  I'm not saying that the food is phenomenal by any means, but for a chain, it's pretty darn good.  Plus 15.99 for a 4 course meal, is not too shabby if I must say.  I will say though, that cheesy biscuits are delicious!!  I will never mind eating at a Red Lobster!!

Of all the pizza shops in my town this is one of the best. I have been coming here for years. The pizza is great and the staff is very friendly.

I normally get either Stromboli or the italian style cheese hot dog (my personal favorite). I'd recommend the Mozzarella sticks and the bruschetta. Also try the margarita pizza! Now I'm getting hungry.

The sauce is excellent... I am a big sauce fan so it's a deal breaker for me. I like my food well done and crispy and that has never been an issue for them to leave it sit in the oven for me. 

I didn't realize the had cannolis until I asked. YUM ;-)

What a wonderful find!  Although this adorable little diner has been open since April, I just tried it today for the first time.  I WILL be back!!!  The food is fresh, creative and melt in your mouth good.  With such a lack of these types of places in Hudson, I was so thrilled to finally find a great place for breakfast and lunch that serves really good food, with very attentive service at an affordable price.  You will not be disappointed!  It's just around the corner from Bayonet Point Hospital, on US19 in the Winn Dixie shopping center.  Open everyday from 7am - 2pm.  Try this place - it will become your new favorite!!!

Great food here! Peanut Butter, bacon and cheddar of cheese steak. Who thought it could be so good. My associate had the brisket and wasn't thrilled which shocked me as all of rave reviews about it on here.

Next time Ill have the pig sandwich.

They have added some yummy new things to their breakfast including fruit pancakes. It's a pretty good buffet, better than the chains but not as good as Vegas. The seafood feast on Friday nights has some yummy treats, too. Definitely not the best place to eat at this hotel but the dollar value of the breakfast is unbeatable. We'll go back but we're not dreaming of our next meal there.

Yet another authentic S. Philly business lost to gentrification.  What'll replace it...more overpriced, sustainable, farm-to-table, free range, organic, whatever-the-new-trend-is BS?  The "new" East Passyunk is really getting obnoxious.

Update 7/2018:  So, true to my prediction a vegan restaurant called "The Tasty" took over.  I didn't want to like it, but dammit it's good too.

Loved it!!!  What can I say, am a seafood lover and we certainly have our seafood on the West Coast...so it was really nice to find a place on the East Coast that had it's own flair...different but very good.  I had just the two roll combo...crab and lobster...and loved that it wasn't full of "filler"....loved it so much we went back twice!

Will definitely be on my list of favorites in Philly!!!

My friends and I went on a Saturday afternoon because I was recommended this place by a colleague. And let me just say this place shocked me beyond all belief. As soon as you walk into the restaurant, you are hit with amazing music and very beautiful paintings that embody the southern culture. Unfortunately, we couldn't hear the jazz band, but the ambiance in this place was incredible. Our waitress, Shannon was absolutely amazing and so helpful. The food came out extremely fast and it was amazing. Probably the best soul food that I have had in awhile. Not to mention the cornbread was heavenly.

This used to be a 5-star restaurant in both quality of food and service. I used to take clients here all the time for lunch and dinner. Sometime ago though, it got increasingly bad.

My first complaint is the wait staff. During my last visit, I got an exceptionally fake waitress who was not only not attentive, but seemed to have a sarcastic tone to her that I could not quite figure out. I watched her  interact with other tables too and her general attitude was insincere and manipulative.

When it came to ordering, I asked some questions and decided on a meal with a full size salad. I noticed the patron next to me ordered the same thing and hers was a generous size.

Well, when I got mine, it was half the size of the other patron's. When I asked if this was the full size, the waitress said it was. The salad was about the same size as the person next to  me.

When I got my bill, I noticed I was charged for the main meal and a full salad. However, I learned from talking to the patron next to me, that she ordered the same thing but her salad was a side one. I was charged for the full even though the side salad is the same price!  The waiter didn't bother to tell me that...that the combo option is actually cheaper than ordering them separately and you get the same amount of food. When I asked why she didn't inform me, she mumbled somethign but never credited the difference. I should have stiffed her right then and there.

For a full salad, it should have been plate sized since it should be a meal. So, she charged me for a full but gave me the side salad on top of giving me half of what the other customer got on the main dish. Needless to say, I wasn't happy.

After cooling down, I contacted the owner Pat and issued my complaint. His nonchalant response told me a few things. 1) He is getting other complaints like this and didn't seem surprised by it 2) He is probably the one telling them to cut back on customers portions and not explaining the menu and 3) he really doesn't care about his customers like he promises. He actually said customers raved about their big portion sizes (which I know is a lie, since I  have since talked to numerous former customers who complained about the same things...overly pricey, poor service, skimpy on portion size). 

Like I said, this used to be a great restaurant several years ago. I don't know what happened, but like a lot of Tucson restaurants it has really gone downhill. I don't take clients here anymore. I don't go at all. It was the owner's response that pushed me to end my patronage. Service is such a huge factor in a restaurant and this places blows when it comes to customer service.

First and last time customer.    Have been driving by here for years and since the food truck we wanted to stop at was closed, we decided to try it.  

First off, I can't imagine that they pass code.   The inside felt so dirty and I am wondering to myself why we even ordered food.  

I got two hard beef tacos.   They come with seasoned beef, lettuce, tomato and salsa on the side.   My taco crumbled everywhere and was nothing special.    The beef wasn't really seasoned, so the whole taco was bland.  Could have gone to Taco Bell and got the same thing for half the price.   

The food is already prepared and they just heat it in the microwave.   Cheese doesn't even have enough warmth to melt.

I can't remember the last time I gave a business a 1 star, but this place truly deserves it.

After a couple years I decided to give this place another chance (my first visit I was unimpressed with the brisket). This time I got a hungry pork dinner with beans and yams. I arrived within ten minutes of ordering on the phone, so the order did not sit around. I drove it straight home 5 blocks and opened it to find both sides tepid. The meat could have been hotter also. The flavors were pretty good, but not enough to bring me back.

Very good food, very attentive staff. 
They didn't have a liquor license when I ate here with a party of four, but I really enjoyed the food and the service. Definitely going back soon!

I love Jimmy John's in general but this location needs to change out their staff......The guy who is there at 4pm - 5pm everyday thinks he's the shit and is more worried about how cool he thinks he is than interacting with the customer.....The other guy making my sandwich is trying to flip his hair around right over my sub....very unprofessional place that needs a manager on site to straighten them out or get new personnel.....My company gets deliveries from them and seems okay but once I told them about the service and what's going on as they make your sub they stopped ordering Jimmy John's from that location.

Never disappoints! Have never ever had a bad meal here over my dozen or so visits. Menu changes daily to frequently but the jackfruit carnitas is a fav so is the cauliflower but also the machaca. Idk cant really pick a fav!! Oh and the soups sublime!! Everything is fresh! The staff is always friendly. Love the atmosphere, the drinks and cookies. Don't skip on this one.

This is hands down one of the best meals I've had in my life. The flavors were just crazy good. 
Presentation included a bit of deserved fanfare. I've been trying to get a reservation for almost a year and it was worth it. Whatever you have to to get in, sell a kidney, your soul, do it!  Food was so good and pretty that I felt it disrespectful to take pictures.

First time here today. It was the day after Christmas and they were open. I was greeted warmly by I believe the manager and his staff. The food was prepared great and didn't take that long. I got the teriyaki chicken rice bowl. Chicken tastes great and the veggies were great too. They were completely alright with substituting the side of pickled cucumbers with a side of hard-boiled eggs. If you want a tasteful relatively inexpensive meal during your lunch break or in the evening this is the place to go. 5 stars for service, food preparation. And good taste. I'll be coming back!

We went in here for dinner on an empty Wed. After waiting 15 mins for a host, he flippantly tells me there are no big booths. We'd have to wait; how long, I ask? He can't tell me that, he rudely looks at me and says - when people get up or a server shows up. After sitting for 5 mins and listening to the host rudely talk about how we wouldn't put a baby in a booster at a table we left and went to bertuccis and were treated the way you should treat customers. Last visit for us - you lost customers.

I went to Le Viet for dinner with a group of seven. I had been interested in trying this place for a while but never got around to it, so was excited to finally try it. Service started out okay - drinks arrived quickly and our orders were taken. We received one of the appetizers very fast... but then the other apps we ordered didn't come out. Another server came over to ask if we had ever gotten the remaining apps and she made sure we received them. 

I think the delay in apps also caused a delay in entrees. I kept seeing other tables receive their food super quickly but it seemed like our table in the corner was forgotten. When dishes did come out, they were extremely staggered. I got my dish first and it sat in front of me for at least five minutes before anyone else got their entrees. 

My dish was fried shrimp in a ginger sauce with a side of rice. It was pretty tasty though I wish it had more vegetables involved aside from just onions. Everyone else seemed happy with their entree choices, which were mostly noodle dishes. 

I liked Le Viet but the service left a lot to be desired. It makes for an inexpensive night out so I'd give it another shot sometime, maybe with a smaller group.

Best pizza this side of Italy.  No, really.  Light crispy crust, super ingredients like you'd find at a street market in Rome.  Eaten there a dozen times at least and the quality is always top notch - service is unfailingly fast and friendly, the manager almost always stops by to make sure we're having a good time.  

Also an excellent place when you don't have much time.  Their million degree oven can get a pizza on your table in just a few minutes.  Just super.

I went back recently for lunch and had the best duck tamale! Also tried the pistachio guacamole made table side. Great! They adjusted a bit since opening and I will continue to return for their original and delicious food.

Even with a reservation, they couldn't seat us in the amount of time (30 min) that we chose to wait.  The only explanation was that "your table is currently occupied by a party who's been there for 3 hours, and it doesn't look like they're leaving soon".  Clearly this place is popular but clearly there's no management in charge of moving along the tables.  Thankful that we made a quick reservation and walked right over to At The Table.  Always a great experience there and happy to support a truly local business.

This was a great Chinese/Vietnamese place! Only order one dish- it's HUGE portions! At least 2 people can eat great on 1 order of food. I really enjoyed the vietnamese food here. They have hoisin sauce and siracha sauce on demand. Great find on yelp.

This place is amazing. I was incarcerated at bccf and they released me with no phone call just whatever money I had in my pockets. This place not only let me use their business phone to get in touch with my family, they have amazing food and are very hospitable. I had the cheese steak and fries and being from philly I'm a pretty bias review when it comes to cheesesteaks but theirs was absolutely delicious. I wish I lived closer because I would make them my go to pizza place, but for now, I'll enjoy them when I pass through the area. I highly recommend stopping in at least for a slice.

The food here is ok. But I had an issue with their menu. In the attempt to look sleek and trendy, their menus are difficult to read and deceiving. After listing the menu item on the left side of the page, the price is all the way at the right side of the page making it deceiving what the price for each item is. It's not exactly level with the menu item in left. So price could either be the lower one or the upper one. To add to confusion, the first menu item doesn't have the price listed this way, it was in a paragraph. I brought this up to the server who said I understand why you would be confused by the price, then walked away. The price of my meal was $7 higher than I thought it was going to be, I was upset over the principal of it. It took the Server a long, long time after she gave us the check to come back and answer my question about the bill. She was cleaning the kitchen trying to get out of there. Probably 1/2 hour we waited for her to come back. So I remediated the difference by giving her15% instead of 20%. The menu  issue was not her fault but the crappy service was! Note to Catch 23- UPDATE YOUR MENUS TO A VERSION WHERE THERE IS NO CONFUSION REGARDING THE PRICES! In this unstable economy added to the virus issues, people have many choices where to eat, and many going out make it a rare treat to do so because of money. Why not shut down any reasons a person has to NOT go back to your restaurant? After this virus is over, only the best of the best will still be in business.

I took advice from a local I sat next to at Ruby Slipper who said Drago's had the best oysters. 

I had heard of the establishment before in food lists, so I was surprised to find that around dinner time on a weekday it wasn't crowded at all. I was seated immediately and got my food promptly. 

I only ordered 1/2 dozen of the charbroiled oysters and was a little scared since I hadn't had oysters for years. The shells came out so hot, had to wait before eating. The flavor packed a punch for sure, it was the best thing to dip the pieces of bread in the leftover sauce and savor the butter and salt and...whatever else they put in there. 

I can't compare to other oyster spots in the city, but I enjoyed the dish a lot!

Went here with a party of 6 . The restaurant was 75%full and we were seated right away. 
Our waiter took great care of us as he got our drinks out really quick. He also kept refilling our chips and salsa. It did take longer than expected for our dinners to come out but it was worth the wait. 2 of us got the burrito texcano-- beef and shrimp stuffed with rice and beans topped with green salsa and white cheese sauce. Everyone enjoyed their meals and they were reasonably priced.
This place is consistently excellent. Best fried rice I've ever had, and my boyfriend loves the Spicy Basil and the Pad Kra Pao... but be sure to get those with ground chicken!

The drinks were really good. We tried two different margaritas, pineapple and blood orange.  The food was mediocre.  Service was mixed.  Some servers were very engaging, while others had to be engaged.

I love these burgers so much! I've had the LOUIE and the Classic (get the triple. The patties are small so a double just isn't as good). 

Both are awesome. Louie has kinda a cheesesteak vibe with the peppers and onions if you're in the mood for that. If I go with a friend I like to get one classic and one Louie and split. 

Rip fries are always great here too. Definitely get those too! 

My favorite spot for burgers right now love this place

I have given these guys a few shots to get their food right. Showed up and saw couple of employees had another employee stranded outside:  owners paying dead labor. 2nd manager on duty doesn't give a rats ass. 8/23 23:30hrs. 

Avoid them at any cost!!!! Go home and cook!!!

We stopped in for the hibachi grill a couple weeks ago, having just recently done hibachi at a different establishment a month or two ago. Based on our previous experience, the bar was already high.

Unfortunately, we didn't have that great of an experience. The service was just fine. Nothing over the top or offensive, just serviceable. The main course and hibachi show could use a lot of work. The guy we had seemed to be 'phoning it in.' He didn't put on a show like some of the other spots around here will.

The rice was just ok, kinda bland, and the sauces didn't add much of a punch of flavor. We had the filet and chicken and they were just ok also. Not a lot of seasoning or flavor there either.

The pot stickers we had as an appetizer were the best thing we ate. Even the edamame was just 'meh'. Next time we're hankerin for hibachi, we'll head elsewhere. We did not sample any of their other dishes or sushi, so we can't speak to those.

I give this place 4 stars because they're more affordable and for a strip mall sushi place, their stuff is fresh. I have had worse Sushi in Santa Barbara for twice the price...yep, not too many decent Sushi restaurants in the area. The only downside of this place is that sometimes the rolls do not hold well together ..they come undone before you get them to the soy sauce. Anyway, I'm more forgiving of this place because the fish is fresh and  I get enough of it  for my money and then some.

Amazing place to dine at on a special evening! I have been here maybe a year ago or so and felt quite underdressed mainly because it was an unplanned visit. We were starving and felt like some Italian food and without really researching this place, we drove to their parking lot, walked in at the door and surprisingly were treated in a fabulous manner regardless of the jeans and t-shirt deal. I remembered the food being fantastic (I had the vitello alla diana) and the service to be equally top-notch.

Last night, however, was much better and much more of an anticipated event. I started with a glass of cabarnet (it was a Sonoma wine), browsed for a bit but then ended up deciding on one of the specials, a 14-oz strip steak charred to my liking with some white wine and shrimp concoction on top served with potatoes and green beans. I had the steak cooked medium rare as usual and it was quite a mouth-watering treat. Dining partner ordered the other special which was the wild boar pasta. I had a slice of the wild boar sausage and it tasted quite lean and gamy (not a bad thing). Delicious!

I had the tiramisu for dessert. Wow! I think I might seriously be in love with it. It was my type of dessert... Not so sweet. It had some element to it that I couldn't quite put my finger on.

Service was wonderful and so was the atmosphere. Water glasses were kept full and server returned in a timely manner with orders or just to check on things. He was also very personable but kept things professional. What a lovely dining experience!

This place is a gem!  We stopped for lunch after driving around the area seeing if we could find a pick up truck.  

We are k side the restaurant. The service was great the food was probably the hottest I have ever had. Right off the grill!i highly recommend if you want some great soul food!
The food was amazing and the service was incredible.  Definitely recommend this place!
Awesome food, kind staff. Made my house fried rice without mushrooms. Try Veggie dumplings they are awesome

Ok, it's a little strange to walk into a house to eat Indian food.  However once we got over that it was on.  The service was great and the atmosphere was nice.  I had lamb biryani which was off the charts and my wife and daughter split a tandoori chicken which was perfectly cooked and well-presented.  We got a couple of appetizers, plus some extras they brought out and our entrees for $38 and I'll be eating Indian for the next two days.  Cant wait to go back.

Decided to try this place for lunch. Lunch menu prices were ok, except the price listed on the menu didn't match what I had to pay. I ordered the crab roll lunch special which gives you a choice of a side soup, chips , and drink. Overall the crab roll was good! It was a bit colder than expected. Plus for the lunch special, it seems as though they give small (1/2) portions. I would definitely order the lobster corn chowder again!

No way this gets less than 5 stars; i'm still on a cheesesteak high after having eaten mine last night.  I was in Philly yesterday and was disenchanted with the mystique of Philly cheesesteaks after getting Geno's a few years ago, which was so forgetable.  I came in last night around 8:15 and it was packed, but i read ahead and decided what i wanted so i got my order in quick: cheesesteak w/ american, grilled onions, mushrooms, and a bottle of coke (10.75 total).  I ate it in my car afterwards and it was heavenly.  So many things were perfect about it, but i think it starts with the meat, which is finely chopped and piled on liberally.  It makes a world of difference that it's chopped fine, as it gives room for the cheese to soak in and the other stuff to blend.  They use banging mushrooms as well, the kind that explode with juice when you bite into them.  I think they used white american instead of yellow.  And the cheesesteak is enormous, which means that they give you a lot for the moderate price that you pay.  If i had to criticize, i'd say that the onions and mushrooms weren't mixed in well with the meat and that it's messy as hell to eat. Also if they charged a dollar less, my order would have been under $10. The most powerful food transcends taste buds and uplifts your day; this definitely did it for me.
Amazingly fresh salsa made at your table with fresh everything.  Had a delish chimichanga. Super service and all around great.  Great for families. They had a mariachi this last Sunday evening.

always the best chinese food !!    this place is always open late and the food is th best in the city , open till 3am on weekdays ,what ??   the best wonton soup ,,,,we walnut cream shrimp,salt and pepper soft shell crabs ,,,sauteed baby bok choy ,,,,and delicious short ribs ,,,,,,and it was all amazing ,,definitely try their salt and pepper dishes

The food was decent, but the service and general uppity attitude of our server ruined our experience, as did the surprise mandatory gratuity on our check. And the prices? Holy moly. If this place could truly justify charging upwards of $20 for a dish of pasta itself with no salad or sides included, then they wouldn't be distributing coupons to drum up business.

The first frustration was that we were told we could only use our coupon for entrees, 95% of which contain veal. My boyfriend and I explained that we are both vegetarian, so our server (a middle aged woman with short dark hair -- I don't recall her name) said she would make an exception and allow us to use it for pasta if we both ordered salads at $5.25 a pop. We reluctantly agreed, even though we didn't understand the reasoning because some of the pastas cost as much as an entree anyway.

It was surprisingly tricky to find a completely meatless pasta dish. I ordered the mushroom risotto, and he had mushroom ravioli which was served in some kind of milky broth rather than a sauce. Although the portions were decent and everything tasted fine, it was not impressive enough to justify the cost. I would describe it as slightly more upscale than the Olive Garden, but with cheesier decor.

Throughout our meal, our server did not pay attention to whether or not we needed refills of our beverages or bread and butter. We had to repeatedly flag her down and ask. We also ate the majority of our meal surrounded by our used salad and bread plates because no one cared to clear them. The place wasn't busy at all. She was just preoccupied chatting and cackling around the corner.

To add insult to injury, when we received the check, we saw that a mandatory gratuity had been added. The concept of a mandatory gratuity for two people is ludicrous, and if this had been stated upfront, we would have left immediately. The customer alone should be able to decide what to tip (or whether to tip at all) based on their experience. But the guaranteed tip explains why there was no incentive to provide the kind of service that would encourage repeat business, just as the inflated prices explain the shiny black BMW we noticed parked by the back door as we were leaving.

Pad Thai, lemongrass soup and spring rolls, mmm! My medium heat was exactly as I expected (from Siam here locally), and while the soup wasn't strained as much, the taste was perfect. The owners are friendly, and  interested in knowing their customers. They do have a small staff, so beat the weekday lunch rush by getting there at 11:30 on the dot, right now you can walk in and grab a seat for dinner. Earlier reviews called attention to inconsistency in heat levels during the early days of opening. The owners have been aware and worked that out between cooks. We'll be back to dine in and carry out!
Great meat lovers pizza. Buffet had lots of options too. Has televisions inside for games and other entertainment.

A staple of Indianapolis cuisine, Jordan's Fish and Chicken is truly one of the finest dining experienced you will ever have. The staff is quite friendly and they love their regulars; nothing can beat the outstanding customer service at this five star restaurant. Jordan's is a great place to just kick back and relax for a bit. The atmosphere is delightful, so bring a good book and chill out for a while. When you go to Jordan's you'll feel right at home.
Great Lunch and Dinner spot. Had the special at lunch, the Roasted Bell Pepper with a side of garlic smashed potatoes.  Fresh homemade rolls are also specially good baked fresh.

What is not to love?  Awesome food and great service!

I love a restaurant that treats customers with respect and value the business...the First Watch customer commandments  reflects that.  But enough about that, let's talk food!

The food at First Watch always seems fresh and tasty while offering a rather light meal. I have enjoyed several meals here but love the farm house tacos for breakfast.  Grilled chicken with chorizo and a California blend on top.

*We have only gotten delivery from this location*

Food came fresh and delicious, which I'm pretty sure is the standard with as popular CSC is and how much chicken salad they must go through!  

They're wheatberry bread is wheat bread...don't let the name steer you.  It's soft and delicious!  It's my goto bread with the Classic Carrol.  I add lettuce and tomato and the tomatoes are always red and perfectly ripe.

Their soups are ok.  Hit or miss for me, but I like to get it with the 1/2 sandwich combo b/c a whole sandwich is a lot of food.  I'll get my husband a whole sandwich with their yummy yummy pasta salad and trade the soup/pasta with my husband.  Win/win we're all happy.

My daughter likes their mac n cheese.

$8-12 pp.  Tip - cookie comes with most of their meals, you don't have to order one separately unless you want extra.
Good pizza. Not great....they over cook mine. It even looked burnt but they served it anyway and I ate it so I guess it was alright? Strange location but again, pretty good pizza.

The food is good, and service is decent. Prices are a bit high. My husband loves the special chicken with the peanut sauce. I like the pad Thai, which I get with extra egg instead of meat or tofu. I would recommend it if you are in the area!

Very busy place.  Took about 20 minutes to get a table.  Waitress was very nice and informative!  Tried the chicken tenders and shrimp.  Shrimp were good but the chicken was just plain, no flavor.  Mac and cheese was good.  Wife had ribs and they were spicy and tasted good but a little chewy.  Overall not a bad meal.  Would go back and try the chicken fried steak to see if that passes the taste test.. lol  Manager came by and gave wife some cookies and thanked us for visiting (our first time).

The pizza was awesome!!! Great food and great service. 
I will definitely drive 30 minutes again to get a pizza

This restaurant is the type of the place I would never go again, bad attitude,sour face,like we owe them money. In the end
STRONG SUGGESTION！！DO NOT GO THERE！EVER！！！！！！！！！！！！！！！！
They don't have now the happy hour, none of the offers from yelp were available. Food is not very fresh. Service was ok

I am obsessed with the potato/carrot gravy they put on their chicken katsu! I could eat it every day if obesity wasn't an issue, haha.

The staff are super nice. I usually order via phone for pick up since there isn't a lot of space to wait.

I have absolutely no idea why this pizzeria I stop yesterday - the pizza was TERRIBLE. Absolutely everything was wrong with it, from the consistency of the crust ‍whole pizza was burned I will 
Never ever stop and I wouldn't recommend this place

They say, "Jack of all trades, master of none," and that more often than not applies to Asian restaurants that try to cover too many types of Asian food, but that is definitely not the case with Hoshun.  Hoshun's chef takes pride in his take on Asian cuisine and it shows.

The Chinese items are clearly cooked to order.  Many Chinese restaurants rely on pre-made sauces that are gooped on as the nondescript meat and vegetables are cooked up.  That's not the case here.  Everything that comes out of the kitchen is exceedingly fresh.  I used to sing the praises of Five Happiness's hot and sour soup as the best in town, but Hoshun has Five Happiness beat in every respect, including the hot and sour soup.  

In addition to some great soups, the appetizer menu features some jalapenos stuffed with crab, crawfish and cream cheese called J-shun pops, I believe.  They are absolutely wonderful.  They also feature a Thai chili-beef salad that is out of this world, one of the best salads I have ever eaten.  And as for the sushi, Hoshun's sushi bar can hold its own against the better sushi bars in town.  The happy hour specials include $1 nigiri, which for anyone with poor impulse control like me is a god-send.  I've ordered the chirashi on more than one occasion, and the presentation is different than many places where the sashimi is placed atop a large bowl of rice.  Aesthetically, it's one of the most striking plates of food I've ever seen in a restaurant.  

Hoshun also has very reasonable lunch specials and offers delivery.  All in all, there's not much to not like about Hoshun.
Very delicious! Had 9 different rolls split between 3 people walked out stuffed and Happy! Very friendly and clean ! Love this place

Becky was a great server! She made sure that I always had my drink, before I ran out. I did not even have to ask. She brought out dips for my wife and I. I really appreciated that because other place have a tendency to just bring out one dip. The chargrilled oysters were sooo flavorful! The cup of gumbo was very tasty. My wife and I shared the shrimp platter and it was so delicious. I love this place and will come back. Definitely recommend.

So I went to this place on a spur of the moment "I forgot to bring lunch to work" type moment. I'm so happy I didn't opt to get Pho across the street (not that's its bad, just had it so many times). So I got the chopped brisket dinner which came with two sides and a drink. Just over $14 for brisket with Mac and cheese, collard greens, cornbread, obviously chopped brisket, and a bud light to wash it all down. The hot bbq sauce is amazing and I used about 1/3 of the bottle. 10/10 would recommend to anyone looking to get some quick BBQ.

I understand that when you come in for a lunch special it is just a quick bite, but you still want to be treated special. My mans soul had a eyebrow hair in it for starters . Our food literally came out five minutes after we ordered. Our egg rolls and crab puffs were cold on the inside and the fried rice just looked like they half soy sauced it. The beef with broccoli was steaming hot and drowning with sauce. They never came back to ask hpw our food was or anything . Go to Panda. At least you know what to expect.

I'd love to give them a higher rating but we couldn't even eat here as we were told that the wait time was 40 minutes for take out. They are deep frying chicken tenders here folks and not creating a gourmet meal.
Hopefully they get their sh*t together as the concept is great and I'd like to review their food as it compares to Nashville establishments.

Great service and fantastic food.  Getting in and out is a bit hectic.  The crowd in the lobby is always big and getting seated is a bit of a cluster.

It's worth it.  Food is great
This is the place for hot fried crispy chicken. Fried okra as a side makes for a good meal. They accept phone in orders which is a convenience I really like.
one comment: they need to stop pretending lemon is the same as lime....JUST STOP!
Very yummy, I recommend the chorizo quesadilla although it is pretty expensive for a quesadilla but it is huge and tasty

Visiting from out town and this restaurant was very welcoming and the perfect end to a long traveled day. The service was fantastic. The waitress was very knowledgeable and was eager to help very charming. For appetizers we had the tuna tartar, fried calamari, and the shrimp arrabbiata and they were all amazing. For an entree I had the cavatelli al pesto genovese and it was excellent as well. The others at my table had another pasta dish and pizzas but we couldn't finish much as we had eaten so much of our appetizers. Will definitely go back if visiting St. Pete again.

I was looking for Indian style food while visiting the Market for lunch and found it at the Spice Box. I tried the Bombay Box which had grilled chicken, a spicy green chutney and sweet orange chutney over basmati rice. A soft boiled egg was also included. The staff was took the order very quickly and had my bowl ready within 5 minutes. Everything was well seasoned, spicy with a large amount of chicken. This ended up being too much food for me for lunch but I was able to take the rest back.
This place satiated my craving for Indian food while in Indianapolis. Check this out for a tasty and satisfying lunch during the week.

Atmosphere: The Gumbo Shop has a great vibe that was more lively and fun than chaotic in terms of energy. 

Service: We were seated immediately and our server was very funny and welcoming. Our food came out pretty quickly and all within 5 minutes of each other.

Food: We ordered the chicken andouille gumbo, jambalaya, and crawfish and pasta in tasso cream. The jambalaya was a bit underwhelming but still good. It just did not taste as rich or developed in flavor as the other dishes. The crawfish pasta had a great sauce; however, the crawfish was a bit tough/chewy. The big winner of this meal was obviously the gumbo. Voted the best in town, it was super flavorful, and we ended up cleaning the plate with the great bread they provided.

Overall: The Gumbo Shop has great food, most notably the gumbo! If you come to eat here, focus on what they do best. The gumbo lived up to its hype, and we'd definitely come back to try more gumbo.

Tried this place out twice, pretty good. The best place I've been to is 9th and Washington, but this place wasn't bad at all. I've had the Shrimp Pho and recently I tried the Bún Tôm Nng (Shrimp over Vermicelli) and it was delicious! (Not  a lot of shrimp though, maybe about 4 pieces)

Went for happy hour, I was nicely dressed as was my husband.  We sat on the patio for 15 minutes without one server acknowledging us.  They were bringing drinks and apps to everyone else around us.  Eventually we went inside and ordered our own drinks from the bar.  Both cocktails were ok, nothing special. After we finished we left, at that point still no one had even offered us a menu, water, ect.  We were happy to take our business to one of the many other bars on Royal Street where the service was much better.

I love the Capitol location. I've been coming to this place regularly since last fall, and have always had a nice experience. The service is always on point and breakfast is always filling and delicious! 

I'm a huge fan of their hashbrowns and pancakes. The bacon is also good. And when I ask for my eggs overeasy, they are!

Weekends are super busy, so plan ahead if you've got a schedule.  It's to be expected since the location is close to downtown and the university.

Decent seafood for land locked Alberta. There is a pretty diverse menu and lots of options to choose from. Our server was friendly. I can't say that I found the atmosphere was amazing, however we managed to get a table on a busy Sunday night.  The cheddar biscuits are always a fun starter. My friend had the steak and shrimp. She enjoyed it. I had the choose two option - I chose the seared scallops, and the Walt's favourite shrimp. I chose rice as a side and added on the fire cracker broccoli. It was hit an miss. Seared scallops were fresh, however a bit undercooked. However, for scallops I would choose undercooked to overcooked anytime. The Walt's favourite shrimp was average. Simply a battered shrimp. Nothing fantastic, however it tasted fresh as well. Rice was really well cooked but not enough of it. Really disappointed with the broccoli for an extra 3 bucks I expected a lot better tasting stuff than what I received. It was blackened waaay too much. This was my friends pick. Not sure if I would go here again.

This is a no-frills Mexican joint.  Don't have high expectations, but do have an empty stomach. 

The burritos and tacos are good and will do the trick if you're craving something spicy.  Skip the nachos and cheese though as the cheese is gooey and resembles cafeteria-style cheese whiz.  

Bottom line: It's cheap.  It's decent.  You can grab lunch for under $5.  Try doing that anywhere else in this area.
The endless sushi is good, and so is the hibachi. Takes a little while to get your food though, so be prepared for a wait.

I ate dinner last Wednesday night at Kayne. It was so good, i returned Thursday with a different group. Everything from the greeting hostess to dessert was fabulous. The service was great and everything at this place sells itself without any coaxing. 
I will go everytime i return to Nashville in the future.

Hogwood BBQ was exactly what we needed after a long drive. We stayed at a hotel nearby and this was a perfect choice for dinner. First of all, the place is spotless. So clean you could eat off the floor. The food was fantastic. I really wanted ribs but they were all out. The pulled pork platter was great. My son had the brisket and it looked amazing. I chose the grits as a side I loved the hint of spicy. The service was great as well. While we were eating a staff member came over and gave us all free
mini moon pies to as a thank you for coming in. Nice touch. Don't miss out on this great place! Next time we will come earlier for ribs.
Best fallafel outside of the middle east.  Also very cheap.  The eggplant sandwich is also good.  Get the carrots and olives on the side.

Not impressed.

For what I'm paying. I would expect something bigger and badder. 

I wanted to be impressed. I like Emeril. The restaurant was beautiful. 

But nothing ever really popped out and shocked my taste buds. It was uneventful for my mouth.

Sorry dude.

It's like watching the most epic movie trailer, only to be slightly disappointed with the movie itself.

My family has gotten take-out pizza from Piccolo Trattoria several times and the Old World Brooklyn Pie is dynamite!  Fresh plum tomatoes, basil, and mozzarella di buffalo.....absolutely delicious.  They have a huge menu, but I have yet to try anything else other than this pizza....which is wonderful.

What. A. Gem. 

Came in for a late brunch and was blown away. Justin is a masterful chef and exceptional guy, and what he's done here is magnificent. From the house-cured ginger infused salmon rolls, fish dip, and conch wonton to the best Benedict I've ever had (roast plantain stuffed crabcake Benedict), topped off by a guava jelly doughnut...this was one of the best brunches I've experienced in the Tampa Bay area. You'll love this quaint little find in the middle of Safety Harbor!

So sorry to write a negative review but I think it is public service.

Having a serious craving for vegan dim sum my family and I planned lunch at New Harmony.  We ordered about eight items and...seven were not worth eating.  (The eighth, scallion pancake, was ok).

Bean curd skin roll, pork bun, turnip (especially horrible), vegetable dumplings (hard to ruin!) and oh--that sesame beef in mustard sauce which tasted like a really bad candy.  Ugh!

We ate a bit of each (enough to find out we didn't want more) and left.

I know this place has many great reviews.  We've eaten here, long ago, and been very happy.

What happened???

Giving three stars because their PIZZA AND ZIA FRIES ARE OUT OF THIS WORLD. 

I do love me a good cheeseburger (our family dog is name cheeseburger!) so I got excited when our (mine and my fiancé's) favorite pizza place for votes 'Best Burger in Nashville'- should have been voted 'Best Burnt Burger in Nashville'. I don't mind paying $19 dollars for a burger but if I am going to, I want it to be perfect. This burger was BRUNT, and that is all that was tasted. Never again getting Le Matt.
My 12 year old son's new favorite restaurant.  He said the ribs were the best he ever had!

My beyond favorite place to eat!  From the sweet tea, to those delicious rolls and the blackened chicken pasta it's all so good and worth the drive every time!  Also the prices are very inexpensive! The Oven Baked Chicken is amazing, so much flavor!

It is an excellent restaurant ! The food was delicious. It was a quite place to enjoy with our friends and family. A good old jazz music was playing during our lunch. Also, the waiter was an old guy very funny and talkative, his service was great. The wine that he recommended us was very good. We were 8 and everyone were happy with it. We hope we can go back soon to suite 103!.
Best waffle I ever had. Clean and laid back atmosphere. The owner was very open to feedback. I expect this place to catch on real quick once they get their liquor license.

Ordered 2 giant BLT's, and 2 large fries.  Cost me 24 dollars.  Got home, and each of the sandwiches had 2 pieces of bacon on them, one tomato, and an entire head of lettuce.  Food tasted good, but for the ridiculous price, I would have thought some bacon on the sandwiches would have been appropriate!
Great food! Great service! Very knowledgable staff. I don't drink alcohol, but they brought me a soda that was made in house and delicious. Definitely would go again!

Sushi Saga is definitely a hole in the wall place hidden inside the Siena casino resort. There is not enough credit given to these guys and the work of art they create. Chefs Daniel, Justin, and Don are all very accommodating and personable. Don't see anything on the menu that you want? No problem. They'll be more than happy to make something special for you. Not into sushi? Not a problem either. They have Chinese food too! 

Sushi is always fresh here, with the highest quality ingredients available. The flavors these chefs put together are spectacular. They have a very wide variety of different sauces made in-house (around 17 last time I heard) so it's not only unagi and spicy mayo like most other places. Everything they send out on a plate is phenomenal, as you can see in the pictures yourself. 

Definitely give this place a try next time you're craving sushi. However, it IS a smaller joint (about 10-12 seats at the bar and 10 tables) so if you plan on going in with a big group, reserve! Especially on weekends!

Good place to get a quick cup of corn and crab bisque before your flight. My food only took about 5 minutes, although there wasn't a line when I was there. Bisque was pretty good, and the biscuit was great. I would recommend this place as it is reasonably priced for the airport and the food is pretty good.

The service was excellent but the food was disappointing. My eggs were undercooked and the spinach quiche was hardly a quiche. It was layers of whole spinach leaves topped with gobs of melted cheese, barely any egg. The taste was straight cooked spinach and I had to return it after a single bite (something I hardly ever do!). Staff was very helpful however. We've eaten here before and loved it-was very surprised by this experience.

Eat-Rite Diner was a place I would go while downtown late and looking for anywhere to eat.  Now, I will go to Eat-Rite when I'm looking for that yummy diner meal.  The new owners have done a great job with the menu (very little changes) and the decor was nice and clean!  The slinger and Reuben were great, and I believe the sliders were the best in St. Louis.  And, thank you to the staff for the great service.  We will be back!

Gumbo had cubed, processed chicken. Jambalaya rice was over cooked with very little tomato or any flavor really. It also had no noticeable trinity (bell pepper/onion/celery)  in it?! Red beans and rice were good, but not anything exceptional. The French bread was basic and I've had much better/authentic. Go elsewhere for New Orleans flavors.
I've been visiting this place for years. I always enjoy the food I order. The shrimp and broccoli is my favorite!

Had dinner at 5 PM and highly disappointed! The food was not fresh! I am certain it's the left over from yesterday's food! Definitely was not prepared/ cooked today!! Thee worset Indian food I ever had!

Do not come here.  This is the fish sandwich. Do you see the fish?  I do not. We had to send both meals back and just pay for our apps. The other meal was a fish meal that was way way over cooked and dry.  The waitress was not surprised we were sending thing back. Save your time and money and go elsewhere.

Wanted to try this for the service (was looking for the grumpy old man) but instead I was greeted by extremely friendly people (I am thinking the son of the owner and maybe the wife of the owner). They addressed us and spoke to us and treated us really well! The food is probably the most affordable Korean food (or any food, maybe) around this area - and it is also very delicious. Seating is mainly standard bar seating on stools with a few tables for 2. This is a cash only place.  I would definitely come back! Hoping to meet the infamous grumpy owner.

Went here for date night last Friday night. Place was totally empty when we sat down, which was odd for a Friday night. Staff was fast but not very conversational. We ordered the crab rangoon, vegetable lo mein, and general tsos chicken. Food came out fast and tasted GREAT! Service was fast and prices were decent. Another group came in while we were there so it wasn't just us. Heard they had a buffet option but I guess it is for lunch only. Overall very good food, would definitely go here again if we were in the area.

As a half Middle Eastern person, I'm always on the lookout for good Middle Eastern food. We had noticed this place while running errands one day. We eventually decided to try it. We got a sampler of all their meats. It was absolutely delicious! We also tried their Zatar with Lebneh and a desert. Everything was fresh, tasty and well packaged. Reminded me a bit of home. 

My wife was actually the one to go inside to get the food. Since it's still during COVID we've been doing takeout. She absolutely loved the atmosphere there and said the staff were "extremely friendly and attentive." It is a little more expensive than what I would expect to pay but not so much so that I'd be deterred. They delicious flavors and excellent service are worth it.

I had a 7:30 reservation for dinner last night. The host told me he was still waiting on tables to free up and to give him my phone number and wait in the car. I called an hour later and he told me there were four other reservations in front of me. I never got another call back for my reservation. I waited 90 minutes in the parking lot before leaving to find somewhere else. They could have at least been courteous and told me when I got there that there were others in front of me waiting. Rude.

Food was good but not great! We wanted gumbo but was disappointed it wasn't amazing in New Orleans at this spot. Drinks didn't have tons of alcohol. Beautiful ambience but the hostess was terrible trying to get folks seated. Honestly a very touristy spot.

I celebrated my 40th Birthday at Kanye Prime. Traveling in from KY and my guest from Florida I started the planning process in early September and after reviewing several reviews selected Kanye Prime as the venue of choice for a formal dinner party of 10. I contacted the restaurant and received an almost immediate response back from the event manager (Kelly) who was FANTASTIC to work with. We went through private room options, menus, and ultimately I secured a reservation with the venue. During the months that led up to my reservation, Kelly and I kept in constant touch and I felt reassured my bday dinner would be perfect and it was! Thank you Kanye Prime for a first class and Amazing experience. Oh... the food was FANTASTIC! Yes you all want to know about the food! Everything including dessert was phenomenal!
The BEST pizza! Very small menu, but everything is delicious. I generally like topping on my pizza, but the best way to enjoy the red pie at Gennaro's is plain. Cash only and BYO.

This place has got to be one of the most rudest places i've been to!!! Customer service sucks, and only 2 of the waiters had pleasant attitudes. They have a dif attitudes&behaviors when serving blacks vs others. Will not be returning back there!

We each had a very tasty pita and soda.  I needed a moment to figure out how it worked, but then it was good.  Staff was nice and helpful.
The place needs better signage as it is easy to miss.

I love this place. Every time I'm in IV, I stop here. I love the Avo toast altho' their supply of avocados has been inconsistent lately. The pineapple salsa is excellent.

I also get the HiWi Beef Burger but get the salad with the mango dressing. My only feedback for that item, is the size of the burgers are inconsistent. Sometimes, if the patty is small, the pineapple works but if they make the patty bigger, one ring of the canned pineapple isn't enough. And one time, they put the sriracha aioli on the salad instead of the mango dressing. It wasn't great.

I do love the atmosphere here. It's usually clean and friendly.

I did have an issue recently with service and it was so out of character compared to my past visits that I emailed them. The manager called me within hours and listened intently to me and explained what might be happening. She assured me she would address the situation with the employee in question. She was sweet and kind and very service-oriented.

The next day I received a long apologetic email from the employee in question. She totally owned her behavior and was very humble.

I certainly hope they survive because it's something different. The quality of the food is great especially for the price. It's mostly healthy (except the spam) and tasty.

This is the first hibachi grill that literally gave me this amount of food. Granted all hibachi restaurants give you a lot of food but Kabuto took it to another level with rice, veggie, meat and noodles. Kabuto had a lot of options to choose from as well. I'm definitely not complaining. Well yes I am...they didn't have plum wine (they were out). Oh and they were out of calamari. Because of that I had to take a way a star! Besides that the food was good, the cook was good as well and listened to everyone! Definitely will go back in the future.  Hopefully they will have plum wine then!

A terrible customer service experience.  Sunday evening 5/24/20 - approx 4:45 pm.  

My order at the intercom was taken professionally and accurately.   I ordered cole slaw as a side dish.  I was informed they did not any - I selected macaroni and cheese as an alternative.  No issues.

The problems started at the pick up window.  
As I pulled up the young, black male employee was looking/reading his cell phone before opening the window.  When he opened the window - I requested no gravy on my mashed potatoes and asked the employees to verify this info.  He did.

I paid using a debit card.  Upon examining my receipt - it was not my order.  I attempted 3x to get the employee's attention.

I finally got his attention.  I told him it was not my receipt - and he then said - "you can pay for the car behind you".....

This type of unprofessional response astounded me.  

Then the employee and 2 others were having a heated discussion over work schedules.  A female employee voice became louder.  She may have been a manager.  I could not see her.

The male employee begins to hand my order to 
me - holding the bag with with one finger as a "hook" thru the drive thru window with no eye contact, no thank you and no correct receipt!  

Fast food restaurants must realize that customer contact and professionalism is very important - not only at the counter - but at the drive thru as well.

Obviously, this was missing in my experience.

Train these teenagers to act professionally when dealing with customers.  A please, thank you and a sincere greeting/smile is all it takes.

If this employee had worked for me - I would have fired him on the spot.

If I could rate my experience as ZERO I would.

This was my first visit to Popeye's and it is my LAST.

TLDR: Food was terrible (i.e. food poisoning), service was worse.

I REALLY have to start listening to that little voice when it tells me to just leave a place!

We walked in and stood by the sign that says please wait to be seated.... And we waited, and waited, and waited.... As the three workers at the counter were having a conversation (having clearly seen us) we SHOULD have left then but were starving. 

Then after we were seated, even more waiting! Out waitress was watch GHOST!!!!! I mean it is a good movie and all but can we place our order. Eventually she came over to take our order.

The kitchen made French toast that was somehow burned AND under cooked..... I didn't think that was possible. The eggs were bad and the bacon had zero taste. Seriously HOW DO YOU MAKE BACON TASTE BAD????  

Then we got the bill and while not expensive, it wasn't exactly cheap. So let's review:

Service: Crap
Food: Made us crap
Prices: Crappy

That's a crap trifecta! Save you time and just go to Wawa
Great food really nice flavors and portions. Orange juice tasted great along with everything we ordered.

Me and the fella ate here yesterday and it was ok.  The building is huge but isn't the most appealing, especially with a loading dock out front. We had the buffet, of course. Some of the food was good and fresh, others not so much.  The rolls, chicken, and fried shrimp was kinda old. The sushi bar was a little suspicious looking.  I am BIG on sushi and I just could not bring myself to eat any sushi off the bar.  Looked like it had been sitting there a while.  I thinks it's best to go to this place early for fresher food instead of later in the evening as we did yesterday.

In town from Orange County,CA--the land of bad pizza--and tried Cristino's for lunch. The pizzas and calamari brought a tear to this Italian man's eye. Excellent food. Our server was fantastic. Definitely recommend it.

Yum! Only good things to say. I got the special offered for Philly Vegan week and it's delicious (still eating the leftovers). The feta and olive salad is one of the best things I've ever eaten. The hummus is delicious and the gyro was awesome too. The staff was also super nice which is always as perk. Thanks for a delicious meal. I'll be back.

Note that this place is no longer a Chinese buffet, or a buffet at all. It is ordered dishes. We showed up looking for the Chinese seafood buffet. 

We were disappointed at first but the staff and food were amazing. Pound of headless shrimp and crazy good and $7.99 LBS during the special!

Good prices, good food and nice people. Give them a try.

Spending the long Valentines weekend in Tucson and found this amazing restaurant on Yelp. Started with the pomegranate margarita and the prickly pear margarita. So tasty and served in cute Mason jars. Ordered the guacamole, super tasty. They make their own chips, so that is always a huge win for me. Ordered the 3 taco plate with rice and beans. The shredded beef tacos were so fabulous! They were deep fried which I haven't had in years.  Right when our food came a awesome mariachi band started playing. We topped off our meal with the churro nachos. What a great dining experience for our pre Valentines meal. We will be back again

Love this place! Bit of a hole in the wall but food is delicious. I would recommend to everyone I know. Gyros Plate and Greek Salad together was amazing. Roasted squash, onions, tomato and the rice with the yogurt sauce was mouth watering and delicious. Most definitely coming again.
I am so glad that I found this place...  It was worth the drug be from King of Prussia!   They have amazing cheesesteaks and awesome cheesy bread
Great lunch spot. The sandwiches and hot plates are delicious. Any weekday during lunch hour, there's a crowd. That's a good sign.

family favorite!
never been disappointed from eating there. food comes in huge plates. i'm a big eater and always take home left over from there. and its not cheap food either. its good food and lots of it. the bread and salad fill you up halfway before the meal comes out. and on some nights there's an old man who sings oldies.. great to take a date out.. or a wife. 
BYOB!!! or you can buy their wine, some from south jersey vineyards.

my fav is the seafood medley with pasta. :)

would go there anytime

Zero! Short cut story the place is zero, the food is zero and the owner is zero. Don't let the 5 stars confuse you! It's only the cheap price!Please. Get yourself a real sushi. I will save you my experience because it's long, but the owner told me to go and never be back because I wanted to return sushi that I asked nicely to change(I didn't touched it).He wanted to charge me 5$ , on sushi that took him 1 min, and I counted, ONE minute to prepare. At least make it look like you really make it! Shame on you. As an owner of a restaurant, the customer is always right, lucky  you that I'm not from this area, otherwise I would work hard that you would be out of business. And to whome reading this, don't go there. Save your time and your money. There are a lot of good sushi restaurants out there!

Location is cute but food is expensive and greasy.

My pizza was way too oily and the butternut squash ravioli drowned in butter and cream. I will not go back.
Homemade Dominican cuisine made with LOVE. I feel right at home here. The owner and staff are so hospitable. They have an array of options that are sure to satisfy the palate.
My go to place in the CBD for lunch. Fast, fresh and always consistent. Very happy they've hit their stride.
Favorite spot for BBQ in the city staff is friendly and food is amazing I'm getting hungry right now just thinking about it.  Memphis Queen is highly recommended.

I used to like Manee.  I haven't been for quite awhile, but i ordered carry out from them on Easter Sunday when they had no customers.  First I asked if we could sit while we waited since there was no one in the restaurant. The waitress rolled her eyes but complied.   Even though there were two waitresses and a waiter standing around doing nothing. we were told we would have to go to the bar to place our order.  Seriously? The worst part of the experience was the food.  SImply inedible.  The pineapple fried rice was terrible.  How can you mess up fried rice?  It tasted like they threw flour in it instead of seasoning.  it was terrible.  The curry and pad thai were just as bad.  I'm not sure who was in the kitchen, but it was bad.  I usually tip  for carry out because of the effort that goes into packing things up.  But, I felt the lack of service and overall complacency in the empty restaurant was ridiculous,
The service was great and the food was really good. The drinks were huge too. Lava cake was super rich.
Best Steak I've had in a long time. The Bone in Ribeye iwas spectacular!! Service was excellent!! Thank you to the staff that assisted our party!! I will be back real soon!!

This place is ridic.  Sure, the salads are great but, I just can't stomach paying 16 bucks for one.  Spinach, egg, cheese, and shrimp.  That's it.  Add on charges are crazy.  One buck for the egg, one buck for the cheese, and four bucks for a few tiny, tiny shrimp.  Rip off.

Less than "Stella"r.  I've been to Stella! 6 or 7 times over the past 4 years.  Unfortunately tonight will most likely be my last time.

I went with 4 other people and had called to make a reservation about a week before.  I asked for a 6:30 and was told they didn't have anything until 7:30.  Ok, well we figured we'd go early have a drink at 7:00 and then have dinner.  We arrive at 6:50 and the place was empty except for a single 2 top.  Strike 1.

The menu has changed to a Prix Fixe deal which I'm not crazy about. Strike 2.  We are told the table has to have either the 4 selection prix fixe or the 7 selection.  There are supplements for kobe beef or lobster.  hhhmmm. Whole thing was fishy. Speaking of, I had a smoked fish first course that seriously looked like raw chicken.  I took two bites, bleecckk.  Second course, had octopus that was good, but not great.  Third course I opted opted for the lobster and paid the $15 supplement.  Was just Ok.  Lastly had a bacon ice cream and baked apple dish.  Interesting dessert but overall not what I had come to expect of Stella.  

For $650 with no alcohol for 5 people all I can say is you can do much better in New Orleans.  #jumpedtheshark

Everything about this place screams amazing food! Location... Decor, staff ,environment even the chef.. but I was let down! Few reason why.... the menu is hard to navigate. A Kobob  isn't a kobob .... it didn't come on a stick with my main meal I didn't get any naan bread a staple of Indian cuisine. The portions were ridiculously small maybe a cup and a half of rice but copious amount of the main curry... an I to drink it? 

The flat bread is amazing I will give them that! And the shrimp appetizer I got was good as well. 

The one problem I did have was with acoustics if you're trying to have a conversation with somebody you have to end up talking over everybody else in the restaurant high ceilings big open area causes a lot of background noise makes it  hard to hear the person across from you or even your waiter or waitress! 

Price wise if out this up in the high end for what you get. I don't think the price value to food quantities is worth it. I probably won't go back again.
There wait isn't long and the food is always good. I've gotten the chicken Parmesans sandwich, the pizza and the chicken fingers so far

I would suggest having dinner before going.  The restaurant was hard to find since they have not had time to put up any type of signage because they are so busy, presumably.

I couldn't figure out why I could never get a reservation, it only seats 22 people....that's why.  On this Saturday night, they never filled up.

They only offer wine, beer & gin...no vodka, no tequila, no whiskey.

The twelve course tasting menus really was 12 tablespoons/teaspoons, at best.  All the food is prepared a head of time and re-warmed.  I will say the 3 dessert courses were very good.  A total of 6 of the twelve course were good, not great...however the other 6 needed revamping.

All the food was served beautifully with an artistic flair with amazing table top presentations...however a 1" circle of celery root that was grilled with a sour sauce is hardly a course...it's a bite.

The chef and sous chef were so somber & sad....bringing the mood down for this open concept, "we'll tell you what your eating" restaurant.  The loud rap music was simply annoying.  I was hoping there would be more interaction with the chef and his patrons...more of an communal environment.

The servers & manager were very nice and eager to please, however I felt the the entire experience was under whelming.  The "forgotten vegetable" courses were basically using parts of the vegetable that traditionally are not used...great idea, but not a $75 per person, plus the wine pairing.  All in for two people about $300.00.  Save your money, go to Elaia....a little bit more money....but a greater culinary experience.

Only reason this isn't 5 stars is the acoustics when dining in...very loud...Service was stellar, I think all family members. Menu is huge, but manageable. Went with my mother for a late bite, since neither of us were starved, decided to share the mixed appetizer platter which was enough for 4 people easily! I spotted others eating lamb chops, pork souvlaki, Greek salad and all looked happy with their orders and the amount of food was beyond sufficient....in fact most tables left with carry out containers. Mom is excited to return for the lamb shank or the charred octopus!  This is the real deal and totally unexpected! Go, go with a group and try lots of food! I know on my next visit to the area, we will definitely be back! Oh, and save room for the desserts...you won't be disappointed!

A very cool place w/a very cool vibe...a little on the pricey side, but the service was great, n the food was tasty...at least that night. It was our first experience.....looking forward to trying it again.

First off if the food wasn't so good I would never come back. I've been a couple times and service isn't all that great. There are usually people waiting to be seated and people waiting in line for pick up. 

The reason for this review is I called one night to place an order to go (waited on hold for 20 minutes) just for them to tell me they ran out of jerk EVERYTHING. How do you run out of jerk chicken/pork when your establishment is known for its jerk meals. 

I decided to give them another chance the following night. They ran out of spicy jerk chicken. They only had regular jerk chicken. Ok fine. Oh but they also ran out of white meat. Ok fine I guess I'll take dark meat only. If you can't keep up with the items on the menu then I suggest you take them off. 

The location is convenient for me but I might have to try the other locations from now on or just give up. The inside is a bit dingy and run down.
Now this is some good chicken. Chicken is perfect Nashville style, served ON A GRILLED CHEESE SANDWICH with spicy pepper cheese! Amazing!

This place has easily become our favorite Chinese restaurant. The service is amazing. Everyone here is so nice. We love the food... Our favorites are the mongolian beef and Spicy crispy beef! I am a huge fan of the spring rolls as well. We have dined in and done take out. The food is always quick and fresh. There is always enough to bring home too! Did I mention they have awesome service?

I have been to Nando's a couple times now and I can assure you of one thing - You will have a great experience here!

When you walk in, you're immediately transported to a different place.  With a big open space, lots of light (both natural and artificial), and so many colours, you feel like you've left Canada and entered an exotic location.  The aromas in the air are a mix of spicy and sweet, causing your mouth to water and your stomach to start grumbling.  

Nando's has such a laid-back feel to it, and this also shines through in their service.  Walking in the door, you are greeted by (usually) very friendly staff and seated quickly (unless it's lunch time - then expect a bit of a wait for both dine-in and take-out).  You peruse the menu then it's up to the counter to place your order.  One of the things I truly enjoy about Nando's is that you get to choose how your chicken is marinated.  For someone like me - who doesn't like a lot of spice - you have options such as "Plain-ish" and "Lemon and Herb".  For those of you who like your food to burn your esophagus on the way down, you can choose anywhere from "Medium" to "Extra Hot".  This is honestly some of the best chicken I have ever tried!  With their 24-hour marinating technique, the chicken is juicy and delicious, not dry and flavourless.

Even their desserts are pretty good!  I tried their Carrot Cake, and I would definitely order it again.  A couple people in my party tried the Nando's Kisses and seemed to enjoy them, too (but who wouldn't enjoy chocolate surrounded by chocolate surrounded by even more chocolate?!).

Do yourself a favour the next time you're on the south end of Edmonton and craving chicken - You'll be glad you did!
This location is relatively new and we stopped in to get food to go.  We had the Cuban sandwich and some tacos.  The Cubano was pretty average but the fish and chicken tacos where very tasty.

Long story short: The food was AMAZING. The service was ABYSMAL. 

Long story long: We found Eni's on Yelp and ordered through here/Grub Hub. We ordered 1 Eni's Salad, 1 Doner, and 1 Qofte. The delivery was surprisingly quick. When we opened up the bag/boxes with our order, there were 3 boxes; Box #1 with Eni's Salad, Box #2 with Qofte meat, tomato slice, onion slice, 12 French fries, 1 pepperoncini, and a packet of mayonnaise, Box #3 with somun style bread. We were obviously missing the doner in our order. I cut the bread in half thinking the doner meat was inside, because it was so thick, but nothing was inside of it. We called the restaurant to check and see if maybe they forgot to put it in the bag for the Grub Hub driver. The owner immediately raised his voice and yelled at us, "THE BIG BLACK MAN WHO PICKED UP THE ORDER GOT EVERYTHING!!!!" So....first of all, I'm not sure that a specific description of the delivery person was all that necessary but whatevs. I tried to speak calmly and politely to the man on the phone but he was having none of that. He just kept saying things like "I AM THE OWNER AND I KNOW WHAT I COOKED! WHY WOULD I LIE?!?!?! I AM RUNNING A BUSINESS HERE! THE BIG BLACK MAN TOOK ALL YOUR FOOD! HE MUST HAVE KEPT IT! YOU NEED TO CALL THE BLACK MAN" He kept talking over me and I finally had to raise my voice and tell him to stop talking and that I would call the delivery driver. Then he yelled again, "CALL ME BACK! ID LIKE TO KNOW WHAT HAPPENED!" So I hung up, then I called the number that texted us when our food was delivered. The driver answered. I told him what was up and he, politely, said "I just delivered what the restaurant gave me. Just one bag". So then, I called Grub Hub. The customer service guy at Grub Hub put me on hold and called the driver. He said the driver told him the same thing. He asked me what was missing, and refunded me for the doner. Since the angry owner of Eni's asked me to call him back, I did. I tried to tell him what the driver said and what Grub Hub said and that they refunded my money for the missing doner, but he just kept yelling and defending himself and saying that he knows that he put the food in there and that it isn't his fault. It's like....dude, I don't give a crap whos fault it is. From the get-go he could have just said "Oh know, I'm sorry that happened, let me call Grub Hub." Or "Oh wow, I thought I put it all in there, I'm sorry the doner is missing, I can make another if you'd like to come down and get it?" Like...anything would have been better than the way this guy from Eni's reacted. Anyway, it is a real shame that he was such an a-hole about the whole thing because the food was fantastic and we would have definitely eaten there in the future.

What a wonderful experience. I am so glad St. Louis has a restaurant this nice, fun and different. I loved everything about the experience from the time we were sat until the very end. I loved each bite. Portions were small but we did the 12 course meal so we were satisfied by the end. I would recommend doing the 12 course meal  over the other options. Pricey compared to other restaurants butvery decently priced for the type of food and the foodie experience you are getting. Very personalized service with a lot of attention given to you.  Can't wait to go back!

Delicious! My husband and I eat here often. I recommend the chicken satay,
Karuna's Fried Rice and the Gang Garee with chicken. Service is slow so go when you have some time, but the wait is worth it.

have updated with new pizzas we've tried. but there is a clear favorite...

WINNER: cheese louise is the hands down favorite with everyone we've taken here. recently, we decided to get it without the bacon and substituted ham, and we like that combination slightly better. honestly the ricotta is what
makes the cheese louise so irresistible. the texture may be difficult for some to get past at first, but just spread it around on your slice and you will be a believer!

-slaughter house five was good but a little greasy for my taste (with all the meat).
-thai was really delicious and like nothing i ever had before.
-the roasted potato was delicious, but we didn't realize it came with tomatoes. because tomatoes was listed after potatoes, still good.
-caprese was good but it wasn't as amazingly nuanced as some of the other special concoctions
-denver (hangover, sat& sunday special) SO GOOD. i was surprised how delicious a "breakfast pizza" could be.
-luau was delicious & housemade BBQ was on point
-dillinger was a little spicy for my taste, but those who prefer things with some heat really enjoyed it.

BREADSTICKS: get the creamy parmesan dip. it is addicting. the nickname crack sauce exists for a reason!

Overpriced and underwhelming. 

This was a second choice after we found our first destination too crowded. My companion was starving, and it's only out of desperation that we ended up at Varalli.

The decor is absurd, cheesy even - although I found the life-size giant squid on the ceiling somewhat endearing with a Santa cap placed whimsically on the end of its huge conical head. 

The squid was a sign that this is primarily a sea-food restaurant. My fellow diner had the lobster ravioli but I settled for the marinated chicken breast with a side of linguini. Both were sub-marginal yet cost a good $10 more than they were worth. There was nothing exceptional about the preparation or the presentation that justified the price and while the food didn't taste bad, it didn't taste particularly good, either. 

The only good thing about the experience was the live jazz that accompanied our meal. I

We took our daughter out for her 40th birthday.  We were greeted with complementary flutes of champagne, and a free dessert for her.

Open, airy, socially distanced gave a feeling of comfort. (unfortunately, we could NOT schedule a heated outdoor 'igloo' table, surrounded with plastic, and well heated). 

Staff was very attentive, food was magnificent. The spicings made familiar dishes taste just a little different, with a 'Solstice' touch.  For instance the Bouillabaisse had all the traditional tomato-y tastes, but still maintained a signature tang.  Short ribs and scallops were also SO good. They had a good variety of speciality drinks to start off with.

Moderately pricey, (this is Newtown!) but a great place to share special occasions.  If you can, you may want to ask for the 'igloo' seating outside, though we were warned, they got a little chilly in extreme cold temps.

Wow!  Humpty Dumplings in the beautiful shopping area of Keswick is the real deal.  These are about the best dumplings I've ever had in my life.  I tried all of them and liked everyone.  My top two favorites were the French Onion and Philly Cheesesteak dumplings.  The Buffalo Chicken, Honey Chipotle Chicken, Roast Pork, and Broccoli & Cheddar were also truly tasty.  There are also bbq and hot sauces to add to this flavorful feast.  In the children's nursery rhyme - Humpty Dumpty sat on a wall and had a great fall.  Maybe he fell because he spotted a delivery guy carrying Humpty Dumplings and he was too eager to eat some and just forgot where he was!  Def five stars!

Food was very good. They have a huge selection of bbq sauces which were all great. Mac was a bit cold, but overall enjoyed all the food and atmosphere. Service was on point with friendly helpful staff. Will return.

We've had excellent food at Bacco several times. But on Father's Day, we made a reservation for 5:45 PM, and were promptly seated with our 2 daughters (8 & 10, well behaved). A waiter, clearly struggling with his English, took our order, brought our bread and beverages... and we waited. And waited. Our waiter said it was coming soon. And we waited. After over an hour, my wife spoke with the manager, who checked and said oh, he would put our kids' order in right away! The delay was never explained. Around 7:15, our food came; not together. First my wife's order (which they got wrong because the waiter hadn't understood it, but a second waiter was able to help). Then mine, then 5 minutes later, the kids' ravioli... and 10 minutes later, my wife's correct order, which we told them to put directly into a box. To cap it off, my tired 8-year-old accidentally spilled her water on the table. Our pleasant but clueless waiter watched, walked away, and didn't even bring a napkin.
