No apology from the management for the wait. The food really is good; the service ruined our evening. I've uploaded a photo of my 10-year-old daughter's letter of complaint, written in crayon on the paper tablecovering.

This pizza is going to rock your face! We used to be fans of Sauce restaurant until they raised their prices and lowered their quality. The last time we went it was $40.00 for my husband and I without any alcohol included, all at a place where you practically serve yourself! Ridiculous! At MOD, we can grab two brick oven pizzas, unlimited toppings, and two fountain drinks for less than HALF of what we paid at Sauce. Plus, they offer a military discount and have a quiet patio with attached parking. It's such an easy and inexpensive date night, not to mention the fact that it's incredibly delicious! It's quickly become our go-to pizza place. I can't recommend enough!!  

Try the MOD size with garlic, basil, red sauce, mozzarella, spicy sausage, pepperoni, bacon, all spices, extra mozzarella (at the end of the line), and parmesan on top. You won't regret it!!

I have come here several times with my younger sister on "treat yo self days" and the service is always good. Nothing worse than going out to eat and having the waiters/waitresses treat you like a child. The food is excellent, we love getting to eat all the meat we want. The salad bar is huge and always fresh. The grilled pineapple is to die for yum!

You know that girl (or guy) in HS. 
That angelic being, the one you pined for thinking if you could spend just a few meaningful minutes with her, Heaven would open & God himself would proclaim you are meant to be together. 
Then you actually got those few minutes, or something else happened, & you learned this angel came from below rather than above. 
Yeah, Catbird ain't that girl!
After more than a year of anticipating a dinner at the #1 rated restaurant in Nashville , I was actually prepared as much for disappointment as I was pleasure. 
Thankfully Chef Poli & his staff proved they're worthy of that rating. 
We entered the nondescript doorway into a small 
room big enough for just 5 people. Andrea greeted us as our hostess for the evening then ushered us into an elevator (the only other egress besides the door we came in through). 
Stepping off the elevator was somewhat breathtaking as the heady scents of a gourmet kitchen in full swing enveloped us & beckoned us down the hallway to that very kitchen which serves double duty as the dining room. 
All 22 guests (the max they seat) are ranked around the outside of a U shaped bar with Chef & all his staff on the inner working off a large infrared cooktop and ingeniously crafted cooking space. The 22 guests are seated in 3 groupings so the chefs are always working on a 6 or 8 plate serving. We happened to be seated in the last grouping so we got to watch virtually every plate constructed & served before ever receiving our own. 
Watching this expertly choreographed dance occur as the 5 chefs seemed to effortlessly pass off semi-prepared dishes from one to another quickly became the highlight of the experience, but not so much so that it overshadowed the delicious plates that dance produced. 
It seems superfluous to describe any of our dishes to you because the menu changes frequently according to what the season offers. Instead, I took pictures of each course & of the souvenir menu they sent us home with, so let your eyes feast & if you have a sense of adventure or enjoyment of the wonders a great chef can create, then by all means go & experience it for yourself.

Best banana foster.  Drinks were expensive and lacked the kick.  Great show and story with the dessert.  We only had drinks and dessert.  You have to buy atleast two bananas foster to get them for dessert.  Well worth it

This is my favorite place for PHO other than home. I highly recommend this place. Today, I took my family to eat there. I ordered Bun Bo Hue (spicy beef and pork noodle) and my brother got Noodle and chicken. One of my sons however made a large mess by grabbing my hot bowl of noodle and it spilled over the floor, my right forearm, and my son's right hand. Oh my Lord it was so scary, but the ladies that work there came running to help apply cooling aloe gels on us. I am so thankful for those ladies helping us. They are so sweet. I know, life with 2 toddlers right?  Luckily I still had a lot left in my bowl to eat. It was very flavorful and delicious. The only complaint was the pork belly which needed to be cooked a little longer since it was chewy.

Another visit, so adding some recent pictures.  This place continues to produce great food and service.  

Also noticed a sign today that indicates additional parking available at the long-closed Chinese restaurant on the opposite corner of the intersection. Good to know because parking has always been challenging here.

Today I had the Pineapple Pork bowl.  My friend had the 'All-In' bowl which basically gives you a meal to take home.

The service continues to be top notch with very friendly help behind the counter when it comes to making your selections.  They employees act like they  want you to be there, almost like they are the owners.  The restaurant is always clean.

Keep up the great work Dirty Taco on an all-around great exoerince.

Not so impressive. Dim sum was salty. Hand drawn noodles were nice but the pork sauce was watery and flavorless. Shanghai sui mai were not much more than rice inside rice paper. 

Worst part is you are not advised in any manner whatsoever that the place is cash only: not on door/window, menu or anywhere else until you are brought the check. The little check wallet says cash only on the inside. Of course you the. Have no choice but to use their ATM and get raped for $1.75 on top of any fees your own bank charges.

I honestly don't know why the reviews here are so high. I'm from San Diego and traveled extensively throughout Mexico and today (my first time here) the 3 Carne Asada tacos I had here were bland. My local friend agreed. Utterly tasteless. I even POURED on the salsa and still, nothing.

The staff were very friendly, the tortillas were good, not great, but def better than most. Lots of old favorite drinks available too, Horchata, Tamarind, etc. Plenty of seating and such, but the carne was dull. The "fixings" stand was a bit poor too. No peppers of any sort. No hot carrots. None of the traditional (and typical) favorites.

So yeah, I've experienced better.

Okay I've read your complaints, and I'm going to veto them. 

My burger has always been cooked per my request, the sweet potato tots are fab, and for a burger joint the drink list is spot on. As a young mom I appreciate that this place is SUPER kid-friendly. Not just kid-tolerant, as I've found many "good for kids" are. 

Regarding the décor, or whatever people have complained about, it's a "burger boutique," not Rotiers. It looks the same as the original in Atlanta, which everyone LOVES. So chill out. 

In sum, any place with a functioning high chair/changing table, good food, good drink (incl beer) selection, good service (even the bus-gents are super friendly) and a spacious patio makes for 5 stars.

Never leave me, Flip.
First time trying from them! Very good lo mein and sesame chicken! Egg rolls were pretty good but the dumplings weren't my favorite! Would definitely order again though!

Really not a big fan of this place. The location is fine with plenty of parking. The atmosphere is obviously chain restaurant and just okay. The rolls are by far the best part of coming here as they are delicious and the cinnamon butter that comes with them is pretty darn incredible. The food itself though is really bland and not very good. Their margaritas and cocktails are pretty much junk. The service the last two times I went was really pretty poor. I think they spread their waitresses a little too thin. Overall I really think there are so many better places to go in the area than texas roadhouse.

Came here for a Saturdate night. Ate in the lovely courtyard. It was about 50 degrees that night, but with the heater and the roof situation back there, it was comfortable enough for my date and I to take our coats off. 

Great service, and I can tell the food was made with care. We ordered too much to name, but for our main entrees, I enjoyed the gnocchi (without the sausage) and my date's chicken parm could have used a bit more seasoning. Oh, and our "warm" pumpkin cake was NOT warm and wasn't anything special. Overall, it was a really cute date! The food was enjoyable but the ambiance and great service really set the evening right.

SoFresh is definitely my new go to place.  I have been a couple of times and the food was perfection- piping hot, healthy, and good sized portions.  I love that I can order the standard options or create my own deliciousness.  The frozen yogurt is freshing during these hot summer days.  A wonderful addition to Westchase!

Its my first time here. Groupon offered a really nice deal, thought id give it a try. I have admit that I was a bit scared, the dining room was completely empty!
Shockingly, food and service was fabulous. . Everything we had ordered was  very tasty. Service was great.  I'm definitely gonna go back again.

Surprisingly large, tasty and reasonable lunch specials.  They run from 11-3 on Monday through Fridays.  For the kitchen entrees (#1-10), you get your choice of soup or salad and main entree plus a side dish of edamame, 2 pieces of gyoza (fried dumpling), a full size California roll and rice.  It's more generous than most places & a good deal for a $9-10 lunch at a sit-down restaurant.  There are also lunch special soups and sushi available as well, but they only come with a salad or miso.  The maki specials are nice though because it's a choose your own adventure type.

The restaurant itself is nothing fancy but welcoming and cozy.  It's strictly Japanese, not one of those Chinese/sushi hybrid spots that are so common these days.  They also have hibachi tables that are open during dinner service.  Adding in the fact they are BYOB with plenty of parking makes Mt Fuji worth the trip.

One of my favorite casual places to go, ever. Free margaritas (okay basically lemonade with a hint of tequila), fast service, and deliciousness. My boyfriend always gets steak fajitas, but I love getting either the fish tacos or tostadas. Oh, and don't forget to get chips, salsa, and guacamole. Can't say enough good things about this place.

In the spirit of Michelin reviews, I try to limit my 5 stars to places that are worth making it the sole travel destination. Las Cebollas does not quite meet up to that standard. If yelp supported half-stars, Las Cebollas would've received 4.5 stars. 

I'll start with the bad: the food here is quite good, but not quite reaching the sublime exceptionalness that would be required for full 5 stars. Avoid the buffet; as with almost all restaurant food, it degrades quite rapidly under buffet situations. 

There. That wasn't too bad, was it? The rest of my experiences have been nothing less than pleasant.

This is the first time I have been to a jack on the box. First of the restrooms were horrible and the place felt dirty. My gf got a burger and I got the tacos. I tried her burger and it was bland but the fries were good. The tacos are cheap and the shells were either old or toasted in such a way where when eaten they gave a feel of a stale taco. I will give Jack in the box another try just not this location
If you are looking for that rare find of a great family owned restaurant, this is it.  Great steak and even better company at the bar with Steve and a few locals.
Sub par food and sloppy service.  It reminds be of an upscale Applebees.   I can't imagine how this restaurant fills their tables when there are so many good locally owned options.

Well... it wasn't what I expected. Probably because I had such high expectations, I was pretty underwhelmed. 

Truth be told, I ate with some picky diners who, no matter where we go, consistently order the worst item on the menu. Seriously, it's like they research ahead of time which dishes that restaurant doesn't do well. I plan on going back very soon, because I did like SOME of the dishes and there was a lot I didn't get to try. Will update with second review soon enough.

We started with the hush puppies and the smoked tuna dip. The hush puppies were painfully dry, but had a good flavor. They were suuuper salty, and I like salt. 

The smoked tuna dip WAS AMAZING. I love spicy things, and this had some type of creole pepper that the (very knowledgeable and sweet) waiter identified for us. Of course, the two picky diners thought it was too spicy. Really, you're from New Orleans. We both know this tuna dip was not THAT spicy. 

I got the baked drum, which was so delicious. It comes in a winey, yummy, brown buttery broth with mushrooms. It was so good. I also got a side of the horseradish sauced beets. I don't think the beets were roasted/sauteed/whatever enough, because although delicious, they were pretty tough. 

The whole roasted fish was good. The salsa verde is so perfectly complementary to the fish, I couldn't believe it.

Didn't get a chance to try dessert. Was dyyyyyying for the flourless chocolate cake. 

I will reassess when I get the chance to go back and try more appetizers and entrees. Right now, it's a straight up 3/5 though. 

Also, it DOES get really loud.

This town is in need of a great hot dog spot.

I grew up on Nathan's Original hotdogs. There was a company store in the Livingston Mall I frequented and I loved their natural casing dogs(well done) and fries. 

We lost Hot Diggity on South Street - easily the best I ever found in Philly. Crunchy casings style dogs with 30 options of dressing, some of the best pomme frites you'd ever eat and some great drink choices.

We lost the Last Stand in Ridley. Great dogs or sausage with draft birch beer on tap. No, they weren't natural casing dogs but they were delicious, all beef versions.

After driving by John's too many times to count, yesterday I decided to see what the place is all about. I was impressed.

Got two dogs served John's style - mustard, onions and chili sauce. 

They were delicious. The dogs were smaller in size, not naturally cased, but good. The bun was fresh. The chili sauce was spectacular. I truly enjoyed it.

Does it replace Hot Diggity? No. But it definitely warrants a return visit soon.  This is a solid, hot dog restaurant.

In the words of a famous old sit com character ...those dogs were DYNAMITE!

It is what it is.... super #basic Mexican food. It's close to my house, so I come here when I get a craving for the same dish that I order at every Mexican restaurant that isn't a hip taco spot; enchilada combo, one beef, one chicken, rice and beans. And cheese dip. The cheese dip is solid, could be a little thicker but whatever. The five stars is for the service. Everything is super fast, and hot. They bring individual saucers for salsa, so you don't have to worry about cross-contaminating salsas and cheeses, if you're into that sort of thing and your friends aren't.

Came two days in a row for the breakfast buffet.
Needles to say it is OVERpriced, as expected. The food at the buffet was good- particularly liked their pancakes and their smoked salmon. Eggs need an improvement; the scrambled eggs were watery - literally. I do not mean undercooked, there was just water in the serving dish. Random. Coffee and juice galore, also lots of fruit. Overall it was ok. Anticipate 30$ bill, but do not be tricked- tip is already added!

The server was good, he was very patient. That's where the good ends. The food gave our entire party ( two children two adults ) diarrhea, vomiting and an allergic reaction. We all are this today and had nothing else the same in the last two weeks.  Normal digestion is 12-24 hours, anything under 3-5 is typically food poisoning. I confirm with the FDA and doctor. We called the manager and he said it was not possible and not his problem. He didn't offer any sort of assistance. We will not return to this place.
This was really good, I had been craving Mexican foods for like a week and this hit the spot!!
I went a few weeks ago with some friends. The food was good. The service was very fast. I'm not a fan of the lack of seating & long lines, but overall a must for Philadelphians and tourists.

YUMMMY!!  I know it's a chain and I like to support  local businesses, but this place is a great exception to the rule.  It's located in the Lakeside shopping mall so you have to fight the shoppers for parking spots.  

I usually get the orange peal beef.  Their entrees are large servings, but I usually take home left overs and eat them for lunch the next day.  So, at least I'm being economical.  

There's usually a significant wait for a table, but they have a bar where you can have a drink while you wait.

As busy as they are, the servers are extremely friendly and helpful.

Let me take the pro/con approach:

PRO:
- Grilled toast for hot buns added a uniqueness
- Larger dogs and flavorful
- Selection

CONS:
- Very, VERY messy
- The toast + condiments (in my case, chili) just became a gelatinous mush ball
- Expensive -- although, one dog should do ya

if this is the only thing you read: ORDER THE SOUP DUMPLINGS NO MATTER WHAT

Came here with a friend on a Saturday for the dim sum. It was pretty busy so we had to wait about 10 minutes. We ordered the soup dumplings, shrimp dumplings, and shao mai. Huge huge fan of the soup dumplings!! Very soupy and flavorful. Absolutely delicious! We even ended up ordering a second serving. Personally I was really disappointed in the shao mai. It was very bland. I looked at it and thought it was going to taste amazing. But then when I put it in my mouth, it was a disappointment. The shrimp dumplings were ok. Also pretty bland. If I come back again, definitely would substitute those two dishes with two more servings of the soup dumplings. 

The dim sum logo/mascot was really cute though. So that was pretty cool. I think they sold t-shirts with it too.

This place has the best chicken fried steak in Reno! Hands down awesome. It is a real piece of steak pounded out and hand breaded. The house made gravy is second to none here in the Reno area. The service is great and the staff friendly but not overbearing. We have been here many times and the same experience was had each time. My wife just tried the Banana Rum Belgian waffle and thought it was terrific. I had a bite and thought the rum sauce was exceptional. I have had the Ham slice also. It is cooked in some type of "honey" glaze. Makes my mouth water just thinking about it. Bottom line: This place kicks ass!!

Really disappointing experience , not in a sense of food but service, burrito I ordered was rewraped 3 times by that time most of the topics were on the counter or the floor , hygine is horrible , simply don't go there

These are probably the most authentic tacos I've had in Reno/Sparks. The tortillas are hand made, the meat has delicious flavor, and the salsa is a little spicy, but good. Customer service is also very good. The workers are polite. Highly recommend.

I usually love their food, but had to demand a refund this past time after we waited 45 minutes and still no food. The last straw was we caught them serving the item for which we were waiting to customers who had just ordered.

Don't be fooled by the fact that this establishment is attached to a gas station and a classic barber shop. The food was fantastic and all in my group nearly licked the plates clean.
You can walk into the gas station side and grab a beer (or 6) from the coolers and bring it into the restaurant after paying for it.

Was getting deliveries from DES Peres but when it came to a driver named Luke who thinks he owns the store would not deliver and ended up being very rude person so the Des Peres jimmy John's is off the list as long as he works there.

This is my favorite place to eat in LA. We intentionally drive down that way on the way to and from Houston on a regular basis, JUST TO EAT HERE! Catfish and soft shell crabs are mind blowingly good. THE POTATOS.....OMG you could go in there and just eat them and leave in love. FOOD IS OFF THE CHAIN!

Omg!  If this restaurant was near me, I would be broke.  Great food with impeccable service.  The chicken dumpling soup was super fresh and definitely home made.  The gyros were out of the world good.....and I am from Chicago with a ton of gyro places. The greek potato salad was a very yummy surprise.  My husband had the fish fry and it came with super fresh mixed veggies and fries. The fish was fresh battered and huge.   The kids grilled chicken breast was a huge portion.  I swear the kids kraft mac and cheese was a double order, too. Even the iced tea was good.   Stop by and see Austin, he'll take  good care of your family.

This place is great! Small and cozy interior. The food was fresh and delicious. My husband and myself couldn't help but eat each others food, it was all that good. The chorizo mussels are a must and the Thai gumbo was different and fantastic. We came in aroud 8pm for dinner and we were very welcomed and the food tasted very freshly made, not what was left over from the day. Also it's byob! Can't wait until my next visit!

Came here today with my boyfriend to grab some food and waste some time before a flight and ended up pleasantly surprised! The food was absolutely fantastic! We got dumplings for the appetizer and bulgogi and short ribs for the entrees which came with rice and unlimited side dishes. Absolutely none of it tasted less than fantastic. I would definitely say this is the best option for Korean food in the New Orleans area that I have tried (and as far as I'm aware, I've tried them all).

It was capped off with fantastic service (the second anything was even close to empty, whether it be drinks or the side dishes, they were checking in to see if we wanted any more). The kicker was once we were done, we ended up just hanging out for at least 30 minutes because the flight wasn't leaving any time soon and there were still empty tables - we never felt pressured to get up to go or any less welcome than when we were eating. Just a super positive experience all around!

simply put, some of the best Italian food in the city.    I've been going to Joey's since I moved here in 18.   The pizza is fantastic.   Simply the best pizza I've had outside of New York.   The Gladiator (stuffed pizza) doesn't have enough sauce for me, but it is excellent and very well received and a lot of my co workers flock to it.   The chicken parm is maybe the best chicken parm I've ever had and the eggplant parm is DEFINITELY the best eggplant parm I've ever had.  Be advised it is chaotic inside and Friday's especially (blue collar pay day in a blue collar part of town) can be overwhelming.  The atmosphere may not be for everyone and if chaos and yelling bothers you, wear earplugs because the pizza is worth it all.   The family is incredibly family and despite the busy business always take time to talk to their customers as you go down the aisle to pay for lunch.   Don't miss out.   This place is the best

Had a great night out for dinner. The environment is nice and casual and the staff is friendly. 

We had a lot of food and our server Kendra was great. She made sure our drinks were refilled and that we had everything we needed for our meals. 

We were a group of nine and we didn't have to wait for anything. The manager came out to bring us food that was a bit late but was nice. 

I give this restaurant 4 stars because our space was nice but tight. In they have red be the outside and inside of this restaurant a few times but they never seem to have expanded the inside. 

Great service and great food. A great win win!!!

I'm gonna go with a 2.5 on this one.  I've been here twice, and both times have just been alright.  You order at the bar and they bring the food to you.

The first time around, I got the Korean BBQ chicken.  Heed the warning on the menu... This sucker is spicy!!!  I liked it until the spice was too overwhelming and I couldn't taste anything anymore.

Second time around, I went with the hubby and we shared two sammiches and the duck fat fries.  The fries were good, but nothing special.  They come with a good dipping sauce though.  And I love fries, so I ate them all.  I got the shrimp BBQ and the hubby got the braised lamb.  The lamb was the better of the two.  Although it was a little dry, it had a huge amount of meat.  The white bean cassoulet spread was also a nice touch.  I thought the shrimp was overly seasoned with some peppery sauce.  But again, there was a ton of shrimp.  So they get extra points for the amount of goodies inside the bread.

Everything on the menu sounds so good with a lot of interesting combinations.  Maybe it was an off night.  We may give this place another try.  It's got a lot of televisions and a bar, so it may be more of a hang out, drink some beers and watch sports kinda place. 

To end on a positive note, the guy who helped us was really nice.
Love the place, clean and cozy and our server was friendly and the food was delicious definitely coming back....

This place is soooooo good! It's a favorite of mine to use for catering. They have multiple packages you can opt from with assorted sandwiches and sides. The pasta salad is an office favorite! Also, fruit salad YUM! 

They also have very soft bread used for there sandwiches! Office favorites are the pimiento cheese and the pesto chicken! If you're ever in the area pop in it's delicious!
Best bar food style place in town. Food is awesome.  Don't get scared by long lines.  We were told 2 hours and got seated in 15 mins and there were 12 of us!

Cafeteria style restaurant where you order at counter and then seat yourself in the dining area. 
Clean washroom.
The pizza was good. They cooked the pizza and brought it to me in a reasonable amount of time.
Good lunch spot if you're not looking for fancy amenities. I would recommend this for the casual lunch experience.

This place has a great downtown Nashville vibe even though it located in Franklin. I went here with my family the day after Christmas and had a great experience. The food, atmosphere, service and experience were wonderful and unique. This is a great place if you're looking for something a little out of the box and not so cookie cutter. Highly recommend.

Great place to get a pizza at, you can get a huge, fresh pizza, with 3 or more toppings for roughly 20$. And that's not all either they have delicious subs and they have a whole other variety of options! This is probably one of my favorite places me and and my dad go to get a bite together.
Great food.Amazing chats loved eating here very hospitable workers. Although a small place has a nice ambiance and totally worth the food.

Rounding down from a bit over 3 stars

Naan and Kabab offers an interesting fusion of Mediterranean/middle eastern (Iranian/Persian) dishes.  The kitchen staff seem to have a consistent style of cooking, but opinions will probably vary if you're a big fan of, say, Greek cuisine - things will probably taste different than you'd expect.  We had the falafel plate and beef/lamb/etc. Koobedeh plate.  Plenty of food for two.  No drinks.  $22 w/tax.

Pluses:
- courteous and professional servers
- nice evening ambiance
- good, seemingly consistent food (though only one visit so far)
- parking, but we saw people taking spots who were headed to the Peppermill (a-holes)
- warm naan (more like pita bread than Indian naan) served promptly after ordering, meals served quickly
- meat and vegetables grilled perfectly
- Maurice made the rounds in the dining room and is a very nice guy

Minuses:
- I detected no saffron taste to saffron rice, which was too dry for my taste
- texture of falafel a little too "doughy" for my taste
- bench seats near door are very uncomfortable; wooden plank at shoulder height means you can't lean your back against them, so are forced to perch on the seat.  We could have moved, but had our child with us and didn't want the hassle.  Maurice: please get this fixed!!!
- bathrooms open almost directly into dining room, though are screened by the uncomfortable bench seats and a trellis of sorts (those on the bench seats may exposed you to, um, unpleasant odors, as we were)
- Maurice encouraged a neighboring table (two young women) to positively Yelp-review the place, offering them a complimentary dessert, apparently to 'sweeten' the deal.  Pretty tacky, and I guess I'm not cute enough to get offered anything for free ;)
- water refills were slow

I really want to love this place. Unfortunately, they seem to have very challenged people taking orders. My Mom orders there all the time and has for years...so I can honestly say that 3/5 times that she will order her Turkey Club, she will be asked something like 1) What kind of meat do you want on your sandwich? Or 2) I'm not sure that we can do toast. (In the background: She wants it toasted. Can we do toast?) I promise...these are literal examples that are too much to make up. You don't have to be rocket surgeons...but this is your business...and it's toast. Overall, decent food (even if they can't seem to consistently deliver toast) and nice people...but I will try to find a replacement neighborhood pizza joint for my Mom...hopefully one that has mastered the culinary challenge that is toast.

Came here for brunch with a friend and had a great time and great food. The location is bigger than expected and thank goodness as it was busy! They have both indoor and outdoor seating. The outside was shaded and had fans so we felt cool despite it being August. 

The menu is large and there are plenty of sweet and savory options from omelets to crepes & all have various options/styles in each category. We ended up with the banana, strawberry crepe & the Florentine eggs benedict. Both were huge and delicious. 

The staff was very attentive and friendly. Thank you to Amber who was attentive and had great recommendations. Additionally she was very observant and knew people all around who were regulars and even recognized and was able to help a group in the parking lot while helping her tables. 

Great breakfast or brunch stop!

TERRIBLE! Slow service, with a manager who kept yelling out everyone's personal business. She made a scene in throwing out a man in a wheelchair then proceeded to talk on the line about it as well. Completely unprofessional and dirty! And to top it off the food was burnt, probably because it took almost 45 mins to get our food and it wasn't even busy. Completely disappointed

Thai taste is a decent little place.  I've eaten in once and it must have been so-so because I'm not sure I remember what I had or that of any of my table mates.  I recently ordered carry out when I was in the neighborhood for a Trader Joe's Trip.  I ordered the fish cakes.  Absolutely love them.  They're bite size here..I undoubtedly could eat about 100 of them.  I also ordered Pad Kee Mao.  It was okay. The flavor was pretty good.   However, I prefer really large WIDE rice noodles that are cut from the fresh sheets.  Son had the chicken satay...very wonderful flavor but they were very dry. Fortunately, two year olds don't complain too much and they are really happy when they have peanut sauce with in reach.  I'll certainly try it again when in the area.  On a side note the family that owns the place seem really kind and concerned.

The food is ok, but not really worth the price. If you have to spend $40-50 on a steak there are better places to do that. Servers were nice and the ambiance was good, just didn't think the food was all that special.

Great breakfast at a great location. Huge portions at low prices. I am always getting a to go container for the other half of my food. Big tip,. Get there early or you will be waiting in line with Alot of people. The food is worth the wait.

The atmosphere here is very nice!! They go with a tropical theme which I find to be awesome!! Me and my girlfriend stopped through for a quick bite to eat before cathching a movie during their happy hour. The short time we were there we really enjoyed ourselves!! The customer service was good and the drinks were as well. We only got Nachos and they hit the spot! We'll be seeing more of this place for sure!!

This has been my very favorite Thai place to go to in the greater Indianapolis area for some time.  I've definitely tried the others but for me, the combination of great food and good service from the couple that run the place make it worth the trip out to Castleton.  Being in a strip mall isn't exactly glamorous, but it's such a relief not to have to fight for parking or wait in line to be seated.  

Maybe it's just because I tend to prefer places that feel a little less polished and a little homier.  But, whether its pad thai, their very spicy curries, or my favorite dish (which I can't pronounce, but it's the special peanut sauce and vegetables) make this place an old favorite.  Also, the rice is heart-shaped.  I mean, c'mon.  That's cute.

I've only been here for dinner, and I've never come for the buffet or lunch, so I can't speak to those, but it's a winner in my book for a casual, flavorful evening meal.

Zero stars would be more appropriate. Tiny, meatless pigeon wings. No flavor. I actually had to make my own sauce when i brought these home just to choke this garbage down. The fries were soggy. Soggy is really the only way to screw up fries but they managed with ease to accomplish it. Never. Again. You'll have a better meal at McDonald's or even the dump.

Love this place! We live south Reno and used to have to drive downtown for decent Indian food which we no longer have to do since this place opened! Super clean, friendly/helpful staff and great food. Their garlic naan and butter chicken are soooo good! Lots of choices on the menu and staff was happy to suggest their favorites. Would highly recommend.

Three stars is all this place can hope for.... My wife detests this place with a passion, so when /if I am able to convince her to try it again I am sooo lucky. that night was this week.  bad decision, they were completely out of mild sauce. (so out of suace that they added a sign to the drive thru window......) hmmmm maybe yal should get some more mild sauce before your dinner rush.... anyway   food was ok but not the greatest and it was simi cold...

that coupled with the fact that they were out of sauce earns them a thin 3 stars and they barely staked by with that..... some work to do at this store me thinks

I have come to this place a few times since my friends bought a house in Conshy and I must say, it's not your average diner. 

Their food is so good! I had had the grass-fed beef burger, which is delicious, and my friends had the reuben and the chicken salad. All of us loved our meals. They seem to offer a lot more healthy options than your average diner as well.

The service is also very good. But for some reason, every time we have come here, the place has been empty. I can't understand how a diner with great food wouldn't do better. Are we just coming on the wrong times? This place needs some good PR and needs to do a better job of marketing itself. If the atmosphere was a little more fun, I would want to come here more often when in Conshy. But being the only table in the restaurant is a little boring!

The food was really good but the customer service was so rude. So we ordered table side guac and it was good but the server just had a horrible attitude. We asked for extra chips and we were waiting so long for them that my friend had to finish her drink for the other waiter to notice that we needed some chips. We ordered veggie fajitas and they were really good but we got it to go because we were waiting so long but it was alright.  After we received our checks he asked to see our IDs which was very suspicious to us because he only asked us and no one else in the restaurant so I'm my overall experience was very disappointing.

After being turned away from two restaurants in Downtown SB for not having reservations (even though one had about 5 tables open just sitting there) we ended up getting a table here...we were visiting from LA and have had our share of good sushi but this place was amazing.  The Yellow Tail sashimi was like butter, best I've ever had.  It wasn't cheap at $14 for 5 pieces but I was VERY close to ordering another plate of it before we left.

Food is more Spanish than Mediterranean.
Salmon is fresh, moist, buttery & well seasoned (simple but tasty). Wife loves the Sangria. Service was very good, despite the language barrier (I don't speak Spanish and most of the barmaids didn't speak English). I was able to communicate with the help of other patrons). Place has a very familiar vibe, most of the customers appear to be regulars and/or well known, but still we were brought into the fold and treated like family. The owner is a nice guy, seated quietly at the bar observing, and you would have never known it until one customer pointed him out to us. They grill fresh meats for lunch daily, so I will have to try lunch one day.

I missed you dim sum. I live in Florida now- so the closest we get to good dimsum is getting some panda express, taking a bite and then throwing the rest out because it's panda express and why do you keep thinking that it's going to taste good?

  This place gets busy on Sunday's, but we got a table pretty quickly- we went for it- snagging the last few orders of soup dumplings available. These were great- the flavor you expect, the right size, delicate--and very reasonably priced. Get a few orders and enjoy.
  Other standouts included the eggplant with garlic sauce, salt and pepper squid, scallion pancakes- it's all good here. BYOB if you wanna bring a bottle of scotch to wash it all down with. Or beer. Just no panda express. Get that shit out of here.

I ate here a few days ago. I ordered shrimp lo mien. It tasted OLD! I threw it away after eating half. The next day I had stomach aches, body pain, nausea and vomiting. I had that for two days. I will never eat there again.

I've got to stop assuming these new burger joints are order at counter. I kept avoiding coming to 5 Star Burgers until I read the reviews closer and discovered they had table service. I like table service, so I was thrilled to have a new burger place to explore. We immediately were greeted by a cheerful hostess who seated us near the front windows. I am really starting to enjoy tables next to a window, natural light does wonders for the presentation of food. 

A bus boy or server assistant to be politically correct, was quick to bring small glasses of ice water and a wine bottle filled with backup H2O. When our server Molly approached the table, we already knew we wanted an order of Fried Pickles as an appetizer. My drink of choice was the Salted Caramel Shake and the BF "just" had ice tea.  Damn, I was hoping to try two flavors of shakes. 

When our drinks arrived, we ordered our burgers and a Mixed Basket of Fries. I enjoyed a Natural Angus with Pepper Jack Cheese with the works, (lettuce, tomato, onion and pickle). A pure delight, it was as good as one fresh off the backyard grill. The BF had the absolute best burger either of us have enjoyed in a long time, Dad's Green Chile Cheeseburger. Oh my, Crispy New Mexico Hatch Chilies on top of Angus beef patty, with Pepper Jack Cheese and  Green Chile Mayonnaise. As a bonus, we added bacon. As every Yelper knows, everything is better with bacon. Both burgers were nestled between a Butter Toasted Brioche Bun. Culver's, you should be scared, these burgers put your butter burgers to shame. 

The Fried Pickles were crinkle cut dill pickles served with Tzatiki Sauce. The pickles were true dill pickles in flavor with a crisp outer shell. The Tzatiki Sauce was a perfect accent to these fried treats. Our Mixed Basket of Sweet Potato Fries and Regular Fries were served in a cute deep fry basket.  Each were served with Little Cups of Ketchup and Maple Cream. The Sweet Potato Fries stood alone on flavor. Also, the same sauce for the pickles were a better accent then the Maple Cream. In my humble opinion, the Maple Cream doesn't know what it wants to be, it was rather bland for both of us. The fresh Natural Cut Idaho Russet Potatoes, were seasoned with the right amount of salt. 

I've saved the best for last, the Salted Caramel Shake was perfect. I had them hold the whipping cream, but still had the toasted marshmallow and cherry on top. The sweetness of the ice cream from the infamous Serendipity in Webster Groves and the saltiness of the caramel created a perfect balance. It was so well balanced, that I was able to sip it and enjoy it through my entire meal. 

I'm coming back for sure. I've got a lamb burger and a bison burger to experience.

Very nice atmosphere and friendly staff , the fresh juices are a plus and quite healthy. As for the food I wouldn't really expect much it is a chain restaurant (which I'm not a huge fan of) but it is a decent meal and the food still tastes good.
Great Food, Atmosphere and Service! The deep dish pizza was outstanding. Best I have had in the St. Louis area. I Will definitely be back soon!

This was just not good. I just spent $45. The kids meals are $7 a piece and it's just a tiny burrito cut in half. No rice, beans or drink. We were the only ones in the restaurant & the waiter never checked on us once so we sat with empty drinks most of the time. They only have green salsa and it's the thick stuff right out of the jar. Not even the tortilla chips were very good. My chicken taco was literally just shredded chicken with no seasoning whatsoever.

The food was terrible. We asked for a tamale and it was frozen. We also had ceviche and the fish not fresh. When we asked the waitress when the ceviche was cooked cause it tasted like it has been from the day before she got really defensive. Needless to say we won't ever be back. That was $80 down the drain.

Good but slow.

The pretzel bites are absolutely fantastic. And mustard sauce on those is really really good. The burgers as well were quite excellent. But on a Saturday at 1 o'clock it took over an hour for us to get food.

Another of the "gem show" stop offs for refueling.  South of I10 on 6th Avenue, E side of street, big yellow independent building.  What the facility lacks in ambience the customers make up for, colorful weathered characters wearing their rancho attire, lovely lunching Latino women of all ages and the large happy Mexican families make me feel like I am back in old Mexico.  Well South Tucson is old Mexico.  
Seat yourself and a small bowl of refried (bacon grease) beans are delivered with fresh corn chips and salsa in a plastic squirt bottle.  So many memories of my childhood with that plastic salsa bottle.  Salsa is classic thin with nice oregano and heat from the tepin.  The food is Sonoran style mexican.  I like the two eggs with chile verde breakfast, served with beans and potatoes, hold the cheese.  Way too much food, but fun to try and eat it all without bursting.  If I order taco's I go for the old style which is deep fried, really crispy and you can order just one for $2.50.  The meatball soup is good and hearty.  Big menu and the servers are friendly and sometimes mix up the language, part English and part Spanish--funny!

Everyone has their own tastes, but I was not a fan.   Did not like this pizza at all. Sauce was too sweet and almost tasted creamy. It almost tasted like american cheese melted on the top of it. Waitress was nice, though.
If you actually like Indian food don't eat here, not worth the time or money.

There are 4 Frenchy's in Clearwater Beach, but this one is right on the beach with beautiful views and live music. We were there for sunset on a Friday night and there was about a 25-minute wait to be seated outside. It was a little chilly out, and they had heaters which made it very comfortable. The sunset was perfect and the food was excellent. my son had the kids fried shrimp, my husband had the fried grouper nuggets and I had mahi Florentine, with onions tomatoes and spinach cooked in a delicious coconut sauce.  We also had an ahi tuna appetizer. Highly recommend for the view and the amazing, affordable food!
I'm so glad we have this taco shop in Vail.. their food is delicious, their service fast, and location is excellent. My favorites are the breakfast burrito, chili releno, and enchiladas.

The service was EXCELLENT. Extremely nice, extremely helpful, offered several things on the house to try out, and just had a great positive attitude. Food was quite delicious: tasty and spicy upon request, big servings, and good deals. Definitely recommend if interested in authentic middle Eastern cuisines
Delicious!!! We all got a mix of Asian cuisine yet none of us were disappointed! From the curry, sesame chicken, sushi, to the apps we were pleased with everything! Two thumbs up!

This is one of my favorite brunch spots in University City! The menu is extensive, the food is delicious, and the drinks are so fun & tasty. The staff are so friendly and provided excellent service. We had the privilege of meeting the owner and his wife today when we went, and they were so incredibly kind. 10/10 recommend for the food and the experience!!

Delicious authentic fresh food
This is a really authentic restaurant fresh food very flavorful great service and nice ambiance. I will be back:) you must try the manti, pulse Samarkand, khan Ho and luyla kebab... I will be back.
HUGE Portions, good food, got the lamb and chicken wrap it was pretty good but the meat was a little dry and there could have been more sauce. The donuts were Fire though with orange sauce!

Never heard of this place and randomly decided to come here and really glad I did! It was super delicious got the Thai boba tea pork dumplings, crab Rangoons with Hawaiian shrimp fried rice and omg this place is definitely and must try if you haven't already! Customer service was on 10 and the server/manager, she was so sweet! And made sure she checked on us often and took everything out of our way, I honestly had an amazing experience overall.

Went to your store late night, There was three cars in front of me in the drive-through and I was waiting over 45 minutes in line just to get two whoppers. I ended up leaving because I waited too long.

Stopped in here after trying some wine at Engine 8, so glad we did. Had the fish tacos fresh and very tasty, the wife had the Carnitas which she said were very good also not greasy at all. Food was fresh and the service was fantastic thanks to Dennis ( hope I got the name right ) so nice to support the locals during this time. The Jalapeño Marg was so awesome. Give them a shot,. you won't be sorry. Definitely going back.

Panera Bread?  Yeah that's about all they give you.  Bread.  First time here with husband and sister to try this place.  Prices didn't seem too bad.  Sister ordered Turkey Panini with Avocado and a Baked Potato soup.  Husband ordered a Steak and White Cheddar Panini.  And I ordered a Turkey and Flatbread with Tomato soup.  They had soft drinks I had a Chai Tea cold. Also got pastries to go for dessert later. Looking at this food we weren't sure if it was a side dish or snack.  My sisters Panini had like a slice of lettuce, 2 tomatoes and turkey dust with a spot of avocado on it.  With a big piece of bread to go with her bland, watery and luke warm soup.  My husbands Panini had a sliver of a steakum and lots of cheese.  Came with a bag of chips.  My Flatbread was burnt with Turkey dust as well, like 8 halves of cranberries and a few leaves of raw spinach.  The tomato soup was spicy with mushy crushed tomatoes and big croutons in the middle.  And of course big piece of bread.  My husband and sister dissapointedly ate their meals.  I tried the soup.  Gave it to my husband. Took everything else home.  Now Chai Tea is naturally sweet.  They got this premixed stuff that comes with milk and sugar in it.  Was way to sweet and nasty.  I threw it out and got a regular ice tea.  Later that eve.  I tried to eat the flatbread thing for dinner, well the stuff inside, it was even worse.  In the garbage.  The cinnamon roll i got was like hard bread and cinnamon dust and icing.  In the trash.  I don't know about other locations or what everyone was raving about.  But for $36.00 I expected we were going to enjoy a little healthy type food with a treat and take the food home because it was good.  Bucks so waisted this time.
So far, I've had the Seafood Muffelata, a tuna salad po boy, a shrimp po boy; all of it is great; it's a must stop for your first po boy in the Crescent city
From the location, to the servers, to the food. Everything was so good! Drinks are amazing! A wide variety to choose from when it comes to drinks and food! BOMB SALSA!

I've been to the Philly location once and the KOP location several times, and each time the service is impeccable, the food is delicious, and there are plenty of options to choose from. The managers are hustling and always walking the floor- checking on tables and cleaning up and helping the servers which says a lot about the efficiency of the staff and the example set by leadership. The Market Bar has plenty of fruit, veggie, salad, meat, and cheese options. The full experience of the meat service has rotating gauchos carving a slice of 8+ variety of meats table side, asking if you'd like a medium, well-done piece, etc. Bacon-wrapped chicken, garlic beef, etc.
Plenty of wine and drink options, full dessert menu and after dinner drinks. The food and dining experience is worth every penny!

The pizza is incredibly quick and great quality. The salads, however, are my absolute favorite. They are in deep bowls with lots of interesting toppings that work well together. The only reason that I didn't rate this place five stars is because it is often crowded (probably because it is so delicious!).

The service here has been really horrible lately one of the servers is really ignorant and disrespectful to customers. There was a misunderstanding with my order and i was brought the wrong item, when i politely pointed this out to the server he began shouting and accusing me of lying just because he had to send the item back to the kitchen, this guy was acting like an animal he really needs to learn some manners and proper etiquette. I WILL NEVER COME BACK HERE AGAIN its really a shame since i used to love the food. DONT WASTE YOUR MONEY ON IGNORANCE. I wish i knew that pea brains name, way to ruin a persons day.

I had a couple of different menu items form here last night so I'm not too sure which item it is but I am super sick today and have major food poisoning. Throwing up multiple times. I will never eat here again.

This is a great spot for takeout or delivery. Quick service, good food. Always get my order right. Sesame chicken combo is great and I'd recommend the steamed dumplings. The steamed chicken or shrimp with mixed vegetables is great. Sauce on the side and you can get brown rice. This is the only place I use for Chinese food delivery!

Wow.  Never again.  Quite possibly the rudest waiter to ever wait on me.  The fastest time ever where an automatic $5 tip dropped to $1.
This guy should know better than to get bitchy and talk back to a customer!  Guess if I was seated at the 4 top next to me, I might have been treated better...

So its reopened now, Ha Long Bay is running the show. I believe they're going to be expanding the menu to match the St. Pete. location that would be nice.
I'm not sure they will have some of the HK/Guangzhou dishes the old owner had that's a little disappointing.
Over all I was impressed. The har gow were shrimpy and delicate, shu mai were good and hardy. Overall this meal was as good if not better on the dim sum front then before.

After doing some shopping with my friend we decided that grabbing something to eat would be our next best step. There were a few choices close bye, but we decided on Burger King. My friend ordered The Original Chicken Sandwich. That's her favorite and she liked hers and ate it all. I ordered the Whopper. I have always liked them because of all of the stuff they put on them and the flavor of the burger. The last one I had wasn't very good and I thought it was a fluke so I ordered one again today. The flavor was good, but the meat itself was dry. It's pressed hard and dry and cooked to death. When I forget how they have changed my Whopper, I might return for another one.

A true NOLA classic restaurant with a great seafood and all other variety selections to choose from. Service was great, the place was pack on a Monday night. They had a great selection for specials too. Food portions were HUGE.

Stopped in on a visit to Villanova. Very impressed by the fresh ingredients and creative combinations for salads, bowls and sandwiches. 

The staff were super friendly the restaurant was large and clean and the food was great. 

The food was fast but it was a far cry from fast food.

Well.  Shouldn't have high expectations for a fast food restaurant but prob one of the worst experiences I've had.  About 6 girls working and all just standing around laughing and looking stoned.  30 minutes to make chicken and fries when no one is in line.  I sat and watched 8 cars pull around and be told it would take 30 minutes for "EVERY ITEM" yes they made sure to emphasize it, and drive off.  
To the owners: if you want to make a profit at this new location, train the staff!
I had the best steak fajitas today. They were perfect! The chips weren't fresh but that was fine since service and meal were great! I will be returning!
I come here at least once a week to eat for over 10 years the best  Asian fusion food on the main line

I came from Philadelphia area and remember those delicious cheesesteaks very well. When Frankie and Deb owned the business every visit, every cheesesteak brought back great memories. Things have changed now that they retired and sold the business. The steaks are average and not representative of those from Philly. No more peppers on the tables. When you ask for them they are pre promised but not always delivered. It's not the 'special' place it was once. Sad. You still can get Tasty Cake products which is nice.Miss you Frankie and Deb !

We were trying to detox after 5 days of French Quarter eating and drunken debauchery, and looking for options that offered ample delicious vegetarian choices that weren't restricted to Southern food. We found this gem in our Airbnb's neighborhood and decided to walk there for lunch on New Year's Eve for brunch, and felt like we were "home" - back to our Californian/Michigan hippie haunts with the art, avocado toasts, and health-conscious food. My friend got the veggie press and I ordered the egg white press with a flavoured coffee, and boy, we were refreshed.The ambiance, taste and quality of ingredients made this a haven for us for the next few days. I am absolutely disappointed that we didn't get to try the Oreo beignets since we were stuffed from trying other beignets around town, but if I end up in NoLa the next time, this will be on top of my list.

Little place in the back of a bar but packs great food back there. We had the loaded fries which was a huge portion - thank God we shared as it could be a meal on its own. Then we split the catfish burger...whoa that was absolutely DELICIOUS!!! I'm still dreaming of it...Price is affordable, food comes out quick, hot & fresh. The bar vibe was weird but was only there for the food so did not care. Will definitely return & recommend.

There's no server and this cashier girl seems to care nothing. We asked for water twice and even in the end, no water for us.  The order is also messed up. Three tables came after us and got food before us.  

For food, the taste is really so-so. Everything's salty and not taste fresh. It's definitely not worth for the $$ we paid. 

Not good experience and definitely put in my black-list.
very good simple yet enough and more authentic than anything else I've found here in middle Tennessee. It's purely a take out place and they make great general tso chicken!!

Excellent all around for take-out food yesterday! We ordered over the phone and picked up our food in the parking lot about 20 minutes later! Very efficient and I felt they handled things very well. Our chips and salsa (the best in Tampa) were so good! Quesadilla and chimichanga were also very good. They packed things up very nicely. I'll order again soon and of course, visit when all this COVID 19 madness is over. Support this local business if you can!

No White Meat!

It's 12:30pm and I pulled in here for a snack and the drive through server stated that they were out of white meat. Really!!  Nostalgia is the only thing that keeps me going back to KFC but it's  quickly wearing off. 

I politely thanked the young lady and pulled off.

Some of the best hot chicken, potato salad and baked beans in the city. I live in the Hermitage area and usually to to HattieBs or Pepperfire for my hot chicken fix but this place will have my business for now on. ********** 10 stars

Goody Goody burgers stirs up a sense of Tampa history and I'm so glad they brought it back. Their menu is simple and boy, do you get a lot of food! One thing I've learned from eating there - it's best to share!
Delicious milkshakes, delicious omelets, sweet sweet potato pancakes and those burgers!! 
I always get friendly, prompt service and a full belly!

This place so was good 10 years ago. It was hip and they had really good sushi. This time when we drove into the parking lot it looked closed, the windows were covered with paper. Inside it looks outdated, the glass panels were streaky and dirty. The appetizer sampler we tried was gross. Every item of the appetizer tasted stale and musky. It tasted like it was in the freezer too long. They did not have the uni I order. (Not a big deal). The fish was not fresh and had a smell to it.

Their phone doesn't work so decided to go to best place in town for el pastor 
The red shack never lets me down

I don't have much experience with Korean cuisine, however, my visit to DuBu left me wanting more.
First of all, I had no idea that entrees come with a plethora of small dishes before the mains were brought.  I thought it was a mistake before I realized that all of the tables got them.  The small whole fried fish was completely unexpected but oh so tasty!  I haven't had much kimchi in my life but theirs made a good showing. Spicy and flavorful and too different for my kids, which meant more for me. 
We split three of their vegetarian dishes, which they have a good selection of for those who don't eat meat. Our vegetable dolpan bibimbap was tasty with burn (in a good way) rice at the bottom of the dish.  We skipped the egg but I could see it working.  The vegetable bibimbap was also good, however, we both got more of a salad vibe from it than a main dish. Tasty none the less.  The vegetable japchae was what we both went back for again and again. Lots of good savory flavors and nice textures madethe dish really enjoyable and the highlight of our visit. I look forward to going again.

Loved, loved, loved this restaurant! The food was "farm to table" fresh, the flavors complex and nuanced, the ingredients unusual, the service unpretentious, and the prices reasonable. In other words, everything we like in a restaurant. I was a bit taken aback when I looked at the wine list (NY state and places in CA you've never heard of as being good wine producers), but I ordered a sauvignon blanc from NY and it was very nice. (Pays not to be a snob.) It's a small tight-knit group that runs the place and they take great pride in what they serve. There's a menu option where you just let them decide what to bring you; we opted for that and were very happy we did. The place is tiny (11 tables or so), so I'd definitely make and advance reservation. Also, it was raining when we left and they actually sent someone outside to hail a cab for us. Well done!

It's hard to beat the price of this place! Went here on a Friday night and had the place to myself. Staff was friendly and got our order in right away.
The salad was good with a little kick of spice in it. The pad Thai was a bit on the sweet side for me but I still enjoyed it. 
I also got a Thai iced tea which was from a can and not homemade like it says on their menu. But the server did warn me when I ordered that it was from a can. 
Over all I think I got what I payed for.

I hate to give this a one star review, but since I recently visited I was incredibly disappointed to find it had been sold to new owners. These new owners apparently can't run the grill like the old ones and do no serve dinner platters on weekends. (It said on menu due to grill size). We paid 8 bucks for four drinks and left after watching the "kitchen" using a microwave and a toaster oven to heat their tiny menu. Not to mention the booth was dirty, dried food on the wall. Not at all what it once was. Incredibly disappointed, and disgusted. Got applebees for lunch instead.

We couldn't wait for our favorite chinese take out restaurant to open...GOLDEN CHINA unfortunately we did not. The Wonton soup had NO TASTE tiny strage pieces of meat in a huge wonton paper. No spice, reg fried rice with nothing but a bit of soy sauce no veggies or even fried egg. Orange chicken combos not worth our hard earned "take-out" money. Whole families Tummys hurt learned our lesson. YUCK
Soooooooo good! We love this place, their kalbi and spicy pork is our favorite. Don't forget to try some soju
Great Ethiopian restaurant.  Everyone, including my seven year old loved the food and atmosphere.  Will go again!

Had lunch from here the other day.  Got a hot pastrimi.  LOVED it. I've gotten one of their lunch specials before.  One thing i have to say was that the sandwich was large so I was able to have the second have later.

A definite guilty pleasure of mine.  I have been trying to shy away from eating out to save money, but when I do, Panda is usually at the top of my list.  Standard Panda menu is quite large and full of great options.  I typically order online and take home, food is always fresh and ready when I arrive. Consistency is also a big factor, you can always expect that the food will be delicious. This location is always clean and inviting.  Definitely recommend!

We had a wonderful dinner at Perfect Taste!  The atmosphere is very European, charming, really lovely music playing softly in the background.  Tatiana was our server and was very pleasant and Tony was the chef (owner?).  He came over to our table to meet us after we ate.  

The food was delicious- my Spring Salad was enormous.  The French Onion Soup was perfect and not overly salty.  My parents both had Veal Oscar and were HUGE fans.  We can't wait to go back on a Tuesday  night to try their Tour of Europe specials!  It was BYOB, too, which was great.  

It's a shame the person who rated them in December didn't like the food- we had no problems and enjoyed the veggies that were served.  I also had the crab and shrimp quesadilla, which was really interesting!  We didn't stay for dessert (mom's b-day so we had cake at home) but I look forward to trying one next time.  

Overall, a really great experience!
Owner, Dave, has a great place and fabulous food. Don't forget to chat him up and enjoy the whole experience. I had the hit and it didn't make me want to die, but I like spicy.
Decent place to eat. Nicely sized for a sports bar and grill. Plenty of tvs around the place

This modest byob place is probably my favorite Chinese spot in Philly. It's nothing fancy and can get a bit crowded but the food is always great. They're happy to accommodate groups eager to share a large meal and a few bottles of wine. 

They have an Americanized menu but I always stick with the specialties and the veggies. I love the vegetable dumplings in chili oil, Chinese cabbage with dry pepper, fish soup, dry pepper tofu, chilled beef tendon appetizer, or dry pot anything

I have been in this location a few times and they always have had nice and fast service. Ingredients are fresh and bread has been freshly baked. They have salads too. The place is average in size and they also have outside sitting. Parking is tricky in the Gulch so it's not the most convenient, but if you don't mind paying parking then be my guest, pay for mine too while you are at it lol . Definitely better than the unmentionable SW!

My family and I LOVE this place. Hands down my favorite Korean restaurant in town!! Their bento box comes with so many items including a choice of a crunchy shrimp roll or California roll. The seafood pancake is the best here. I highly recommend it to anyone who loves seafood! The galbi is perfectly cooked and so tender. The portions here are really wonderful too Such an amazing meal every time I visit!!
This resteraunt has good service, clean resteraunt, and the best fries in town. If you want to eat, this is the place to get food. Although the bathrpoms are locked, it is goos overall.

This restaurant stunk from their sewage
I will not be returning anytime soon two ladies left the resaurant to go eslewhere which i should have done also

I love their fries!! Been here twice and the falafel pita was better on first time. Second time it was a bit wet and cold, maybe too much sauce or something? However the taste was still good! Would go again and definitely recommend.
The food and service is amazing! The sushi is always fresh. The people here are always very attentive.
Awesome food and great service.  Took a chance and stopped here on our way home ..we live over an hour away!  It's byob, so we picked up some beer from wegmans nearby and enjoyed a nice early dinner.
Over priced, bad coffee,  and poor quality food.  This is clearly a no go place.   It convient for guests of the hotel and that's about it.
Love Bacquet's! Staff is always so friendly and knowledgeable and the food is great! Thanks Franck
Amazing food offered with perfect service. We enjoyed every bite and the cool environment

My daughter ordered dinner from here tonight. It was $10.87.  It was below the minimum for delivery, so I went to pick it up.  The owner lady ran my credit card and handed me the receipt to sign.  I signed it and handed it back to her and did not leave a tip, because they didn't do anything out of the ordinary to deserve a tip.  She showed me her copy of the receipt that was $13.87.  She automatically put a $3.00 tip in that I had to write in on my receipt.  I understand if you have employees working there for tips, but this lady is the owner and gave herself a $3.00. The food is average, it's just convenient.  She's pretty rude on the phone.  We'll never go back there again.  Not even sure how she came up with $3.00 on $10.87.  That's at 27% tip?!?!

Tucked away behind Gennaro's bar. Park under the over pass instead of in the street.  The poboys are pricy but it's well worth it considering the amount they put on it. Onion rings are big, fresh and crispy.

So much yum! I've recently moved to Boise from Southern California area so I've been on the hunt for good tacos. This place is it! Although I still haven't found a place to enjoy bomb carne asada this place has some really delicious and unique tacos. There's BBQ brisket taco with coleslaw on it and I fell in love. You won't be disappointed here.
Their pho is delicious. I like going here on rainy days. Nothing like a warm bowl of pho. Their boba teas are good too

I haven't been to a Burger King in years but someone talked me into tracking one down in Philadelphia.  It took about 20 minutes for the food to be ready, and the soda machine was broken for about another 10 minutes after that (there's only one soda machine).  The food was below average, about what I remember.

Not a single decent beer to be hadall are dark, lager, Pilsner, or light. Not one single normal beer to be hadThe grouper was good, the Hawaiian Steak not so much. I'd go back if they had a normal beer. Just 1 choice!
Great prices and delicious food. The blackberry margarita was fantastic!  It was very busy and service was still good. Can't wait to return for another margarita!

I have thorougly enjoyed the company of this phenomenal establishment. Who wouldn't prefer a delicious 3 dollar margarita, and within three sips you have: a buzz and homemade tortialla chips with their crack cocaine salsa, talk about getting distracted from life. 

I've been there several times, but this time was different, on the occassion, I tried their carne asasa tacos.... Mind blown pshooook. 

Now I hate the dilemma it has created, when I want Chipotle, I know it sounds blasphemous, but seriously... I think this place is much better hands down. 

Enviorment, a bit outdated, but hey I'm there for the food, so let the next guy give his bias opinion. 

Overall, I'm going to be craving mexican more often.

Lenny's has been one of our family's favorite breakfast spots in the Clearwater area. Just pull up and the crowds make it clear - Lenny's is yummy to everyone's tummy. Order a breakfast entree and you get an awesome little pastry basket with it (be warned though: EVERY breakfast entree gets a basket but you do have the option of "refusing" one of the baskets if you don't want to be tempted to scarf down too many of these tasty treats.)

I'm a traditional eggs and bacy kind of gal and you cant go wrong there. My hubby loves the chili omelette - it is HUGE! It's super kid friendly with an awesome balloon man for keeping the kids entertained on the weekend but do keep in mind the weekend crowds are pretty intense so keep a bit of a pre-sitting wait in mind for timing (though once you sit down service is fast and friendly). I think my only "complaint" was at our most recent visit. They changed their home fry recipe. It's still tasty, but the old one was soooooo much better. So that made me a bit sad. Other than that it's our go to breakfast spot.

I am addicted to the mi hoanh thanh (egg noodle won ton soup) with pork, chicken, shrimp, crab and the most wonderful tiny bits of crackin floating in the broth. I eat versions of this dish all over town, and this is by far the the best of the best.
Just went for drinks here after shad fest.  Have been here before.  Love the view which is right  on the water.  Great place to have a few drinks in the nice weather.

We were intially skeptical about this place after reading the negative reviews posted on Yelp. But we had a Amazon deal to use, so we decided to give it a try. And boy were we pleasantly surprised! I don't know if they took the comments on here to heart, but they seemed to have amped up their game. We would drive or walk by occasionally and always see people waiting outside, so we figured there'd be a bit of a wait on a Friday night. We waited at the upstairs bar area for about 25 mins. The ambience was pretty nice (going for that trendy lounge-y look I guess) and the bartender and wait staff were professional and pleasant, but they need more than one bartender there. For beer lovers, there is no beer on tap, so beware.

Once we were seated, our waitress came to take our drink orders and give us recommendations. She was very pleasant, attentive and professional. The cornbreads were nice and hot and the strawberry butter made it seem almost like a dessert. We ordered the teriyaki chicken wings as an appetizer since we saw someone upstairs order them and it looked and smelled good. They were nice, hot, meaty wings with a not-too-sweet teriyaki sauce. My husband ordered the fried catfish...delicious! His sides were the cabbage and string beans. Cabbage was tasty, but string beans were a bit mushy. I ordered the fried chicken with collard green and black eyed peas. The chicken was crispy, juicy and hot, and the sides were pretty darn good, too. After eating all of our food, I also ordered the red velvet cake for dessert. Yes, I was that hungry...and it was that good!

All in all a very pleasant experience with good food and service. Maybe I'm giving them a great review because I didn't expect much? Possibly. But I have to say that even if we had gone in with high expectations, I think they would have met them. I hope they continue to strive to provide excellent food and service.
My favorite part was blackened Tilapia, flavorful and cooked perfectly. Chimichuri was good, I had it on side. Nachos were average. Chocolate island was a bummer.

Very clean. Staff was great.  I am a senior citizen and we split the shrimp and grits and still have a large amount to take home. I would advise to order a half potion of what you order otherwise you will be having leftovers. My son and grandson went down to Jacksonville at the Metro Diner in Bay Meadows in December of 2015. They brought back their experience and both of them helped spread the word of this location. I was impressed with my first visit here and I can say it won't be my last.

This Arby's is AWESOME! Yes, it if fast food, so judging it for that reason isn't strong enough for me, but this location gets it right. It is probably one of the cleanest fast food joints I have EVER seen. I am regularly greeted and told to have a nice day when I leave. They always get my order right, and it's never cold. They are also magicians with the fries. On most of my visits, they are perfectly done. Get a cheese cup with them. 

If I were to recommend anything, I would say the #1 classic roast beef sandwich, contrary to popular belief, this can actually be one of the healthiest fast food sandwiches out there (I'm a big fan of "Eat This, Not That"). So it's still a guilty pleasure, but I don't hate myself for eating it. Until I have fries with cheese and the occasional cherry turnover. 

So if you are looking for something other than another cold deli sandwich or burger, do check out this Arby's. It's a very clean place and a good escape from a crazy work day. 

My only complaint, I so wish that the Arby's chains here had breakfast. Back in my home state of Michigan, many of the locations have breakfast with an awesome ham and cheese croissant. So, Indianapolis franchise owners take note!

Got there only one person was waiting tables but a very nice guy. Took awhile to get menus and drinks but food came quick my girl ordered the Tampa Cuban but they didn't put the salami on it. I got the chicken al la placha with yellow rice cause they were out of white.. but was very good with black beans chicken was a little bland but everything else was good

I hate to give anything less than a good review when the staff was so helpful and friendly, but this is not a place I am looking forward to a return visit.  Start with salad, iceberg lettuce, canned black olives, canned artichoke with a thin slice of salami on top and a sprinkle of dry mozzarella cheese.  Dressing on the side.  Not too exciting.  Sausage roll, dry sausage wrapped in pizza dough.  Yes, that's it.  Meatballs.  Meat handled way too much which made them tough.  No sauce on top.  We had to ask for some on the side and it was way too sweet for me.  Inedible and I eat everything. Muff was very good.  Not a lot of meat or cheese, but toasted made a nice sandwich.  Pizza looked large, but not appetizing.  Crust looked thick.  I like a thin crust you can fold while the grease from the cheese drips down.  I will say the place was packed and I saw a lot of take out pizza so they must be doing something right.  I am an Italian from Jersey so I have very high expectations.

We were going to hit up another spot, but the place was hip and had a long line. Eh. Then we found Rosales. Really good Tacos, salsa, rice, beans and chips. The place had no wait whatsoever and a parking lot. The prices were really cheap. Hella good. Hella cheap.
We got the the pork quesadilla it was very big could feed 2, very meaty and spicy, good but expected more cheese with it. The place is very clean and the service is good.
Food was excellent kitchen forgot to cook two items but when we asked about them the food came right out. Sangria is the best!!!

This is definitely one of my fave Italian restaurants in Philly! I've been coming to La Locanda Del Ghiottone for awhile now and it has never been a disappointment. 

This cozy BYOB is nicely fitted in Old City. It has a welcoming atmosphere with a friendly and entertaining staff. It feels like a happy family get together when you are here because everyone is enjoying their time and their company. It's affordable, especially since it's a BYOB. Also, it's great for a date night or for a group outing (I've done both). 

Now to the food...let's start with the mushroom crepes. As most other reviewers have mentioned, they are a must try. Seriously, they are to die for and you will be missing out on pure deliciousness if you do not try this appetizer. This is a dish I get every time. They are melt-in-your-mouth good!

My fave dishes for dinner are the gnocchi and the ravioli. The last two times I ate here I had the mushroom ravioli with a sausage bolognese sauce (it was on the specials menu for the night). My boyfriend and I loved this dish so much that the following week we made our own mushroom ravioli with sausage bolognese sauce. See, their food will leave you craving more (like I could seriously go for some mushroom crepes right now). Everything is just so NOM.

If you want some tasteful, authentic Italian food, this restaurant is your jam.

PS: If it's your birthday, they'll sing "happy birthday" to you and give you dessert! The cannolis and tiramisu always hit the spot.

While everything was delicious, I have to say, it was very over hyped, as a friend had warned me a year earlier. While I had put off going since, I found Valentines day a good excused to visit. We went at around 2. The atmosphere is following the latest trend: urban industrial rustic, with rad plastic chairs and old Edison bulbs. We had a crispy polenta starter, and it was delicious, though adding truffle oil had never been my idea of updating rustic Italian food, but every Italian place in town is pretty obsessed with it, so what can you do. My partner had the blackbird. I think they make their own ketchup? It definitely wasn't the glorified tomato soup that's Heinz. I got the lunch platter, which was pork side, butternut squash soup, a small salad and a sugar cookie. They said the salad was Caesar, but the dressing was so minimal I couldn't tell, it just tasted lemony. Also there were daikon radishes. It wasn't bad, but it was predominantly bitter. The soup was the best part, savory and a little sweet, but definitely a great creamy texture that, to me, exemplifies refined rustic and while the pork was tender and well seasoned, there was huge slabs of fat on it. I might try it for pasta or pizza at some other date, but I was not very impressed. Great location, salumi, and friendly service is its biggest attraction, I think.

The owners of this place used to work at China Grill around the corner.  The food here is pretty good for a Chinese buffet and they have great service.  They make effort to get to know the customers and build relationships.  I really like that about businesses.  Their food is always pretty well stocked and hot.  They have 2 areas which are blocked off which can be great for parties or group events.  We had booked one of the rooms for mothers day and it worked out really well for us.
