The have a great variety of food from hot and sour soup, egg drop soup, wonton soup, beef noodle soup, scallion pancakes, fried rice, lo-mein, fried shrimp, pork kabobs, sushi, shumai, and et cetera.  You'll definitely find something here you'll like!  I  hate buffets because I always leave stuffed but strange enough I'm always ok coming to this place.  Maybe it's the friendly environment!

This was a great place for someone who's a little afraid of sushi to go. Lots of great vegetarian options, everything was very tasty.  The bar area is close to pitch black so it was hard to see what I was eating. $20 bottles of wine on weekdays and I am ALWAYS a fan of that action.

We get take-out from Kaslik about once a month.  It's consistently delicious.  We always order Hummus, Lebnah, and Baba Ganoush with some extra pita, then an entree or two.  I really enjoy their Shrimp Sandwich.

The service is very friendly and the restaurant is always clean.  We should probably eat-in some time, but we find the food to be a great compliment to other activities, be it taking in Shakespeare at the Park or watching a favorite movie at home.

For the price, I think Kaslik is hard to beat.  Highly recommended!

I love pizza, which makes me very picky when it comes to rating a pizza. I also travel a lot and dread airport food. I was so pleasantly surprised when I stumbled on this joint. Delicious! The ingredients are top notch, I had spinach, artichoke, olives and mushrooms and they take pride in making their food. The best choice if you find yourself at this airport.

I love the place.  It's quick, tasty and a good price.  I lived in San Diego for a while and of course the mexican food was un-real!  Moving back to delaware was a disappointment.  There is a hugh gap in the world of mexican food here.  But La Tolteca will do.  

The staff is always been friendly and quick.  My only problem is that every time my wife and I go we seem to always get the kids section.  Lots of crying babies.  But there is nothing that the restaurant can do about that.  

If anyone has a mexican place that they are crazy about let me know!  I would love to find another spot!

Korean BBQ in SB. Finally! That said, it's not the cheap, all-you-can-eat-meat places you expect to find in the San Fernando Valley or K-Town. We're in SB, so the prices are higher, the portions smaller, place is trendier, etc. etc. With this in mind, I'd give this place a solid 3.5 stars. The food tastes good, the prices aren't outrageous for the area, and it was fun detour than the regular SB eateries. 

We came on a Tuesday evening when it was fairly slow (there were three other tables besides us), and our server was really sweet and came around fairly often. It did take a little while for us to get our drinks and soups, etc. but was otherwise good.  Our server was a little mistaken, however when he told us the "Taste of iGrill" special fed two, because it was a bit skimpy. Luckily we supplemented it with a couple extra meats and seafoods. It did give us a good idea of the range of things served. 

The air was surprisingly smoke-free, considering there are no overhead ventilators, but we concluded they might be built into the table grill. We hope so, cause our table made a weird humming noise. The room itself was fairly cold and loud, as others have noted, but it's not unpleasant. Either way, I'm looking forward to coming back for their AYCE lunch!

This experience was kind a hit and miss. We decided to go here after my brother-in-law graduated from college as a graduation gift receipt it was immediately and our waitress was super awesome and informative. 

One are the few sad things that  happened at dinner was that they were out of one of the entrées that two of our party members were going to order.

The constant flow the warm fresh bread did help ease the sadness of this though.

The servings where good sized for the price, which was on the higher side. Our salads where out fast but that was the only thing. 

Our whole dining experience took almost two hours we were not prepared for the long haul. When we got our food it was hit and miss. One of our steaks was over cooked and chard to much. My salmon was good and I loved the apple topped pasta. Unfortunately the girls that settled for the shrimp scampi when they ran out of swordfish said the prawns had an off texture. The ribeye was good according to our grad.

All and all we would go again maybe a little earlier in the evening

This is by far our favorite BBQ restaurant. 

The food is always very 'clean' looking and is presented well.  The food had always tasted better than you might think it would given the price point. 

The staff has always been friendly, and attentive, and tonight, Katie was our server, and she excellent. We have also had Kimberly (the manager) approach us to see how everything was, and tonight, she remembered us from a prior visit. 

Lastly, the decor is pretty neat as there is a play on the whole, country / down home / down south vibe, which works well in a BBQ restaurant. Also country music plays throughout the restaurant, so if that's your thing, than that's a plus. 

Overall, I would recommend this BBQ over any other in the area.
Wings are amazing but overpriced and the seafood smells like bad fish. The atmosphere in the restaurant is a little to Trashy Hooters-ish for me

They have the best specials i have ever seen. Please go here and try it out. There lunch special is from 11am to 3 pm. There food is so yummy and they accommodate me not being able to eat red meat or pork and switch my taco out for a chicken taco

Went to Hamburger heaven today, Michelle was there and we talked about the TV series.

Got a Mo and a cheeseburger with fries plus 2 cobblers 

Burgers were pretty good fries were good and the prices were cheaper than what is posted by previous reviewers

Wow!!!  Beautiful modern garden decor. The server was attentive and provided great suggestions. She recommended the kimchi spring rolls. She said they were very popular. She also recommended the kale Caesar salad. I got both. I do not particularly like kimchi but these spring rolls were absolutely outstanding! They surpassed my expectations 100 times. The salad was good as well but the spring rolls were the clear outstanding highlight of the meal. I got an herbal tea that was delicious. And I finished my meal with avocado chocolate pie that was recommended again by the server. It was absolutely delicious. Not too sweet perfect density and the walnut date crust was excellent. I will definitely be back!!!
Great pizza, very impressed staff was helpful and gave me a coupon deal without asking ( I love when places do that) and I'd be a regular if I lived in the are... Thanks

This place reminds me of the old neighborhood places I used to go to as a child. It's has a casual, family style to it. The decor is reminiscent of an old Italian restaurant you might have seen 20 years ago. It was clear that it was a favorite among the locals. This is not the kind of place for a fancy anniversary dinner but it is the place for some very good food and laid back atmosphere. The food was very good but not quite great. Having said that, this place is definitely worth your time and money to check out. I hope nice neighborhood places like this never go away!

Best sushi i have tried since moving here from the  Northeast. All sushi i have tried here is fresh and delicious, as it should be. Service was always excellent as well. I recommend the sunshine roll , and DEFINITELY the shaggy dog with shredded crab on top. One word YUM. I also recommend the 4th street location over the 9th street one. The one on 9th is alot smaller and tends to get very crowded and there is always a wait.

The food was excellent.  I had a fettuccini special with crumbled sausage and my wife had the pork chop.  The waitress was friendly and helpful.  The restaurant is a bit loud, but worth it given the quality of the food.

-Awesome server!
-amazing shrimp & grits
-good bourbon chicken
-good rice & beans!
Very authentic

The food was just good.  I had the burrito rodeo and the wife had a quesadilla verde.  The burrito was served "open faced" with a small side shell of toppings.  Interesting presentation, but with a little effort, very satisfying.  The quesadilla verde was a typical quesadilla but there was not any "verde" present.  It was short on chicken and vegatables and with a little sauce it was average.  The server forgot our chips and salsa and brought us our queso, still without chips.  We had to send the queso back for a new one.  They were very apologetic about this and did not charge us for it.  47oz margaritas were nice for a house marg at $9.50.   We will go back but we will hope for some improvement.
Excellent food and staff. NEVER had a bad meal here, been here multiple times. Highly recommend.

Got the shrimp po' boy and fries with gravy here. The po'boy was amazing! The place is very small though and you may not be able to get a seat. 

Note that portions here are huge. The large po' boy would've been enough for two people to share IMO.

Okay so I ordered a dozen grilled oysters as an appetizer. I also ordered the fried oysters/shrimp platter . Overall this was the worse food that I've experienced while visiting New Orleans. The grilled oysters had sand/ dirt that you could taste with every chew . As far as the fried shrimp / fried oysters go the food was salty. The best thing on my plate seemed to be the slaw and potato salad. My friend ordered fried crab cakes and also was upset with the quality of the fried crab cakes. To make matters worse my group and I had to wait outside for 30 mins in the cold which honestly wasn't worth it.  The prior night we ate at a Resturant that we visited the night before called boiled seafood house which was 100% delicious , and all of there oysters were clean, and all of the crabs, oysters , mussels, crab legs were loaded with flavor. Take my advice get an uber and go to boiled seafood house which is located on 3340 magazine street.

This is my convenience/comfort food spot for breakfast. I've been a few times for a lunch or dinner, but I'm not sure I was wowed. For breakfast/brunch, however, the food is always really good. I especially love the Two by Five (I think that's the name) where you get two eggs, two strips of bacon, two sausages, and hashbrowns or two pancakes. The eggs benedict are also a huge favourite for me. And my mother is nearly addicted to the Chorizo Brekkie Bowl.

I noticed one reviewer said Ricky's was comparable to Denny's, which I think is fair on the basis of having very standard brunch choices, nothing overly exciting, but in terms of food quality/freshness, I'd say Ricky's has definitely got Denny's beat.

From the half rotten lettuce to the cold curly fries this was a mistake. If I had not already driven off with my food, I would have demanded my money back and gone elsewhere. 
Very disappointing for a chain that usually does better.

I am so excited about finally stopping by this place. I live in the area and I have to say the post-apocalyptic Ponderosa exterior worried me a little. Well, fret not. No end of the world gear is needed for your sushi adventure. 

We ordered the Sushi for two, some saki, and green tea. It was great. Fresh, well made sushi on the east side! They also have some other great Japanese staples like udon and soba noodles. It looks like the lunch speacials are pretty cheap. It was pretty average for the price of a sushi dinner. 

This will definitely be my new go-to sushi place in Indy. Close to home, clean, adorable full bar, and great service. Kampai!

A favorite special-event restaurant for my girlfriend and me... for just the two of us or when family's in town.  It's also an excellent spot for happy hour and snacks - the 3R cocktail in particular is amazing!  They change the entrees on a regular basis, but there's usually a St Peter's fish dish that's delicious.  For dessert, the fruit crisps are really good.

Came here after seeing the reviews on yelp overall not impressed the only reason I'm giving 3 is because of the decor, home town ambience, comfortability,and service which was all exceptional. I felt very comfortable walking in and service was great

    However the food was sub-par which is what I would expect however these yelp reviews had my hopes up the food I tried was all very disappointing. The sushi was ok but not what I consider "professional". 

         The desserts were all old I could tell from the staleness of the creme puffs and red velvet cake they had sat for awhile. The tea was not fresh. 

         For 6.99 you get what u pay for food is 2 stars comfortablilty/service/ambience is 4. Same food as hibachi defintaley if your looking for a cheaper alternative there almost identical in food options and quality is a lil lower. I like this resteraunt but might only come here 1 or 2 more times this year max. I'll get my sushi from Public and Jewels haha (jewels sushi is phenomenal, best I've had)

One of the few vegan restaurants in NOLA and it turned out to be a good one. The vibes in the cafe were really good. The sugarcane juice with the broken noodle salad was amazeballs. NOLA for me is gumbo, jambalaya, creole, cafe au lait and  broken noodle salad. This cafe is a must visit for everyone who is visiting NOLA to get the complete NOLA experience.
I had the prime rib and it was great.  Our server Marissa was awesome and recommended a great wine, her description of it was spot on.

My second favorite Vietnamese restaurant. 

You have to try the banh beo appetizer. It's worth coming for. 

I absolutely love their seafood soups. Service is excellent too!
Breadsticks are amazing and pizza is good, have had less than great service experiences there on several occasions. Some of the workers can be rude

Bella's is one of our 'go to', not a chain food, restaurant. 

Their specials are always delicious.  And their Chicken Mudiga is one of my favorites. 

And don't forget the dessert!  We've even gone in for just coffee and dessert after a movie.

Food quality wasn't very good, in fact, it was downright awful. Fish tacos were made with old, stale corn tortillas and clearly made by by an employee that doesn't know how to make fish tacos.  I ordered a  Patty melt  cooked medium but the waiter thought I said Buffalo burger (cause they sound so much alike, right?). 

What I got was a cold, horribly overcooked buffalo burger and then a hastily thrown together patty melt (cause I refused the cold burger), which was ok, hey, at least it wasn't overcooked. 

It almost seemed like the kitchen help was trying to pass off one of their previous rejects to me. The waiter was clearly new and poorly trained and apparently didn't realize he was being played. When we left, passing the bar on the way out, it looked like the place was being run by a bunch of high school kids. 

 I even left the waiter a fantastic tip since he did try to correct the problems and he was new, but good grief, I'll go elsewhere next time.

Great service and wonderfully prepare dishes.  Mussels , the broth , and the bread was a meal in itself. Co-op/local vendors provide meats & veggies. Best gnocchi north of the Truckee. Also recommend the Shishito peppers to start.

AWESOME food. The staff is great, and Kenny is the best. They make their own sauces and pizza dough. They'll serve you right there in the restaurant, or bring it to the sports bar next door, Roundin' Third.

When looking for pizza places to order office lunches from, easy delivery is one of the most important factors. Pizanos has easy online ordering, extremely helpful and kind employees and delivery drivers that bring the great experience full circle! 

Today we put in an order for 23(!!!) pizzas at 10am before they opened. Even though they didn't open until 10:30, they were able to get all of the pizzas - which were deliciously perfect might I add! - ready and delivered to us within an hour. I would highly recommend them to other businesses and friends alike!

I've come here for lunch during my break several times. It's clean, quick and the staff seems friendly. Trying to eat healthier for lunch this time of year is hard with limited time. I love the daily subs for a cheap lunch. All foot longs are $6 right now.

Still sucks.  The oysters Bienville are just plain strange, stuffed full of a mixture of crumbs and bacon with the off-putting texture of powdered dry dog food.  The pecan infused alligator was unevenly cooked, with some bites cooked to jerky like toughness.  The corn and crab soup was heavy and lacking flavor notes.  A disappointment.
Best corn tortillas I have ever had. As a taco connoisseur, I appreciate the good flavors and simplicity with just meat and tortilla, leaving it up to me to perfect my tacos.
Good Mexican food. Much better than San Jose or Vallartas!! The food is full of flavor. The restaurant looks nice inside and their drink menu is nice. Fed a family of4 for under $50!!

We went to Hiro's for dinner in Philly - The host/server was very nice, attentive, and happy to explain different dishes and condiments.  We both had the Inch of Heaven - the broth was delicious with deep, caramelized meat flavors.  The portions were large and well priced.  The place was clean and service was quick.  Highly recommend to try this place

They have a new owner (within the last year) and the food and service is  improved. If you liked it before, you will love it now! I am not a vegetarian, but I can go for weeks without eating anything that had eyeballs. If you are a vegetarian or vegan, this is the place to go. The vegetables are fresh and the flavors are vibrant.

All of the dishes I tried were excellent. The lunch buffet is a good value and great way to experiment.

Currently, this is my favorite restaurant for vegetarian cuisine in Tampa Bay!

History: In business for several years, Laziz developed a great reputation for high quality food and good value, but slow service. I would only order takeout due to the delays. Under the new owner, all this has changed for the better!

I have to say I really appreciate how patient the lady taking our order was. I ordered a torta but made it vegetarian. By veggie I expected, just lettuce, tomatoes, rice, cheese, and guacamole but I got more which I was pleased with. They took "veggie" literally and included mushrooms and squash too. It was actually really good. The bread itself was delicious, despite being a bit soft and hard to eat gracefully. But Mexican food is hard to eat without being messy most of the time. My sister had the fiesta combo and said was great; just the chilli releno was a bit watery. Fair warning though, the green sauce is actually pretty spicy! On the hotness scale: about a 3.5-4 out of 5. My usual must have: horchata was a hit on the sweeter side but I like the distinct cinnamon taste. This just may become my new go to place for Mexican food.

Our first experience was last night. Before I proceed, you should know that we really do not know much about Indian food but do know good food.  

The general manager was very friendly and helpful.  The menu is huge and the waiter very nice.

We shared some mulligatawny soup and naan. Both excellent.  

I had the lamb tikka masala.  Outstanding sauce, spiced perfectly for me.  The lamb was a little bit chewey but flavorful.   The iced tea served was super.

My wife had saag chicken which was quite spicy and very nice.

We both took home a lot of food.  

Overall, the food was great, the service friendly but a bit slow and prices were a little on the high side but worth the experience. We will be back.

We have been to this restaurant several times with high hopes and always leave unimpressed.  Today was the last time that we will ever return.  The food is good, but the over all attitude of the seating staff and manager (owner?) is pretty contagious to how unhappy they are. Every time we have been here, we have been unacknowledged by the hostess for several minutes.  She would not even say she would be right with us or that she was in the middle of something.  We just stood there while she looked right through us. Then on her terms, she would greet us in a short manner.  It just kills the mood for a fun dinning experience.
The waiting staff always seems overwhelmed and on one occasion our order was messed up and the waiter said "I never make mistakes".  Not that the mistake was a big deal or that we are not easy going...but who says that?!
Today we waited 30 min to be seated.  We were told to place our order as quick as possible to make sure that we would get our food within 45 mins.  We did so.  After an hour I flipped the waiter a $5 for our coffee and headed home for some Cheerios.  Never again. Not for breakfast, lunch or dinner.

I had a feeling the slow cooked lamb sandwich would be my favorite, and it was- so soft and with just enough flavor to hold up to the vibrant vegetables and baba ghannouj eggplant spread. Iceburg lettuce is pretty much useless; I was impressed that Argan uses quality mixed greens inside all of their sandwiches. The meat sandwiches ($6.99) come with 3 vegetarian options, and you can choose from both the vegetable list and the spread list. From the ones I tried, the white beans and the carrots were my favorite, the carrots brighten up the whole package and the white beans add to the texture. I have to admit that in general I am on Team Baba Ghannouj and not Team Hummus, so while I didn't get to try the hummus, both the baba ghannouj and the zaalouk (also made of eggplant) impressed me and would easily please any vegetarian as the featured ingredient.

We had cheesesteak egg roll, cheeseburger and fries, Greek Salad and Caprese Chicken in our trio.  We all enjoyed the food.  No complaints, clean and good service.  We look forward to coming here again.

Stopped by here after an afternoon at the Hong Kong Market.  Three of us ordered a table full of food and everything was delicious and VERY cheap. The po-boys are a great way to start off the meal...that's right...START OFF.  Fried Spring rolls were fantastic and we even ordered a noodle plate with rice papers to make our own spring rolls.

I seriously can't give enough stars. This place is wonderful and accommodating even to the largest of parties. Their food is delicious and the staff is the best. If I could give infinity stars I would. The best. 

The food is amazing. We did the chef's tasting for 20 people and I felt like I was going to burst after. Everything was amazing from their first course to their last. It's a must.

My favorite restaurant in St Louis before it was even voted the best new restaurant in St Louis! Service is impeccable (Ask for Jordan!) and the bar staff is always whipping up amazing unique drinks. Food is SUPERB and my head is still spinning over the chittara and roasted chicken. I dream about the food here seriously. If you haven't gone yet then wait no longer.

Had a bunch of work to do last night so I stayed in at the hotel. I don't usually write reviews on hotel restaurants cuz they're mostly just ok. but the room service I had last night was very good. black and red fish cooked perfectly not too well done. boudin balls which I had to try and were very good. Caesar salad very nicely dressed. and a great coffee creme brulee. Breakfast was okay in the restaurant but not as good as my room service last night. If you are staying at the Renaissance Marriott and don't want to go out you can trust that the room service will be good

Tonight, I enjoyed the best beef tartare in the city while watching a parade and I did all of this from the comfort of a spectacular patio. Everything we ate was delicious. The venison was perfectly cooked. I mean perfectly. It may be a new restaurant but this chef knows his stuff. In a city of great food, this place has to be considered among the top and it was all for a very reasonable price

My boyfriend and I came here for valentines day after reading a lot of reviews on Yelp - unfortunately we were really let down. They seemed to be having an off day. 

Our server seemed to keep forgetting about us/ or orders/ or requests for extended periods of time. The ambience wasn't that great and the food was incredibly salty.

We had the truffle oil edamame dumplings (i LOVED THEM)
dry pepper chicken- so so salty and very hard to eat with rice 
Green tea ice cream (pretty standard, nothing out of the ordinary).

Normally the food is great but if you've ever had a Po'Boy in Louisiana, do yourself a favor and don't order one here. It was like someone used a bad cookbook with no pictures. The sauce was wrong, and the shrimp were like popcorn shrimp only smaller.

Wow! Amazing food! Food was cooked to perfection! I love that this fine restaurant is ran by a very sweet and friendly family!! It's a very lovely atmosphere. It's also a byob! I would highly recommend this restaurant for it's fine gourmet food!

Had seen the yelp reviews on the empanadas at this place on Orchard. Stopped in for a quick lunch., comfy small seating area and quick turnaround on our order. We ordered a variety of empanadas, they were good but not super impressive like the rest of the reviews stated. Going to try some different ones next time and possibly add cheese to them as The ones with hamburger were just dry and I plain.  Definitely ask for the chimichurri sauce you'll have to get many as they come in a teeny tiny sized plastic container.  Wanted to love this place but just plain didn't.

I wrote this on the Wajaeat Tucson Facebook group page:

If you are into Mediterranean restaurants you got to try Zayna Mediterranean restaurant.  My wife got a Gyro and I got the lamb kifta plate. OMG, the food and service was incredible. That are located at 1138 N Belvedere (directly across the street from Unscrewed Theater). Absolutely one of the best meals we have eaten in a long time.

One time I went here and there were some tactical police in full gear in front of me with their rifles still strung around them. It was kinda freaky because guns make me nervous. I know they probably couldn't leave them in the car for many reasons, but it was still weird to see anyway. I assume they had just gotten done with training or something because there were only two of them in line.

This place does their gluten-free option right. They wash everything down and use new utensils and all that. It's pretty neat to see they take it that seriously. I'm not gluten sensitive, so I was actually kinda pissed of that the lady asked for it because it held up the whole line for an extra three minutes and I was hungry, but I will give her the benefit of the doubt that she has Celiac's or something. 

If you aren't actually allergic to gluten or have Celiac's (yes, those are two separate things), please don't ask for gluten free. You suck if you make me wait just because you read a post from your favorite health blogger that gluten once made her baby frown too much after breastfeeding. You probably don't even know what gluten is, you dip. I'm here to eat meat and gluten and you're throwing off my whole evening!

I also watched a guy build up the courage to ask out the cashier with his friend's help one time. His friend was very encouraging. They were right behind us in line so I didn't get to watch it go down in full, I only caught the start of his asking-out pitch, but I can't imagine it ended well. He wasn't bad looking or anything, but don't ask someone out while they're working. It's bad form, man. Plus you didn't even get a burrito. You just got chips and guac and obviously only did that as an excuse to talk to her while she rang you up. That's $2.65 + tax you just paid to get shot down. Hope it was worth it, you weirdo. 

Overall, solid Chipotle location.

First of all, I'm a huge fan of Morimoto. . . 
If I could I'd rate this 4.5 stars. . . food was great. . . fresh. . and presentation was perfection. . . would of given 5 stars but, to be honest the portions was a bit small, service was okay, and the atmosphere was so so. . .

Funny thing, I was out on the lake and someone said anyone want pizza?  Somebody had the nerve to say from where ? As if Lakers no of any other place than Riv!   They said Riv and it was gone , silly kids didn't know I was waiting for the water for my dinner. Yummy!!

I rarely eat at this location but would not recommend. As soon as I walked in there was an odd smell and the entire place was filthy. I had my grandson with me and would not even put him in a high chair due to the accumulated filth on it. Ended up leaving and not even ordering. I was too afraid to let my grandson touch anything in the place let a lone order any food from there.

My husband and I visited last night 6/22 for a date night. We ordered the plantains as an appetizer and I ordered the mahi tacos as my entree. The mahi was so dry and I could only eat one taco. The green beans that came with the meal (which I didn't touch) looked like they were going bad as I pointed it out to my husband he is in produce industry. 
After getting home I ended up getting sick from food poisoning all night! This wasn't our first visit here but will be our last so disappointing!!

Chef Mark- I am not one to complain at restaurants but I would have if I knew I would be leaving with food poisoning hence the review 12 hrs later
My drinks and lamb chops were surprisingly good. I shall return. The interior and decor were all great.

I'm solely writing this review and leaving this many stars because the 1st time my roommates and I ordered from here we did an online order to pick up for take out. As my roommate is picking up the food, the waiter who was working the register made multiple comments about how our order had "so many modifications". He made two comments about this and almost made it seem like a bad or wrong thing for us to do. My roommate ultimately apologized though we did not have a ridiculous amount of modifications for our orders. Either way, it definitely left a bad taste in all our mouths, which is so sad cause the food was pretty good except they forgot the avocado in one sandwich that comes with it. Not even modification.

Hands down the best Mediterranean food that I have ever had. We have eaten in the restaurant but mostly do take out because it's small. I have to say  we are never disappointed. The meats are so full of flavor and the homemade pita bread and hummus are to die for. Love this place

My friends and I went here for lunch one day. We ordered a lot of food, and everything was absolutely delicious. The only thing is that you have to go towards the back to grab a menu. The interior is not too bright and not too dim. And the restrooms are labeled Adam and Eve!
This place has been my family's favorite for over 20 years! Great crab rangoon and exceptional service! I've never had a bad experience! Always amazing!

We just got pizza at their Bellevue location.  It was for take-out.  The pizza was awful. The crust tasted very stale and was like a mixture between cardboard and beef jerky.  There were very few toppings on the pizza.  I am wondering if they make their take-out worse then the pizza they serve in the the restaurant? I just spoke to the manager and she questioned my posting online without "giving them a chance to make it right."  My response is that when you pay good money for a take out pizza, it should be decent. Thanks for listening.  Maybe next time they will do better?  I won't go back.

Today I went into this location feeling stressed from the day and looking for something new for a quick lunch. I was not only pleasantly surprised by the freshness of the California Club but the customer service to help me decide what to order. The  young man, Luke had a big friendly smile and cheerful spirit, set my lunch experience to whole other level. Wish everyone could take after his customer service, it makes a big difference!!! Thank you I will definitely be back.

Very disappointed Little Caesars puts out a better pizza than that in fact as I'm driving away I see a round Table Pizza I tried to the winter round table ordered a pizza and then back at my table at pirates finish with my pizza by the time I got my feet so that I had ordered took 28 minutes just to get a salad nothing so special about it paid 599 for a half a head of lettuce overall service sucked never asked me once if I'd like a refill the weirdo behind the counter behind the counter kept staring at me not sure if he's gay or not but I'll never go there again wasting my money and time

Affordable hand-crafted pasta dishes?! Indeed it is true!! 

This spot is definitely one of the more unique restaurants in the city. It's a causal atmosphere where you can mix and match sauces, pastas, and proteins to build your own dish. Or, go with a ready made classic like the John Belu-cheese(!)

This is my go-to spot for a meal before seeing art or music on st Claude. Delish dishes, wine, and cannolis, great staff and humorous sci-fi decor. 

My only complaint is that they're sometimes out of my favorite cannoli. ...it's pistachio.

Yessss... they serve Field Roast vegan dogs--my favorite brand! You get to pick your dog, then choose from a wide & unlimited array of toppings and condiments. They're open late, too, so this makes the perfect drunk food.

I had the smoked apple sage vegan dog with chipotle mayo, mustard, mother-in-law slaw, pico de gallo, and jalapenos, plus a side of seasoned fries. My friend got the kielbasa dog. We were both impressed with our respective orders.. 

The fries are awesome--super crispy with a bold flavor. I'd love to know what sort of spices they use. The spicy toppings complimented the veggie dog quite well, and I demolished the thing in the space of two minutes. My only complaints? The bun, while soft and fresh, was a bit skimpy, and I'd have liked more slaw.

It was busy the night we came, but we didn't have to wait overly long. We sat upstairs on the gallery, and watched the drunken revelers staggering about Frenchman Street. Fun place; great meal; would definitely return.

Kimchi KBBQ is definitely on the pricier side, but given that they are the only KBBQ place in the SB area, I am very grateful they are open and serve reasonable quality food.

The stars I would highly recommend are the Brisket, Short Rib, Jowl, and Bulgogi. These are easy to cook and very tender due to the fat content (or because pork cheeks are generally tender) and since they are thin, the sumptuous dipping sauce absorbs very well. The sides they provide also nicely accommodate the heavy nature of these meats.

The pork belly and finger meat is harder to cook because they are a lot thicker, and are not as ideal in this setting. They taste good, but have little reason to try this over the preferred choices above. The beef tongue is very tough (perhaps our party overcooked it) so definitely proceed with caution. 

In terms of all other dishes, I would recommend those last when you're more full since they're "less expensive" and "more filling". The dumplings are actually really good, the skin is thin and the filling is tasty with the dipping sauce. The miso soup is quite refreshing, though kind of a weird mix of nontraditional ingredients. The chicken can be good at times, especially when it is not overcooked.

In terms of the service, it was generally pretty well. They were definitely understaffed and sometimes you have to chase down the waiters, but overall they were friendly and respectful. Note that the space is a little tight.

I came to Noble Crust on a Friday night to catch up with a couple girlfriends I hadn't seen in a while. So glad we made a reservation! It didn't seem like the wait was that long, but we were sat right away in a nice outside patio table (the weather was beautiful).

Our waitress was absolutely wonderful- one of our friends was running late, but she was attentive and kept the drinks coming before she got there. 

We chose to try Noble Crust because one of my friends has celiac, and they have a gluten free crust. Her and I split pizzas and I admit I was impressed with the crust. And the toppings were delicious as well.

It's got a trendy feel to it- I personally wasn't a fan of the house music they were playing earlier in the night, but around 8 or 9pm, an acoustic guitar player started and I was enjoyed his music quite a bit!  

As a plus, it's a central location between where I live and where one of my friends lives, so I imagine we will be meeting her often for gluten free pizza options!!

Note: They also have gluten free pasta available.
Superb restaurant. Everything we ordered was fresh and delectable. The wait staff was unbelievable. This will be one of my go-to spots whenever I visit Philly.

Terrible! We waited an HR for a table and then an additional HR for our meal. All plates were ordered WELL... The kids burgers came burnt and all the steaks had pink!!! Unreal! What a dissapointment! Not to mention we are here for a Bday celebration!  The server tried her best but then started to display some irritation over us complaining! Considering our bill is about $200 we could have invested it else where!!!
Super fresh. I love made to order food. I got the fish combo and it was delicious. The fish was hot and the breading was crisp. Definitely coming back especially during lent!
A number of vegetarian options and interesting atmosphere. Not sure I'd go back but it's a good dinner.

This review is regarding a serious health concern. Last night, my mom and I witnessed one of the worst unsanitary situations ever. One of the women working there (seemed to be the manager, not sure though) was coughing into her arm (closer to her hand than her elbow) and then directly  packaging up customers food afterwards (putting her hands on the food and then putting it into the bag with her whole arm. Luckily she did not bag ours.) Additionally, we saw her drinking out of her personal water bottle at the counter, which also raised concerns regarding sanitary conditions. The cherry on top is that a gentlemen coming out of the restroom delivered a lost grey sweatshirt that he found in the bathroom. After she took the sweatshirt behind the counter (which undoubtedly has to be extremely dirty, especially after being found in the men's restroom), she proceeded to wait on customers at the register, exchanging money/ cups, and bagging up food WITHOUT washing her hands or using sanitizer. All of this happened within a matter of a couple minutes while we were waiting for our food inside the restaurant, Monday September 13th at approximately 7:25pm at the Habit Burger on Eagle in Meridian ID. 

In normal every day conditions this would still be disgusting, but given that we are living in a PANDEMIC with rising numbers, we were mortified and needless to say we will not be back. A note to corporate / management: please help your employees understand that customer service goes beyond just being nice; in this climate they should also be extra considerate of cleanliness and basic decency when preparing and serving food to your customers.

Nachos! NACHOS! 

I didn't try any of Juan's burrito, sorry it's not going to be that kind of review.. I had nachos on the brain and Juan's was suggested so why not, right?! So glad I did. Everything happened just as fast as you'd like it to on a lunch break, I was greeted and seated promptly, server showed up right away, ordered placed, hot nachos in my face! I opted for the Hawaii 5-0-4 plate which features an all star cast of applewood smoked bacon, pulled pork, mango salsa, pineapple salsa, cheddar-jack, chipotle sour cream, avocado, cilantro, and jalapeños. Chips were nice and warm and had a nice crunch. Soon as I was wiping my face the check arrived and I was out the door. I like this place and will be back to try some other dishes for sure. Oh mann.. now I'm stuck on nachos again, guess I need another fix..

Great for lunch and nice at dinner because nobody will rush you from this byob.  They can handle groups of any size and did a great job twice when I was there with a big group.  Deicious variety of favorites to choose from ranging from standards like pad thai and pineapple fried rice to curries and many other noodle dishes.

Reasonably priced.

YUM!!! It was so good!! I loved it. Great food and very family friendly. The theming was awesome and the food was just fantastic! I wanted more even though I was stuffed. Best Philly Cheesesteak I've had in a very long time, bread was fresh and it was a great philly. I highly recommend and I will be back. Well worth the 2.5 hours we drove to get there. I forgot to get photos of my food it was so good.

We decided to check out Season 52's (S52) Fall menu and were not disappointed! In place of the artichoke an goat cheese flatbread is the very tasty mushroom, truffle, and goat cheese flatbread. It is savory & unctuous - and, if you have some leftovers, it goes perfectly with an over-easy egg for breakfast. We went with some staples: the "New" Old Fashioned was awesome - not too sweet - just a hint of muddled orange. Wine was reasonably priced ($74 for a bottle of Markham 1879 Cab). Dinner was delicious: trout and a perfectly cooked 6 OX filet with 52's outstanding cheesy grits instead of mashed potatoes. And Mat took great care of us, as usual!

I've had a lifelong romance with Asian cuisine, and since you're reading this, perhaps you have, too.  For many years, Indianapolis had run-of-the-mill Asian restaurants, all serving the same mundane variety of dishes.   The times they are a-changing.  The city's recent boom in international cuisine has provided diverse options, with offerings from a host of nationalities and cooking styles.  Taichi Asian Grill House, located in Ransom Place near the IUPUI campus, combines Chinese and Japanese cuisine to create a unique and interactive dining experience.

It would be a gross understatement to say that Taichi Asian Grill House has a large menu.  The choices are epic and myriad.  With a menu catering to those desiring fairly pedestrian dishes (e.g. General Tso's chicken, moo goo gai pan), it might seem fairly basic to the diner desiring a more interesting meal.  However, this couldn't be further from the truth: Taichi offers a large sushi menu, and it also feautres Chinese hot pot. The sushi and the hot pot can be ordered a la carte, in combinations, or on an all-you-can-eat basis.

I strongly suggest the all-you-can-eat options.  For just $25, you can eat to your heart's content: freshly made sushi, hot pot, and various other appetizers, sides, and soups.  In order to get the best sampling, it is best to dine in a group if this option is chosen.  It is a terrific value for the price.

The fresh sushi is wonderful, and the hardest part of ordering was actually making a decision.  I really enjoyed the California roll, the crunchy spicy tuna roll, the Indy rock & roll, and especially the heart attack roll.  I also found the crab stick and squid sushi to have excellent flavor.  Get the heart attack roll if you get anything; it's rich, so call your cardiologist first thing the next morning!

The Chinese hot pot is my favorite aspect of Taichi.  Not only are the offerings sumptuous, the experience of cooking the meat and vegetables at the table is a fun, interactive activity for any group.  I found the pork belly to be the most delectable of the meats. I also loved the top sirloin and the beef spare ribs, although they took a bit longer to cook.  Pair the beef with cabbage.  While basic, adding some chicken and onions is a great choice.  If you're in the mood for seafood hot pot, try the jumbo shrimp and the squid.  The hot pots come in mild and spicy.  Get it spicy!

You might ask, "With all of these tasty and diverse options, why only four stars?"  It was the service.  I found it to be terrible.  From the moment our group entered the restaurant, we encountered problems.  First, it was the host/server seeming put off by giving a party of five a hot pot table for eight (when the restaurant was nearly empty).  Second, since many of us had never eaten hot pot, we felt an explanation was deserved.  We got one, but it was poorly delivered. Third, we were told several times that if we failed to eat the food we ordered, we would be charged for the excess. We fully understood the first time, and we explained that we were hearty eaters.  Fourth, our server was lackadaisical: refills on drinks and hot pot ingredients were difficult to obtain. Fifth, she was rude.  Sixth, she turned off our hot pot twice before asking if we were finished eating, leaving me completely nonplussed.  Taichi Asian Grill has some serious work do to on their servers' tableside manners.  Fortunately, the food trumped the service, and it will not deter my business in the future.

Overall, Taichi is a winner, and I will definitely look forward to return visits!

Went there for a luncheon. Food was good and our server was awesome. Great sense of humor but kept a straight face. Funny guy. I only had a salad but there were compliments all around from the table. They need to kill the flies buzzing around the windows. 

Will go again

This place is clean, authentic, and the real deal. Feels like it's owned by a group of college students. The menus are phenomenal and the choices are unique but delicious.  Used to be our little secret but I feel like now everyone knows about this awesome place.

Really good for an all vegan restaurant option. Their burgers are good, but the buns are never toasted enough. If the buns were more toasted it'd be so much better. Their fries are great!! Other sides are good as well.

BISON BISON BISON!!!!

for those have not had bison it is wonderful!  its not as gamey as one would imagine and has a subtle sweetness that is not a sugary sweet, but more of a savory sweet.  with that being said, i had the Delmonico and it was rich, juicy and i couldn't help but eat every bite, knowing that the deliciousness would be worth the impending food coma.

service was average as well as decor, but the food was well worth it!

Came here with my parents for dinner on a weeknight. Restaurant wasn't very crowded at first but we did get there fairly early (parents like to eat earlier) and started to fill up as our meal went on. I started with a salad special I don't recall the name and didn't take a picture but it had melon, fresh mozzarella, golden raisins, arugula and a pomegranate dressing. Great but not uncommon combination, the fresh mozzarella was my favorite. I ordered the pappardelle with crabmeat, tomatoes and spinach with rose cream sauce. Pasta was super fresh and cooked perfectly and I really enjoyed the sauce. Parents got the mushroom ravioli (also really food, great sauce and fresh pasta/mushrooms) and chicken parmigiana (can't comment on the parm, didn't try it). Service was good, food came pretty quickly. Would definitely come back and try other dishes.

I have to admit this place is worth the wait (40 min at noon on a weekday). Everything about this place is homey and delicious. 
Start with the unsweetened ice tea. It has this natural sweetness and floral after taste. It really is divine. 
The fried chicken is so juicy. I don't know how it can be that moist. The breading is thin, not too oily and seasoned perfectly. I usually love Cajun seasoned fried chicken but this is the most flavorful "original" chicken I've ever had (including Atlanta and NC) 
The catfish is underrated here. It is a giant filet with a nice flavorful crust. I like a side of lemon and homemade tartar sauce. 
The only hot sauce they have is crystal but the acidity helps cut all the fried goodness. 
I def would come back

My Aunt and Uncle (who are Vietnamese) treated me to lunch at Pho Ha Saigon. It was my first time here, I didn't even know this place existed. This place looks like your typical pho resturant,  so first thing I noticed was the amount of Vietnamese families in here. It was packed and that's always a good sign.

I opted for bun bo hue (rice noodles in a spicy beef broth) instead of pho. And I'm glad I did! The broth was full of flavor and spices (not bland and watery like some other places on Washington). It's also very meaty. They give lots of variety of meat including thin sliced brisket, flank (I think), and ham hock. Be sure to add the lettuce and bean sprouts provided, it adds a great texture to the dish!

Can't wait to go back for some delicious hangover food, I mean pho!

There are actually two restaurants here. There's one downstairs and one upstairs. We travel quite a bit.  We would say that this is one of the best meals we've had. We had the swordfish and one of the best sword fish dinners we have ever had.  The service was spectacular.

This a fun place with great selection of items made to order magnificently prepared. even  request not on the menu are happily made. Their spicy tataki sauce is a house special that should not be missed. True recommend for any outing.

Staff is helpful and very friendly. 
They made sure my water was never empty!!!

Three cheers for yet another reason to get your mojo up the mountain. We had a large group and were able to taste several dishes. Brisket and salmon were big hits, topped off with delicious cherry pie. Something for everyone here. Staff was most attentive, and we loved some of the little differences that make the restaurant unique.

If you're looking for a pho place, this is your only spot! I came with some friends Sunday for dinner and I'm never going anywhere else. It wasn't very busy when we got there but a large group came in so they asked us to move tables. We were flexible but the restaurant was so apologetic they comped our drinks. I could tell the customer experience was a top priority. We ordered egg rolls and they were savory and crispy. For dinner I got the chicken pho and I should have tasted it before adding hoisin sauce because it was favorable and only needed a little. I messed up and used a lot but it was still tasty. Our server came around often to check on us and always came with a refill. When we finished paying my friend and I left not realizing she left her purse. Our server came out of the restaurant to let us know before we drove off. They truly went above and beyond.

Love, love, love to eat breakfast here. Fresh fun food, always plentiful and filling. Personal favorite is Million Dollar Bacon. Thick cut bacon with sugar and a little spice. I truly enjoy a breakfast joint that brings me a coffee pot to the table. You can't go wrong at First Watch, just be ready to wait during those peak days and times...

This is such a cute little place, and I liked that they were open until ten. Many of the places in haddonfield close rather early, and I eat dinner later so it was a surprise to see. We started with calamari and it was great. We squeezed a little lemon on it and it hit the spot. For dinner I got the rigatoni all' matriciana. It was a spicy pasta with pancetta. It came out piping hot and i was pleased with my meal. My partner got the gnocchi alla vodka. The pasta was delicious and had prosciutto and spinach. Our meal was great, and the service was speedy as well. We will definitely be back.
Delicious! We had the Foodies club and the pollo and plantain sandwich. What a tasty little place, definitely a hidden gem. Can't wait to come back!

As someone who hops off the plane in Los Angeles and runs to In and Out I was excited to see a take on a West Coast burger! The burger was great, crispy and well seasoned. The only thing I would've liked to have seen is a better lettuces and more of the sauce. The lettuce was hardly there much like the sauce. The fries were just like In and Outs, AMAZING! I did also get a strawberry shake, it was pretty typical, nothing too exciting. It was a big serving of milkshake though. Will definitely be back, a great meal for $10.

This is literally one of the best pizza places I've ever eaten from and no doubt the best around here. Great food and great service every time, delivery is always HOT! I am never disappointed and a huge selection of food!
Few years ago I ordered a large pepperoni pizza with cheese sticks and I asked a manager where's my cheese dip? She said you don't need one since you have cheese sticks that have cheese on it. ‍
This is our go to breakfast spot. Wish they would get more options for stuffed French toast, but the food is good anyway.

We enjoyed a wonderful first meal at Noble Crust (we hit the Sunday brunch time so missed unlimited mimosa Saturday). My husband has heard their brunch advertised on the Bone 102.5 so we made it out to see firsthand this combination of Italian and Southern food. 
We did not have a reservation (although I'd recommend one for a busy time such as brunch) and were lucky to be seated immediately. 
I could not decide between the fried chicken biscuit or the chicken/waffles so our server helped me by recommending the chicken/ waffles - success! The dish was amazing - fried chicken was tender and juicy with perfect crunch on the outside, and the waffle was as big as the plate! (Think fried chicken that is potentially better than Publix) ;) 
I choose the grits as my side (I went to school in the south and am a bit of a grit snob) and they were ok, not out of this world.
My husband selected the egg sandwich on a toasted brioche bun and it was above average (bun was nicely toasted and warm when it arrived at the table).
For his side he selected the potatoes which were crispy smashed potatoes (nice twist).
The atmosphere was pleasant and the outside seating was popular as it was a beautiful Florida spring day (in January).
My favorite Chinese restaurant in Nashville. Affordable, good portions of food. Definitely try the Hunan chicken!

Where do i start, mt daughter talkes me into this place and we had such a good time. Everything we tried was amazing, i had thai broth, highly recommended,  my daughter got mushroom.  We did all you can eat bbq and tried everything we could.  Highly recommended a fun date night or mother daughter was amazing!!!
Freaking amazing ziti, pizza, and freshly baked bread! The owner was our server and WOW just exceptional service. Do yourself a favor and get some Italian Touch

I very much enjoyed the food. Really quite delicious. The decor is clean, uncluttered, and attractive while staying casual. And, the pricing was excellent for what I got.

The only reason I did not give this five stars was the service. While our server was extremely cordial and helpful with the menu, the place simply got too busy for the limited staff to handle with appropriate attention once we got the food, particularly toward the end of our meal. This no doubt is a testament to the quality of the food, but the lesson, it seems to me, is if you're going to fill your restaurant, you better be prepared to staff up.

I'll definitely be going back. Quite a wonderful discovery.
Amazing food & amazing service. Highly recommend and will definitely be back! They're also a BYOB which is another plus!

Absolutely! Everything is fantastic. My husband and I often sit at the bar; the dirty girl martini is delicious. They are oysters are amazing as well as all of their sushi options. You can never go wrong With their surf and turf. I highly recommend this place!

The first time I came here, I was very fond of. This place has spacious space, with lots of delicious food from VIETNAM. With that, I am very impressed Grilled Pork Chop Fried Rice. I will come back here one day.
The service and food is consistently fantastic here. The chicken saganaki is delicious. Have been going here for years and have never been disappointed.

I'd recommend taking the trolley or walking instead of trying to find somewhere to park. It is a cute place though and is near Tulane and Loyola. 
I do not eat sushi but I got the lunch special that was a chicken teriyaki bento box. I really loved the chicken it wasn't too sweet it had an amazing balance for flavor. Also it came with a little bit of noodle salad which is good. It came with tasty fresh fruit that paired well with the chicken. White rice and a house salad was also included that was good as well. Keep in mind if you order soda you will be paying for refills. 
The service was really nice.

The pizza was the superstar at this place.   The customer service however is the only thing keeping me from giving 5 stars.  Drinks were never refilled. We weren't really checked on until our meal was done.  How ever the pizza is some of the best I've had in a while.  Will definitely be back, but maybe for a to-go order.

Taste bud heaven!  Those little Poppers are round, fried, sweet, hot surprises!  Though not Poppers in the traditional sense with peppers and cheese, which I think would make them other worldly, the fresh, sweet corn definitely qualifies as comfort food!  I appreciate the reference to the Trinity,  their 3 sons, 3 nationalities,  3 specialties, and I am thrilled with the emphasis on GMO Free and locally sourced.

I love Bahama Breeze and the location is the best. The food was good but our server was a bit slow. We did have a party of about 10 so it took a while to get small things like a straw. Besides that, I enjoyed and can't wait to return!

I came here on a first date and it was a nice, quiet spot to talk, have a bite, and try some exciting cocktails. the spot has just enough character to be interesting but not so much that it becomes spuriously quirky. to start, i either had the mad hatter (with the habanero tequila) or some kind of margarita with jalapeno something. whatever it was, it was spicy and perfectly sweet and resulted in me commenting on it after every sip (which i think my date found annoyingly charming). my next round of Makers and ginger went down as beautifully as it always does. My date was equally pleased with his fancy beer and we were both silenced (pleasantly) by the "date with a pig" appetizer - which was also great fodder for corny first date banter. The burgers were less noteworthy but definitely tasty and definitely hit the spot. I had the BoRo sliders and he had the classic cheeseburger with bacon. The caramelized onions and sweet Hawaiian rolls were a win on my sliders but the aioli was undetectable, unfortunately. One server was super amicable and chatted us up for a while - giving us newcomers some tips on local attractions. I'll certainly be back for more dates - with pigs and gentlemen alike.

Amazing food! Real authentic Thai food! Great flavors on the pad thai, massaman curry, pad see ew, tom yum soup, eggplant, salad rolls, pineapple fried rice.. all the Thai faves for me. And the mango sticky rice was delicious to finish everything off. 
Great service too. Served quickly with a smile. Would come back here in a heartbeat

Staying over by the Grand Ol Opry and found Rudie's on Yelp. So worth the five mile drive! Great service and exceptional Jambalaya. Chef was great when he took a fast minute to answer a question. If you're close it is worth the trip.

This place is awesome! I had the chicken noodle soup and that broth was delicious. My friends had the pineapple curry with beef and shrimps and they both loved it. See the photos. Service was on point as always. They gave us soup and an eggroll that was very good too.
Old school type restaurant.  But the food is really good and the drinks are  too.  Lots of locals and it gets very busy.  Fried shrimp and chicken cordon blue are really good.
It's KFC what more do you want. It's a change from normal fast food.

I won't hold the size of the place against PF, but I do wonder why their employees are miserable and rude! Every time I walk in I get the feeling that I'm interrupting whoever is sitting behind the counter and they come off as angry that they have to put the iPad down and actually help someone. They rarely smile and make the experience very unwelcoming and I'm not sure I'll go back after this last trip in. The baked goods have become bland and the sandwiches are mainly bread. For their prices, they are lacking in both service and flavor.
Great pizza and salads in the middle of wonderful Rock Hill, MO!!  Worth the drive for this down-home experience with three styles of pizza.

First off, one star for Google™ maps--at least this time. I literally got lost and drove around for two hours trying to find this place only to discover that I could've taken Page off of 70. Cheers to dim sum, jeers to ridiculously compu-routed directions. 

Because of this, we got there around 2:00--not exactly prime dim sum time. Several of the items were cold because they turned the steam cart off, but the things that were hot were very decent. The staff did wait on us hand and foot, and quickly turned everything back on. One note is that they carried several items that aren't to be found at Won Ton King (literally the only dim sum joint worth going to--anyone that says differently doesn't know their stuff). I'm going to have to go back during "normal" dim sum hours to truly grasp how good this place is, but thus far, not bad. 

What was that? No witticism? No standard Brandon-humor? I've decided that its under appreciated. Go ahead and give a "Cool" vote to people who can't spell or use modifiers correctly. Bitches.

I kid!

I've always loved Red Robin since I was a wee foodie. Normally wouldn't write a review about a place that is always so consistent. Went today and asked for no Onion on my burger and it came with onion. I tried powering thru but I HATE onion, especially raw red onion. They were so kind to take it back, make me a new one and not make me feel like I was putting them out. I received the bill and only my beer was on it. Totally unnecessary but I'll take it. Thank u Longstreet RR. I will always go back

Andy

Been to Villa Gallace many times and it never disappoints! The apps are great and my fave is the calamari fra diavalo. The sauce is wonderful and the linguine in white clam sauce is the best I've ever tasted. Going back tonight for eggplant parm!!!

Wonderful (sassy) staff. We went for a bachelorette weekend brunch and they actually serenaded the bride! The woman had an amazing voice and totally made the experience. Eggs Jonathon is delicious: eggs benedict with tomato and special hollandaise sauce. Grits and biscuits are amazing. The shrimp poboy is overflowing and really fresh shrimp. Great place!

I am a fan of Pizza Squared Detroit-style pizza. I especially like the carry out special 6 Square Cheese for $7.99. It is delicious! I save half for the next day and it warms up well. The crust is especially good. The only thing I might suggest is to have some small chunks of tomato in the sauce, but I may just ask for tomato on my next one. I love the pizza at Pizza Squared and would recommend it. One note - I did have some concern with cleanliness and a bad smell recently at the 30th St. location. I may try the other location for comparison.

Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation - check out restaurant health inspections for all your favorite restaurants at:

http://www.myfloridalicense.com/dbpr/

Choose "Search Food and Lodging Inspections" on the left side.

The food was sooo good here. I had the Guinness hotdog with fries. One, the bread is phenomenal! Two, the toppings are plentiful. And three, the drinks are strong!." However, this place is jammed packed and very loud and you have to listen out for your name when your food is ready.
We arrived near closing time but they still sat our group of 6. I got soup dumplings and a side of bok choy. Both were good but I'm not very picky when it comes to either dish, I just like them!

I thought I had a review on here already for The Journey; buuuut I guess not so here goes. So it's been over a year since I've been and for some reason I just had a craving to go. Had an opportunity to go today and was underwhelmed.

First, i want to say, that I absolutely love the selection of food but everything was lacking flavor. When we came a couple years ago, it was great and worth the $19.99/pp charge, but this time, I'm so glad I came during lunch which is only like $8.79-$8.99/adult (children are less depending on age). Which right there boggles my mind. That's quite the jump price wise. Lunch: 8.99 Dinner: 19.99; or is it just me?!?

Anyway, there was a busy crowd out, but it didn't feel crowded. The layout of the restaurant helps keep traffic moving (which I appreciate with 3 children along for the trip). 

Overall, I like the location, food wasn't great. The children enjoyed it so I'd come back for them at lunch. I'm sorry I would NOT dare pay the dinner price. Or maybe I'd come to see if they step it up for dinner.

What I got: Nigiri set ($30), oyster shooter ($?). 

Benchmarks: Doma (PHL), Sushi Yoshida (NYC) and Sushi of Gari (NYC)

Chef's choice nigiri set consists of 16-17 pieces of choice fish. The variety of white fish (shiromi) is quite impressive considering most "sushi" consumed in this locale is of the rolled form. Shiromi is more difficult to keep a regular stock than tuna because it does not age. (A test for your premium sushi restaurant. If they are not aging their tuna, they are not buying them in blocks. Hence sub-premium) Kudos to the 2 guys behind the counter for having quality white fish (sea bream, kinmedai, etc) week in, week out.

This is my go-to place for sushi in Philly. On par with Yoshida (NYC) for white fish. Tuna is not great here (better or as good as any in Philly), but there are only few places outside of Japan who would know what "hara-kami-ichiban" is....

If you are stuck having a sushi snob as a guest, take them here. You can blame me and Philly if they are not satisfied.
A+++++++ excellent service... Excellent food...Very reasonable pricing. Highly recommend! Glad we don't live closer I would founder on the cheese bread. Bedford, IN

The shredded pork tacos here are absolutely insane! I always get a few when I order from here. The servings are great for the price point. I recommend stopping in for some real authentic Mexican food.

I cant really complain about my pizza and (4 fountain coke refills) here a few weeks back. The pizza was average not bad but not stellar i did its job which was to soak up the after math of Friends Giving and late night dancing at L'tage. I will say service was great i have never felt better after 4 fountain cokes and half a small pizza

Flawless.
I would describe the service that our large party received at Merchants as  nothing short of just that.

As soon as I was seated, the server acknowledge my vegan diet and offered up options on how I could tweak the menu or offered to have their chef whip up an innovative dish for me. I chose the latter and was smitten as soon as I took my first bite. The drink suggestions were thoughtful and well-received. Even their valet service took good care of me as I was entering and exiting for the evening.

I fell in love with the dark and moody vintage layout that had quirky nooks and crannies everywhere. This is the perfect place to host a large dinner party or romantic date....hint hint.
Good selection of specialty pizza. Never busy. Good pies. Prices are a little high. $4 for a 2 liter Soda with my to go pizza was too much.

Personally I love the food at Lameberti's. And the Bananas Foster dessert is  a favorite also. Prices seem reasonable to me (I'm looking at some other reviews, confused). I think the place is awesome. Not 5 stars like Tavern on the Green, NYC, 5 stars like- a perfect treat to yourself at a reasonable price, that you can afford to do on a regular basis. Great for a date too, due to the atmosphere and nice smell of the place. Try it!!

The best tacos in St. Pete!  This great alternative to the much revered Taco Bus offers equivalent or better food at better prices.  Although many people may be too skittish to try TacoSon's Lengua Taco, it is probably the best taco in Florida.  To sweeten the deal even more, when you buy 5 tacos, the 6th is free!

The TacoSon atmosphere is also nice, but less creative that the Taco Bus concept of serving tacos from a school bus.  Instead, TacoSon has an atmosphere similar to your typical semi-fast food style mexican interior, somewhat similar to Pancheros or Chipotle, with high top and booth seating.  However, the TacoSon walls also contain intricate murals depicting several of Mexico's diverse landscapes.  Additionally, there are several TVs allowing you to watch the game or the news.  If you prefer to sit outside, there's also a patio to accomodate those wishing to feel the Florida breeze while sipping on a Corona.

While most people will still prefer Taco Bus to TacoSon, the person willing to stray from popular opinion and peer pressure may pick TacoSon as the better alternative.  I certainly don't think any tacos can beat TacoSon's Lengua Taco, at least not in Florida.

Love  Los Nopales. I always order the shrimp chimi. It is delicious and the service is always great. My friend orders the cheese crisp. Mmmm good too. If you haven't been there, stop on your way to Old Tucson or the Desert Museum. Better yet make a special trip-You won't be disappointed.

Seriously, this place alone makes me want to leave all of Phoenix behind and move down to Tucson.  It's a bit of a dive, definitely lived in and when there is a band next door you can barely hear your waitress and for all that, I think I'm in love.

Went here after a show at the Rialto.  Starving, thirsty and ears ringing, my buddy and I couldn't have been taken better care of.  The waitress' "Pug Life" shirt iced the greasy cake.

The menu is brief but everything is accounted for.  From great sandwiches to salads to breakfast, the menu is traditional and eclectic all at the same.  The emphasis is on soul here, not soul food per-say, but hipster soul nonetheless.

My buddy had the Haystack: Bed of fries, burger patty, gravy, two eggs over top.  It was the most savory thing I've ever tasted.  I had meatloaf and mashed potatoes.  Huge and delicious, garlicy with an awesome mushroomy gravy.  Came with a salad too that a creamy pesto dressing that kicked ass.

I recommend this place most for breakfast and late night meals, but it is just a great throwback kind of place for anytime, bring the parents, come hungover, it's all good.

Great service!
Can't understand why the parking lot is always full. The steak I had the one and only time there had the texture and tenderness of the loser of the last race at Tampa Bay Downs. Never been back.
The bad reviews shocked me.  Service was quick and food was great.  Will definitely come back again!

The flavor of my dinner was DELICIOUS! The service was great. And the manager Seth bent over backwards to help me with my dietary issues. I WILL Be Back. And Thank You For a wonderful dining experience.

Great happy hour. Oysters seemed very fresh which is impressive in Philadelphia. Nice wine selection. Very busy when a friend and I dined in for happy hour on a Thursday. Service was still very attentive and pleasant. A bit pricey overall but to be expected in center city with oysters. Happy Hour highly recommended.

Ok.  This is hard for me to say because I am an avid carnivore and I have eaten steaks all over the world, but I had the best ribeye steak of my 43 years at The Merchant.  It was perfectly marbled, aged and prepared.  I could have cut it with a spoon.  It was amazing and it ruined  me.

The place itself is cool, if not a bit full of itself.  I waited an hour plus for a table as a walk in which I NEVER do, but the bar was fun and admittedly, downtown Nashville is not exactly flush with fine dining.  But after we were seated, the service and food made it worth the wait.  In fact we were in town for three nights and I immediately made a reservation for the next night when we were leaving.  

No doubt, you pay for what you get.  And there is pretension in the place.  But when the food is served, all is forgiven.  Even the bill.

Lately service is lacking.  Used to be our favorite place.  Takes a bit to be served and servers don't check on you often.  I hate having to ask for more bread every time.  They should bring it when you sit down and continue until you let them know.  Customer service is just lacking.  And servers should speak loud enough to hear.  Last one I could barely hear.  Frustrating. 

Tried again 2 days ago.  (June 3) About 12 people in place.  Slow service.  Hardly checked on us.  Had to get my own pepper.  No sweet n low.  No ice in iced tea!  Food came out.  Never checked on again.    Staff just standing around clueless.   Such a bummer, used to be our favorite.  Seriously gone downhill.   Waiters need retraining.   Completely ignored. 

Ran out of bread. Gave us something different.  Then got the new bread.  Never came back to serve it to us.   The bread is the best.   We got cold yucky store bread.  

That 2nd location really hurting the state street one.   Terrible.

We never made it there to eat! The phone encounter was a turn off. We had a res and called to add an additional person. While we were trying to decide between one of the two time slots offered,  the female phone hostess was abrupt and definitely not inviting. We looked over the wine list on their website. (We are dedicated to really good wine with fine dining). Generally the offerings are nothing special or even solidly good until the price is $90 and above. You may not bring your own wine; there is no corkage fee. I fear we may be missing out on delicious food, but given the lack of a warm welcome, that's okay.

Our night started by having to wait over 40 minutes for a table that we had an 8:00pm reservation for.  Due to the fact that the restaurant was so over crowded the host actually suggested we go wait at the bar across the street, Manayunk Tavern, which is not even owned by the Tomato Bistro, which should've been our first clue to go somewhere else for dinner.  Our table was still not ready at 8:45 so the host literally threw together 2 mismatched tables that were wobbly.  When we were finally greeted by a server, our waitress brought us samples of soup with a biscotti, which was nice but she started to clear the soup within minutes and take them before we were even finished tasting our sample; because she was rushing she spilled soup all over my friend, who was luckily wearing black.  The waitress then came back with a napkin and proceed to physically wipe off my friend despite the fact that my friend asked her repeatedly to stop touching her.  We ordered 3 pizzas for our group of 6 and when our food arrived one of the pizzas was missing, by the time we got the forgotten pizza we were done eating.  The late/forgotten pizza was undercooked and taken to our table right out of the oven without cooling so when you tried to pick up a burning hot slice it all fall apart. I am typically not a person to write bad reviews but our experience at this restaurant was just SO awful from start to finish.  A person who I think may have been a manager came to our table and asked how everything was and we told him about the crummy chain of events that happened...he did not seem to care very much.  He offered us no compensation and was not very apologetic.  As someone who worked in the restaurant business for many years, I can honestly say that the Tomato Bistro should be embarrassed.  I recommend eating at the Manayunk Tavern across the street, they have great service and great food.

No, it's not in the quarter

It's a trip back in a real time machine.

And they pile 'blue crab' meat and carrots on lettuce with italian dressing.  And that alone deserves some sort of culinary award.  

SImple, authentic, and real New Orleans Italian cuisine.

This is food that you think about when you're not there.

I've been to this little hole in the wall a few times with my 4 year old. I don't think I've ever had anything that I didn't really like. My kid absolutely destroys his dinner when we go here so we will definitely be going back. Don't expect a trendy place or anything too hip (actually not a fan of trendy/hip) but the food is solid and they do have a few beers to choose from to go with all that spicy happiness.
Meatballs are the best!  ...goes great with antipasta salad.  Nice people!  Will definitely return!
My boyfriend said his quesadilla was just okay.  But my nachos were overpriced at almost $10 for what was basically chips with unmelted cheese and cold chicken. Not impressed.
The Love Burger is to die for. Don't be swayed away from the "high prices" the food is good quality. Quick and friendly service. The place is a must to try if you're in the area!

The food was quite good (B+) though certainly not in line with the hype or my expectations. Main beef was with the waiter who relentlessly attempted to up-charge my party to the point where we questioned (after his third attempt) whether or not he might be competing with his colleagues to bring in the most dollars for the evening. Thoroughly off-putting for an aspirational dining establishment.

Awesome down home cooking.  Best breakfast we've had in a long, long time.  Biscuits were the lightest I've ever had and everything was cooked perfectly.  Even the pancakes were rated best ever by my son.  Definitely recommend if you are in the area.

Fantastic quality pizza. The breadsticks are rather plain, but they do tell you this in the menu. I ordered a side by side large 2 topping pizza (which was a $12.99 summer special). On one side (my side) was bacon and ham, and on the other was pepperoni and pineapples (my dad's). The sauce is different, but good. 

They offer free delivery, but I haven't tested this out yet. I will update this review when I have. 

Their place is decorated with Beatles, Elvis, and I think I even saw a picture of John Lenin. I'm not really into those genres of music, but it did offer a homey feeling to the place.

The location often scares off some people. It's located next to a gas station (Marathon) and some other store. It's pretty empty most of the time, so I wouldn't be too worried. 

The cashiers and management were great. They made sure we were comfortable, provided plates, napkins, silverware, and gave us an extra table. The man who answered our questions was polite and checked on us regularly. 

I'll be ordering again, by delivery.

So I've been here thrice now--twice for dinner, and the latest outing was for lunch.  They have a nice lunch special where you can choose a soup or small app, a salad, and an entree for $7.95 to $10.95 depending on if you choose chicken, tofu, beef, or shrimp for your dish.  Smile Cafe, a mere one block away, has the same exact lunch special.  Coincidence?  I think not... I wonder which came first?

Anyways, I went with the coconut soup with chicken, the Thai garden salad, and the classic green curry with beef.  OKAY, so when I went for dinner the first time I also tried the coconut soup.  Now, both times the actual soup has great flavor--rich and sweet coconut with that slight tang of lime.. I really love it.  What I don't love is the overcooked and SUPER DRY proteins I've gotten with it BOTH instances.  The first time was wayyyyyy overcooked shrimp, and this time it was almost inedible chicken.  Do they not cook the protein to order for this soup or what?  On to the green curry... again, same problem.  My beef was dry, but the green curry sauce was nice.  Not the best I've had, but decent.  

Onto the service; seems pretty friendly, but the waitress we had for my lunch outing was kind of short with us.  That and she kept spilling water on my friend and all over our table..
