Atmosphere here is however, pleasant and intimate.  Makes a good date place, good for small groups, or just good if you want a quiet place you can hear your company in.  Overall though, I just don't see the big appeal.
Some of my friends rave about this place, but my experiences here have been entirely average.

This review is for the veggie drunken noodles alone. There is really no need to order any other dish at Pattaya. Great for when you want food but are too lazy to cook for yourself. 
If you want to eat in, they have lunch specials on weekdays as well.
Really good Thai food!! Had the roasted duck and Thai iced coffee!! Will definitely be back again

I can't write anything here that hasn't already been written, but I will chime in nonetheless. Amy and I went for our first anniversary last night, and it was really, really good. We had the benefit of being served by a good friend, so the service was friendly, prompt and very sweet. Our meal was delicious. Best oysters I've had in a while, really good, moist, tender grilled octopus. The chowda was a little thin but very tasty with lots of clams. The clambake for two was awesome too. Love the atmosphere and people-watching. We will be back!

I originally gave 5 stars, but after a second visit decided 4 stars was more accurate.  
--------

Went here for the first time tonight.  My wife had the bulgogi while I had pho.  Both were very good, although the bulgogi meal probably stood out more between the two.  It came with a nice small salad, miso soup, then the main entree which included beef, sautéed veggies, and rice.  My wife, who loves many Asian foods said the veggies were the best she'd ever had.  The beef was exceptionally flavorful and tender.

The pho was pretty much exactly what I'd expect from good pho.  Just enough steak in it to keep it interesting with good spice add-ins.  A little smaller bowl than what I usually get when eating pho, but the price reflected the smaller size.

We also had the gyoza dumplings, which were very good too.

The interior is straightforward, but new and in good condition.  There's no ambiance to speak of, so it's all about the food.  Our server was quiet but attentive, which worked well for us.

I'd highly recommend this place and we will definitely go back.

I was really excited for an Italian BYO to open in our neighborhood. Its a lovely space with outdoor seating. Some of the dishes needed more seasoning, like the octopus. Prices are a bit high for the portion size. They have some interesting things on the menu; the uni carbonara actually makes a better sharing appetizer than main dish because it is so intense.  I will be back to explore more of the menu as they get in to their groove.
Great burgers & shakes, This is  a new White Castle it's very clean and a very nice place to eat!

My favorite neighborhood coffee shop. Great coffee that's not too overly priced. Wonderful baked goods (a personal favorite is the coffee cake). Friendly people who work there and frequent there. Plus, lots of natural lighting for my studying needs. So glad to have this gem of a coffee shop close by.

Pizza is fantastic. I've had salads there twice and they were so disappointing, so small and lacking in salad. I've had a steak entree and others non salad sos that were good. I like this place so much. I wish they would fix their salad thing but I'll always go back for the good pizza and drink selection.

ive always loved this place. The flavor combos are delicious. I've never had any of the meat dishes but I've had almost all of the vegan dishes. They're not all my favorite but they have all been really good. The service may not be as in your face as some people expect, but it's just right for me. The owners and their son seem Friendly and caring and I believe they are still using some organic ingredients.

Such a great addition to the area! Food is phenomenal, prices are unbeatable, and the staff is great! We had the BBQ burger, guacamole burger, and chicken fingers. Everything was perfect. You gotta give them a try. You won't regret it!

I have tried this place at least 3 times and have ways said I am not trying it again due to the costumer service and length of waiting; BUT I forget...so I end up going and realizing why I hadn't been there in a while. Food is ok. They missed an order of white rice and gave us 2 pork dumplings instead of chicken. I dont eat pork, so that's fun-at least other's did. Anyhow-I'm not a fan of this establishment.

I had watched and waited for this place to open and then it became the darling of some of the local food bloggers. I finally stopped and gave it a try. First bite was so good, I'll keep coming back!

I have to love a place with pork and grits!  The chopped pork was excellent - moist and with plenty of flavor from the dry rub. Extra kudos for having a vinager sauce (they also have a mustard based sauce, but I don't care for that style). They also rotate the days they have ribs, salmon and chicken. They also have brisket.

The sides are awesome and home made. The Mac and cheese was  great and the squash casserole had nice, big pieces of squash.  I had a hard time deciding between the cheese grits (served daily) and the grit of the day.  The nice lady behind the counter gave me a taste test of both - AWESOME.  I bought the "show me your grits" t-shirt.

Last, but not least, get yourself some of that chocolate cake with chocolate gravey.  It will make a really bad day all better.

As this place is on my way home from work, I will become a regular on those nights I'm too tired to cook or have to have some smoked  pig to sooth my soul.

Located in the same stip as the Melrose Kroger (off Franklin Road)

it's a pizza place, it's fine.

friendly and fast service, nice clean place, pizza is decent and the cheesesteaks are above average.  salads are typical pizza-place salads and the entrees are hit-or-miss (you have to know what to order).

a good place to bring the kids or grab a pie, but don't expect to be blown away or anything.

Order the Fries! The sides and appetizers at Besh are better than the mains and the mains are good. The fries in particular were awesome and the pickles that come with the burger are worth fighting over. Make sure you save room and energy for the bread pudding. It is also a dish worth sparring with spoons for. 

Atmosphere.  It's a casino. What do you expect? Its pleasant enough inside but the smoke and noise from the casino intrude on the space. George Rodrigue's massive blue dog canvas cover the walls.

The "Steak & Egg's" was interesting. The presentation of the quail egg topped tartar was appealing. The spinach salad accompaniment was less so. Overall, a pleasant teaser, but not wow.

The Bibb Lettuce Salad was good and the dressing was fantastic, but it was seriously overdressed. In essence, the bed of lettuce was little more than a delivery device for a buttermilk blue cheese blanket. 

The burger was very good. It was simple but very flavorful. Prepared perfectly, the juicy beef tasted like beef. The bun had great texture and flavor. Come hungry because this is a massive main. The fries and sweet pickles that accompanied were excellent! So good that I would return just for fries if I could. Wow!

The service was trying hard but very disorganized. Their pace was random and we experience both moments of double coverage then long periods of drought. Given the layout, you could be entertained (or unnerved) by the confusion that plays out over the whirlwind that surrounds the staging area for final plating. We saw chaos at peak!

The bar is good. They are not stocked with what I would consider a full selection but the French 75 was an excellent cocktail. They have no beer on tap. 

~m
Small cozy place. Excellent healthy food. Tried several dishes, one better than the other. Very affordable with excellent street view. Highly recommended!

Prices waaaay to high for what you receive. 3 people in our party, 2 meals brought out together, 3rd person had to wait 10 mins for their plate. Not sure what happened. Older crowd atmosphere. Food wasn't bad, but just not worth the $$$. Would have to drop their prices by half for me to go back.
I feel like the last 2 months they have been going downhill between the poor service dining in and with takeout their dishes have become more watery and not as flavorful.

If somebody told me they were taking me to eat at a buffet that served everything from sushi, gyoza dumplings, and beef teriyaki to mashed potatoes, pizza, and mac & cheese, I would shudder. My first assumption would be that anybody who did this much stuff couldn't possibly do any of it well. The Journey actually surprised me with having decent quality items for the selection that they offer.

The place is certainly popular, and there will often be packed for lunch, at least on the weekends. One of the big draws for the lunch crowd is that their prices are surprisingly cheap at $8.50 for lunch during the week and $10.99 on the weekend. It gets more expensive for dinner, but the entirety of my few visits here have come on a Saturday or Sunday for lunch. Their parking lot is just barely big enough to hold all of the cars that it needs to when they are full, but since people who tend to go to all you can eat buffets also don't tend to want to walk very far to get there, it can get pretty crowded with people getting dropped off at the front or waiting to see where the family walking out the door is going to be pulling out from. 

The inside is much nicer than I expected. They have a big place, and there are plenty of sections where they can hold large groups. I think there are even some semi-private areas if you were having a large party and wanted to reserve one of those. Once you are seated, a server will take drink orders (drinks cost extra) and you are free to go hit the buffet. They also have a small selection of beers, sake, and wine. The service here is hit and miss. It's never great, which is expected at a buffet, but sometimes it is really bad. 

They really do offer a little bit of everything when it comes to food. I usually stick to the sushi selection, which is very respectable. They have maybe 10 or so different rolls to choose from, along with some nigiri, sashimi, and even temaki. Outside of the sushi, I usually end up with some of the more popular Chinese food that we see over here, including fried rice, lo mein, beef teriyaki, general tso's chichen, and a crab Rangoon or two. They also have a lot of seafood offerings. For those not willing to branch out to even Americanized Asian dishes, you can get mashed potatoes, steak, ribs, mac & cheese, pasta, and a handful of other comfort items. The buffet is dominantly Asian though. They also have a salad bar, side items, and quite a bit of fruit. Their dessert selection is pretty expansive, and includes a big chocolate fountain that I have seen way too many kids (and adults) get their hands into to ever want to eat anything from that.

The place looks clean and there are never any items that look like they have been sitting in a warmer for way too long. A look at their health department inspections reveals a few concerns, but it's certainly less than what I expected to see for a giant buffet with a lot of seafood and raw items. 

This is a place I will make it to once or twice a year. It's worth a visit if you like going to all you can eat buffets and want something different from a Golden Corral type place, or something better than a Super China Buffet. Just go in with the proper expectations that you should have for any AYCE buffet and you will be pleased.

I tried the sushi out here last night because its super close to my house and I'm a sushi fiend! 

I gave it 4 stars for a few reasons. The food itself was ok. Most certainly not the best sushi I have ever had, but for a quick fix? It's worth it. The prices were fair and their delivery was lighting fast!

I would go there again to grab a roll or two!

Where do I begin?! My visit was perfection from start to finish. The atmosphere was pleasant and the staff was very friendly. I was surprised to find the place has 3 floors. As soon as we were seated I knew the service was going to be exceptional, I cannot say enough about the positive energy and friendly staff. They immediately brought us water and kept our glasses full. The menu immediately got my attention. There were several dishes that I wanted to try. Initially I was going to choose the tomato soup and grilled cheese sliders. It's been so cold and it sounded like the perfect comfort dish. I am glad that I didn't order this because while we were trying to decide the waiter brought us these amazing pumpkin, almond and orange biscotti with tomato soup in small tea cups. It was fantastic and the biscotti teased my tastebuds. My fiancé and to decided to split the short rib and marscapone Gluten free pizza and the fried goat Cheese salad. We live in nj and we don't have a many restaurants that offer gluten free options. So we were definitely impressed by the amount of gluten free and vegetarian dishes that the couch tomato offered. Both dishes arrived quickly and after the first bite I was already planning my next visit. The flavors were impeccable. The couch tomato used quality ingredients and they know how to run an establishment. One of the best dining experiences that I have had. I can't wait to return and try something new.

The food here is fantastic - in the bustle of Lower Broadway, this is an upscale-enough restaurant without feeling pretentious or out of place. We ate downstairs in the more relaxed/less expensive part of the restaurant but didn't experience any lower quality in the food than what I would have expected upstairs. The ambience was very cool. Only complaint is that our waiter was a little slow/not completely knowledgeable about the drinks, food, etc. I think she was new, so we cut her some slack. The wait was also long (we didn't have a reservation) but they have a system where you can leave your phone number and they text you when your table is ready. We grabbed a few drinks and listened to some of the great live music down the street and then hopped back in when it was time. I had the sweet tea pork loin, molasses green beans, and pimiento cheese grits... all of which were amazing!!! So glad we picked this restaurant above the louder, cheesier restaurants in the area.

With the passing of Korea House, may they rest in peace, the lady and I lost our favorite Korean restaurant in STL. She feared that she'd never come across a decent bibimbap ever again, while I was more concerned that I wouldn't find another restaurant that allowed me to dine under the soft glow of a K-Pop music video.

Kim Cheese isn't a replacement for Korea House, but then again, it isn't supposed to be. It's a Korean take on fast food, and it's delicious.

My Kim Cheese Burger was great. Nice soft bun, well seasoned rib-eye, melty cheese and the bite of kimchi. So good.

The lady's bibimbap was very good, for what it was. No, it wasn't in a hot stone pot, and it didn't come with a half-dozen ramekins of fun sides (fish cakes FTW), but it was a good bibimbap. That you can get from a drive through. Did I mention the drive through? Tell me where else I can get drive through bibimbap. No seriously, tell me and I'll go there too.

Service was great and super friendly. Prices were very reasonable. If there were in the city and I didn't have to drive 30 minutes to get there, I would probably visit once a week. Give them a shot, you won't be disappointed.

We had take-out. It was delicious. After our move from South Florida, we frankly have had issues finding GOOD food, especially ethnic. We have found our Thai food place and are happy, happy, happy!!  :)

My husband and I went here for dinner earlier tonight, and unfortunately we will probably not be returning.

Hubby ordered a bacon cheeseburger cooked medium-rare, but what came out was raw (cold and blood red throughout). We brought it to the attention of our waitress, who said, "so you want it cooked medium?" I told her no - it was ordered medium-rare, which this burger obviously wasn't (it's not that my husband changed his mind about the way he wanted it cooked, but that he was given a raw burger!). 

She took it away to have another burger properly made, however she then brought out a completely different burger (not a bacon cheeseburger, but something with onions and mushrooms). We told her it was not even the correct burger, and she took it away again. By this time, I was done with my dinner (smothered chicken, which was pretty good - the main reason I am giving 2 stars instead of 1) and my husband had yet to eat. 

When they finally did bring out the correct burger, it was still not medium-rare, but well-done (no pink at all), but at least it was edible, although definitely not worth the wait. They told us they would comp his beer for the trouble, but then when the check came it was for only $5 - instead of taking off the beer, they took off everything except the beer! We probably could have just paid the $5 and left, but we (again!) hailed our waitress to point out the obvious mistake, which she corrected and then brought out the correct check (about $24 for just the burger and chicken).

Any 1 of these mistakes would have been easily disregarded, but all of them put together in a single experience made for a very unpleasant dinner that was not worth the wait, money, or drive (we live about 45 minutes away). Too bad, as we have been to other Texas Roadhouse locations that were phenomenal.

I was introduced to the Hoagie Factory by a good friend. He took me there during the West Oak Lane festival. 

Their cheesesteak was okay, however their chicken cheesesteak was delicious. After the first bite my hoagie mystery disappeared. 

They had a variety of drinks in the glass refrigerators that are located right behind the counter where you place your order. I had not seen a Tahitian Treat (fruit punch soda) in my moons and had that along with my hoagie.

We had one to dine in. Their seating area is located in the back of the restaurant. You would not know they had a seating area if they did not ask you if the order was for here or to go.

I took the other one for the road. 

The service is great. They were very friendly, which shocked me, because being an avid food channel watcher they always say folks at the hoagie places are rude. Definitely not the case here.

When in Philly I recommend you giving them a try.

Rule of thumb: if you enter this restaurant and there's more than 4 customers in line,  turn around and go elsewhere. If you stay, you'll regret it. They'll mess up your order, delay it because they're out of something by a half hour, or forget about you altogether. I was 1/2 hour late getting back to work because they were out of wings.
 God help you if you ask for your order "To Go", at the drive through or counter. You will for sure discover that they gave you someone else's order when you arrive home.
 Too bad I can only give one star, because the food really is pretty good most of the time- they just need to  fix some of  the basics. After all, it's not the first week they've been open.

I stopped in for a quick lunch - and let's just say, I can really get behind a place like this! Clean, fun decor, SCRUMPTIOUS, eye-catching display of pizzas! 

I opted for the buffalo chicken and a slice of supreme! Wowww! The buffalo chicken was loaded with chicken and zesty buffalo sauce! The crust was light and airy and tasty so fresh and just delish as hell! The pizza was perfectly flavored and I really enjoyed it! The supreme pizza had fressssh lush veggies and was just super hella yum! The sauce was perfect - not too much, nor too little!

I would recommend this place to others! You will be pleased and your taste buds will do flips!!
This pizza place  is amazing!!  I had the formaggia calzone and it was the best I've had!!  Everyone here is very friendly and fast service !!!  Highly recommend

Pretty good Delmonico steak with great service!  The sear on the steak was very well done and was the highlight.  Was seated quickly, and everything came out quickly.  

Not much to complain about, but do not order the bone marrow with your steak.  It was partially cold which was quite heebie jeebie inducing- fixing this would have made for a 5 star meal.

The break was good- the cracker and the butter were the highlight.

Also, their rare tuna was pretty good.  The sauce that came with it didn't fit, but it was cooked well.

Plenty of parking nearby, always a plus.

Something to note is that their dry aged steak doesn't taste aged; I prefer my dry aged meat to taste different, so this was a but of a disappointment for me.  The dry aging done at Del Friscos is far superior in this regard.

Finally, their creme brulee was the best i've had in the main line area.

Worth your time, but not as good as downtown steakhouses unfortunately.

the place looks really nice, tidy and modern look for a diners.
I like the color combination of the interior and the costumer service is great and waiting for the food is about 10mins which is good and they serve all day breakfast but I didn't see any pork in their menu, I think this is halal restaurant. 
I only order two food in their menu but all I can say about the western omelet is like tease of home made breakfast there's nothing else maybe I was expecting more about the omelet I hope they'll find new texture or flavour on the omelet but the blue berry pancakes there not too sweet which is really good not over power the honey or sweetness and you can still tease the blueberry!! you have to try it! I recommended!!
overall four star!
Im Greek so I really appreciate the authenticity of the food. The owner/staff are very polite and attentive. We will be coming back again.

I could not imagine a better first omakasè experience. The chef was very friendly and extremely passionate about his craft. The nigiri itself was pure bliss, melting in your mouth with a chorus of perfectly calibrated flavors. The only downside was the fleeting nature of the experience as each delicately sculpted piece faded in to oblivion on your taste buds. Thanks so much for a great meal, Sakana!

Okay fellow travelers and foodies! Just wanted to jot this down for y'all and to give you a good tip if you get to the Big Easy! (See I put in "y'all" in that last sentence and it's in relation to New Orleans or "Nawlins" as the locals say) So, if you would like some good, cheap, and tasty Nawlins cooking, then this is the place for you.

I had the Red Beans and Rice with Fried Catfish and it was a big bowl with a generous slice of battered fried catfish. I also had a nice glass of Arnold Palmer with it and, let me tell you, it was soooooo delicious. The catfish was flaky and tasty that I savored each bite I took. The red beans and rice was the perfect balance to the catfish that I almost hated to put my fork down to take a drink of my Arnold Palmer.

The restaurant is tucked away off the North Causeway which is right off the I-10 and if you didn't know that it used to be an old Denny's restaurant, then you might miss it because it looks attached to the La Quinta Inn. However, the restaurant will fool you the delicacies laying in wait for the unsuspecting traveler. I would highly recommend this stop. Additionally, the waitress will definitely make you feel at home with her southern charm.

Ah, the Brunchery. I love this place. I mean it, I do! Whenever we come here, there is just so much to choose from but the friendly staff is patient enough to wait while my picky little mind makes a choice from the vast menu!
The Food: delish! I LOVE the stuffed french toast and there are always specials that I like to try up on their chalkboard. The best I had recently was their lobster omlette...and it was around $10! Not too shappy!
The Atmosphere: down home and laid back, just the way I like it
The staff: Super friendly, very homey feel to everyone
This place is great for brunch or breakfast and they have so many items to choose from like biscuits and gravy to pancakes (yum, banana nut) to muffins to omlettes to breakfast specials to sandwiches!

I went here a few days ago and noticed some things. One was the reason why whenever I call no one answers. It's not because the store is closed, it's because there's only 2 employees in there. They just looked at the phone or look around in general as if they don't know what's ringing. I guess they no longer have a driver and will be damned if they answer for a potential delivery. The second thing I noticed is that they don't want you to dine in either. All the chairs that use to be up by the table are behind the counter being jacket and bag holders. Yes you have to stand and wait for your order you can't call and pick up because they won't pick up. There were two teenagers in there running the place. One guy left because he wasn't going to wait for the girl to come back to the register. Customer service is way down. I asked for mild wings and got buffalo wings. Luckily I ordered pizza too because my daughter couldn't eat the wings.

Closest to the real deal in Chicago. Awesome burgers and  salads and their subs are amazing. Cheese fries remind me of White Castle cheese fries which me coming from up north miss very much so I'm pleased. Try this place you won't be disappointed

Yikes. This location was terrible. I ordered the beef tacquitos, and the corn tortilla was still raw and falling apart inside. 

The location itself has the same decor of other Nico's locations, though it was a tad dirtier and smaller. The prices are on the lower side, but I definitely wouldn't recommend this place to anyone else.

I love the food but the service isn't the best. The young gentleman in the window is always nice but the young girl was extremely rude... I pulled up to the window and the manager read my order and I told him there was 2 items not one. The girl who took my order proceeds to roll her eyes and puts the second order in. Then the young man took the window who was very nice and she's behind him with attitude rolling her eyes on her phone. I'm a manager of a restaurant and if I had someone with that kind of customer service I would not have them sent home... And my meat had a big bone in it!! I love the food but I'm just not sure what's going on today!
Simply put, the food is excellent! They had nice jazz music playing in background. Pleasant place to eat in and it's BYOB. Highly recommend

I am absolutely DIGUSTED. I stopped in today to pick up a doordash order. As soon as I walked in, I noticed it was very humid inside of the restaurant. I waited to be greeted by a host/hostess for a few minutes. The restaurant was completely empty. No employees or customers in sight. There were Styrofoam plates scattered across each table filled to the top with a mixture of potato chips and tortilla chips? Very very weird. I was greeted by a waitress who was NOT wearing a mask. She walked right on up to me and stood about 2 ft from me. I had to back up because I was very uncomfortable. She told me the food would be out in a few minutes. 5 minutes later, an elderly couple walked into the restaurant with NO MASKS ON. They were greeted with a hug by the waitress. I just stood there in shock and GET THIS. The elderly lady walked up to a random plate of chips and started eating off of it. They didn't even end up sitting at that table either. The man kept walking in and out of the restaurant and didn't seem to think twice about Covid or about other's personal space. I just shook my head in disbelief. About another 5 minutes passed and another elderly couple walked in with NO MASKS. I mean did they put out an advertisement that said, "If you don't wear a mask, you eat free." God knows they couldn't afford  it since the restaurant is empty most of the time. Just sickening to me. I was in disbelief at how these people didn't seem to care at all about the pandemic and didn't seem to think wearing a mask was appropriate at all. They didn't even think that they could possibly be putting another life at risk because of their own selfishness. The chef walked out 15 minutes later with my DoorDash order and he wasn't wearing a mask either. THE CHEF. People, if you want to risk contamination, then order food from this place. I will not be back here again.. for DoorDash or to eat. This place is disgusting...

Great atmosphere and delicious food! The sangria was good! Our waitress told us that you could split one of the steaks and it was such a great suggestion! The steak and potatoes were both so good! The service was a little slow, but I'll definitely be back to try other items on the menu.
Food comes out fast and they have really good prices. I always ask for their Potsticker steamed instead of fried.

If I could give zero stars for my order I would because it's been over an hour and twenty minutes for my "freaky fast" food that's literally located right down the street from my apartment. Not to mention it's been "on its way" for at least an hour. I've called the store twice neither time was I offered a discount or anything to make the situation any better. Apparently customer service isn't a big deal to them. I will no longer be ordering from this location.
What a great change from the usual takeout of pizza, cheesesteaks, etc. We got the hummus, falafel, tabbouleh, chicken and beef kofta platters. We will definitely be back!

Little Caesar's is better. By far... the worst pizza experience I've ever had. The pizza was falling apart. It's like they pre cook the dough and when orders come in they just broil the toppings instead of cooking it into the pizza. The pizza was ready within 5m of me placing the order and I was suspicious, but whatever. So I went to pick up the pizza, only to find this. Pls view photo evidence.  slice, the app, had 4 stars for them... little did I know Yelp had given a 1.5 star rating
Awful all around. From the customer service, to the delivery, to the food. Simple as that.

Visiting Philly, looking for great sushi, tried Fat Salmon based on reviews. Solid choice. Great value. (If they charged more I would lower rating.)

They have a lot of house specials. So if you are looking for something new and different, this is a good place to experiment. 

The fish was fresh and good. Solid place. 
Service was a little slow and very helpful/cheery. 

The spicy edamame was addictive. Little messy to eat, and addictive.  Sweet and spicy sauce.

For fun: they  presented my son's order in a boat. His first one. He was so excited. (Thx u!).

So I said finally I will try Popeyes and this magical sandwich they want to talk so heavenly about. But here I am 15 minutes and still in the drive through line with this one car sitting at the window for about 7 minutes now. How do you not pull any one up. How do you have a line taking this long. This is the reason of not wanting to try Popeyes because of all the complaints about service. Now three cars including myself just pulled out of drive through even though we have ordered already. I would steer clear and go to anything but popeyes. You want a good chicken sandwich with great service then go Chik fil lay.
Biscuits where soft and gravy was amazing. My only thing was the chicken was a bit spicy for our taste, but it was cooked nicely. So next time I will get something else next time.

The sushi is beyond excellent! The variety are good too. The rolls were melt in your mouth yummy:) I ate way more than I should. I was so fully that I had to go home and take a  nap afterwards. The place is very modern upscale. The staff was great, seated us fast, checked on us often, and brought our drinks to the table right away. 
My only small issue was the parking. was less than ideal but okay for good food and good service I will have to accept that.

They gave us stale food and food was really smelly. We had been to numerous Mediterranean restaurants but never tasted stale fo. When asked they told that this is a normal smell but it was disgusting and we just left. I am not going back to this place ever.

We visited this Vietnamese restaurant for a couple time now. The first time we walked in here, an older Asian waiter was by the door, we told him we needed a table for two. He grabbed some menu from the counter, then walked away without saying anything to us. We thought that was kind of rude. We ordered a couple bowls of noodle, thought they were okay. A lady came by while we were having our noodles to check if things were fine, she was nice. 

We came back again for lunch today. The same older Asian guy was by the door, I told him how many people we had. He grabbed the menu, walked away from us. So we followed him assuming that he was going to take us to our table. But no! He took us to walk one round of the restaurant then walked into the kitchen without saying anything to us. That's beyond rude!!! Then a younger waiter saw us and seated us instead. But the whole experience has ruined our appetite. The noodle my husband ordered was undercooked and broth was watery and flavorless, and the beef in my noodle was fatty and grisly, I didn't eat any of them. 

As long as that rude waiter is working there, we will not ever step foot into this restaurant again. I will give my business to the next door Taiwanese restaurant which has much better food and definitely better service.
Absolutely INCREDIBLE spicy ramen and boba, and they're super friendly and accommodating! They've got a permanent customer.

I will start by saying that we use to love the Cheesecake Factory. The quality and service has taken a serious nose dive. We went here for lunch today. My wife got a salad that came with no dressing. She asked for salad dressing and the server said with an irritated voice "you want MORE dressing?!" She told him there was none at all, so yes. I had the chicken avocado bacon club. Please see my picture. It was pretty much all tomato. It was suppose to have an herbed mayo but was completely dry. The fries were cold, soft and overly salted. The iced tea was so sweet it was not consumable. I asked the waiter to add some not sweet to my cup so I could drink it and instead he just drops a cup of unsweet at the table and walks off. At the end he was pushing cheesecake. He could see we hardly ate a bite of out $45 lunch and said nothing except to ask me 3 times why I didn't want cheesecake. Will never go back again.

Did Somewhat tired after a long day I found Dan Dan close to the hotel. Got some steps in by waking. 

Chose to sit at the bar and watch the hockey / basketball games. 


I ate the dumplings which were a bit to carby for me and I didn't taste much filling. So I'd pass on them if I went back and left 1/2 of them. Maybe a good item to share amongst a group.

My main was the scallions with chicken. It was quite good and hit the spot. Wasn't spicy but the menu offered a good range of spice levels. 

I had a old fashioned and it was ok but not a signature drink. I like the cherry and old fashioned fixin's so i guess I'm spoiled. Got to be careful. 

Service was good and the server gave some solid suggestions as opposed to those times when your server claims everything is good without listening to what you're telling them you like / feel in the mood for. Good job!

On the would I go back I'd say yes there so more stuff I'd like to try out.

What a great hidden gem! Came here for brunch and tried the Hillsborough Hash. Great portion for the price! The occasional hint of sweet from the fried plantains and green pepper combined with the salty fatty goodness of the tallow home fries made for a perfect combination. They don't serve just normal drip coffee, so I had an Americano instead. Very good, and locally roasted!

Connected to the restaurant is an amazing butcher shop. Bring your chefy friend here and they'll be like a kid in a candy shop. Real bone broth/stock, tallow butter, fresh cuts and sausage/jerky. I'll be back just for the sausage alone.

Yesterday I came here for lunch. Normally I wouldn't go for a 2.5 rated restaurant but here we are. This food is comparable, if not worse than Applebee's. I got a pulled pork sandwich and tater tots and honestly it was a sad excuse for a lunch. Moving on, my drink was always full and the service from Manuela was impeccable from beginning to end. This place needs to do better food wise, I'd only come back for the service if I ever do come back. Probably won't, there are too many good BBQ joints in St Pete doing way better than this one.

We came for lunch on a Friday (just after opening) and the place was filling up. Between the two of us, we split a Margherita Pizza and a Small Squick Ink Tagliolini pasta. 

The pizza was good - thin crust, very flavorful. However, it was a bit watery, not sure if it was from the tomatoes or what.

I have been craving a good squid ink pasta since literally Dec 2019 and this was a good satisfaction to the craving. There were bites of blue crab and the herbs were pretty strong. I can't place the herb that was the strongest.. dill? Fennel? But it was a classic squid ink pasta taste that was enjoyable.
Fantastic food and service would highly recommend, nice atmosphere with an Italian flair.

Well, they finally opened back up. Of course it's to go only but I think they have delivery coming soon. They told me they plan on doing big slices soon so that people can walk and have a slice. I got the Italian sandwich and it was very good I love their dough and can't wait to try a whole pizza with a friend.

Great continental breakfast. Right by the water, and you're able to sit smack by the river walk. Food was plentiful and huge variety of eats :) I chose to get a veggie omelet, fruit, and oatmeal (with greek yogurt). Our server was on point with the water and freshly squeezed juices!

Atmosphere is really chic and yet approachable. Staff is super accommodating and friendly. Food is delicious. Stopped in after a soccer game with 6 players! There was something for everyone! We are in the bar area and Stephanie was gracious and sweet. Def would return. Thx!!!

I wanted to really like Mamma Mias because we're Crieve Hall-ers, so this is one of the closest restaurants to us. But I was underwhelmed. The service was slow, but the servers were very friendly. They're BYO and have a corkage fee, which was nice. The food was meh. We started with the bruschetta, which was fine. My husband tried the gnocchi. The texture of the gnocchi was actually very good; I just wasn't crazy about the sauce. I figured my pasta all vodka was a pretty safe choice for neighborhood Italian-American spot, but it was a pretty underwhelming version of the dish (the sauce was really bland - a jar of vodka sauce from the store would have been better). Also, the menu said that the dish had pancetta in it. But this didn't taste like any pancetta I've ever had - it was the texture of a vegan meat replacement and was cut into very long strong strips. I ate around it. The prices were good, and the portions were enormous (I think my pasta was around $17, but it came with a salad as well and it was about 3x as much pasta as I could eat in a single serving, so you'll have leftovers for sure). My favorite thing about the restaurant was the decor. It really felt like an old school neighborhood red sauce restaurant. Charming interior. But I think one visit was good for me.
Fast and friendly service and big delicious helpings of food. Fruit is always beautiful and fresh and the hollandaise sauce is fantastic. Definitely our go to place for breakfast!

Last weekend we stopped by to see if the patio was open for dinner, we got a perfect table right next to the fire pit. Our server was great, very attentive and friendly and our first meal here was excellent. We sampled the crab cake to start, then shared the filet and pork chop. We splurged on the apple crisp for dessert, which was warm and super delicious! We rarely go out and it was our first time eating out in 6 months, so worth it.
Incredibly okay. Despite the good view and location, the food had no taste. While the customer service was great, I can't imagine why people would go here apart from the waterfront view.

Got here just in time for happy hour on a bustling Saturday evening. While we waited for a table inside we enjoyed happy hour outdoors under covered heated seating. Great prices & some warmth, really can't go wrong. We waited no more than 20 minutes and our table was ready. Inside tables were spread out and limited to a certain amount of people there were also dividers around the raw bar. I felt totally safe dining here and they did temp check us at the door.  Now on to the food- superb. The dishes are really thoughtful, beautiful & fresh. 

After having one dollar oysters and beers during happy hour we started with some grilled oysters, seafood salad and lobster bisque for dinner. The lobster bisque was creamy rich and delicious. It had nice chunks of tender lobster in it. The seafood salad was a pleasant surprise being a special of the day, we didn't really know what to expect. The dressing was very light and citrusy while  being a tad bit creamy pairing very nicely with the fresh shrimp mussels and calamari. Another showstopper was the grilled oysters! They somehow get the Parmesan to remain in a liquid state melting together with the butter and oyster. Might need two orders of those! Also loved the sea bass and the fried seafood platter.Everything we ate was delicious.

I don't know what I though coffee places open before 6am, I guess because my Starbucks (that I HATE) opened at 5:30. Anywho, they open at 6am and I was there two minutes til on my way to work. Doors opened at 6 on the dot and I ordered my large cafe latte. 

Fast and efficient plus my espresso fresh beans and strong like I love it. I'll be back because it's the most time efficient stop on my way to work. The pastry selection was lacking big time but maybe because I was so early?

By far one the best breakfast joints in town. The staff is extremely friendly and they make you feel as if you were apart of the family. The French toast made to perfection and the eggs were just as good. I recommend this place for locals or visitors that are looking for a warm  place to eat, definitely go here.

I was really excited about ordering a pizza and watching a football game.  Instead, I ordered a piece of cardboard with some cheese on it.  It was really bad.  I should have ordered crappy pizza hut or some thing.  Very disappointing.  I would never order this again.
My first time here an the bartender Steven was great, so informative...  Helped us put together a great meal an drinks can't wait to get back.

Tino's, you majorly redeemed yourself with your Greek pizza.

On my first visit to Tino's, I wasn't in a pizza mood, so I ordered their eggplant parmesan sandwich. But what I was served didn't look to have eggplant on it - it looked more like fish or glue. It was inedible. Literally, I ate the bread. 

Last night though, we had the Greek pizza and I could NOT get enough. It was incredible. It's got sliced tomato, black olives, artichoke, pepperocini peppers, garlic, and feta. The crust is light, chewy, and crispy. YUMMM.

My tip: Stick to the pizza.
This place has an amazing pizza not only it's at a great price but also so good And customer service is

Ridiculous! That's the only word I can use to describe my recent trip there. I will say I do love the salad bar and crab salad. Every time I go there they are always out of almost everything or maybe just the food I like. Today I had 3 separate gentlemen ignore me while I was trying to ask them for soft taco shells. After standing there I finally got a womans attention and asked her. She spoke with one of the gentleman that was ignoring me and was told it will be about 5 minutes. Mind you I had already been standing there for about 5 minutes. I waited 20 minutes before I finally gave up. During that time our server also let the manager know. I figured I could at least get some cotton candy but like everything else it was out. I will never again go to this location again!
This place is delicious, I haven't been vegan for very long but this place deffinetly gives me hope for healthy eating, highly recommended.
Average food, tourist place. Waited a long time for food. Good fries. Just by the beach.
Ijji is a really good spot to satisfy your Korean bbq, they have good meat and appetizers, the kimchi rice is really good. Their brisket and Hawaiian steak are the best option always!!
Highlight of my weekend! Everything we tasted was excellent. We went with the 4 meats 4 sides and 2 drinks the sweet tea was amazing.

general Tso chicken had good sauce the chicken fried rice was funny looking and stringy.fried rice is not like yellow fried rice in other chinese places its like burnt white rice.yuck! Singapore mei fun thin rice noodles with pork, chicken and shrimp had nothing but stringy looking flimsy chicken shapes, no pork or shrimp and none all the food lived up to the restaurant name, not spicy at all and I asked spicy.also they dont give extra vegetables at all when you asked.they seem cheap.GIVE CUSTOMERS MORE VEGGIES AND WORK ON FRIED RICE AND MAKE YELLOW LIKE PEOPLE ARE USED TO FROM OTHER CHINA RESTAURANTS HERE IN USA AND MAKE FOOD SPICY OR BUSINESS WILL LOSE MONEY.I READ NEGATIVE REVIEWS BEFORE ORDERING BUT THEY WAS RIGHT IN EVERY WAY

Flat out not a good experience. I had never been to this fairly new location of Five Guys. When I walked in the cashier was being nice and greeting me but I couldn't hear a word she said because every other employee behind her was talking very loudly and laughing and there was an echo inside. I should have left then. I ordered a Little Cheeseburger with pickle and grilled onions and a small order of fries. It's about $12 for this and I think I had water to drink. After about 10 minutes the food was ready and I got my ketchup etc. It was horrible. None of the food had any flavor. I don't think the French fries even had a shake of salt.  The only thing I really tasted was the ketchup I put on myself. I ate about half and tossed the rest in the trash on my way out. The table next to me was dirty the entire 30 minutes I was in there. Not good and probably won't be going back to any of their locations.

Went here for my husbands graduation dinner and man we're we satisfied. Of course we ordered everything that we wanted to try and received it on 2 boats despite the stares we got because it was so much for 2 people but please believe me we arrived here with empty stomachs and left satisfied and full.
The waitress was very polite and checked in periodically to make sure we were okay. The atmosphere is amazing despite the covid precautions that are in place. Definitely would recommend this place if your looking for a appetizing sushi experience the only thing is make sure you make a reservation.

I was not expecting to find Chicago Deep Dish pizza in the Tampa area. I was not expecting it to be amazing! 
The sauce, made from actual tomatoes and not a "paste". 
The crust does not even need the toppings. It is like some amazing bread stick that they decided to turn into a deep dish. 

The employees were polite, energetic, and welcoming. The restaurant was clean and had a good ambience. 

Bring a fork and an appetite. 

Cannot wait to explore the rest of their menu.

I absolutely love this place! The Naked Gyoza appetizer is so unique. They're basically like mini japanese tostadas with a crunchy chip on the bottom and covered in cooling sour cream and pico de gallo. We've gotten the 

My favorite roll is the Rolling Fire featured roll which is phenomenal the Broiled Spicy Scallop sauce on top is so flavorful. I haven't found a roll like this anywhere else. 

To top it all off, I've had the green tea mochi and the green tea fried ice cream. Both of which were great!

Lunch was great at this little franchise sandwich shop. I give 4 stars bc they were out of pastrami... and another meat. Also it was frustrating to have to set up to find the napkins and utensils... hidden in the corner. I would thin  in they would hand you a couple napkins as you pick up your meal. Meatball sandwich was delicious.

We went for breakfast and dinner.  My husband had the Eggs Benedict and I shared with him.  It was really good.  The sauce was delightful.  For dinner, he had a hot pastrami with fries.  I had the blt with greens.  Lots of bacon and it was greats. Definitely a solid choice!  Also, $6 pitchers so great to hit when you are on the strip!

This is really a review of the bar.  The restaurant looked lovely, but I can't say anything about the food or table service, since I didn't experience it.

Shortly before the pandemic hit, I went on a bar crawl with my wife.
A friend who is one of America's foremost Cocktail Wonks had recommended Couvant on the basis that it had a new cocktail menu created by a supposed genius bartender.

I was not misled.

Brian Maxwell is a genius.  I hope after the world returns to a semblance of normalcy, that Brian is shaking drinks somewhere in New Orleans, because if he's not, I'll have to be road-tripping to whatever bar in whatever city is the place that Brian has landed.

Meantime, I found his website - google 'shaker of spirits' - you'll be glad you did.

I go to the TJ's in this complex at least once a week. When I saw the sign that "Cup it Up - An American Grill" was going in my curiosity was piqued. Surely the "Cup" in the name meant it would be a coffee place. But the "Grill" in the name suggested to my taste buds that it may be a (Yippee!) new burger joint.

I raced to the website only to see "coming soon". When the website went live and I saw the concept I went, "Huh?". Didn't seem like a place I would like.

I went the first time with a group of co-workers that had formed a football pool. The winner got to choose the spot and they chose this place. Some in the group groaned about the choice... including me. OK... I was wrong.

You can choose one of their pre-designed bowls or build your own from:

Grains - white or brown rice, quinoa or barley topped with...
Meat - tender, flavorful short ribs, grilled, citrus herb or blackened chicken, grilled shrimp, Portobella mushroom or  pulled pork.
Greens and Veggies  - (Grilled or Chilled) Romaine, Spinach or Kale; onions, peppers, mushrooms or tomatoes 

You can then top your Frankencup from a list of "superfoods" with nuts and everything from avocado to edamame. Beware though, I asked for almonds on my bowl and they told me it would be an extra $1.50. Yikes! Add your choice of dressing or CIU sauce made from the short rib braising juices.

Go with the mac and cheese if you're not feeling the rest of the menu.

This is where you either let the guilt factor get a hold of you or not. There are healthy options galore. You can go with those... or opt for good ol' comforting mac and cheese. Willpower pays off later; weakness pays off now.
Great restaurant.  Pick the dinner with the flamenco show.  Worth the wait and watch.  Food great !!  Sangria is good!

I meant to give Cochon 4.5/5 Stars... this place is simply the BEST. A friend in New Orleans recommended it & I salute the chef. Very adventurous & interesting menu, i wish i can try all of them in 1 seating but alas, even a pig needs a break...

We started with fried alligator & fried rabbit livers, OMG they were unbelievable, the flavors were like explosion in my mouth, just remind me of the food back home in Malaysia, full of flavors & spices... as for entree, of course i tried their specialty, the cochon, which was ok coz the appetizers were so good & domineering in terms of taste, they pretty much made the evening meals special already...

Very contemporary interior, love the bar stools, great ambiance & crowds. Services were immaculate too, so, overall this place is TOP on my list for Best US Restaurant (besides da Marco) so far. However, as you will see, i dont normally give out 5 stars easily... so, maybe some day!

I am impressed at the interest in dietary issues of the customers. They have gluten free options for the crust. I do not have a need for any dietary concerns, but it is nice for those who do.

Parking is in front, and is the diagonal parking. It can be difficult to leave, because even if you pull in and you think you will have a clear shot leaving, you might not, depending on the size of the vehicles around you as your trying to back out. Keep that in mind when your coming in as Metairie Road is quite busy at times. You walk in, there is a sign to seat yourself for table service. On the day the St Patricks parade was going by, they had limited options, and you ordered at the counter for your food, and they were cash only for that day. 

The interior could use a deep cleaning, as could the restrooms. The restrooms are single, so I imagine that they could be gender neutral, but they are not specifically designated as such. There are gambling machines in the corner, walled off. During the evenings, it can get packed with families and kiddos sports teams celebrating or just walking it off and eating.

The pizza itself is good, the crust is not greasy, it is thick (how I like it) and not soggy from what I have experienced. I came recently as a reminder as it had been awhile since I have come. I have never not had a good food experience. I would come back for the food, I just hope they give the interior a good cleaning.

The menu is very misleading, I thought. 
We ordered the shrimp appetizer, which said it was fried shrimp with a Thai chilli sauce and it came out covered in a mayo based sauce. 
For dinner I ordered the smoked salmon salad with a citrus vinaigrette and the salmon was hard and cold, and the dressing was thick and didn't taste citrusy at all. I ended up sending it back. 

The mac and cheese was just okay too! 
Nothing exceptional,  nothing special. The server was nice.
The manager took the time to respond to my review--great follow up. It's so nice, as we're locals, to see how restaurants care about the community. Thank you for improving the experience!

I was a little worried when I showed up to see it was empty at lunch time. Turns out I was just lucky, I have never been here and was in a hurry, without a crowd I was in and out. The food was certainly not the reason the place was not crowded, it was fantastic. I had the lunch special and it was very generously sized and really tasty. I also had a 2 piece salmon, which I order at every Japanese restaurant I go to and it was very fresh and also pretty large. I was impressed overall, of you are around for lunch and feeling Japanese food, go here.

I had a good experience here. The last two times I have ordered the food has been good. Service was good, delivery drivers were polite and pretty fast. I can say I was impressed since it is dominoes. The one in High Ridge wasn't that great, so I expected the same here.

It's by convention center. I had Louisiana cochon (Very tender and juicy), wood-fired oyster (awesome!!!), Mac and cheese (good, I ordered it because that's what yelp reviewers said but I don't know, if you want to try, try it it's only $6), Pork Cheek (Good but kinda salty, I would order louisiana cochon over this), Alligator (it's chewy, I liked the aioli sauce, Should try!) If you sit at a bar (the one in front of kitchen), you should get chef's special thingee that has pickled tomato and some kind of ham.

I was pretty excited to try this place out after reading all the positive reviews, and ended up coming here on a weekday night with a friend. The decor is nothing special of course--the restaurant is basically a classic metal-clad diner with booths and a few tables inside. I got a meal of lamb chops, naan, salad and chickpeas, and also a soda--although the cashier was pretty confusing in terms of telling me which choices I was limited to, so I took something that looked small and inexpensive. The food took some time to be ready--the restaurant was hardly full, yet I waited probably 20 minutes or so. Everything is served in styrofoam containers, and all utensils/napkins/water is self-serve.

Of course none of this matters when you're at a divey place like Kabobeesh, so I was excited to finally try out the food. The lamb chops were just OK--a little bit too salty and greasy for my tastes, and just not enough meat. The beans weren't too bad, and the naan was good, very fresh and crispy. $12 is pretty reasonable for lamb chops IMO, but I was definitely expecting larger portion sizes as I finished my meal pretty quickly. Would be willing to try again sometime in the future and get something else on the menu, however.

For lunch today I had Grumpy's Sloppy Thanksgiving Special. It was great. The filling was delicious, turkey was tender, the roll smothered in gray made it live up to its name.  His soups are great. Breakfast sandwiches are delicious. Can't say enough about the place except you have to try it.

Love this place! Family owned delicious BBQ. Lovely southern style porch seating, but it was too cold to sit outside on my recent visit.

I went for the first time back in 2004 and it was a favorite. I recently went back and still super great friendly service and awesome food!

The BBQ ribs are yummy, pulled pork- great! Fantastic sides, I got potatoes and green beans. 

My favorite thing about Kojak's is the homemade chocolate cake for dessert. 

Kojaks is a Tampa tradition for my dad and I.
The food and service at E Hur Wei are excellent. Just posted a photo if my Kung Pao chicken. It's killer!
Barbecue platter was too "smokey" for my taste but, overall, the meats were pretty tender. Sides were a good consolation prize.

This is the very best I've ever had! 
The chai, fab! The korma, divine!
The service,  over the moon!
Wish I could take the place home to CT!

Have been a loyal patron of Sang Kee Duck House for over 30 years (since i was a freshman in college).  I've move away since but this tops my list of the things I miss about Philly.  Ye, I can get roast duck anywhere these data but no one does it like Sang Kee...here's to a Philadelphia institution.
Best pizza around this neighborhood, friendly fast service and always clean.  Sandwiches and other menu items are great as well, this place is a definite regular on my list!!

A solid 3.5 really. I went on a Wednesday  I ordered the empanada sampler, beef was my favorite and the salsa that came with is tasty. I also order the sweetbreads, YUMMMMMM. It was seared and covered in a tangy vinagrette with pieces of chopped garlic and parsley. I wanted to order it again but I will wait until my next visit!
I've had better pizza.  The only exciting thing about this place was their $3, big as your arm, pizza slice.  Not bad.  It's still good and cheap.

I purposely didn't aim to expect much--after all its location in Vietnamese-starved Brandon and housed in an obviously former Pizza Hut building didn't bode too well.

GOOD ENOUGH PHO YA
Although pretty to look at (Contained all the typical ingredients with bright, crisp Veggies) my Meat Combo Pho was lacking... something. I don't want my Pho fake-ly flavorful but it definitely seemed like something was missing. But it was definitely not horrible and the nice warmth from the broth comforted me. Other dishes such as the Pork slices in the Noodle Bowl were also crisp with the fresh ingredients. Delicious and filling.

OKAY ENOUGH SERVICE
Over the course of our meal several different workers approached us in addition to our server. Although it was always helpful and a rather muted smile, the timing was always off. The menu could also be simplified a bit to focus more on what their best sellers are instead of trying to appeal to everybody--because it's doing the opposite.
I love this place the spicy ramen  is so yummy and the tea are delicious definitely would recommend this place

Rollers is probably the most quaint little Chestnut Hill breakfast location. Probably the only one where you can get mimosas as well. 

A plus because at the end of the day it's yummy and people have been friendly to us. The location is cute enough and I'm glad we have it. Kind of similar to my other reviews of Chestnut Hill breakfast locations, there really isn't one that is a clear front runner. I mean that to say that no breakfast spot really does anything too special. 

You can expect normal sized portions at a slightly expensive price. I had bacon and eggs and my wife had French toast. We each had coffee and the price after an 18% tip was about $24. 

The location is small, it's laid out something like a long table against the wall. Not a good place if you want an intimate breakfast. 

That's a fair assessment I think. It's nothing too special, but not at all bad.

My order is always correct. The staff is very personable and helpful. The prices are very competitive and the salad & breadsticks are amazing. Plus they deliver to a larger area than the other pizza places.

So disappointed.  First visit was amazing.  Tonight, the pizza and roasted cauliflower were delicious.  I then got the arugula salad with grilled chicken and goat cheese.  There was no goat cheese, and the chicken was overcooked and tasteless.  I asked for goat cheese and it was shredded and tasteless, not soft like you'd expect, AND they charged me 6 bucks more for less than an ounce of it! When i said something, i got nothing but attitude and "it's REALLY expensive cheese."  My boyfriend's pasta bolognese was a total chef boyardee beefaroni flashback.  I can't decide if I'm more upset about the food or the 'tude.  The 'tude, definitely.
Then i think about my friend with cancer and realize it just doesn't f-ing matter. 
dropping mike...

Three of us ate early dinner here a few nights ago. Megan is an excellent server. We sat in the little room on the left, just past the toilets. A bit too easy to hear conversation at adjacent tables, but the air conditioning was not intense in that room.

Split personality pizza was great. One half had shrimp with goat cheese, and the other half was loaded with mushrooms. Crust was best I've had in Tucson: crispy and chewy. See photo. 

Other two dishes were pepper loaded with chicken meatballs and smothered in tomato sauce, plus eggplant parmesan. Both were good, but not outstanding like the pizza.

This is the closest I have found to authentic Mexican food in Reno. It is not fancy, so it might not appeal to everyone, but if you are looking for the real deal, then this is your place. I would recommend the wet burrito, or the tacos. I have had almost everything on their menu at this point, and have not been disappointed. I might caution against the seafood only because Reno is in the desert, which makes seafood not perfectly fresh, but they do a great job on it considering that it has to come by truck from far away. Also, the prices are on the low side, so you can't really go wrong.
Great food and wonderful atmosphere! the Mushrooms and Peas in the lunch buffet were phenomenal, along with a couple of the chicken dishes. And all that buffet for only $10?! I'll definitely be back!

They pressure customers to give them a 5 star rating
  :( it's a sham. Good, over rated though. I arrived at 6:05 on a Wednesday and waited 2 hours 15 minutes. It was not magical medium chicken was good, Mac and cheese great, but there are several other great restaurants in walking distance.

Debby downer : (

The fajita tacos are just amazing, especially with some of their amazing salsa!  I have yet to find anything on the menu I didn't like and the staff is great.

I don't like their mobile credit card machines, but that doesn't distract from their food and service which is all top notch.

Revisited for lunch. It opened under new management with some American menu items but they still have the same great ethnic dishes. The eggplant appetizer is delicious! Great customer service! I hope they do a groupon or something to get people in the door.

They need help in the decor area. It feels really old and run down.

Every time you go, you find something different to love about it.   From the roasted onions and jalapenos to the multitude of condiments (each salsa is unique and all are delicious) to the incredible amount of food you can get.  

If it's your first time, the hot dogs are a must.

Caramelos and Carne Asada Burros are great as well.

But don't just stop there, explore, there is SO MUCH good food on the menu.

Or, buy a pound or two of meat and bring amazing tacos to share with your friends.

We went to this restaurant instead of the Taqueira El Bajio, down the street, to find a new place to eat since El Bajio is no longer customer oriented.  This is a new restaurant (since January 2012) - very good food, totally fresh chips and salsas (green and red), great attentive waitress.  Their menu has a great variety of all types of dishes.  I had the carnitas burrito and my husband had the chile verdo burrito (wet) - both were VERY tasty and fresh.  This is a sit-down restaurant with real tablecloths, generous amount of chips and salsas (at no charge), and real plates / silverware unlike El Bajio.  They open at 9:00 AM, 7 days a week and are located next to the new Fresh n Easy market.  Their prices for the food is very modest.

After visiting the Belle Meade Plantation we were hungry, soo we ducked into this strip mall and found Cheeseburger Charley's.  My hubs and I are from Orlando, and have not heard of this chain before, but I'd compare it to a smaller version of Fuddrucker's.

You can choose from a variety of burgers (Turkey, salmon, chicken, black bean, buffalo, and beef), several cheeses, and various breads...and then you top it off at their toppings bar.

The burgers were great, in fact my only complaint was that their onion rings although they were really tasty were SUPER greasy.  

Overall, a good choice for burgers.

Finally got over to Sampan.  Good to see another restaurant opening in that area.  Start out with cool cross woody architectural detail thing which I actually watched them doing while sitting at Vintage.  

Anyway, the layout is kind of cool with backlit purple walls on either side. They have some "outdoor" seating, which is like a lot of Philly outdoor seating, as in, it's you on the sidewalk while peoples' butts pass you by and dogs are restrained by their owners from eating your dinner.  Or if you are threading your way in Rittenhouse trying to get thru the RougeDevonParc, you may get bitten by a small dog in a purse, or accidentally step on someone's big glasses...  

Back to Sampan, we were seated quickly, super-friendly host people, and a nice laid back server.  For drinks, I had the Spicy Pomegranate Margarita, which was basically like a not too sweet margarita with a kick.  It was good.  They serve mostly "small plates" (don't call them tapas!) and "big plates," which are about 1 inch bigger than small plates, but $5 more expensive.  For food, rabbit dumplings (too salty), Korean BBQ satay (yummy), tuna tar tar (yummy, but weird presentation, like a big bowl with a pile of tuna and stuff at the bottom), and their version of Pad Thai ("big plate"), which was okay.

Bottom line, reasonably cool atmosphere, friendly staff, overpriced decent food (heavy on the salt - beware all you congestive heart failure people!) with small portions.  For the two of us, it was about $80 and we left hungry and really thirsty for water.

This place is such a dive, but so good!

A friend and I went to Zinas on a Sunday morning.  There wasn't a line, but all tables were full.  We did not have any expectations going in as this was our first experience here.  

I decided on the crepes with strawberries and my friend decided on crepes with blueberries with fetta cheese.  Both came with a side included.  Mmm good.  

Serivce was quick and attentive, except out food took about 30 minutes to bring out.

Combined, our breakfast came to $15 total.  I will be back!

So, I'm getting my wig trimmed, and the barber suggests a BYOB called Indian Restaurant.  I love me a good BYOB.

A couple days later, Cabernet in hand, I walk with a lovely lady, in the midst of a snow storm, to embark on a fine Indian adventure.  I enter and am greeted by a tall, slender manikin dressed in clothing you might find in India.  She was friendly, however, I was a bit confused.  Given the name of the place, I was expecting feathers, leather garb, long braids and a buffet of beef jerky, wild corn, pemmican, smoking tobacco, peyote and other famous Native American delights.

After getting my bearings and realizing that the restaurant served Indian food (like that found in India), although a bit caught off guard, I remained very excited about the meal to come.  

Overall, the food was great, but I think the owners could do a bit to enhance the clarity of the restaurant name.  

The place is a sit down spot, but pretty casual.

Here's what we got:

Lamb Saag -- love Saag.  The sauce is made from pureed spinach.  Looks gross, tastes awesome.  I dig it because it's a meat dish with a solid serving of veg built into the sauce.

Chicken Tikka -- Can't go wrong here.

Mixed Veg Platter -- can't remember exactly which one.  I asked the waiter for a recommendation.  He hooked it up.

It came with rice.

Everything was great!!

Due to the snow, the place was pretty empty.  Although, there was a table of four sitting next to us and we were able to eavesdrop on a interesting conversation about pharmaceuticals, drug abuse, withdrawal, etc.  I'm pretty sure one of the girls was a  medical professional.  Did you know that alcohol and xanax are some of the most dangerous substances to withdrawal from!?!

Service was totally adequate.  They tended to our needs, but didn't bother us.  We enjoyed a long dinner with flowing conversation punctuated only by bouts of hysterical laughter.  A night to remember.

Oh and if you are a crazy paleo weirdo (like me) or gluten free, Indian food is pretty accommodating.   ENJOY!

The ravioli pizza is an indulgence my body can only handle about once a month. The ravioli is big, fluffy, and stuffed with ricotta cheese - which would be fine on it's own but on top of yummy, doughy, cheesey pizza - it's just all too much. It's usually served with a pink sauce, but it's all too much cream to withstand in one sitting so I always ask for plain tomato sauce on mine. Delish!

As a vietnamese person, im well acquainted with my pho. Service sucked. And the noodles in my pho well clearly made 4 days ago. The noodles were mostly clumply; surely as a result of being cooked then frozen/refrigerated. 1 star for service. 1 star for food.

Viet Orleans is not your typical Vietnamese restaurant. Although the food is all Vietnamese, it has a sports-bar like feel. The service is excellent. The waiter was very nice and the food was out within 10 minutes. The food itself was decent - not the best I've ever had- but definitely good. I got the veggie sushi and spring roll and my friend got the beef fried rice. Both were good! But nothing really special. 

If you're looking for a decent sushi/Vietnamese place Viet Orleans is definitely a good choice.

This place rocks!

Great Italian dishes with arguably the finest PRIME STEAKS in town.

Food was spot on, service was friendly and inviting our dining experience was excellent. 

I highly recommend this restaurant and their steak.

I had heard about this burger chain from a relative who loves this chain. Well in my opinion a 2 star rating is generous. The burger was nothing special or memorable and the fries were probably the worst I had ever eaten. The fries were as dry and unflavored as any I had ever eaten. Sorry to say but I wished I had eaten in the restaurant as I would have asked for my money back. There are other fast food restaurants to choose from.
As standard as it gets.  Yes it is super fast.  The sandwiches are so automatic, that they're impossible to mess up.  You can't really go wrong with Jimmy John's.

It's sort of like Commander's Palace except way too modern and with much worse service.  It really is quite a disappointment in many ways if you're used to the other Brennan restaurants.  This is more of a very casual establishment certainly designed to cater more to the tourists in the hotel than the locals.  I would certainly steer most people that want to experience a traditional NOLA fine dining experience to other spots but for those people that want to wear their sloppy velour tracksuits, shorts and blue jeans please come to Cafe Adelaide instead of ruining the locals fine dining traditions at Commander's Palace.  There are times and places to be casual and there are times and places for dressing up and we do like our traditions in NOLA that set us apart from the rest of the all casual all the time world outside our civilization.

Great fried chicken. Stopped here passing through Nashville on a road trip to FL for spring break.  Definitely worth the stop, call ahead and order to beat the lines since you get to walk right up to the counter and pickup your food.  Medium was plenty spicy but I tried the hot too and there were bites that were making me sweat and tear up.

They deliver and the pizza is excellent, great flavor.  The only thing we had to do is reheat it.  We have been there several times and even though they are close, it is always nice to be able to have it delivered.
I have been eating here for years.  Chef Victor is awesome and the food is fresh and delicious!  Try the crab rangoons, they are the best ever!

Best breakfast place around! Try their green chile chicken hash!!! Amazing!!
Great food and service! Very attentive staff. Make sure to go in a bit earlier since this place is popular and you might have a short wait.

Definitely not as good as it used to be but still decent. Really wish they would make their food the way it was five or six years ago. But it is one of the better on east Washington street if you are over that way.

Went here after avoiding for years. Food was decent service was good. Cheesecake was top notch. Much better than my visit last year at WEM. Cheesecake cheesecake. Made fresh in house. Everywhere else orders in frozen. Miss the conductor beverage. I want to still take the glass home

Prices are fair for what you get, atmosphere is friendly and relaxed, food is good except for the asparagus which I would bet lots of money is from a can, and the calamari which was basically a bowl of breading around tiny squid fragments.  Steaks were really good, ditto for other veggies, sweet potato fries, and salad.  Overall, I like the place, and would come back if in the area again, just paying mind to the aforementioned unsatisfactory menu items.

Living in Roxborough, I've been in here several times.  The wood-bricked pizza is excellent but I haven't been a huge fan of the pasta dishes.  I think you're safe if you come here for pizza.  Since my wife and I have started making our own pizza on the grill, this place is not as much of a delicacy.  I will also say that it's pretty overpriced.  The service is very good and the people are nice.  Also, it feels a little crowded inside so don't plan to bring a huge party.  On Friday/Saturday evenings, expect a long wait (which I always consider a good sign).  Given that it's located in a shopping plaza, parking is not an issue.

The Kathmandu Sekuwa is a delicious chicken dish made in the tandoor oven and in a mint marinade. Garlic naan is a classic. Ordered lamb dumplings but they never came. Great food, service needs improvement.
Love the colorful deco and the food is always of a generous portion (after I finish my meal, I can still take home a box of food). The frozen margaritas  is very good.

I went here on my recent trip to Nashville. We can on a rainy week day for lunch and got in right before the line. It is a laid back atmosphere which was perfect for stepping right off the plane. This chicken was tender and juicy and it tasted good! I got the medium and it was spicy! I love spice but I don't know if I could handle the hot. My fiancé and friend each got the large and they had trouble eating it. The small came with PLENTY of food and I was stuffed the rest of the afternoon. I got the mac and cheese on the side which was very good as well. I also enjoyed the assortment of local brews!

I made an order for delivery from Joey's today. I was pleasantly surprised by the delivery guys manners. The people that work there take pride in what they do. I ordered the fried calamari which was perfectly crispy, tender and came with delicious dipping sauce. The chicken marsala I ordered was super tasty and was also a very generous portion. I would definitely recommend trying this diverse restaurant. Very unique for the area.

My new husband and I ordered some pizza to be delivered at our bed and breakfast, and it was fantastic!  It was a Friday night, so it took a bit of time, but it was completely worth the wait.  A very delicious pie, and it was just as good the next day.  If we swing through Boise again, you can bet we'll get some Flying Pie!

I am totally in love with their tomato and goat cheese bisque....I wish I could make this at home but I could never make it this good. We stopped in for lunch on our way home and glad we did. The staff was very helpful they knew the menu very well. I had the brunch scramble eggs and chorizo with Cajun salsa..it was very good...but be warned you may want to get the salsa on the side. I ordered the salsa on the side and I am glad I did...I like hot but this stuff was eye watering hot...but delicious! I hear this place is great for dinner as well I would try it again when back in town.

Everyone has their favorite hot chicken in Nashville and Hattie B's is mine.  There are other places in town that claim to be older or more authentic but Hattie B's is just a better all-around experience.  The chicken is GOOD and there is actually a range in their levels of spicy.  Medium is nice and right in the middle but I prefer the Hot! It's spicy but you can still taste how delicious the chicken is.  They have great sides too but it's really just all about that hot chicken, white bread, and pickle. 
It will be crowded during lunch and on weekends so plan ahead but it's well worth it!

Was here last night with my father. I shared their sushi boat for 2 with him. It was beautifully presented, a great mixture of fresh rolls and nigirizushi accompanied with sauces, wasabie, and ginger.  

Both of us enjoy watching the sushi chef and how fast and precisely he prepares his plates. We always sit at the sushi bar where it is like watching an artist prepare edible work. We were able to see how fresh all the ingredients were that he used to prepare each order.

The atmosphere is clean, dimly lit, and gives a Zen Japanese feeling. The staff is very friendly, professional, and helpful. 

It is hard for me order something other than sushi here, because that's what I prefer. When I have tasted items off of the regular menu, they tasted delicious as well.

The service wasn't great... I got mozzarella sticks and they were cold in the middle. Our food was pretty blah. I don't think that the food is worth the prices. Just not a good experience. The times I have went here I always end up regretting it. There's so many other great places in delco to go to better than this.

I was wary of this place at first. It sounds like a round Hot Pocket, and I'm going to be honest, that's an adequate way to describe it. I prefer to say "glorified Hot Pocket" myself.
