It doesn't seem like one would be filling, but it very much is. Try the AllStar or All-American. If you're more adventurous, they've got that covered, too with the Beach Ball or whatever other balls I'm not remembering because I am not one of the adventurous folks. I think the donut holes accompanying the meal are a cute touch, not to mention yummy. Go there during happy hour, though. Apparently it's $7 for a ball and a drink otherwise, which is stupid pricey. 

They "messed up my order" the first time I went there, meaning they forgot to add an ingredient they had recommended I add. After this, they gave me a card for a free pizza ball. Pretty cool dudes. 

I also went there recently when they were having some sort of photo shoot of people enjoying Pizza Balls. It was quite adorable. This place is a winner. Check it out.

Best poboy I ever had, and I think my boyfriend felt the same. We got the shrimp and sweet potato poboys and were stuffed. It was our first day in NOLA for a wedding weekend and we were very excited to be in this vibrant city. He got a local brew that he loved, and the poboys were eaten within a couple minutes.
Pad thai noodles are delicious! Best I've had. The Fried rice is not so good, kind of mushy

We were in Nashville for a business trip and decided on Jimmy Kelly's based on a recommendation of a friend who frequents Nashville.  After reading the reviews I was hesitant but not having another place in mind, decided to keep our reservation.  We are so glad we did!  We were with a group of 9 and sat in one of the rooms upstairs and were lucky enough to have Claude as our server.  He was attentive, prompt and incredibly entertaining!  I had a blue cheese crusted filet and added some shrimp (I didn't get the Mixed Grill because I wanted the full size filet...I was starving and eating conference food all weekend...don't judge me)  The filet was delicious and the shrimp were some of the best I ever had.  We ordered a variety of sides and the only disappointment was the asparagus as some others have mentioned on here.  There were only a few stalks and didn't justify the price.  When I return to Nashville I will definitely come back and request Claude.  The food was great but the service was even better.

First visit tonight.   My wife had the malai kofta, was very well pleased.  I had lamb vindaloo (always my first order at new restaurant); meat was tender, sauce had lots of flavor & heat.  The mango lassi was very good & served as a fire extinguisher.  Also had the onion naan & Kashmiri naan; both very good.  Server was friendly, attentive without hovering.  We visit quite a few Indian restaurants; happy to add this one to that number!

Here's a little lesson in ask and you shall receive! For a little while now I've thought it would amazing to have Steve's Chocolate Cake wrapped up in Isabel's ice cream. Well consider it done! I stopped by today as soon as Renee tweeted Tucson that this amazing creamery had folded rich cake into vanilla ice cream. Divine! (And because I'm not feelin well, I didn't mind 1 single bite!)

Tonight my hunni and I ordered from here for the first time. I must say everything was wonderful! I got a crispy chicken garden salad, LOADED with everything! It was full, fresh and delicious. I also got breaded mushrooms and thought they were very tasty. I throughly enjoyed my meal tonight and will definitely order from here again!

I feel silly rating a Domino's.  Its a Domino's, you know what you're going to get, its chain pizza, kind of generic, not authentic NY pizza.
However, if its Domino's you're craving, this is the location to order from! They're amazing, fast, accurate, and with the most polite, most honest delivery employees ever!
Seriously, this location rocks!

You have to have the slowest drive thru line in the area.  15 mins to get 2 cars through is insane.   The it was my turn to pick up my coffee which then took another 5 mins to get a coffee.  Go send your employees to chick-fil-a to see how it's suppose to be done.

The service was terrible the food was not that great and it was over priced 50 cents for ranch are you serious could not understand the person I was ordering with got put on hold for over 15 mins over 3 times never ordering here again

I don't know if it's just the location or what but tonight the service was terrible and the food didn't taste right. Doesn't taste like how I had it when I first moved to Philly . 

So as the drive thru person was taking our order he'll pause in between our order and you can hear him laughing through the speaker like what was so funny? Professionalism? I think not. 

My boyfriend ordered the chili dog and it was so sloppy . I had ordered the #7 which was the spicy chicken sandwich meal . The spicy chicken tasted so sour I don't know what happened there . Was the mayo that was on the sandwich overdue or something? Who knows. My fries were very mushy and tasted like it's been drenched in oil that has been sitting there for months. The Sprite fountain drink tasted so gross . It tasted like I was sipping water from a molded filtered sink . Never ever coming back here again. The food and drink was just trash. Excuse my language ! Go to McDonald that's nearby it'll probably be better.

Roller's is really popular only because no one has found the space (or perhaps desire?) to open another much better reasonably priced place for breakfast in Chestnut Hill.

Service is often slow and occasionally unfriendly.  They also allow customers to order something as a side and fail to tell them they will be charged an entree price.  At least that has happened to me.

The front room seating is small and uncomfortable, esp. when the place is busy.  The back room seating feels like Siberia if it's slow as it's cut off from the rest of the place and activity.

it's just not worth writing home about.  Good coffee, and some some decent food, but the experience overall is just waiting to be outdone by someone else.
This is my go-to Thai place in Tucson. I've only gotten curries from them but they've all been good. I look forward to dining-in post-COVID.
Amazing food and their food truck visits my work location so I don't have to go to Pythian Market.  Try the carne aside tacos
I always get the #8 flounder, wings, fries soooooo good best in the city since I was a kid.

We were in a pinch on a long drive for the day and found Holler & Dash and wow! The food was delicious (we each got a chicken biscuit but different ones) and the staff was gracious. Thanks for a great experience!

Well this place is a St Louis gem, been around for year since my wife lived there. Got the privilege of taking my family there a few months ago. Our waitress was really great and helped us decide since there's so much to choose from.  Great place in St Louis. Now we've move to the Denver area and we see them here. Personally we'll give them a go here as well

Tried it tonight for the first time.  Ordered online.  Young guys running the restaurant were very friendly and polite.  One even brought me a wing in a different flavor for me to taste.  We had hot, Parmesan garlic and Louisiana Rub.  I thought all were good but be warned the Louisiana one is very salty.  Next time I'd get lemon pepper with Parmesan... that was the one I sampled and it was really good. My daughter liked the fries with the seasoning.  It was a sweet/spicy taste.  We will definitely be back.
The Indian-style chicken cheesesteak is amazing. So genius to make this spin off of a cheesesteak. The flavors are powerful and amazing. Must try!

I absolutely love shake shack. I first had it on a trip to DC and have been in love ever since. Whenever I made a trip to the east coast I had to have it. I was excited to find out we now have one down south. I tried several times to visit this location but pre Covid it was often packed to capacity. The food is great. I wouldn't call it fast food more of a fast casual experience. The ingredients are fresh and everything is always hot and made to order. The only reason they didn't get 5 stars has nothing to do with the food. The location is on a busy corner in Metairie. Parking is tight and limited. Several times I couldn't go to the restaurant simply because I couldn't get into the parking lot. This visit I had the shack stack and added cherry peppers with a side of cheese fries. It was amazing and worth every penny. The shack stack is a cheeseburger topped with a cheese stuffed friend portobello mushroom cap. 

Pros: good food, good service, 

Cons: parking is terrible, kinda pricy for a burger and prices but well worth it. 

Covid regulations: although the location is small they are taking several Covid recommendations as far as table spacing and cleaning.

Worst Chinese food I have ever had.  It was so salty I couldn't even eat it.  I ordered take out and my food came with no soy sauce, mustard, or duck sauce.  I ever had to request sauces at any other Chinese restaurant before!!! Not sure why I needed to requested them. BTW...it's not cheap either! Plus the staff was rude when I called and basically hung up on me.

I came here for lunch with Cherie O.  This place is just ok in my book.  The gyro itself wasn't foodgasmic.  Seriously.  The baklava was puny & kind of cute.  The employees are helpful & cool.  Ambience is simply put a hole in a wall.  The bathrooms are outside, & you need a key to get in.  Weird.  Anyhow, fast service.
Great service, friendly staff, clean, bright interior and great chicken, beers and sides. What more could one ask for?

Each year my wife's work has their Christmas party here.  

This year, we were seated in the same room as before, greeted and seated quickly and professionally.  

The wait staff were attentive without the annoying over-attention one often receives.  Our servers kept us smiling an laughing throughout the evening, all the while being discreet and not stealing the show.  

Last year I found the same service and, as this year, the food was outstanding.  

Wherein last year, there were several items I tasted and did not go back to, this year, there was none of that.  The dishes presented at the buffet were fresh, colourful and beautifully presented.  My favourite meat, prime rib, was just perfect.  

After my first plate I attended to the souse chef's station and ordered scallops and prawns which were prepared with flare and lots of flame.  Delightful!  

When the desert selections came round, I did not partake as I am not a desert person.  Everyone attending loved the deserts, I just went back and got a couple more slabs of rare prime rib, with wonderfully sinus-clearing horse-radish.

This was mid-December and I've been meaning to give this wonderful experience it's due.  Today I have the time.  

If you need a great experience I can't recommend this restaurant too much.  

Absolutely wonderful.

This was amazing! Workers were super nice too. Best Mac & cheese! The parking is the only downfall, it was luck we got a spot! But the chicken was worth it. Great place for the price too, not bad at all!

When my girlfriend and I visit Wasabi, we usually dine in but lately we've been getting take out. The last time we tried to eat in the restaurant, we arrived 30-45 mins prior to closing time and were told we could not have a table because the restaurant was closing within an hour. I was less than thrilled, to say the least. There is one female waitress who is rude and obviously hates her job but the great food (amazing variety of both chinese & japanese dishes - all are fresh, flavorful, great portion size, and make great leftovers the next day!) overshadow the service-related issues. However, we will not be dining in the restaurant ever again - I will only choose take out from now on. I have never been denied entry to a restaurant with 30-45 minutes remaining until the official closing time. Don't let the service effect the experience - just get take out. The food is very, very good and will taste just as good at home. 

I'd like to officially rescind my statement in my original review about the service at Wasabi - it has definitely gone downhill. The hostess is always nice but being turned away (see above) was a huge turn off and a big let down for us. We'll still get take out but we won't be eating in.

We love Carrabba's!  One of our favorite restaurants in town.  Spiedino di mare (Breaded Scallops & Shrimp in a lemon sauce) is my favorite! We also love their endless soup and salad lunch menu! Only $7.99! Yum!

Very disappointed that they refused to refund our fully paid reservation after a death in the family. Manager did not even have a courtesy to call us back. Now we are burdened with a $600 credit... Think twice before making a fully paid reservation.

Bernini Restaurant on 7th Avenue in Tampa is a great place to go for all things quality Italian food and drinks in an unique atmosphere. I really like how everything is set up and the food is delicious and well made. They provide an excellent overall experience that is well worth it.

I am totally in love with their modern concept and usage of new technology (ordering off the ipad) it's great and easy to access to whenever you want to browse the menu or order more. Big win here.

The menu isn't large but they do have a good number of rolls. The quality of fish isn't amazing to me as the tuna and salmon on my roll was dull in color and tasted a little off. Yet, the salmon in the sashimi dinner tasted and looked way better. So I would have to say it's a hit and miss with certain items? Their use of sauces and presentation is an A + though. Tasty!

Over all I thought this place is pretty good. I wouldn't say they have the best quality fish, but the vibe is nice. The restaurant is clean and ordering off the iPad is just too fun!

My husband warned me as we were pulling into the drive thru, that this wouldn't be a good experience, he could not have been more right!! OMG, I literally, 2 hours later cannot get over the waste of petroleum used to attempt at this foul location. So, we get to the intercom and the animal, I mean "human being" loudly mumbles something on the speaker to the extent, "Come to the window instead of the speaker." So, we begin to go and she/he/whatever it was, said something else that we could barely understand. She said, what do you want? My husband send our order through, we wait for about 30 seconds without hearing a word and then all of a sudden we hear an extreme burst of laughter and IT saying "ohhhhhh ohhhhhhh". ARE YOU KIDDING ME? We looked at each other and he said, "That's it, I'm not doing it." Then, we left. I never in my life have dealt with such incompetence. We went to east Washington Street, instead. Please, just don't even bother going here. I wish that I could rate this place a zero.

We were seated fir 20 minutes before someone came by for our drink order
We ordered a pitcher of margaritas and it was three glasses and a pound of ice
We ordered tortilla soup and it never came. We finally just left

Went to Beefy's for the first time today absolutely the bomb. Definitely one of the best burgers I've had. Service was great. Food was great. They need to feature this place on Diner's Drive-ins and Dive's. I told the owner he either needs to open another one or move to Gardnerville. I will definitely be back!!!

This is probably the 3rd time I've ever been disappointed at this location. I would never go back around the time they are just opening. I live about 5-10 minutes from this place and when I got home, the wings were cold, dry, hard, and shriveled up. Not what I'm used to from this location at all. I honestly think they gave me chicken from the day before  Only a few were actually edible and I just threw the rest away and ate the salad I got with it. Definitely would recommend only going in the evening time. A waste of $12 smh

Haven't tried it yet, ordered for tomorrow but ordering was very difficult. In this day and age they should be more conscious about materials, I'm happy to see a couple of vegan options but, in addition to having difficulty ordering, I ended up having to cancel after calling in to confirm that their drinks were in fact in plastic cups with plastic straws. I tried ordering again to just get the two Veg wraps they have, but it wouldn't let me move forward without ordering any drink at all, which I really really don't want, I avoid plastic. I mentioned this on the phone but it doesn't really matter. I was able to cancethe iced tea but it wasn't easy to figure out. Then there was something called, "make it a meal "added to my order for $5.25, wasn't sure what this was so had to remove it. Hopefully the food is better than the ordering experience.

Being from Westminster, Ca we have high expectations for Vietnamese food. That being said Viet Kitchen met those expectations.  We went here twice in the 6 days we were here. First time got the com gia đinh. 3 dishes were great. Second time got pho and com tam again both dishes were great. Pho is my favorite food of all time so my high standard is even higher for Pho and they held up! Service was good and the place felt like home/cozy. If you're in Tampa and are craving Viet food go here!
Best pizza we've had in Jersey so far. Highly recommend. Order it well done for a nice crispy crust.

My grandmother and I were hungry and driving in Hendersonville and saw Rio Bravo so we stopped to try it. Little did we know how AMAZING the food would taste. The service was great and stayed on top of our drinks and such. The food came quickly and hot. My grandmother and I were very pleased and will be returning for days to come.

Mediocre pizza in an overly lit, generic setting.

Menu on its face appears tantalizing with loads of options and while ingredients are for the most part, fresh, execution is lackluster.  It's hard to mess up pizza, but Mama just doesn't measure up.  Crust is tasteless, pizzas themselves often dry, and their red sauce tastes like it comes straight out of a jar.  The basil sprinkled on top does not save the eating experience.

Salads are massive,  as are the pizza portions (even the individual size is big, six slices) so be sure to order to share - prices super reasonable.  This is a family style environment, good for kids but not for a date.  Bright, overhead lighting - the place seems more like a take-out joint than a BYOB.  The service feels like it too.

You can buy brews on site, but no vino.   And speaking of vino, Mama also has a ridiculous house policy that limits bringing only one bottle of wine per two people (or a couple) -  Kill joy.

Yesterday after I left Schnucks I noticed the gyro company was open and I was hungry so I popped in. I was the only customer (it was early) and there was one women cleaning the windows and another behind the counter. Neither acknowledged me or greeted me and I actually thought maybe they were not open yet and felt like I was an intruder. I stood at the counter for a few seconds and was getting ready to leave when the women behind the counter asked if she could help me. I ordered the basic gyro and the spinach pie. The food was outstanding but the customer service and welcoming atmosphere was horrible. I felt completely unwelcome as if I were putting them out somehow for being there. If the reviews were for the food alone it would be a 4 but this experience was a 2.5 to 3.

Absolutely one of the best Indian meals I have eaten. My whole group was pleased. We have dined at Desi Vibes 3 times recently. 

Had Chicken Bunah Curry last time and every bite was delectable. Other times had Bhatti Murg Tandoori. Love the charcoal chimney set on table.

We were looking for a sit down pizza place to go with our family of 6 during Covid.  Our favorite place was only seating outdoors and I was too hot that day.  We ordered 3 pizzas....one was pepperoni, one all meats, and one with artichoke and chicken with a white sauce.  The children had soft drinks and sweet tea.  My husband had a beer and I had a house wine Pinot noir which was delicious.  I didn't realize the alcohol was so expensive but I should've asked how much.  My glass of wine was 11.95.  I thought it would've been maybe around 7.  Again, it was my fault but it was certainly delicious.  The total was a little under  $100 which is more than what we usually pay at most places.  With 4 children, we usually choose more reasonable priced places.  The marinara sauce was awesome.  I used my Yelp check-in to get free garlic butter knots which were a big hit.
Excellent choices and the food is always fresh. Sushi is also very good! Dinner menu is better than lunches, more seafood variety. Excellent staff! Good prices!

Team at Carmel impressed me with these new rules around social distancing. They have stickers on the floor where to stand, they let you know what table too sit at, they have employees wearing masks ( even though it's hard to understand them) they are really stepping up in food safety. Store is much cleaner than the last time I visited. Definitely some good changes since my last visit.

I really wanted to like this place. Spent nearly 15mins debating over the menu bc everything sounded good.  The young lady (host I believe) seemed nice and was helpful. I placed a Togo order for a dynamite roll. As I waited for my order I could see the chef behind the bar making the sushi for other guest. He was wearing gloves however I saw him rub his nose several times while preparing the food. Not once did he change those gloves nor wash his hands. The young lady took those plates to the guest! I then see him blow his nose with the same gloves on and place food in a carry out box. I asked a young man (waiter) if that was my food, he said yes. I privately asked for a refund and told him what I had saw. He and the young lady laughed about it. She said something to him as though she was angry that I had asked for my money back. I didn't understand, their frustration, I would have been the one to eat it. They gave me my refund, I left. I felt bad for the guest. However, I hope this helps future  guest. As clean as this place looks, they have a dirty chef.

This place is amazing...Authentic Italian  Cuisine. Having just moved to the area, my wife and I were looking for a new favorite place to go on date nights and this place didn't disappoint. Everything was homemade. It took a little time to get our food but it was well worth the wait. We are definitely going back. First time visiting and we left feeling like family.

really not great.. I've gone here a few times craving chinese, and always just felt meh about it. If you eat clean at all, don't go here or your belly will be upset with you. Not the worst.. just not really good at all.
The New York strip steak with a side of shrimp is absolutely delish! The servers are amazing. They are very friendly and attentive.
Racist. I just happen to pass by and would like to try their pizza. Shown the door and was told Asian not welcomed there. Wow so divine.

Ditto on all of the other one star comments. After waiting for a server for 20 minutes and not even being offered coffee or water, we left and went to the IHOP in Metairie off Causeway. It was the Taj Mahal in comparison. My 11 year old son loved it and recommends the hamburgers.

Had an issue with online ordering - contacted Dana to provide a suggestion and was impressed with the desire to go above and beyond. Looking forward to dining here again based on customer service alone. Thanks :)

The good- the restaurant is following safe COVID practices as far as I could tell when I went inside to get my takeout order. Employees were masked as well as customers waiting for food. A temperature scanner is installed at the front door and is a required stop for everyone who enters, even for takeout.

More good- this is a very nice looking establishment. The decor was fun and employees were all very nice and helpful. Pickup for takeout orders was also well organized. 

Thats where the good ends. The food is very expensive here- $16.99 for a shrimp poboy with a side of fries. The "poboy" came with 5 fried shrimp, very wet lettuce, and tomato. The shrimp were pretty big but unseasoned. The best part about the sandwich was the bread. If I ever order from here again it will likely be to buy their bread appetizer so I can make real poboys at home. I'll give them credit for not calling themselves a cajun restaurant because this food is a far cry from anything back home (Louisiana).

I also tried the calamari and liked it. It had a nice texture- steaks maybe?

Last item was the crab meat fries. And I had to laugh at myself for this one. I'm not sure what I expected for $10.99 but a small box of fries with crab meat thrown on top was not on the list. I laugh but it wasn't horrible in comparison with the poboy. I wouldn't buy it again but I did finish the fries that were covered in crab. There were quite a few uncovered fries left in the box but the fries alone weren't my style. 

Overall, I was really excited about this place and now am kinda sad I lost my favorite fried rice for it.

1 Star...hubby and I got so sick after eating here.
The waitstaff were nice and attentive. The food was warm not hot.

Horrible experience dealing with Pizza Factory from start to finish!  Thank goodness I ordered via Eatstreet as ordering through their platform and sharing my concerns with their customer support chat rep Andy were the only things that went smoothly and did not disappoint...

As far as Pizza Factory goes, I started out needing to add additional topping information into the "Special Requests" comment box on both the pizza and side order screen - no worries right? That is what that box is there for... WRONG - Pizza Factory decided to completely ignore my special requests, and the delivery person lied to my face about it when I mentioned to him that what he brought was incorrect, even though he had my Eatstreet order information printed out in his hand (assuming for the delivery address information), and lo and behold, the special request comments that I added online were clearly on the paper in BOLD... 

Secondly, I have never received an order slower in my life - the delivery estimate online was 30 - 45 minutes, which is understandable... middle of Nashville around supper time... an hour after I placed my order, I called Pizza Factory just to get an update as my mother, friend, and I were planning to head to a show shortly (and we thought getting pizza delivered rather than fighting the dinner crowds around the Grand Ole Opry was a great idea for a fast meal -- boy were we wrong!), and the person I spoke with on the phone said that my order was on its way... 30 minutes after that, still no food...  I was getting a bit frustrated and decided to contact Eatstreet via their online chat option to see if they could see what was going on.... I started chatting with Andy, who was very sympathetic to my situation, and called the store himself to see whether he could obtain more information.  A few minutes later, the food arrived...

After the back and forth conversation with the delivery guy concerning the special requests I made, he did offer to have Pizza Factory make us a new pizza at no charge and send it out, but considering that 1) I already caught him in one lie, 2) We didn't have time to wait again before our show, and 3) I had no idea whether we would have to wait for another hour and 45 minutes for the replacement pizza anyway, I was all too hesitant to agree to this.  After telling him that we did not have time and that we would be heading home (we were in Nashville on vacation) the next morning, I asked him if there was anyway we could receive some semblance of a refund (whole or partial), and that was a big fat nope.  So, I took the food and thanked him anyway...

Lastly, as soon as my mother tried a bite of the pizza, she said it was cold, so that was yet another mark against Pizza Factory.  I got back on Eatstreet's website to give them an update on the situation as right before the delivery man arrived, the internet connection went out in the hotel, and I got disconnected from the current chat with Andy.  Interestingly enough, I was reconnected with Andy and let him know what had transpired in the meantime... He was extremely helpful, and took action to help re-mediate my losses in this situation, and I greatly appreciate him doing that.  Moral of the story is: if you are craving pizza or Italian in East Nashville near Music Valley, stay away from the Pizza Factory on Old Lebanon Road.  It was a major blemish to an otherwise enjoyable vacation.

Located past the Alaska/United, by the bathrooms. They have plenty of seating, as well as various drinks, sandwiches and snacks and a bar area. LOL some guys invited me to sit with them. I politely declined. This is a great spot to hang out as you're waiting for Alaska to open (horrid, limited hours, I'll write a separate review for that).

I loved how they had a real live pianist! WOW! At the airport?! Enjoy my video snippet :)

Nice that they have a more upscale dining option here. This would be a good place to eat if you have someone waiting with you that's not your travel companion, as it's before security, so anyone can go.

Overall, it's one of the nicer airport eateries I've seen.

Out of all the Korean Restaurants I go to this a place you must try if you love Korean food and its true. I had one of their spicy seafood soup with the glass noodles and it was really hot but its really good for a person that likes spicy foods. They have many things on the menu from different Korean Hot Pots (its really good), numerous entrees, soups and many more. The service is really good and I like how you can ask for service by pressing a button. Also if you are K-Pop fan you would love this place also because they play K-Pop music videos on the TV Screens a lot. This place is a must if you like Korean Food.

First time here. They overcooked my New York Strip but graciously replaced it. Attentive and helpful. My partner had the chicken Florentine, which was very tasty. The Parmesan fries were delightful and selection of wines were reasonably priced. 
We also stayed the night at La Meridien, the Marriott hotel that houses this restaurant on its second floor. All staff were friendly and polite. 
The hotel architecture is old and beautiful and amazing. Overall enjoyable experience, just a little sad that generally hotel restaurants don't maximize and promote their restaurants and it is a space that could really be special. 

We took a nice walk in the neighborhood after dinner and came upon a lively Mediterranean Restaurant called Dio that we are going to visit soon.

Great Restaurant, great staff and awesome food!  The wine was fantastic!  Thank you Scott and Chris!  Our waiter Scott was the best I've had.  5 stars for the service!  You can tell the owner Chris takes pride in his establishment as he should!  Topped it off with Italian moonshine and Spumante!  Awesome

I expected authenticity, but overall this is great addition to American Chinese Cuisine when one is craving Chinese food. It had American dishes with a little authenticity. Perfect for those not use to the traditional Chinese cuisine but wanting a relaxing atmosphere and decent food.  While the menu is huge, you can find dishes for most everyone. 

We tried the Beef with Chinese Broccoli, which was O.K., but didn't match up to other restaurants or what I make at home.  The other dish we had were the Pork Meatballs.  They were moist, flavorful, but still lacked a WOW! factor.  As an appetizer, we choose one of the best options: Shrimp and Pork Wontons in a spicy chili sauce.  Those were good with just a little hint of spice.  

At the end of the evening, the waiter recommended the spicy hot pot (on the secret menu).  Maybe next time?

Bad service. Today, even though lots of tables were free, I was told that there was a waiting list. After pointing out that literally all outside tables were empty, I was told  that I could sit down there, but wouldn't be served for the next 10 minutes.

I came to Outback with the intention of having a nice dinner with my parents. All was well until my mother found a staple in her pasta dish. Can you believe that? A damn staple! I will never be back at any Outback Steakhouse and I advise everyone to save themselves from this terror of a restaurant.

This place is beyond amazing. I had the Carne Assada Aerepa. The jerk chicken one is my favorite though. 

If you are thirsty order a Papelon. It's game changing.

The food is so affordable priced, absolutely delicious, and servings are crazy huge. The service is beyond friendly. One of the best spots I've ever been to. Any review below 5 stars is a lie

I've been here twice now and tried the regular, chicken, and bean and cheese pupusas. I enjoyed all of them but the "regular" are my favorite. The prices are super reasonable- you can get two pupusas and a drink for under $10! The space is small but it hasn't been super busy when I've went. You can also order for take out. Service is quick and very pleasant.
So I checked back in and what a great Sunday am meal!  Place was busy but they hustled like a well oiled machine!!

The food was pretty good, and the owner gave us free samples and stuff! The place is pretty small, but had a pleasant atmosphere.

I would've rated this place higher if the owner hadn't seemed to be trying so hard to... please us for the sake of a good rating..? Good service is one of the most important aspects of a good restaurant, and that's what makes me want to spread the word and write good reviews.... but when it's so obvious that they are servicing you to get ratings rather than just to be good to you... I feel kind of used.

This is just my honest opinion. The food is still really good, so people should still definitely give it a try.

Great service here, notwithstanding the New Year's Day crowd. Breakfast was well-prepared, very generous portions, and the price was right. Every member of our party either ate every bite or took home what was left. Scrambled eggs, home fries, toast, and bacon all served hot and delicious.

Very good traditional Italian that offers new twists on old favorites too (red beet raviolit are excellent).  Service was just ok as we waited a while and things seemed to slip. But soups and pastas and steaks were loved by all.

Foods good, lunch special needs a flow chart.

Read the top carefully:

Served with pork or shrimp fried rice, egg roll 
Choice of one wonton or hot & sour soup or egg drop soup & soda [or]

With beef or shrimp fried rice or lo mein for $1 more.

So.... get shrimp fried rice with no egg roll and it's $1 extra?

The crust is thin and crisp.  The presentation creative and appetizing.  The ingredients and toppings are very fresh.  But thin and crisp, presented well, and even fresh does not translate to flavor.  And I even had a slice from four different pies.  It was more like a slathering of tasteless toppings on cardboard.  I'm sorry, I don't get this place.  I am a Philadelphia and NYC pizza snob, and I also like "gourmet" type pizzas at various places, such as Parc Bistro in Skippack, but this Jules crap was a step above "sucky".  Sue me, it's just MY opinion.

Love this sushi place! Staff is kind and attentive. Their takeout deals are amazing. We got takeout a couple times a month, even before all the covid-19  madness. They have been accommodating when I  have asked for modifications due to food allergies and so the fish is always fresh!
All this place needs is a bar! Best buffet for the price. Always fresh and the staff is friendly. Bonus for the seniors: discount for summer season.

Wanna see what it's like to wait in drive thru for 30 minutes?  Visit London's!  Famous for their slowest service on earth, they'll leave you waiting... and then leave you waiting for more waiting!  Sure they're open late, but don't think that getting here at 1am means better service.  Oh no, they'll be standing around laughing while you're waiting in the drive thru when there's only 4 cars in drive thru.  They get another star for having good prices and another star for best wings outside of imos.

Can I take your order?

Yes, I'll have the Chicken Finger Platter.

Would you like something to drink?

Yes, I would like a small drink.

You're number 13.  We'll call you when its ready.

NUMBER 13!!!

Thanks!

What can I say... Its what you get at fast food places!

Great tasting food though!

Dear Mom....  You have to come try this!!!!  ;)

Wow! What a lovely dinner start to finish!  The foie gras, beef tartare, and the oyster/lamb appetizers were outstanding. The strip steak special and the black bass entree were sublime.  The chicken entree was the only misstep of the evening.  Rounded out the meal with outstanding apple pie, baklava, and chocolate desserts!  The service was friendly and attentive.  We will definitely be back!

I went there this weekend with my boyfriend and we ordered the steak entree for two with twice baked potatoes and soup. Strictly in terms of food quality, this steak dinner was the best we've ever had in Tucson. The service and atmosphere were also very good, but it's the food that really stood out. Jax Kitchen has some amazing bread and potato chips they serve right after you sit down, as well, which I was very pleased about.

We both concluded that Jax is our new favorite restaurant. However, since we're both college students and the tab came out to about $60 we won't be eating there as often as we'd like. But if you have money, and like to spend it on food, you should check it out!

The best meal and experience I've had in a long time. Celebrating a birthday--we indulged and enjoyed ourselves. The calamari, Arancini balls, bruschetta, meatballs, were all cooked to perfection. All
Of our party got a side salad with their meal with the house vinaigrette--the mix of herbs was outstanding and unique. I like to look at the pictures before I eat to aid in my decision-making before eating. Someone also recommended the carbonara, so I was pretty set on chicken carbonara for my entree. It was unbelievable. Not your traditional carbonara, but I do love a good cream/Alfredo sauce. The fresh sweet peas and bacon really added layers of flavor. The fettuccine was cooked perfectly. Cannoli was served for the birthday boy. Also not traditional, it was filled with a delicious mascarpone cheese--it was crispy and sweet and a great end to the meal. The ambiance of the restaurant was comfortable. The main dining area was a little dark and the place is small and cramped but it was cozy and inviting. 

We are so glad we chose this for our dinner celebration and really look forward to returning!
Roast pork with broccoli rabe and provolone dripping with juice, and beef brisket soft and tender. Wish they had American cheese with the roast pork instead of provolone.
We just ordered four take out beef dishes. Meat was way overcooked and dry. Sorry to see this place going downhill.

I think this is the second time I've ever given a one star review. 

We visited this located with success in the past, but my most recent visit will be my last. We walked in with nobody in the lobby. It was filthy, with food and crumbs all over most of the tables. That should have been my first clue. The boy behind the counter stuck his finger up to tell us to wait because he was helping drive through. I used to work at Hardee's and I'm familiar with the way they spread labor, so I didn't say anything. The manager came out of her office and instantly looked annoyed.

The order was as we asked for it, except they forgot my sauce and one of our cookies for the $5 meals. They gave it to us when we asked. Food was luke warm, except the fries. Those were hot and fresh. 

All of this would be fine, with the exception of the cleanliness of the store, AND the addition of the shift manager talking about going to jail for assaulting someone. Announcing. She was announcing it. To the entire store. And using foul language. I realize it's fast food, so you have to consider that, but that's a stereotype that doesn't need reinforced. 

There is a better location on South Harding. The store manager there is pretty cool and takes pride in her work. I'd recommend that over this location.
The Paulie Gee is HEAVEN.  Awesome!  I cant get enough of it.  The honey on it sounds gross but it makes the pizza, promise!

This place is awesome! Fresh ingredients and fun to put together your bowl. I don't eat raw fish, so I used the cooked shrimp on a base of spring mix, with all the veggies and the sweet sauce, spicy mayo and spicy crab. Delicious! We'll be back!

Very friendly waitress. Filthy bathrooms (my toilet wouldn't even flush). The breakfast meal was good, but the salad was just okay. It would've been great had I not tasted bits of old/wilted lettuce intermittently throughout the meal. Gross. Honestly, if the staff wasn't so nice this would probably be a one star review.

We originally came up into the area for Panera Bread to celebrate our 8 yr first date we had there on Valentine's Day...but when we parked we were intrigued with Honeygrows modern sleek logo and. Immediately look them up on yelp it to see what it was all about...we ignored reviews and were hypnotized by the fresh pictures and decided to experience something new! 

We love idea of what they're serving and the place is super cute! HOWEVER I asked the lady prepping food if the egg white noodles had wheat in them or were they just straight up egg white noodles....and boy did she set me up! She kindly responded they were straight up egg white noodles; so in my gluten free excitement I ordered them! OMG...as we were leaving a server came and inquired of our experience and I asked just one more time if the noodles were reeeeeally gluten free and he nicely explains "well...there's wheat in them to bind the egg whites" WTHeck?!??!? So I spent a nite in agony and I'm still in pain right now thanks to a HUGE misunderstanding! They really need to educate their employees on the ingredients bc it could have been way worse for someone with celiac disease to have my same experience. Not cool guys not cool!

We had a reservation at the Tyrone location 2/23/21 @ 8:00pm to celebrate our 18th wedding anniversary. Missy was our waitress and she was great. App's were good (the Brie & the firecracker shrimp). We Received a complimentary bottle of wine and later a desert to share, all good so far. Then came the the Prime rib, which my wife and I both ordered, after reading many positive reviews on various groups on fb we decided to give it a try. What a mistake that choice was. My wife ordered medium rare and received the end heal of the prime rib and it was unbelievably overcooked and dry mine was ordered Medium and was just as dried out. I usually don't order prime rib medium but did this time based on others reviews of how rare theirs came out. Although it did have decent flavor it was inedible. It ruined our meal to say the least. The reason I gave a 4-star review was due to the service and friendly staff and the fact that they did all they could to make it right for us. Missy sent us home with 2 complete prime rib dinners to go, hopefully they will be better than what we were served in person. I understand it's unreasonable to think that everything is perfect  of the time for that reason I'm willing to give them the benefit of the doubt that it was just a "off" night and give them another try. 

-Greg & Deb K
Although we waited about 90 minutes....the food were on of the kind delicious...i love it! I will come again...maybe a little bit earlier....
Tasty food and nice place to talk for a long time! Maybe a little expensive (breakfast) but it was definitely good.

This place is amazing, I could eat there every week. If you're going with 3 or 4 people than get the sampler! Next time I would swap out the kebabs for something else, they are the only thing we have been disappointed in. But in my opinion, it's hard to cook Kebabs without drying out the meat. Otherwise everything we've eaten had been excellent and they make everything in house. And get dessert! The sofra cake was one of the best things I've ever eaten! Can't wait to try it all
10 minutes off the turn pile but worth it.  Very good food.  Might have to stop here on the way back from our trip

The pizzas are creative, tasty, fresh and with a nice list of ingredients you can even put your own creativity to the test and design one yourself. For me, one of the best I've had is the pear, brie, and almond pizza (haven't tried them all, yet, so there may be better?) - so tasty even the 3yr-old couldn't put it down. Service is also great - pleasant and helpful. One thing to keep in mind (for all of you used to "standard" crust pizza), Jules Thin Crust takes it's name seriously - it is THIN crust - but it works and lets the ingredients shine through.

How ive lived in this neighborhood and never been to Blue plate is a mystery to me. Ive thought about it, its just never happened until now. I found the menu to be pretty interesting, its a cross between asian and greek and its pretty limited. The place itself is quite quaint with a really cute shaded patio. I had the blue plate which is any of their salads along side any of their appetizers. My boyfriend had the gyro special that came with a greek salad. The szechuan dumpllings i had were fantastic, tasted like a home made filling and a tasty, spicy sauce. The salad....ehhh...too much dressing(although it was good dressing) and 25 percent of the salad was friend wontons. The ice berg lettuce wasnt a great presentation either. The gyro was good, but definitely done better at some other greek places in town. I liked it but i definitely didnt love it. I think the same product is found better and cheaper in the same area.

I probably order from Fuel 4-5 times a week and it literally is one of my favorite restaurants. They are always on time for delivery and friendly when they get here. Plus they are a very healthy restaurant!

Me and my family went In to grab lunch. And ordered the orange chicken and ramen soup. The chicken was very fried. And made it difficult to eat. And the ramen noodles were also over cooked. The waiting period for the food was more than expected. The waitress only checked on us once the whole time there. Empty plates were never taken off the table. Honestly the only thing that was nice there I would say is the decor.

Wow I was surprised by this place! They have great sake and the rolls were great. I'm pretty sure the soups are house made as well. Always looking for better closer sushi places in the Kenner area, this is one! We split hot sake then ordered a bottle of cold sake, drank half and took the rest home!
Grow up with Lee's on Cheltenham Ave., moved away, had many hoagies, had fancy meats and  breads,   BUT no one builds a better hoagie then LEE'S.
This visit I did the tofu taco and the duck taco. Both un-frikkin believably good.  How do they do it? I don't know, but I'm comming back to work my way down the rest of the menu. Keep you posted :)
Honestly the service sucks, the atmosphere is very awkward, and the employees are rude. Good isn't great.

Had New Years Eve dinner at FIORINO.
It was our fourth visit and best yet
Four beautiful courses including the finest lamb chops I've ever eaten.
There is not a better place to have a lovely Italian meal with a special someone !!!
Their credit card machine has been broke for weeks. Never put ice in drinks. Worst Taco Bell I've ever been to.

Way over rated. I ordered a $26 6oz filet mignon medium rare only to have it served well done.  Took waiter 20 minutes to come and ask how things were, by that time my husband was done with his meal.  Another 40 minutes later I received my replacement steak just in time to have everything boxed to take it home.  Manager never even came to the table.   I won't be back
Great place!!!! Totally worth the five star reviews!!!!!! Superb shawarma and the owners were really great. A must visit!!!!!!!

Very good food. But very poor management. Our appetizers arrived after 20 minutes, food after 35 minutes. We saw the waiter asking the tables if they had ordered the dish or not. Very poor service!! Maybe they were having a bad day!

Heard from a Philly native that this place had amazing cheesesteaks and he is right! 
I ordered a mushroom cheesesteak with grilled onions on the side and it was to die for. I love that it wasn't too cheesy to the point where each bite is too rich. Perfect meat to cheese ratio! Also got cheese fries - amazing. Also got a chocolate vanilla shake. 
This place is great if you want a casual atmosphere. I loved reading about the history of the restaurant and how long it's been around.

Update to my review... apparently after reading what happened the owner contacted me through Yelp to apologize for the misunderstanding . I'm grateful they owned up to their part in it and then got the address for Grandma and sent a card of apology and a gift card to comeback for a second try. I will thank them for that much and for going the extra mile. It will be up to her .  For that I will up from one to three stars for an apology. Thank you.


Totally outraged!!!! My Mother in law and her husband spent many , many dinners at Brookside. They brought our family and grand kids there. My grandfather passed away several years ago and Grand-mom who is 87 RARELY!!! Goes out to eat and this fall she stopped at your restaurant which is hard enough because she's alone with out Grandpa!!  This fall she went at lunch and it was busy and you refused to seat her because she was alone!!! ARE YOU OUT OF YOUR MIND!!!! Be prepared for a rotten review of your establishment!!!!!! I'm spreading the word. It's Christmas morning and she just told us how hurt she was may you be blessed as the rotten people you are !!!

The sushi here is pretty good. I always get the spicy tuna, spicy yellowtail or any of the speciality rolls when I come here. I wouldn't suggest the salmon roe though... I've had it a few times here and its always been a disappointment. The other sushi items are all pretty good. 

Every time I come here I have to get the tempura ramen. Something about those tempura flakes and veggies in the ramen.

I would give them a 4 but I really do get disappointed about the salmon roe (my absolute favorite). Oh and a plus is that they are open pretty late.

All I wanted to do was eat here for my birthday.   Why did I wait 10 minutes before I had to go find a manager?   Thanks for totally ruining all I wanted to have for my birthday dinner.   The table behind us was seated after us and they saw a waitress?  But we waited 10 minutes and no one approached my table.   This made me very unhappy because this is where I wanted to have dinner on my birthday!

Have been to Wister on 3 separate occasions. Each time we have ordered different appetizers from the cauliflower, heirloom tomatos, squid, and the tuna. All were fresh, well prepared and extremely tasty. Our main courses were the chicken for two, the fish dishes, salmon, and the pork chop. Everything was again well prepared with just the right amount of sauce to compliment the dish and season it without your meal swimming in it. The staff was always friendly and helpful without bothering you very 5 minutes to ask if all was ok. This restaurant is a wonderful addition to the Philly dining scene and since it is a BYOB we are grateful to be able to bring our own wine and not have that feeling " how did the check get so high" when deposited on the table after the meal!!!!

Saw this place when I arrived in Reno and knew that this was going to be my hang out spot while waiting for my return flight.   Chelsea the bartender greeted me upon entrance with the warmest smile. The drinks are amazing. The flatbread is delicious and the service was nothing but exceptional. A patron had entered looking for food after the kitchen was closed, Chelsea and the manager Kirsten, both went out of their way to ensure he was able to get some food. Working in Customer Service, this did not go unnoticed. I frequent Reno often for work, and will always put Timber Ridge on my agenda as well as anyone I know will travel to Reno!!!! Thank you for keeping Customer Service alive. Well done Chelsea and Kirsten.
The staff is friendly and the dogs are great!  I really love the gorilla Mac and cheese, but add the Jamaican sauce to it for a lil extra goodness.

I love this place! I order delivery all the time. Love the chicken on a stick, crab rangoon, good noodle dishes too. Singapore mei fun is delicious. Good soups. Fast service. The lunch buffet is nice.

I got the Mistake roll and the Sun roll. The food was okay but this restaurant is NOT kid friendly. We asked to divide a couple of the dishes (Udon Noodles, Chicken Teriyaki) and we were told no. Our server was not very friendly or helpful in working with us. The quality of the food is not enough to overcome the lack of a kid friendly menu. We will not be coking back!

If I could give this place a 10, I would!

The food is seriously amazing.  Make sure you order the dumplings and dan dan noodles for appetizers.  If you are vegetarian they have many options for tofu with the same dishes they can serve with chicken.  If you do not like spice then this may be an issue but for our whole party of 10 everyone had nothing bad to say about this place.  We ordered the green peppercorn tofu, dry pot with tofu and chicken, Chinese broccoli in soy and ginger sauce, and 2 more chicken dishes (can't remember).

Service could be better since we had to ask for napkins multiple times and our waiter was a little slow to get to us when we arrived even though we had reservations.  

Overall,  this place is a must go when you are in Philly!

Came in here for a late lunch with my hubs when were in NOLA for the weekend.  We had the grilled oysters (YUM) and the seafood etouffee- which was just ok.  They def seated us quickly and the servers were attentive, just with all the amazing places to eat in this city, I would probably skip over this one the next time we are in town.
Great place to come for relaxing with food and nice customer service at this spot , will coming back and enjoy future time.

Pho 75 lives up to the hype! It has a pretty large dinning room that is no frills. Cash only. Only serves pho. You pay at the register in the back based on your table number. They give you tea to start. I have tried various types of their pho and all of it was been delicious. Go for the large, it's worth the money!

Just check the hours before going. it closes fairly  early on a weekend night, around 8 pm.

Met up with some friends here., place was kinda busy an the air was working way to well I was so cold. I ordered the classic reg burger an it was deelishous I would definitely go back I'm a burger fanatic, I wish they would add turkey burgers to the menu as well. The fries were deelishous an could have used some more

Gus's is my favorite place to go. I travel for work and look for one everywhere I go. Wife and I walked in and were seated right away.  A waiter came up and took our drink order right away. Then another lady came over to take our food order. She was not very friendly. Pretty slow actually. Food came out and was phenomenal as usual. We ordered the green tomatoes for an app and they were delicious. I always order the 3 Piece white with Mac and Slaw. The Mac and cheese is so good! My wife who has never had Gus's before ordered the 2 piece dark with slaw and beans. She now understands why I love Gus's so much. We tried to order a T-shirt but our waitress with out even attempting to look said they didn't have any in the size requested in the color I chose. I asked about another color and she said not available so I just gave up. Go for the food and the 40 oz beers. You won't regret it
First time eating here and I loved it. Every time I come home I tell myself I'm gonna eat here and I never do. All I can say is it was well worth the wait. The whole meal was lit
I love this place, but I wish they would stop forgetting the tzaki sauce for delivery orders. This is not a sauce that we normally have in our household. Gyro meat does not taste as good without it.
Great food reasonably priced, I had the fried seafood platter and it was great, light,crispy,not greasy and we'll seasoned. It looks like a dive bar but it's just good food without the hype.
I've been going here since high school, and it's still one of my fave places to eat in Chinatown, I always get the house special pho and it's always delicious!!

This review is less about the food and more about the service. It was absolutely incredible. Our server was Jonathan and he made sure we were catered to every want we had due to us celebrating a birthday. His service was extremely exceptional. He gave several suggestions although being a vegetarian which speaks to his knowledge about the menu. The food was amazing and the drinks were even better. We truly appreciated his service and will be returning again because of him. Thank you for making an awesome birthday for our friend.

The food was overall good and we quiet enjoyed the chicken platter and chicken wrap. And the bread was probably the best part. The bread here is amazing. I did have a few issues with the falafel feast. I was told it would be able to feed at least four people but in reality it's probably only good enough for 2 1/2. They really don't give you that many falafel's for how it cost. Do you only get eight falafel 's, I expected at least 14 to 16, Especially since they are more on the smaller side. The dipping sauces were excellent and the salad was excellent but the baba ghanouj was definitely not very good it tasted like they poured a whole container of liquid smoke into it, so much so that that was all you could taste. And the broccoli and pickles vegetables were a little bit lacking in flavor as well. The hummus was also so so nothing to write home about. But that's Tzatziki and cilantro dipping sauces amazing.

Bring your appetite and some tissue because at NOLA Poboys, you'll need both!
I ordered a 12in shrimp poboy here and was asked if spice was ok with me. Excuse me? Spice is my middle name!!! (Disclaimer: spice is not really my middle name, it's Renee) 
Anywhoo, the poboy was SO big and filled with crispy, hot, spicy shrimp!! The kind of spice that brings tears to your eyes but the kind of flavor where you can't stop eating so you're just sitting there crying and eating like something out of a romance novel. *sigh*
This place is kinda hidden although on bourbon street, it was down behind the construction. That probably explains why it wasn't very packed. 
The customer service was great as well. At one point in time the cashier came from behind the counter and walked table to table checking in on customers by name! Impressive!
I can't wait to visit this place again.

Mobile orders not ready on time. Tried it for the first time and my order was not ready at the 12:45 pickup time. I stood there til almost 1:00 asking what the delay was as I had only ordered one burrito and chips with queso. Employees are nice but it would have been faster going through the line as everyone who was in line were already seated enjoying their meal. Pickup ordering is a fail!
Amazing pasta and pizza!!! We had a Sicilian deep dish pizza and pasta carbonara....both were delicious!
Kojaks is a staple in the community. Awesome food at very reasonable prices. The service is always top notch with friendly fast service. Melissa is the best!

Will not come back!

The waitress that took our drink order was pleasant and successfully fulfilled her purpose and returned  with the ordered drinks.

The food waitress was not even remotely sucessful and on top of it had an attitude.  My wife and I ordered identical meals; salad, soup and garlic knots.  We requested the salad arrive first, and that the garlic knots arrive later with the soup.  Well the soup arrived first and we were a bit surprised but very kind about it, the waitress realizing her mistake offered to return the soup to the kitchen.  We said no we would eat the soup but of course would have preferred the requested order and that's when she took offense and replied, in her exasperated voice "look I offered to take the soup back" but, although a bit shocked, we again begged off (I had already put pepper on my potato soup).  She left and some time later arrived with 3 burnt garlic knots, then later with the remainder of the garlic knot order but no salad.  Eventually the salads arrived.  By the way the bill was in excess of eighty dollars, eighty dollars we will never again spend there.  We came to have a pleasant meal of good and wholesome food and save for the waittress we almost got it!
One of our favorite places to eat. You can get anything on the menu annas know it will be good. Love the shop sauce and chicken with mean greens the most.

For a place called biscuit house I'm surprised they use frozen biscuits. 100% not homemade!  Not really sure who eats here but the breakfast is horrible. Biscuits are frozen heated up and the gravy is from a powder mix with water... the grits they serve are tasteless watery and runny, again instant grits. To make it worse I ordered cheese grits and they threw on a little sprinkle of shredded cheese and charged an extra $1.50 for what amounted to not even half a teaspoon of cheese. I let the server know that the grits tasted horrible and she just ignored me and walked away. At the register I let someone else know the grits were horrible and she just said sorry and continued to ring up my bill.

The food here is all instant mix/frozen/cheap. Nothing is cooked in true southern fashion. Even the bacon is thin as paper and just a pile of grease... how can you mess up bacon?! This is not southern "home cooked" food and I am ashamed to think this is what tourists would venture out to eat. The good reviews for this place must be people that have never actually eaten a good southern breakfast (like my mom makes of course with fresh buttermilk biscuits and actual gravy made in a cast iron skillet)  So many other great places, this is a huge pass for me! Don't waste your money.

I absolutely love this place. 

My personal favorite is the BBQ pork with chipotle dipping sauce but each empanada flavor is unique and robust. 

I would also add that the one star review reads more like a personal attack than a food review. It's interesting that the reviewer would be on a first name basis with someone who "doesn't care at all about the customer." My friends and I have been treated warmly at each visit.

It was a disappointing evening.  My wife had been looking forward to trying the Bistro on the Brandywine ever since we passed by it on the way to Longwood Gardens.  Tonight we got a sitter and headed out.  When we  arrived there were a few parties all crammed into a small waiting area by the door - it made little sense as there was a great deal of empty and wasted space - with a little bit of planning and design sense a more comfortable waiting area would have been easily accommodated in the given space.  The hostess was polite, but brusque and not at all friendly.

When seated my wife and I made mention of the ambiance, or more aptly the lack of ambiance.  The room was quite loud, hot, and way too bright - they definitely need a dimmer switch.  The waitress was pleasant and drinks arrived and our order placed quickly - however my wife did have to ask about bread.  Just to comment on the overall service, it was polite, but not at all consistent and the gaps in service were pretty glaring.

The appetizers arrived and were quite good.  The wait for entrees was long.  At one point (perhaps 5 to 10 minutes after apps had been cleared) our waitress came by and let us know that my wife's short ribs were ready and that they were just waiting on my burger - it was a long wait, and our waitress was not seen.  Our food arrived 15 minutes later - our waitress told us that it had been another diners dinner that she saw.  The short ribs were warm, but everything else on the plate was stone cold - the same for me - the burger was OK, but the fries were burned and cold.  Fries and gnocchi were replaced quickly.  My burger was quite good, but the short ribs disappointed.   Overall, the food had potential, but the inconstancy in service was a killer.

So, the overall experience was pretty lousy.  Dinner was $90+, add in the sitter and it was over $120.  We should have brought our kids with us and gone to Chilli's - it would have been a better evening at a third of the price.

I am updating my review for Nori because it is honestly a hidden gem. I've never had anything here I didn't like. I especially recommend the dumpling soup, clear soup, calamari, fat boy roll, fry me a chicken roll, and royal crown roll. The sushi presentation calls for smoke effects, decorative trees and birds. The food quality is there for dine-in, take out, and delivery. They also have a loyalty punch card.

From the outside it doesn't look like much, but the tacos were quite possibly the best I've ever had!! Our food was out within minutes of ordering and each of us loved what we got! We tried different tacos and the black and pinto bean soup and were not disappointed!

This review is only for the hot pot. 

Finally tried Emei for hotpot after hearing about it from friends for such a long time! We got the half and half pot (but as with most half and half situations, the nonspicy half usually gets contaminated half way through, oh well b/c I like spicy, but careful if you don't!)

What I liked about this place is that you can order individual items from the menu. Portions are smaller (and in the $4 range), which is perfect, because you can order as you go. We got most of the traditional things that you may (or may not) want in your hot pot: beef, lamb, potatoes, winter melon, enoki mushrooms, fish balls, beef balls (with filling!), tofu, frozen tofu, leafy greens (lots of different kinds!), vermicelli, spam, fish tofu, beancurd skin among lots of other things I can't remember. 

We had a table of 8, and after about 6 plates of beef (and all of the above mentioned), we were stuffed, and the total was about $20-25 per person (after tip and tax and with soft drinks!), which is really decent for hot pot!! (vs the $45 post tip tax w/o soft drink per person that Jane G charges!!!) This will be my new hot pot spot if I'm craving it and not feeling like making it at home. 

Some good to knows: The fatty beef is really fatty - each slice is half fat. The lamb is not sliced frozen lamb that you may expect (it's fresh and cut in strips, not what I'm used to but not bad). There are ~3 sauces to choose from (sesame oil, sesame paste, or satay sauce) - when they say sesame paste, that's exactly what you get (naive me thought it would be some great mixed sesame sauce, nope, add your own soy sauce etc. for flavoring). Great for large groups - there's a "party room" in the back with 2 large round that tables, which could fit appx 20 people.

What Spudly's lacks in ambience, it more than makes up for in flavor and value. A Metairie institution, this off-the-beaten dive has been serving up super spuds for as long as I can remember. My wife and I recently went for the first time in probably a decade and were happy to see (and taste) things had not changed. The Bucking Bronco (chipped barbeque beef in a tangy barbeque sauce, topped with swiss and chives) is my go-to while the wife enjoys seafood, so she went with the Nab-a-Crab (crabmeat topped with cheese sauce, cheddar and chives). It's a cliche but the menu truly offers something for everyone, and I'm not sure you'll find a more comforting meal in the area.

Wow, what a strange experience.  Walked in around 5pm and the whole staff was standing by the front door.  Nobody really said hello, only looked at us like we showed up to early, we were the only people in the place, maybe they weren't open yet?  We followed someone to a table and sat down.  The restaurant had a good vibe, but the service from start to finish was really strange, and very bad.  The food wasn't good either.  The need had no flavor. The cheese on the nachos was really funny.  The hard shells, soft shells and chips all seem to be store bought.  To be honest, Taco Bell had better food.  Overall, probably he worst Mexican food I've ever ha
Excellent food and service. Really enjoyed our meal experience at Mad Fish. Best meal I've had on St Pete Beach.

Been to this place a few times and really can't complain. All the servers I've had are friendly and helpful. The place is kept pretty clean, including bathrooms, tables, plates, or silverware. I've had the cheesesteak, pizza, chicken bites, and one of their wraps. The cheesesteak is usually good and never chewy. The chicken bites comes with a spicy sauce and your choice of another sauce on the side (I usually get BBQ). Chicken has never been chewy. I've tried the pizza twice but I'm NOT a fan. Had pepperoni and plain cheese but they're both really greasy and wet. Plus, the cheese slides right off.

Two things they could fix: Prices are pretty high and the waiters and waitresses sometimes take too long to get drink or food orders.

I really like Maple Glen Pizza.  Both the pizza and salads are above average for this area and I always receive prompt and friendly service.  We eat here pretty regularly since its near our school and I have never had a bad meal!

This Burger King is old, slow and the staff is not very friendly.  No smiles, no enthusiasm and no spark left in their souls.  And what's up with that untamed jungle next to the drive-thru?  Take a lesson from the Sheldon & Hillsborough BK, and tear that mofo down and start over!

I refuse to spend money in this place.  they ask for a ten dollar deposit for a credit report on all applicants...   got news for you...   the report is free...  and this is a scam.  Not only a scam, but the lines at the door?  I don't think so.  I doubt that any char broiled oysters they sell, match DRAGO;s where char broiled is king, and also invented.   The service folk were rude when I went in to apply, and that was long before katrina hit.  Same folks still workin there.   meaning?  the credit check scam on applicants is a total hoax... they never hire...  they just take app's, charge ten bucks, and make a killin...  go across the street to felix, or go to desire, or anywhere else in the city... if you want oysters... even deanie's down the street.  or casemento's uptown for the real deal, but spending money at acme's?  Wasteful...
I love this place Very Cozy and Romantic & the people are nice and friendly specially our server and our Food everything was Delicious and its BYOB!!! Definitely Coming back!!!

Have yet to find a burger in Indy that's keeps me coming back consistently. I realize five guys is not just an Indy establishment but nonetheless I don't go out of my way to get to it. Burger is okay nothing to really write home about. I got my burger "all the way" which has all the toppings on it. I find it better sometimes to get all the flavors on the burger and just pick off what I don't prefer. Only thing I didn't prefer on the burger was the grilled onions but again that's just a preference. Don't get me wrong they do their version of the burger well.it just wasn't as juicy as I would've wanted and the bun was super soggy after just a few minutes. I'm also picky about my fries and these just aren't it chief. Menu is reminiscent of In-n-out.

Went here tonight for dinner first time in over a year.  I couldn't believe how bad the service and the food was.. Yuck!  I was there on a Friday at 6:30 and the parking lot was almost empty..I will not go back!

The food is delicious but their service is crappy. I always go through the drive-through and they're short and snappy. They always get irritated when I asked for extra sauce. One tiny little cup of mustard isn't gonna cut it. They rush you when you order and always assume you're through ordering.

25 people in line 1 server and 1 cashier. 15 customers in front of us and it took over 25 f'ing minutes to get to the counter and no apologies. Ordered but they had no mixed veggies, said it was 3 minutes. Ten minutes later I go back up to the counter and they the 2 employees argued about giving the veggies to the drive thru. 45 minutes to finally get our food. The place is a disaster, trash is overflowing, tables uncleaned, and small portions maybe because I said something. I'm very loyal to Pandas but the lack of attention to customer service is driving people away from here.
The portions are great the lobster, seafood and rice was amazing. Although it lacks any kind of ambiance the food made the trip worthwhile.
Good food and service ! The place is book all day so book early ! The chicken and rost duck is very good ! Try it I recommend !
Long wait, wrong order, sour tea and I had to tell him my order at the window again when I got there and they still managed to get it wrong.
Passed by this place for years. Decided to try and was not disappointed. Great taste. Great specials. Filling quantities. Won't make the mistake of missing this place again
I LOVE Xtreme Taco.  Authentic Mexican food and its sooooo delicious.  Sofie is always so friendly and attentive,  her customer service is  A1. So glad there's a new location.

Comforting and Flavorful. Pizza is one of the few foods where I admittedly come off as a snob, but when the mood strikes for a comforting overabundance of cheese, Midway fits the ticket. The flavors on the Kingpin delivered, and the crust was a decent enough vehicle. The space was one of the most casual and enjoyable on Freret, and I'll definitely return.

I have been three times. 
The first time I went, I got the egg rolls with the bacos. The bacos were simple with a soft steamed bun. Everything tastes good and I left wanting to come back. 

The second experience was the worst restaurant experience I have had. I had been in the mood for a vermicelli bowl, so I got the ba chi bowl thinking "The restaurant is called ba chi. This must be one that's impossible to fuck up."
Well, leave it to Ba Chi... It was shit. I got a bowl with a clump of noodles that would not separate. I couldn't pick it up without picking up all of the noodles in my bowl with my chop sticks. The Ba Chi protein (pork belly) was all fat. I know this is the fatty part of the pig and I can eat cracklin (fried pig fat) for days. This grossed me out. It was so slimy, I lost my appetite. I was willing to order just one baco before we left, but the waiter took so long to come back, that my friend was already done with her meal. 

The service is never good here. Ever. 

I did come back one night just for bacos because my friend loves them but I did not want to be there at all. I hope I never go back.
We eat at this Dennys before our toy parade. Service was friendly but a tad bit slow.

Visiting from out of town, this was the first restaurant we came to. Food was subpar and quite overpriced. The ambience and location was quite cool tho as it was right in the heart of the city. Otherwise, I think there are better places to eat.

The employees were very rude to us. The first thing the lady said to me once I chose to get a bowl was "brown or white?" not explaining what it is, and when I responded in confusion she give more attitude and said "brown or white?" The employees were not friendly and looked miserable to be there. I also ordered two tacos to go, and the woman there slapped the guacamole on, and half of it missed the taco and was on the aluminum foil.

If you're looking to explore new tastes, this is the place. The owner is an Eller student in one of my classes, so I decided to check it out. The place doesn't look like much from outside but there was a wait, which is a good indicator, especially, for places that don't lure you in with fancy signs. 

Definitely impressed; the best way to describe it is an Asian Melting Pot. Fondue style but with exotic broths, unlimited variations of sauce mixtures and large portions of meat and vegetables. My favorite was the mushrooms and beef.

I was craving a blueberry muffin and I am grain and wheat free. I walked in tonight and found an almond flour blueberry muffin that was heavenly!!! 
 
I also found chicken broth in a quart . I wish I could eat there everyday!! I feel it's good food prepared with care. I am taking good care of my body and Kara Lynn has what I need. Next time I go I want to eat their paleo bread. 
Thank you Kara Lynn, Coming from
CALIFORNIA recently , and bring spoiled with all kinds of variety of foods, your  place is a safe haven for well prepared healthy food. The place feels like home!

I'm going to have to go against the grain on this one and only give two stars. Let me start by saying that I liked the feel of this restaurant. The dining room was nicely decorated, the lighting was good, there were whimsical light fixtures decorated with empty wine bottles, and another in the next room outfitted with wine glasses. The table settings were nice except that I think they should consider including a second fork. If you want your guests to order more than one course, and lets face it, what good business person doesn't? I witnessed twice where a server had removed a plate from the table from the first course, along with the lone fork, only to have to take one from an empty table when the entrees were served. This could be avoided by simply providing a full place setting in the first place. 

I had the New York Strip, while the partner in crime had Beef Stroganoff. We both felt that they were under seasoned. My steak was served with, or on rather, a pile of mashed potatoes. One of the most abundant elements found on this planet, salt, was nowhere to be found on this dish. I can only assume that this is because every other diner there that evening had about 35 years on us, and that the chef d' cuisine was concerned about patrons salt intake. Gotta keep them from pickling themselves before desert. The only flavor I could make out, beyond the obvious, beef and potato, was an over use of rosemary. One would think that a Parmesan crusted steak served with prosciutto would lend a little more flavor than just rosemary. I did not try the stroganoff, but I'm pretty sure I can take Ben D's word for it when he says it was rather bland. It still amuses me to think that this was a guy who was perfectly happy to call a can of Chef Boyardee dinner when I met him. I digress. 

I'm pretty sure we'll give it another try. Nothing about this visit said they don't deserve another try. It is possible that they were just having anniversary jitters. We did pop in on what happened to be their one year anniversary after all.

Found this gem when visiting my home town. I didn't even know it existed until I found it on Yelp  We had a Greek salad with the Greek dressing and it is one of the best I've had. The pepperoni pizza with extra cheese was sooo good. Split a 14inch and the small salad and still had two slices leftover. I will definitely try the lasagna pizza next time as well. The staff was very friendly and attentive. Great way to wrap up the year!
Like Cici's. If you like the taste of doughy and/or burnt to a crisp crust (but nothing in between!), this is your place

Terrific, straightahead Vietnamese food in Camden. Great pho and everything else.  Super friendly staff.

Couldn't be happier to know about this place.

Two very enthusiastic thumbs up.
