Wonderful seafood place to eat. Indoor and outdoor seating on the inter coastal. Within walking distance to our motel. Lots of variety on the menu. Great service.

This place is an absolute must visit if you're in Philly. Their pountines are to die for if you're a cheesy fries lover. Bria and Allie were extremely helpful and patient with my moderately indecisive wife and me, and every thing came out quickly and fresh. I can't tell you how happy I am with Sandler's. I am not exaggerating when I say this is my favorite restaurant in Philadelphia.

Ordered thru SkipTheDishes app. Order was wrong, cold and took 2 1/2 hrs to arrive... mind you we don't live but 5 minutes from the restaurant. According to skip the restaurant claims they are short staffed.. but we received a call 5 minutes after ordering that they had to substitute an item and were working on our order. The delivery driver had to sit at the restaurant for 45 mins waiting on our order.

Wow. Amazing food, and even greater service. We arrived hot and sweaty after a day of biking around Nashville in 90+ heat, and got recommended to come here by the bike rentals down the street at Green Fleet.

A cold marg (blueberry) and in my case, a pineapple/basil mojito and a half platter of Memphis nachos (bbq, some sort of chipotle cream or something, cheese, the things dreams are made of) we were feeling more like humans and less like sunburned zombies. I recommend both veggie tacos (one with black bean, one with plantains). 

Our server was insightful and generous-- when I ordered both veggie tacos, as well as the turnip green sides, she confirmed that I wasn't vegetarian, telling me that the greens are cooked in chicken broth. In addition, she asked us whether we needed some more water for the road as we were leaving. All in all, this is the sort of neighborhood restaurant that I would keep on coming back to if I lived in the area.

Really good,  but small,  local breakfast lunch place.  Peanut butter burger (instead of ketchup or mustard). But just well executed standards:  eggs and meat btw pork roll and scrapple with bacon and ham. Reasonably priced.
Great place other than the two bartenders at the basement and the scrawny eyed skinny boy man manager. Go upstairs the service is much better.

Everything I've ordered from here is very good. The pizza is delicious and cheesy and doesn't have to much sause. The wings are good size and all the flavors I've tried are very good. You defiantly get a lot of food for the price. I'm glad it's so close to my house because it's my go to for pizza and wings now. Everyone that works here is very friendly.

It was good- friendly service. Broth could be more flavorful. I'm originally from Northern California where there were Pho restaurants in every corner. I took that into consideration giving this review. I ordered a pho tai- the meat were curled in balls- I think it was frozen and thrown in the hot broth which causes it "curl". That was disappointing. The spring roll was good but fell apart because it wasn't rolled tightly. I'll give it another try.

When I was in New Orleans, I was intrigued with visiting this tourist hotspot after hearing about their chargrilled oysters. After getting their, we had to wait in line for probably 15 minutes (seems like this place always as a line, but seems to move relatively quickly). Most of the tables are family style, so don't be surprised if you are sitting at a table with others. The menu contains the full spread of Louisiana cuisine from crawfish and oysters to Po-boys and smoked sausage. We started our meal with a dozen raw oysters, half dozen chargilled oysters and an order of the crawfish hush puppies. The raw oysters were large and extremely fresh. The crawfish hush puppies were perfectly crisp on the outside with melt-in-your mouth crawfish filling. The chargrilled oysters were out-of-this world with the oyster sitting in its own juices mixed with a garlic butter sauce and topped with melted romano cheese. For entrees, we got the deep-fried crawfish Po-boy, the Variety Plate (with samplings of gumbo, jambalaya, smoked sausage, and red beans on rice), and the crawfish etoufee. The entrees were good, not the best but good. What surprised me was the somewhat mediocre quantities for the amount of money they were charging. Service was ok, as were the entrees. The appetizers were the best part of the meal. I definitely suggest dinning hear once to say that you did, but there are much better and more reasonably priced eateries all around New Orleans.

Such a great unique and special place! Thank you for sharing your wonderful dishes from across the global. This place is a must try for anyone that loves Middle Eastern/Indian food! Will visit here many times in the future when in St Louis!
Stopped in for breakfast today a little after 10:00 AM and the restaurant was packed. A very short wait and then I knew why! Great breakfast, friendly and busy staff. I highly recommend Bobo's.

They charge higher prices than what shows on their menu. When I asked the cashier she said "Our menu is outdated and we haven't updated the prices yet" then just stared at me when I told her I'm not paying more than what the menu reads. All she "could do" was give the drink to us for free to offset the difference. Pizza is OK but not worth the money. Their lines do move quickly. Salads are small and not great. Just another over priced location within the resort.

A-MAZ-ING. I got pulled chicken with the corn (get the corn) and the chicken was so moist plus they give you a generous portion. My boyfriend got the BOSS sandwich with mashed potatoes and holy cow it was delicious. I'll be getting that when I return. Not to mention the skillet of cornbread, I could've eaten just the butter with a spoon. Overall this place was awesome and it was a pretty quick meal!! Service was great. Will for sure be coming back

My parents took us here claiming it is one of their favorite restaurants in area. I truly enjoyed all the food I ordered and tried of my family's plates. They are somewhat high priced but worth it if you are not on a tight budget. Definitly worth a try. I will definitly go back .... if the parents are paying :)

As the weather where I live currently is abysmal, I'm reminded of NOLA and the wonderful food here. Words aren't accurate descriptors of all the fabulous tastes, smells, and delicious moments this location provides. 
Trust me- this is enjoyable.

Love this place! First time was on a groupon. Rarely do I ever go back to Groupon restaurants because they're horrible. NOT THIS PLACE. IT IS GREAT! I'm a Jersey Girl and we know our diners.Finally a "diner" that gets breakfast right! Great home fries, large omelets, friendly staff. Will swing by some night for a desert waffle. My boyfriend got waffles with cream chipped beef and loved it!

Service was extremely slow even though the restaurant was pretty much empty Maybe 5-7 tables of people total. Shrimp appeared to be frozen and defrosted. Had a soft texture, not firm at all. Cajun pasta has a very thin out of the can watery tomato sauce with almost no cajun flavoring at all. Jambalaya was pasty and blan. We left most of the meals on the plates and left. No one asked if there was anything was wrong as almost full plates were returned. The worst part was 3 of 4 of our party were pretty ill the next day and had to stay in rooms due to the symptoms, I will leave out the details on that one --- Definitely from that restaurant. We hadn't eaten any other food that meals. I wish we had read the reviews prior to dining there. The majority seem to be the same kind of issues all with a response of sorry we will work with our staff to improve -- that stop does not seem to be happening.

I don't know about the ambiance but the food is where it's at!!!!! This is me and my bf's goto place. We get delivery more often than dine in through grubhub but their food is always so good and comes in big portion. If you do dine in you should try their weekly specials. We usually get the pad thai, drunken noodles and some veggie dish. I personally think it's the best thai place in town. Be aware though that their extra spicy is more spicy than habanero or flavor of india extra spicy level.

Serendipity. That's how we ended up here. We'd been exploring New Orleans all day on a Sunday and everywhere we looked for dinner was closed. Shaya was open but surprisingly packed on a Sunday around 4p.m. Be warned yelpers... reserations are a must especially at dinner. Luckily, they had patio seating on this crispy cool night. 

Shaya is an outstanding place. It's lovely and the food is great. To start we had the tahini hummus (of course) with the house made pita pillows. Perfection.

I opted for the roasted chicken which was tender, juicy and the flavor so blendled to perfection. It's enough for two meals btw and they will happily box it up for you to bring home your leftovers. The guy got the slow roasted lamb and let me sample it. I nearly stole this dish from him. It was so damn good.

We learned while we were there that the upstairs is where they prep the food because of the tiny kitchen downstairs. We also learned there are two private dinging rooms upstairs, one of which overlooks Magazine. I would love to eat up there very soon.

Service was slow...otherwise this would've been 5 stars easily.

Ordering pizza because I'm too tired to cook and study...so...they (Marissa) were SUPER NICE on the phone. I opted for carryout since I was around the corner. I went with the specials.  My pizza was ready right around the quoted time. I arrived early and the restaurant is very clean. The cleanest pizza place I've seen in a long time.  I haven't been this happy with pizza hut in...ever. never will I ever go back to the one near my house (cough, broad ripple). So, absolutely nice & clean & perfect pizza.

We recently had take out fish and chips from Brits, and although tasty, it was really pricey compared to other places.  I'm not sure that it was that much better in terms of taste or quality to justify the cost.

We had two one piece fish and chip meals, and a tiny side of mushy peas for $38.  I would have expected $25 to be a reasonable price.

As mentioned, the food was good, but not exceptional.  The mushy peas were as expected, and a nice treat.  The service for takeout was fairly quick, we had our food within 15 mins.

This place is the mountaintop for Chicago style pizza in Tampa.  

If you've spent any time around DC this might help - Cappy's reps the thick tomato pie in the same way 2 Amy's or Pupatella has enshrined the Neopolitan.  

So back to the story...  It has been about twelve years since I have been to Cappy's, and for those twelve years I have carried around a strong gustal-memory (not sure if that term is legal, but I'm claiming it if it hasn't yet been coined) of what happens during that first bit of a Cappy's Chicago-style pie.

Last night, while the location had moved (but retained the same Cappy's style, feel, and cash-only approach from S. Howard), that taste was there - as round, tasty, and savory as it was twelve years ago.

Impressive.

I mean, over twelve years you are bound to idealize a taste to some degree.  It's like a person - you forget about the rough edges and retain the good stuff.  But Cappy's totally delivered.  Tomato, pepperoni, mushroom, spinach, cheese, and buttery-crust happiness.

Also, GREAT service from Summer.  There isn't a great deal of room in the new place, but Summer did her best to pull tables together and accomodate our large party.  She was informative, helpful, and pleasant.  What a plus for Cappy's to have someone of her caliber spending time with those in the seats.  

Thanks again Cappy's.  I hope it won't be another twelve years.

One of my favorite places for Ethiopian food. The staff are super friendly and the food is out of this world. If you haven't tried Ethiopian food before, the spices are similar to Indian food. I would highly recommend Meskerem if you're looking for a new experience.

Not the best Chinese place, but pretty good, plus they deliver! The sesame chicken was good, and the bourbon chicken was okay.  I was not impressed with the lo mien noodles.   I thought the flavor was bland, and barely any veggies in it, and the pork was average.  They have good customer service.  Best to order ahead! Id like to see them improve overall taste quality.

I don't usually write yelp reviews but my experience was especially bad.  Ordered the thursday special (free mozzarella sticks with any large pizza).  When the food arrived, the mozz sticks were literally frozen, as if they had gone straight from the freezer to the to go box.  It was really unappetizing.  They also forgot to add onions to our pizza. Despite living in the neighborhood we definitely won't order again.
everything was spectacular!!!  If you liked Tortilliarilla in the West End, you'll love this place!!!  Get the chorizo cheese dip!!!!

Love the huge portions.

The food is served in a skillet.

I need MOAR ham.

Service is expedient.

Sushi Land is your typical cheap sushi joint, really only suitable for college kids looking to eat on a budget. I would not come again. 

Our party of 5 was seated immediately. Staff was very friendly. I got the chirashi bowl -- white tuna was discolored and tasted strange; salmon was good; tuna didn't blow my mind. Overall, not impressed and it didn't hit the spot. I just felt "meh" and even gross after I finished. I liked their miso soup and salad with delicious sesame dressing though. I also tried the green tea tempura ice cream, which was fine but not mind-blowing. I love green tea ice cream, but I found the tempura too chewy and not crispy enough.

We typically go out for a special diner on or around our anniversary. We've liked Romano's since 1999 or 2000.I tried the Truffle Mac & Cheese. It was flavorful but lukewarm. There were some pieces of Prosciutto but not that much.  My wife had the Steak & potatoes. She liked it but wasn't satisfied. However, our two guests loved their Pollo Caprese and Penne Rustica. At least two of us enjoyed the special dinner date. Too bad it wasn't my wife and I. Nevertheless, try them out. It is a great franchise.
Worst wings i have ever had! Not sure how this place can say they are a wing joint with such awful wings. Fries and onion straws are good but thats about it.

I came here for the first time for lunch today. It wasn't too busy, considering the fact that it was midday on a weekend inside the market. The service was a little sub par. Our waitress wasn't particularly rude, but she just wasn't very nice or attentive either. The food was amazing though. I ordered the cheesesteak, while my gf ordered the market summer salad. The salad was huge, beautiful, and delicious. The cheesesteak was arguably one of the best I've ever had. The cheese sauce alone was to die for. I'd love to come back here again during my next day trip to Philly.

I have been to a lot of PF Changs but the one at westshore plaza tampa is the worst. We ordered chicken satay in appetizer and 2 drinks. To give us the worst start to the dinner the satay was served so cold that it wasn't even getting off the stick. Then to add on to it further, the server forgot one drink. When we mentioned this to the server we got a really cold reaction. Followed by this we got the dinner, the server didn't have any clue what was on the plate and in which plate there was egg and meat and very conveniently said please figure out yourself. Well we did and found egg in one of the vegetarian entree. So obviously we had to return it and while the rest of the family started dinner one kept waiting for the vegetarian pad thai to show up.
Overall it was the worst experience at PF chang and I am definitely not going back there. And yes before I miss the cherry on the cake- The fortune cookie... Not only it was all moist it had no fortune..wow!!

I always come here for fresh and unique sashimi. The chef used to work at Peter's sushi and other sushi places for a long time before he opened his own restaurant. My favorite dish is live scallops sashimi, uni with quail egg and fresh squid tentacles with house made sauce. Don't forget to try their beautiful and delicious poached egg in beef broth.

This restaurant is a true gem. It's extremely romantic and charming. I went in a group of 4 people and after two bottles of wine, an appetizer, and 4 main courses the total bill was about 1/3 of the price of what it would be in a city like San Francisco. 

The food quality is comparable to some of the top Italian restaurants I have been to around the US and if I'm ever back in St. Louis, I'm definitely going back here for dinner. I have nothing but positive feedback for this restaurant. It was an unforgettable experience.

I dined in here with my dad a couple weeks ago and this place did not disappoint. I ordered the David Letterman Burger with fries and the burger was big, packed with toppings, and tasty. The meat was well seasoned and cooked properly. The thousand island dressing tasted home made and not from a can. The bacon tasted fresh and the fries were hot and crispy. My dad had the regular burger and he enjoyed it as well. Although the wait time was a bit long due to them being busy, it didn't take too long for our food to come out. The decor was all Jaws movie themed which felt nostalgic and cool. As a friendly reminder this place is cash only so leave the plastic in your pocket. I'd come back here for sure.

Had friends fly in for the day yesterday, and they had a restaurant in mind but when we got there it was closed till 5 and we were starving. So on our way back towards downtown I remembered going to Konas with my son a few weeks ago and suggested we go there. When we went, it was past the lunch rush hour. I thought the service would be slow since usually restaurants let employees out after lunch rush. Instead we were seated as soon as we got in, had 2 guys that treated us like kings, and even the chef came out to talk to us. Everything, from appetizers, to main dish, and dessert, we're all amazingly good! I'm very happy to report that from now on I will be taking a lot of my clients and friends there to eat. 5 stars all around!

Go home you New York and Chicago haters. Provel cheese makes for an astoundingly good pizza. The cheese is similar to American, just with a little more kick. 
The thin crust pizza is made to crunch so well in between my teeth, I love it. The sauce is delicious and of course the toppings are awesome (especially the provel cheese). The service at this location is prompt and friendly.
This place and its pizza are definitely a St. Louis mainstay.

So, my daughter had a few gift cards for stores at the Deptford Mall and this Red Robin is right near her favorite store (Hot Topic), so we stopped in for lunch.  Plus, my son had mentioned eating here and bragging about a burger with macaroni and cheese on it, so of course my daughter wanted to go, lol. 

I am not a huge burger person, but they had Nachos on the menu so that's what I ordered.  They were ok. I wasn't crazy about the chili they put on them. 

My daughter ordered a mint chocolate chip shake which was really big and she loved it. She also ventured out and ordered a burger that had an egg on it. She loved it. Plus she loved the bottomless fries. 

Hubby ordered a whisky burger. He said it was good. 

There's really nothing special for me that would make me come here unless we happen to be at the mall but I wouldn't eat in the dining room again.  

The bar area is much nicer and the booths in their are more private.

Been getting to frequent this authentic Mexican restaurant. .. The food is really, really good and the prices are very affordable.   My favorite is the "wet burrito".  Chicken, rice and a heavenly mole sauce over it.  The deep fried cheesecake is deliciously decadent and surprisingly light.  The specials menu is always full of surprises - do not hesitated to order off it!

I really love this place, and not just because it's about a 3 minute walk from my apartment.  Everything I've had from Gusto has been tasty and pretty affordable.  This past weekend I ordered 3 large pizzas for pickup, they had them ready in 20 minutes on a Saturday night and my total was $39. Not bad at all. The ingredients are great too, they used portobello mushrooms on my mushroom pizza and the crust was on the thinner side and crispy (a huge plus for me).  I absolutely would give them 5 stars if they accepted credit cards.  I'd also order from them more often if they accepted credit cards.

A three-course lunch for $9.95? Can't beat it! I went here with a friend today and left happy that I had such a delicious meal without spending a fortune. I had the chicken coconut soup, which was refreshing and delicious, the crispy roll, and Pad Thai. The Pad Thai was a bit bland, but satisfied my craving. The only reason I knocked off a star was for the service (really slow to start, then extremely rushed once we started eating) and inconsistencies with the food (my Pad Thai had 3 small pieces of chicken, my friend's was filled with it). It's absolutely beautiful inside, BYO, and a great lunch deal.

Such a fun atmosphere and the crab cakes are awesome! The manager Tim and our server Jessica were both amazing and very accommodating. We'd definitely come back next time we're in New Orleans for sure!

Definitely a hole in the wall but so so worth it. Love the food and staff. It is the only place in town that has late night sushi that is high quality. Yuki is such a kind server and is always so attentive and giving. I am always get excited to see her there.

Had a wonderful lunch today. I had the apple and strawberry salad with a beautiful blueberry vinaigrette dressing. My friend had the zucchini lasagna and it was delicious. 
Waitress was excellent, service meal etc was perfect.

This does not taste like the ones you get from the Halal carts in NY. Very bland and little flavor. The place itself is nice and has wall seating. If you're looking for authentic halal guys, go to the one in NY or check out the halal food trucks in the area

Another case of Yelp won't allow a zero stars rating. I am really disappointed about this as the other ratings, the menu, and the pictures really had me convinced this was going to be great. Ok, its fathers day so I was prepared to wait a short while for a table. One hour plus is not a short while. Unless there is free beer included with your order, I won't typically wait more than 20 minutes. There are just to many other options available to wait that long. Its an indication of inept management. So after the excessive wait, we were seated, and our order taken very quickly. And we sat, and sat some more, and saw orders going to tables that sat after us. The table next to us, that was seated after us and ordered after us asked for the manager. He came over, they complained, and low and behold their food showed up. To make matters worse, what one of them got was exactly what the wife had ordered. Trying to be a nice guy for my daughter on this day I had not raised any stink to this point, but that was enough for the wife. She asked for the manager and explained to him the seating, ordering vs. serving issue. He said he'd see what was going on and walked off. Next thing was the waitress showed up and "explained" she doesn't know how things are working in the kitchen so can't explain why others are getting their food, but not us. That was it. After over 2 hours in this establishment, we left hungry. This was bad enough that there is nothing that could entice me to come give them another chance. I am quite sure they lost more customers today than they gained return guests as we were definitely not the only unsatisfied people.

This chik fil a is the worst location I have ever been to.

They don't live up to the chik fil a brand.

The parking lot is always slammed and can't drive through it

None of the employee are ever smiling & it's mostly high school kids talking with each other instead of working

Sushi Club offers an All-You-Can-Eat menu, as well as standard and A-la-Cart items. We opted for the All-You-Can-Eat Sushi menu as we had a 20% off coupon from our local mailer. Included were the regular and special rolls, as well as appetizers, salads, and soup items. Make note that there is a charge if you over order and leave waste/uneaten sushi rolls - this is a good rule to cut down on waste and didn't pose an issue for our group. What I really like here is that when you order an item, it comes in relatively small quantities, allowing you to try many things. You can also order multiples of any item - in case you aren't as adventurous and want to stick to stuffing your face with your favorite rolls :) The sushi we enjoyed was fresh and tasty enough. They offered everything from cooked to raw sushi and sashimi. They had simple rolls and some more elaborate "special" rolls. Although we clearly favored some rolls over others, everything was enjoyable. Although the sushi here isn't "the best we've ever had", it is still fresh and geared for the American palate, so it should be enjoyable to most anyone who enjoys sushi. Come hungry and ready to eat, because for the price of the All-You-Can-Eat menu, you really can't go wrong here. The restaurant was clean and service was friendly and prompt. You aren't left waiting too long between orders. Prices vary depending on the meal (lunch vs. dinner) and whether you come in during the week or a weekend. On average it is approx. $25 per adult (less for children), for the All-You-Can-Eat menu.

Although I myself prefer a more traditional sushi that is less fried, less cream cheese filled, and more raw choices - I must give 5 stars for the value here. PLENTY of choices, small quantities per order, that help you have variety on your plate, and although very Americanized, the sushi is tasty.
Very very good. Got a falafel and a lamb gyro.  The flavor, sauce to ingredient ratio, A1.  And they are fast. Would recommend.
The fish sandwich was disgusting. Could bring myself to try!  Errs are good but will not be back!!!

The oysters and cocktails were GREAT!!! The crab cakes were ok compared to The Jazz Playhouse's,  but  not bad at all.  And the customer service is quite nice.  Overall, the food was VERY seasoned and tasty. 

We will definitely be returning!

If you love shellfish, you'll love this place. They fry and boil. I opted for the build your own boil and had blue crab, clams and crawfish. The service was prompt and the boil came out in a ballooned oven bag that fogged up my glasses when I opened it so the food was definitely hot. I ordered the Hot n Crazy sauce which has a moderate spice level and is very good. My only complaint is I wish they had draft beer. The do have bottled beer though. We will return.

We came here for dinner and it was a huge hit. We sat outside, which was a gamble as summer in New Orleans is no joke when it comes to hot humidity. Luckily it wasn't that bad and there were fans outside.

We ordered several plates and they were all really good. The pita was different from other pitas I've had, but was delicious. My favorite was probably the mujadara, a black eyed pea dish. The crispy halloumi was also good. I really enjoyed the heirloom tomato salad. The roasted cauliflower and falafel were ok, but other people at my table loved it more than me.  The lamb smelled amazing and was good according to the people who ate it, but I didn't have it.

Chipotle has been in the Philly area for at least two years; what took me so long to try one?

I thought they were going to be a Qdoba clone, but I was wrong. Their not-quite-fast food is fresh, delicious (not mind-blowing, but far from average), affordable, and I might have heard something somewhere about it being organic. The burritos are scrumptious, and nothing beats a triple threat of soft tacos. Carnitas, barbacoa, and steak, anyone?

Find them in the Plymouth Meeting Mall. Parking can be a bear during busy hours, but it's worth the effort if you love Chipotle.

I visited here while I was in Philly on business, because everyone said that it was a "must visit" experience. I have to admit, the food was great - the ultimate comfort/treat food, and really affordable too. The only problem is, the noise. I visited during lunch time on a weekday and the place was packed, so it was hard to find a seat. It was really noisy too.

Service was quick, and the food was filling and tasty. I would have preferred eating somewhere quieter, though.

If you're in the area and fancy an authentic fast-food experience, this is a nice place to eat, but it's not somewhere you'd go for a formal meal. Maybe I just had the place described to me badly since I'm not from the area, but I'd just like to emphasize that it IS fast food.

Staff is very friendly, restaurant is clean, and the food is tasty. Only issue was I ordered a west coast fish burrito and It came without  fish. It tasted great but would have been a 5 star for sure if it had the fish. We also ordered rolled tacos which were very tasty and the queso dip was very good too!! Make sure to check your order before you leave to make sure everything is there.

Made an onsite take-out order yesterday 5/8. Burgers are always good here, exept my double cheesburger had way to much salt added! On the bad side, their milkshake are a total rip-off as they charged 5.99 each and they come in a 12oz coffee cup!! My bill was $36 and change for 2 single cheeseburgers, 1 double cheeseburger, 2 (harly) large fries and 2 small 12oz milkshakes.................unfortunately I will never return here even though this used to be one of my favorite burger joints.  I will not tolerate being ripped-off!!

The restaurant has had a makeover in the last 5 years or so but the menu hasn't changed much. Why change a good thing? When I come here I either come for the Penne alla Diavola or the Cozze Gorgonzola. The penne has fresh baby lobster and chicken with the best cream sauce mixed with cayenne pepper and diced tomatoes. So yummy! The Cozze Gorgonzola mussels are so delicious! They are steamed in garlic, leeks, and a most delectable tomato cream wine gorgonzola sauce. I am drooling just thinking about them. 

This past Friday after work, my friend and I wanted to hang out on a patio and just chill. We were in the mood to relax, enjoy some wine and have a light dinner. I suggested this place knowing that the patio is wonderful and rarely busy, plus I wanted those mussels and I wanted her to try them too! 

The patio was open and we were seated by the hostess. Our server, Amy, greeted us and told us the specials. She was very attentive and friendly and made us feel right at home. We ordered the mussels to share. I ordered the house pinot grigio to drink and my friend ordered a glass of sangria. 

The mussels didn't disappoint! I would have to say that this place makes some of the best mussels in the city. They have so much flavour to them and you get a huge bowl of them. I can't imagine eating all of those myself but I have a feeling I could manage. We were also served warm bread and we sopped up the scrumptious sauce with it. So good! The wine was good but the sangria was excellent. I would definitely order that again too.

The patio is still lovely. It is especially romantic in the evening with the stringed white lights above. The restaurant itself is also very chic and modern with romantic lighting. I always feel like I am eating in style, however, it can get a bit noisy at times.

A wonderful evening out and I look forward to my next patio date!

Was in on the 21st of June and sat at the bar. We order appetizers and had a couple of drinks. I decided to try the antipasto plate. I was so disappointed. For $24  dollars it should have been a nicer plate. It was a joke. To small for that price. There should have been crackers or toasted bread points served with it. If you are going to serve roasted peppers and cheese you should serve bread points. It looked like the kitchen had no idea how to serve a plate like this.

An explosion of flavor.  Love that you can have as many toppings as you want. The bowls are a nice big portion and everything was super fresh.  It seems a bit pricey for a lunch. The staff isn't very helpful in letting you know how the ordering process works or what the different sauces are. 

I would definitely go back.
Wow. One of the best croissants I've had outside of Paris. You must try it!

Among the top 5 cheesesteaks I have had!  Maybe top three.  Perfect roll, not fluffy at all.  I got extra meat and cheese.  The meat was thicker but moist and flavorful.  Cheese was below the meat and on top.  My wife reports the the shrooms were sautéed just right.

Get the OYSTERS !!!! The grilled oysters are SO GOOD. I know for sure I had another dish with my oysters but the oysters were so good that I can't even recall them because they don't compare. I'm pretty sure whatever I had was good as well or I would have remembered. I always remember when something is either horrible or really good. The service was okay, it was packed the day I went so I understand why the waiter was a bit slow.

I was so excited to try this place because the pictures looked so good!  As you walk in, your go through the kitchen which is weird.  No one really greets you, you just stand there awkwardly for a minute or so.  We were seated in the back of the restaurant and for whatever reason it smelled weird.  Kind of like a heavy cleaning odor on top of something funky.  Anyways, I ordered the chicken and waffles and my husband ordered the banana and prosciutto french toast.  The food wan't bad at all.  In fact, it was pretty good.  Can't go wrong with chicken and waffles.  My husbands came out as a sandwich, which was kind of weird but he seemed to enjoy it.  Before our food came out they gave us some mini biscuits with almond butter.  The wait staff seemed to be moving kind of slow, and didn't have a lot of pep in their step.  Also a few of them were sitting around in the back not doing anything.  Maybe they were management? lol.

Amazing lobster rolls!! If you're like me and crave fresh seafood all the time but live in Indiana then this place is a gem! There is also a location in noblesville. 

It's fast-casual style that also has alcohol. You order at a counter and they give you a table #. Once your order is ready they bring your food right out to you and clear it once you have finished. The food came shockingly fast too, maybe 10 minutes total wait time. 

The Lobster roll was amazing. It was presented so nicely with giant claw meat sticking out of the top of the buttered and toasted roll. I wanted to get 100 more of these. They're at market price so can be expensive compared to other items on the menu but you need to try at least once! The lobster and the "originals" part of the menu come with a side. I recommend the fries because they are seasoned really well and crisp! 

100% cannot wait to go back and start to try everything this place has to offer.

We dined in for a late lunch and had high expectations due to all the 5 star reviews, it was out of this world delicious and the staff was outstanding. Our waitress was very attentive and friendly. The food was fantastic. I ordered the crab cake while my husband got the linguine with shrimp and lobster, I ate most of his meal as I couldn't help myself. My parents who also dined with us got the linguine and as we we're leaving my dad was telling people to get it. Please do yourself a favor and get the pasta with shrimp and lobster. Everything from the staff, atmosphere, food and dessert was impeccable. Thank you for a great meal!

I am OBSESSED!!!! Best burgers I've had in Tampa hands down. So fresh, so juicy, so flavorful, so creative!!! Everything is made in house even all the sauces they offer, and talking about sauces.... their pineapple sauce is to die for!!!!! I will literally keep coming back just for that sauce. Great food, great service!!! Keep up the good work
Best guacamole and chips! Staff is always friendly and accommodating!  I've even had them cater which was awesome as well! Highly recommended!!!

The lobstah roll was excellent as mentioned in many reviews. The roll was $16.99 with an extra $4.00 for really good fries and a drink. That is a total of $21.00 which is a high end lunch. If you want to park fairly close it was $4.00/hour. There was only one waitress who was definitely cheerful and quick moving but to make things move uniformly she gave out all timely checks at the same time, collected them at the same time and gave change similarly creating a lot of wait time. I would go back and park further away plus go later for lunch. The boardwalk is a fun place offering great views, boat rentals and lots of other food choices including ice cream, wine tasting, and salt water taffy.

Spot on service and food! We rarely go out anymore due to Covid, but Saturday night was a treat and planned early dinner date night to avoid crowds. The hostesses were awesome and taking everyone's temperature prior to entering. 

The courtyard appeared to be full and with plenty of large tables. But all we wanted was a table for 2, so the rooftop was our destination. Lots of room, just one other table occupied the area and we had a beautiful sunset city view. Perfect! 

Server was friendly and delivered our drinks pretty quickly- strawberry margarita is made with fresh fruit- yummo! I ordered the chicken enchiladas and fried yucca. They were pretty flavorful enchiladas, as I typically need to drench them in sauce because they can be dry. These were so delicious! My boyfriend ordered filet mignon tacos. He loved them! 

Can't wait to return!

Went here for on our last night of vacation with the whole group. After an hour of waiting we finally got our food. Service was super slow and had to ask for refills every time. By the time we got our food it was just luke warm and he portions were so big I think one plate could have fed four people comfortably. 

Outside of the bad service and huge portions the food was very tasty and well seasoned. Definitely won't be back though..
Horrible!!! Terrible service, terrible food! Meats came out cold, cornbread was almost refrigerator cold, beans came out piping hot like it was fresh out of a microwave. NEVER GOING BACK AGAIN

Ordered from here last night. Got a white pizza, antipasto salad, and 20 wings.  The food was delivered quickly and was still hot.  Everything was beyond amazing. Wings were a really good size and coated well in the sauce,  but not drowning. Pizza was crispy and fresh.  The salad was amazing. So fresh tasting and packed with a lot of meat and cheese and vegetables.  Was really really happy with this order. Usually never take the time to write reviews- especially to standard pizza places, but this is not your standard pizza place.
Service was awesome! Made a reservation on OpenTable and were seated right when we showed up. Good food and even better service from Ian!
AMAZING food! I ordered the chicken enchilada and churros and they were both amazing. Great seating options and nice vibes.

Best Indian I've had in the Tampa area.  Its not the best I've had anywhere, but is really good, and given the lack of other good Indian joints in the area, it comes highly recommended.

I actually love that they always run out of naan, only because they bring out the fresh stuff and go table to table with it.  They have a variety, but I wish there were 1 or 2 more options on there, especially something a little spicier,  After being here more than a few times, I really like the fact that they change up what is offered, just about every time I have been there, though they have the staple chicken tandoori, some sort of vegetarian (normally  paneer and dal), and vindaloo on there.  

I still haven't made it in for anything for the buffet-  If its not broke, don't fix it, and I always get my Indian fix from here!  I just don't like commitment with so many wonder Indian dishes, so this is the perfect place for me.

You'd expect more for the price charged.
I got the Banh Mi which was more bread than flavorful ingredients and there was serious lack of sauce.

When I first moved to Reno 9 years ago, everyone told me this is THE place to go. They were right. They were so unbelievably right. I usually skip or eat breakfast at home, but these days, on occasion, I like to alternate between Squeeze In and Peg's because I love both so much for different reasons.

I go to breakfast places for things I can't make myself, so I tend to order really complex items with a lot of ingredients included. The Sierra Omelette has everything I want and need in a breakfast. It has spicy chicken, avocado, and plenty of salsa and potatoes. It comes in a huge skillet, which is great of course. I get to begin my day like "Hey! a fun activity I wouldn't normally do! Eating from a pan!" And then it is ON! I don't drink coffee, but I hear their coffee is really good. If a buddy is visiting Reno for the first time, I take them here. Location is great too because after eating, take a nice walk by the river. Another thing is one entree from here will usually last me two meals, so the value is incredible!

We like this place and have been several times. The food is really good and the menu is varied enough for salad lovers or carnivores! The service, however is inconsistent. We tried to eat there a couple of days ago and didn't even make it to a table due to the lack of service at the door. We stood there for several minutes and noticed that most of the tables didn't have food and wait staff appeared to be slim. We still waited for a bit longer. Decided to leave when the hostess walk past a few more times. We weren't angry or anything. Every place has their less than stellar day. We went elsewhere that night and came back in Sunday. Sweet server appeared to be new but was attentive. The food was good and served hot. I ventured from my 5 Vegetable plate and got the veggie/chicken flatbread with a side of onion rings. The veggies were great and not overcooked. The onion rings were delicious.  Others at my table got the chicken fried steak, cheese sticks and an order of artichoke dip. We were all pleased and full. 

Although no one ordered it today I must add that the pot roast is an exceptional choice. They do run out of it so get there early. 

I can't say this place is particularly special but for a dependable and predictable plate of meal it's a good choice. It satisfies many palettes.

I love ochatto's lunch menu. It is close to campus and the food selection is great. I love the bento boxes because I can choose steak teriyaki, chicken teriyaki, or salmon. After choosing one, I get tempura, rice, California roll, and can choose between a salad or a miso soup. It is also pretty filling for the price and is about $13 for the bento box. Moreover, for lunch, it is 2 rolls for $9 and dinner is 3 rolls for $14. The pricing is good, food is filling, service is a bit on the slower side, but no complaints! Their noodle soups are also delicious for the days when it's raining and cold outside. Love this place when I am craving Japanese food that isn't too heavy and has a clean taste to it!
Fantastic service and consistent quality. I eat here around 3  times a week for lunch. It's basically the only place I like that's in that mall.

First time customer - was excited about trying Mas Tacos and it did not disappoint in any way.  Tried one of everything!  The soup is so delicious.  All the tacos were great.  Highly recommend this place if you are in the area!  Can't wait to go again and again.
The store outside and inside didn't look well but the taste of their food is good and i cant complain
Just tried the pork and chicken Bah mi sandwiches were great!!!. I definitely will come back again

This review is only for the pizza because that's all we've tried so far and unfortunately because it was just so bad we probably won't be trying anything else from here. My husband and I have drove by this place numerous times and it looks adorable, we live in Palmyra so I feel like we're just constantly ordering from Nick's and wanted to try something different (even though Nicks is delicious, we should've stuck with them).  I wouldn't say the pizza was the worst I ever had or disgusting, just the amount of cheese on it would've been appropriate if we ordered extra cheese but we didn't. We love thin crust pizza but the crust cheese ratio was just so off.  Extremely thin crust, the kind you have to fold the pizza over because you can't just hold it regularly, and mounds and mounds of cheese. Very sloppy. We wound up Throwing most of the pizza out. Maybe it was just a fluke, but it wasn't a good experience.

God knows how Santa Barbara can support so many Thai restaurants, but here we have latest entrant into this crowded arena. TAP feels more "urban" -- I welcome the starkness of the black line art against their white walls -- than a Your Place or even a Galanga, and the food itself seems to veer slightly away from the established S.B.-Thai norm, at least among the appetizers and more shareable plates. Dig them corn fritters.

I put TAP's main dishes to the partial test by asking the waitress to bring me her personal favorite curry, with whatever meat and absorption material best suited it -- and to make it spicy. My buddy tossed in some third-party credibility: "He likes it really, really, really, spicy." She served me a bowl of green curry with chicken and plenty of bamboo slices, accompanied by triangular slices of "roti" -- not Indian roti, but fluffy, buttery stuff like puff pastry. Not the healthy choice, perhaps, but the right one; I've grown weary of the usual rice heaps.

A flavorful combination, sure, but spicy? Not particularly; I'd rate it 2.5 on the five-star. The waitress helpfully set down a trio of chili oil, pepper flakes, and spicy fish sauce, but even stirring generous portions into my curry didn't get it where I needed it. What a shame, since TAP offers a splendor of options to wash it down. If driving, I have the lime iced tea, a surprisingly rich taste without too much thickness or pure sweet that you drink from a Mason jar (or at least a Masonic jar). If not driving, I partake of their four-foot keg-towers (yes, really) of Chang or Singha. But I don't own a car, so...

It's a sad day. I will no longer be able to go to one of my favorite restaurant's. 

During halftime of the Seahawk/Packer game, I ran up to Guero to get dinner. I always get a medium chicken burrito. I was shocked that the price went up, costing me $10 just for the burrito. Took it home with the usual condiments, and had a little feast.

During the night, I got violently ill. Then yesterday, had a high fever and horribly sick all day. I was unable to eat anything. Then last night again, violently ill in the middle of the night. Today I feel much better, and know this was food poisoning.

After decades of being a loyal customer, I can't take the chance of eating there again.

We happened upon this place while in the area and have already been back in less than a week!  Service is prompt and friendly, prices are reasonable, and food is delicious.

So far we haven't had a sushi roll we disliked and well... we have had to have the hakata ramen on both visits. Definitely recommending this for folks looking for some great ramen and sushi in the StL area.
Delicious Shrimp Kabobs! Service is fast, especially if you order online, and prices are reasonable for the amount of food given. Highly recommend! (Some dishes can be a little spicy)

New Vietnamese establishment settled on 202 in the Fairfax Shopping Center. It has only been a week since the grand opening. So my review is based on just what I've encountered during the first visit. 

Was properly greeted at the door. I didn't want to dine in, but like many others, opted for take out. There's stickers indicated on the floor to remain social distancing while we waited. Plenty of seating on the floorplan that are clean, spacious, and open. Also well staffed. Everyone seemed nice and gentle. 

Ended up starting with the basic by ordering 2 Traditional Classic Combo Hoagies aka Banh Mi Thit Nguoi. Baguette was a decent size. I think it was a tad bit larger than most places, which isn't a bad thing at all. Toasted well with a nice crisp. Wish they added a bit more slices or cold cuts and evenly distribute the pate. Other than that, the Mister and I enjoyed them. We will definitely be back to try their Pho and Rice Platters.  

Hope this place is here to stay!

This place is awesome. Great service and they  will answer any questions you have. 

Yallllll. The WINGS are fireeeeeee . Try them and let me know your thoughts. 

Everything I've had on the menu is amazing. Food is seasoned well and I usually go home with leftovers.
Hands down the best crab cakes I've ever had! The service was also excellent. Worth the one hour wait.
I got the epic shrimp burrito for lunch with fries. The burrito is massive, but the shrimp wasn't seasoned and the fries needed salt. Overall the meal tasted pretty much like nothing.

Solid food. Small buns but that's the nature of them. A tad pricey but that's also the area. The shrimp bun and the Buffalo chicken bun are delicious and I'd order again! Wish they opened around broad and South!

FOOD: solid authentic Asian fare well known to the Loop/WashU campus. Spices are spot on and not over powering. We got the garlic eggplant and malaysian chicken- zero complaints. 

SERVICE: lots of help on a busy Friday night, everyone was super nice and coordinated in getting things moving

EXPERIENCE: well lit and busy restaurant. It's going to be loud given how popular it is but a good time for sure. They have a separate take out and boba line if that's what you're there for

VALUE: worth it for the quality. Step above cheap takeout, most entrees will cost you $12 plus. Each dish is certainly enough for more than one meal. Family style share to taste everything!

SUMMARY: Corner17 was the hot new Asian restaurant when I graduated 6 years ago and it looks to continue its roll as the best Chinese restaurant on the loop and possibly STL. Worth a visit!

This location repeatedly makes people wait 15+ minutes in the drive-thru line until people start leaving; typically people need to multitask at a drive-thru, not handle one order at a time. If I were a manager or owner of this location, I'd be appalled at the number of sales that are lost due to poor service.
Dinner was ok, but wonton soup gave us food poisoning.  Only 2 people of 12 who had the soup were the ones affected.  We're sick right now... Never going back.

I wrote an honest review of Fiorino to which the owner replied with a personal derogatory inaccurate and false deceitful review of his former customer who was a loyal regular.
This forum is for customer reviews, not for personal reviews of customers. None of the issues I presented were addressed by Il Duce Franco. His accurate reference to himself. 
Regretfully the review was factual and unable to be refuted. I won't stoop to any rebuttal of his inaccurate defensive personal attack except to address his asking why I didn't address his waiter's behavior to him sooner. This is a blatant lie, as I had done so on numerous occasions. Let me remind Franco of one response he made to me, which was "he needs a girlfriend". 
I am a candid reviewer, and attacking me personally rather than my critique of his grumpy volatile waiter's behavior was off track and inappropriate. This was a restaurant review. His retort was a customer review. Off base and needlessly vindictive.
Eat there Monday's when Tom is the waiter! The ambiance is quite amazing. He is as pleasant & congenial as possible. Like night & day. 

Good fresh food & wonderful desserts always. The food is very good & consistent. Credit where it is due to Franco.

Order, pay, sit down, and FABULOUS will be brought to you.
Salsa bar is AMAZING. You get to choose from extra spicy, spicy, medium spicy, etc.
I've tried the Chicken Fajita Burrito and the steaming hot Fajita Del Mar. All entrees/ burrito comes in a pretty big portion with a side of rice, and chips. 
I mean for 10 bucks or so for ONE order, I basically have 2-3 delicious meals set for the rest of the day. Pretty big portions, but most importantly, great quality. :)
Possibly the BEST MEXICAN FOOD in Santa Barbara thus far.
Everything on the menu excites. Recommend the Catfish Po boy and the crab cakes. 4 stars because service could have been better.

I am a huge fan of General Tso's chicken.. really any fried chicken. :) But more often than not, Fu Wah seems to completely overcook it. Maybe it's being re-cooked over and over again, but it is super chewy, bordering on hard. And everything that is fried is super greasy; I mean, greasier than it should be. 

So my recommendation is stay away from fried. Even the non-fried stuff isn't great, but at least it's not bad. The egg drop soup is decent. I wish it were better because they are close and fast to deliver.

Spectacular Red Lobster experience... 

This review is for this and similar restaurants. 

My party of 3 arrived around 7:30pm and requested a booth in India or Michelle's section.  There was no wait, though the restaurant was busier than it has been recently. 

Michelle came right over and got our drink order, then returned almost immediately with our drinks and a basket of Cheddar Bay Biscuits. The biscuits were piping hot and delicious. We needed a few minutes to look over the menu, and Michelle then came back and took our order. 

About three minutes later, Michelle came out with our salads and included an extra dressing on the side for each of us. That was great. Red Lobster salads are nothing to write home about, but are completely fine. Just a little tomato, cucumber and red onion over some iceberg lettuce with croutons. Nothing special but serves its purpose. I really enjoyed using the extra salad dressing on my biscuits. Michelle also refreshed our biscuits and drinks.
 
Not too long later our entrees arrived. Each came exactly as ordered with the correct sides. All were hot and tasty. My lobster tails were well cooked and delicious. The lobster mac and cheese was just ok, and I found it very similar to a frozen one I get at the grocery store. The baked potato was tiny but very good. Michelle also remembered to bring out the hot tea that one of my guests had ordered at the beginning to arrive with with her meal. Surprisingly, and much to her credit, Michelle brought out  fresh waters and drinks for all of us as well. She also brought out another basket of hot biscuits, and continued to check on us throughout after meal.
 
There were some extra tacos, so we needed a box. Normally at this Red Lobster, they kind of just throw the box in your lap. Michelle on the other hand came out with a clean pair of gloves and packed the taco and some extra biscuits into the box for us.
 
At the end of the meal we were sure to let the manager know that Michelle had been excellent. She is really what made this a 5 star meal. A lot of people that work at restaurants like this don't care and don't try, but it's very clear that she does. She's really the type of waitress that you would like to see at a better restaurant because you know she deserves it and can do the job well.

Been here for lunch twice. Great chef salad and garlic rolls. Lady behind the counter was sweet and courteous. Good experience and $15 bill for two. Food here for dinner is really good but service is very slow in the evenings.
Friend dumplings were great.  Enjoyed would have given 5 stars but they forgot to put my order though when I picked up

Excellent service from the time we walked in the door til the time we left! The man holding the door offered a waiting chair and cup of hot coffee while we waited for a booth to open up. Wait time-5 min for 2 counter seats, which I love because you're right up on the action. The waitress was right there with menus and silverware. After we placed our order and got our sodas, we had food within 10 minutes. The food was amazing--pecan waffles, grits with butter, scrambled eggs, hash browns with cheese, and biscuits and gravy with 2 sodas--$20. Not too bad and did I mention the EXCELLENT, prompt service? I am ashamed to say I didn't get the waitress ' s name but she was attentive and pleasant. I wanted to buy a coffee mug to take home for my dad because he loves Waffle house and she took care of me. Would visit again in a heartbeat if ever back this way!

The service at the bar it's sooo slow! Came here twice and sat at the bar for a quick meal because I was on a rush. The bar was single staffed twice! It's not fair to the one employee who is handling the register, the orders and also cleaning up. Not fair at all!! I would imagine that they make enough money to pay minimum wage to 2 employees, so busy people like us can go in and have a fast service. I don't blame the attendant al all!! They do the best they can. Management should take that roll for a few hours to see how it's like. They might not like it! 
I might not ever come back when in a rush. Food it's always great though

Yep, they did it again.  Been to many Famoso restaurants all over.  This one has nailed it. Great food, great service and great atmosphere.  Came here for a leisurely lunch. Food was fresh, server was attentive and price is very reasonable- about $12 for lunch.  The tomato bisque is unbelievably delicious.

Service is outstanding and if you disclose your occasion, they go the extra mile to personalize your experience.  Having recently dined their for our anniversary, we found the menu complete with a Happy Anniversary card which included our names.  Staff and manager greeted us with well wishes throughout the night.  Food is really, really good and for my wife who is vegetarian, the Tofu Poke and the vegetarian menu is a nice touch.  Sushi is always fresh and while it's a little pricey for one piece nigiri, if the freshness is guaranteed, you won't mind paying.  Most times, nigiri can be risky. You know that going in but at Roy's, you feel pretty confident about knowing what you get.
When done right Dominos is a great pizza for the price. The speed of delivery is fantastic. The app is wonderful. Then there is the Dominos on Rangeline... avoid it.

This place is bomb! Small place serving great food. 

Location: Place is on top of fishermen unloading freshly caught fish. Fish does not come any fresher unless you cook it on the boat. 

Food: OMG!!! Its sooooo good
- Grouper Sandwich: We ordered it grilled because we wanted to taste the fresh fish naked without any breading and it was perfect. 
- Lobster roll: Buttery bread, generously portioned lobster (like a lot!) and light dressing. Really good. 
- Fried pickles: Fun to eat 
- Onion rings: I really loved the breading 
- Fries: you know it was never frozen
- Dips: Forgot what they were called but really good

Service: Amazing!
They only have 3 staff, 2 cooks and 1 server but man they were attentive and does not rush you. 

Parking: You have to pay for parking because its in John's Pass Village but its worth it.

Took over an hour to get delivery. Food had to be microwaved it was lukewarm...Never got an email with suggested wait time so I just kept sitting waiting and waiting it was about 80 minutes. I called at 60 and they just said it's on the way without any real info.
Pretty standard fare if you're in the mood for mexican.  Food is always decent and service has always been adequate even on trivia nights when its really crowded.

Picnic is a great place. I'm not always a fan of their prepared dinner foods, but their pesto is incredible!! And only $5! The coffee and baked good prices are very reasonably priced. Their oatmeal is AMAZING!

Feeling sad , this used to be a favorite restaurant of mine . Nothing fancy , just solid tasty Vietnamese food.
We hadn't been in a couple months but were there this weekend . It was like eating at a different place . The chai Go was all dough with no filling ,
My lemongrass chicken was dry, my plate had appt
More rice and a lot less chicken. My husband ordered what used to be mouth watering Vietnamese pork
Only tonite it wS cardboard with a measles 5 pieces of pork on a huge pile of rice. And a fried egg that was way overdone .
So disappointed in the new ownership! SF kitchen will have my business going forward
Great service, food was very large portions and tasted good. Drinks weren't my favorite, which is my reason for taking a star away. Would definitely go to again.

Coming from NJ, it's hard to find good pizza. But, they do ok.
I always regret ordering from them.. and it's always based on service. 
A year later I thought I'd give them another shot.. BAD MOVE.

My issue is service. THESE GUYS DON'T CARE ABOUT YOU!!
We order a pasta, pizza and dessert. We were staying at a near by hotel and order by room number. Delivery guy didn't bring plates, napkins, forks.. nothing. I called to let them know, guess what.. He said "Thanks" and HUNG UP ON ME..

By the way, I own a catering business and this is just unacceptable. 
Shame on you guys.

Yes it is a chain type Steak house restaurant, but it is far better than any other you may have been to.  I might suggest the Sampler Platter if dining with a group.  The steaks are great.  My favorite is actually the raspberry chicken...Very yummy.  All meals come with choice of side or two.  Jacket Yam or Potato are favorites.  Wedge salad is great as well.

If you want to have a great meal in fun atmosphere and I really think this is a great place to pick.  The manager personally comes around to make sure everything is good.
Visited Weber Grill for the first time on Friday.  The service and the food were great.  Another winner!

Came here after for a late lunch after finding Korean bbq close by wasn't open yet. Coming here was a bit confusing and if it wasn't for reading a few Yelp reviews I wouldn't know what to do! Our server came and we decided on what to order. They have a call button which is nice but the server was the only server on floor and would often come to our table panting. I felt for her haha. We ordered the fried chicken and the sweet and spicy pork with two orders of rice. 

Food came with 4 Korean side dishes, I really liked the bean sprouts and I think it was potato salad? 

My boyfriend liked the fried chicken but i didn't like the sauce. He said the sauce reminded him of BiBimBap sauce. I liked the sweet and sour pork but the sauce ended up making the pork a bit too saucy. It was still good. 

I don't think we'd come back here again but it wasn't a bad place to eat! Nice on the eyes and will definitely leave you with leftovers to bring home.

Went for dinner with the family, food was really good and the service was great and the place was clean, except for the men's washroom had a funny smell. Thanks Shaye for the great service, we will be back!
Just don't go here. The food is good (cause it's Jack in The Box), but the employees don't care about making people wait. Hoping this building gets shut down soon...

We recently moved into Carpinteria and decided we wanted to try one of our local pizzaria's, so we went to Giocanni's to give it a try.  We had the Marfaita pizza with sausage.  Oh my gosh was it ever good!  Just the right amount o tasty crust, delicious tomato sauce, cheese, with fresh garlic and basil.  We highly recommend!!  We'll be back to try other items on the menu real soon.  We loved it!

I just had the most god-awful pizza. It looked and tasted like something you microwaved at home as a kid. Literally worse than Dominos. Probably worse than little caeser's, too.

Servers were nice though.

Found about this place on yelp and went there the next day. I had the gumbo and a side of corn fritters both were a hit. I got the quart size gumbo and got two generous meals out of it. The gumbo which was described as a medium heat was spicey enought to enhance the gumbo without setting your hat on fire. I will become a regular there, what a treat and hidden jewel on Drew st. THANKS YELP and reviewers for leading me there.

Absolutely took me back home again! The staff was friendly, the food was a country comfort that reminded me of my Mother, Aunt and Grandmother. Was so thrilled that a friend recommended this diamond, can't wait to go back again!
HELL NO! Placed order, waited 2.5 hours and no food. No good and no excuse. Stay away at all costs.

Service was great, menu is vast so many options! The portions are ridiculously huge. The food was awesome. I never say hey I want to go back and this is a place that I not only want to go back to but next time I will bring the kids. It has a homey diner feel to it and they serve breakfast all day! Great job!

Yummy 
But.... My tortilla is half cold, 
More than one sauce container costs extra, the topping bar is covered so I can't see what's put on my burrito, and everyone rushed around so I'm not sure who's making my burrito. 
Flew here from CA, should have went to Chipotle.
Fantastic dining experience. Try to crusted mahi! Desserts were outstanding - great way to finish a nice meal.

What a cool family owned establishment! Great outdoor seating under a pretty oak tree with a great atmosphere. The owners are super friendly and damn do they make some great food! Their po boys and gumbo taste straight out of New Orleans. This place is perfect to come with your two and four legged friends. Just walking distance from the local breweries and a short walk downtown too. I will be here again and again

My dining partner and I were excited to try the new Longhorn, but because the wait time for dinner is so long, we stopped in for lunch.  There was no wait (we arrived just about noon on a Friday).  The service was outstanding, the food was excellent.  We started with the cheddar/bacon dip, which was so good!  I had a side salad that was fresh and crisp.  My dining partner had a cheeseburger and said it was very good, and cooked just right.  I had the ribeye steak and baked potato, it was cooked perfectly medium rare, as I'd requested.  Everything was delicious, and I can't wait to go back to try some of the other menu items.  I think our server's name was Brianne - she was very nice, competent, and friendly.  Also, other servers would stop by our table to inquire if we were enjoying our meal, which is excellent customer service.  I also liked that they seem to have a lot of diversity in their hiring, which I think is a good thing.  I'll definitely be returning!  I can't wait!

I had a living social coupon to eat here and even with that discount, I didn't feel like dinner at 400 olive was worth it.  I know the restaurant was inside a hotel but I have eaten some great food at hotel restaurants (finn & porter, chris ruth's, craft, etc...).  400 olive is not one of those hidden gems inside the hilton.  You walk up 3 steps from the lobby to the restaurant.  I took a group of 6 to the restaurant so i could utilize 2 coupons and I feel bad for subjecting my group to the food.  I expected the food to be better judging by the website.  The menu we were handed only had 2 sparse pages of choices, and it took us a while to make up our minds since nothing seemed to pop out at us.  I ordered the chicken, sundried tomato pasta (they only had bowties and one other pasta as a choice) which was swimming in oil.  I had to eat the pasta and stuff on top, that wasn't drenched in the oil at the bottom, NOT appetizing!  The dishes were too expensive for the low-quality food.  We will not be coming back here the next time we are in St Louis, there are much better places to eat in STL.

Went here the other night with my son's baseball team. Good service, especially since there was about 20 of us. They were able to take us right away. Our food did take a little while to come out (we all ordered seperate) some of the group was almost done by the time I got my food. Decent pizza and the salad was good.

I had the vegetarian lasagna. No pasta, no simple carbs and ooh so yummy! Yum Yumm! The place is simply decorated but has that feel of an authentic Greek Taverna. The tables had not been wiped down, and there was no sugar in the sugar thingy, but the owners were SO friendly, and helpful, and wonderful! The food was FABULOUS! And I felt VERY comfortable there. I'll be back for sure.

Jimmy G's is just so good. I get my usual, cheese steak with wiz and onions and it is the best. They finely chop the steak and it is always perfectly cooked. They also deliver, which I do take advantage of sometimes since I live in the neighborhood (sandwich is at risk of getting soggy though). 

They have a nice outdoor area for the warmer months and they cover and heat it in the winter. Very comfortable! They also have a fries and drink combo which is cool. If you're looking for an authentic Philly cheese steak.... Jimmy G's never disappoints.

Love love love Blue Tomato!! The spinach artichoke dip is delicious and so are their garbage can nachos!! Try the shrimp alfredo!! We eat here all the time.  It looks like a little hole in the wall but that is usually where you find the best food.  The staff is always nice and remembers us.  I have never been disappointed when leaving here.

A takeaway Lunch Box (3 empanadas and 1 dipping sauce) for lunch was one of the many highlights of a great day trip to Doylestown. I ordered korean beef, pesto chicken, and spinach/feta, with the truffle aioli sauce. The hand-pies (they're baked, not fried) and sauce were absolutely delicious. My only regret is not taking a boxful home.

The dirty appearance of the floor and food line was a turn off for me. The floors mats have a ton of food debris on them. I am stepping on chips under my table as I type. The subs were OK. The servuce was not friendly. They dont have napkins available in restaurant and when I asked cashier for napkin she insisted three times that they were rolled up in my bag. I said no napkins again and anoyher customer commented that I just need napkins. That is crazy...no napkins out by other cup and condiment items?? Cleanliness gets a 1, service gets a 2, food gets a 3.
Everything we get here is amazing. Never disappoints! Try the tom kha soup. Been here at least three times and everything is very good.

I usually get the Arizona burrito. Gigantic and tasty. Also the super nachos are usually good. The one thing these guys need to learn is portion control. Almost everything you order is enough for 2 people. Good food, cheap prices.

After seeing many pictures of the yummy foods here on YELP, I'd just had to try it myself! It is inside the gas station store. I wouldn't have found it, if there wasn't a sign. It is very small but they make it right there and then. The service was really fast, too. Someone before had their order taken wrong and they immediately fixed it, causing a small delay on my order but that was alright. I had their Carne Asada Carmelo, in which their Carne Asada were very tender and juicy; and their well-known Sonoran. Although I preferred El Guero Canelo's Sonoran better but it was something not to complain about. They also gave a small discount; I didn't asked why; but hey, I'll take it.

The first time we've been to this location.  Stopped for lunch after some shopping.  The place was still nearly empty, yet we still had to wait over 5 minutes for seating ... Seriously.
The food was really, really good, Fettuccini, Cuban sandwich, the Garlic Parmesan bread, cheesecake for dessert.
The service was just okay.
The cost was very expensive for what we had.  Seriously $9.50 for a half glass of cheap wine, I could have bought three bottles at Trader Joe's for $9.50.
Definitely not something place we'll be coming back to. We can eat at most every restaurant in town with comparable food quality for about half what Cheesecake Factory charges.
Between the wait, the average service and cost I feel generous giving them the Stars, only because the food was so yummie

Hands down... 
Best pizza in Tampa!
The owners treat you like family!
Service is 6 Star!
Efficient, friendly, great menu, great atmosphere to meet friends and family. So great to have such a great neighborhood restaurant. It's a gem. More authentic is its the classic fold down the middle pizza... If you know New York, or New Jersey style pizza, you know what I'm talking about. I can't wait to go back. Amazing Food, Warm Atmosphere, a restaurant I'd bring an out of town friend visiting. You've tried the rest, Now try the BEST!!

Having now eaten at Fogo, Picanha, Na Brasa, and finally Chima, the four churrascarias that I know of in the Philadelphia area (as well as churrascarias in NY, CT, MD, and VA, and numerous cities in Brasil including Porcao, the peak dining experience of this restaurant genre) I feel confident in judging Chima to be a very good dining experience. Whereas Fogo has something of a more "institutional" vibe to it, I found the atmosphere in Chima to be much more relaxed and pleasant for being together with friends. We actually closed out the place, being the very last to leave that night, so I think that says something about the ambience.

First of all, the caipirinhas here are fine, though one of the things that bothers me is that you have no other option but having Leblon cachaca in it. Fogo does this as well. This is another tangent altogether, but caipirinhas are supposed to be made with the cheap stuff (51, Pitu, etc.), and the aged versions are to be served neat. The salad bar is more than adequate, and while not as varied as Fogo's, you shouldn't be at a loss for good options. The black beans and rice that they served were probably the most authentic of any to be found in the city. The pao de queijo and croqueta table starters are average, as are the accompaniments that come with the main course.

The meat is very good. For me, linguica is the touchstone of a churrascaria in America to be able to see whether they are trying to provide an authentic dining experience, or trying to pass off cheap stuff on unaware guests. In this regard, I give the linguica high marks. I also found the picanha (top sirloin) to be delicious and very authentic. Filet is great too. I don't think that you can go wrong with the beef options at Chima. For people who might have said that the meat is dry or overcooked, you can get whatever doneness you like, just ask the server and he will cut from the hunk of meat accordingly. The only cut that I did have a problem with was the lamb chop, which was overdone. I also acknowledge that it is nice for them to offer a fish option, though it's nothing that I would get; churrascarias are all about meat! I definitely look forward to going back, especially with the promotional coupons and deals that they do periodically.
First time... excellent!!!!! I recommend to try deep dish pizza with a touch of honey.

This place is new to the dinning scene (I believe less than a week old) and I couldn't have had a better dinning experience!

The service was prompt, friendly and knowledgeable. Before I ordered I learned the name Lola dotted the menu, that there was a pickle bar and the house favorites. 

Everything we ordered was well seasoned, cooked the way we ordered it and full of flavor. 

The fries were seasoned with a touch of Rosemary which added a nice flavor. The salad was perfectly dressed with a well balanced red wine vinaigrette. The burgers were juicy with a healthy burger to bun ration. The buns were buttery and soft. 

I warn you the food is fresh and good so you are going to pay for it. We were a party of two, ordered a burger each plus a side to accompany each burger and one soft drink. The bill was around $35 pre-tip. 

I will be back as I'm a burger junky and this will be in heavy rotation until I try them all.

We just came here for a late breakfast. The outside is super cute and the inside was very inviting. We walked in and were greeted promptly. The menus are in the form of a newspaper. Cute idea but I found it to be overwhelming and disorganized. Now for the food.... My husband and I both got skillets and we split a half order of biscuits and gravy. Both of our skillets were so bland. I don't think the cook seasoned anything. We both asked that the eggs in our skillets be over easy. Mine came scrambled and my husband's were over easy but so over cooked, not runny at all. The biscuits and gravy were good, nothing special, but good. My oldest ordered the kids pancakes they were so dry. They needed a lot of butter and syrup. My youngest ordered the kids chicken fingers and mashed potatoes and gravy. The chicken was very dry and the potatoes were tasteless... The gravy tasted like the canned gravy you buy at the store. We will not return. There are much better places around.
Dim sum was good, not all staff has a grasp on the English language and made a mistake on our order. A bit pricey, place was clean, food was good, we'll be back.

Decided to try the Farmer's Daughter restaurant out after hearing it's a really good place to eat. The service was excellent but the food to me didn't match the price. Nothing that was really special and didn't leave me wanting more.  Doubtful if I'd go back!

We visited this place on 2011-05-10.  
Okay, found the place... now, how do we get in?  You have to drive around the block to the back to get into the parking lot; there isn't a driveway off Alvernon.  Hmmm....
Wow... Christmas in May.. really?  (They still had icicle lights hanging outside).  Hmmm... 
Entering the place was like walking into a busy cafeteria - small tables in rows close to each other, no real decor.  But the place is packed with patrons.... Hmmm....
That's an awfully long sushi counter with 6 sushi chefs..... one of the longest I've seen... Hmmm.....
All you can eat menu for $19.95?  Really?  Oh wait... you have to eat everything including the rice or you pay extra for what you don't eat... Hmmm.....
Okay... found what I'm looking for - chirashi.  It's a rice bowl topped with a variety of sushi cuts.  Throw in a variety of other rolls - Volcano, Dragon, Mt. Fuji, and baked California roll... that oughta do it (we skipped the AYCE for this trip).  
Generally, it's good - but not great.  The chirashi was loaded with quite a few cuts of sushi, and the rice was nicely seasoned.  Quality of the cuts were okay.  The baked rolls are a bit small, but nicely prepared.  Flavors overall were good.  Quality of the cuts was better than average considering we're dining on sushi in a desert (it's not up to the quality of a seaside location).
Price-wise, it was within reason; felt value was appropriate.  With the AYCE and the happy hour pricing mentioned by others, it can probably be an even better deal.
Ambiance-wise, well, what do you expect from a cafeteria-like tables and christmas lights hanging outside in May?  'nuff said...
Overall, I was satisfied.  I'd give it 3-1/2 stars, but we can't give 1/2 stars; fell back to the 3 stars instead of going up to 4 stars because of the ambiance (or lack of it).
Would I come back?  Probably... maybe for a happy hour... if I'm in Tucson at the time.
The Best Pho in town! Food is 5 stars but the service is 2-3 stars depending on whose working.

Great delivery and good execution on the rolls.
A great option for inexpensive sushi.

HUGE BURRITOS and the option of a QUESARITO?  MAN!  Why didn't I find out about this place sooner!!!!!
