Had the quesadilla burrito; Quesarito, which was just awesome.  Esp when you love cheese as much as I do.  And having a quesadilla-wrapped burrito gives it that semi crisp tortilla and just made my burrito all the more enjoyable.  

The portion is pretty huge, so make sure you're either really hungry, or sharing with someone.

Gotta go back and try those fried onions tho.  And the nachos too.  

Parking is near UCSB so it can get tough; there's a small parking lot, so good luck.
Everything was excellent except for the flavored butter for the amazing pretzel buns. The service was swift & pleasant.  The food was fast, delicious & filling!

Excellent menu. Everything we ordered came out tasting delicious ( maybe too much breading on the calamari) and the prices were fair. Good service. Nice atmosphere. Don't let the outside fool yeah. It's a good place to come eat!

Overpriced, tiny food portion -for small dishes.
Again, do not order any small dishes, simply they don't worth the price.
Noodle itself is okay. But still feels expensive for what it is. 
Some servers aren't that friendly but guess they are better than most of family owned Chinese restaurant. Would not go back.

Went with a group of friends to 417 Union for brunch, while in Nashville for a wedding.  We has a group of 10, and they were able to seat us promptly, and our drinks came quick.  Our server was a female body builder who looked like she was preparing for competition.  Her muscled were so jacked, it was a little intimidating at first... but she was a very good server.

As a vegetarian I found it difficult to find something off of the breakfast menu to eat.  I wound up ordering the Southwestern Chicken Salad, without the chicken.  It was fine, and I did eat the whole thing, but there wasn't anything really special about this salad.  (could be my fault for nixing the chicken).  This dish seemed like something that could be ordered at a high-school cafeteria.  

This is a nice little Nashville Restaurant... that could use a few more vegetarian options.
Very good subs and great friendly service just so slow. Going to call in next time and will review with more stars but right now I'm still sitting here waiting....

This place Reminders me a lot of my best years in Italy. It took me years to find a place like this, these guys have the best pizza ever, they make everything in house, even homemade pasta! I had the quattro formaggi with spinach and my friend tried the classic cheeseburger that was incredible. The bun was made to order....incredible. Next i wanna try is that homemade pasta

Had my birthday dinner reserved here. Things went well. Good service, staff was friendly. Good appetizers and if you like Moscow miles, the summer one is yummy!! I had a group that showed up little early, but the hostess led them to sit.  Most places you have to wait for the whole party or for up to 5 people. It wired out well!

This is my first review of a place where very good-excellent food gets relegated in importance behind inferior service and cost.

The pizzas are delicious.  The dough is absolutely perfect: chewy and flavorful and always stretched to the ideal thickness before building the pizza.  The toppings are good too and, while we feel that the pizza toppings have slightly declined in quality since Bella Napoli's opening, it's still one of the best pizzas in town.  

We've also sampled some of Bella's other offerings and have so far been impressed with the lasagna, and pasta.  So the kitchen at this place is legit and, while it's hard not not order pizzas every time, straying off into "non-pizza land" seems like a safe choice.

The bad is the service.  We've never gotten great service here, but the quality of the serving staff has been abysmal for nearly a full year now.  Under staffing is the obvious problem, with a single server sometimes being responsible for more than a dozen tables of four at a time (so nearly fifty diners!).  For a full service restaurant with full service restaurant prices this small server to diner ratio can get very frustrating.  The worst part is that the frustrations are not only evidenced by the diner but by the over worked servers too!  

Consequently (and sadly), we never go to Bella any more unless we have a coupon.  And even then, we never order anything but the simplest things (2 drinks and 2 pizzas is the norm now) to ensure that the experience is enjoyable,

Pretty much the best burritos in town. Our favorite is Carne asada...and kids love the bean burritos. We also get breakfast burritos with fresh squeezed orange juice. Prices are super cheap. Yes, it is a hole in the wall, but that's how you know it's authentic! Use the drive through... (Just don't expect the microphone to work.) People are super nice. Family run business.  We are regulars and love our la fresita!!!

Great service. Giant thai ice tea very delicious. Great lunch special deal that comes with soup and salad. Amazing pad thai. Their peanut sauce is so good even as a salad dressing. Definitely recommend the chicken sate to start and the vegetable soup.

Yea, good Greek salad! Of course with that "famous" dressing you can't go wrong. Had a mini and the price was right, good size not too much or too little. Definitely recommended and I will be back to try an entree soon. I even ate the beets!

First thing, get the baked oysters with cheese, they are to die for!!!!!!! This place is super cool. As the building is three floors and can hold quite a bit of people. Wife and I had a 35 minute wait which wasn't bad at all as we got to sit at the bar. The waitress was super friendly and really nice. We ordered the baked oysters which were amazing. I also got the flounder which I highly recommend.
Happy hour deals are so-so, not a big selection. I had the $6 house marg which came with a taquito. Food and drink was good.

This review is geared specifically toward the happy hour on Thursday and Sunday.  If you go anywhere else for happy hour you are missing out.  Sullivan's has half price martinis and bar menu ALL DAY.  They also have an awesome heated outdoor area that seats first come first serve.  So.  Good.  Don't tell too many people because it already gets really crowded!!

What a disappointment!
Two years ago, I gave Teppanyaki Grill (the former buffet on this site) 5 stars. What happened?
The shrimp at the stir fry station were not cleaned (head and tail attached, not deshelled) and so tiny they weren't worth eating. The mushrooms and other vegetables at the stir fry station appeared dirty.
The sushi selection was very skimpy.
The ice cream disappeared, and was replaced with tasteless artificially flavored ice cream pops.
The bathroom was filthy, as were the floors throughout the restaurant.
Very pleasant waitress.

Cute place with a large patio area and fountains for brunch or lunch. The food was a bit underwhelming so I probably would not return when back in New Orleans.

Crab cakes were full of crab meat and was seasoned heavily and a tad spicy. What I did not enjoy was the thick sauce on top of the crab and greens. A thinner less syrupy sauce would be better.

House salad - nothing special 

Shrimp and grits - 3 shrimp were well seasoned and cooked perfectly. The grits were average and the corn sauce a little heavy. 

If you plan to dine here, definitely make a reservation. The wait for lunch on the weekends can average about 1.5 hours.

Yes this is in a food court but I am in love. The food was amazing and the guys working are the best!!! I ordered the falafel plate and was not disappointed. The falafel was fresh and so tasty that I ordered some to go.

Yikes! I wasn't expecting a 3 star taco , obviously, heck I'm not even sure why after 2 years I decided to come to Taco Bell anyway. Probably a commercial.  My taco was supposed to be crunchy but was stale. I think the location of this restaurant added to my displeasure. A very old , dirty , rundown building. Did however enjoy the customer service from the woman who took my order ..she was amazing!

Amazing food and service. Flavours that pop in your mouth, honestly some of the best food I have eaten in a long time (if not ever). 

The only negative is the parking however there is a shopping centre across the street.

After a recent visit to Montego Bay, Jamaica, I was craving jerk chicken and rice and peas. I happened to stumble upon Riddim N' Spice, and I'm so glad I did. The chicken, Jamaican rice, Mac and cheese, and rum cake is absolutely delicious! It is a place that I can visit often without getting tired of the food. The service is great, and the food is reasonably priced.
One of my go to spots in Tampa. Great selection of steaks and wine, knowledgeable and friendly staff. Pricey but worth it for quality beef. Highly recommend

Very good paratha roti, doubles, and polourie.  Loved all of it! I would have given it 4 stars but the customer service could be improved.  The woman that's usually there when I go is not friendly nor welcoming, her attitude is kind of off-putting.  Their children are always in the restaurant playing or sprawled out sleeping on the chairs in the dining area where customers are supposed to be eating.

I was in Indiana last weekend and was recommended to try out Blaze. Heard good things, just hadn't made it to one. IVE BEEN MISSING OUT! Not only was it the best pizza I've ever had, I had the best help in their manager, Kristen. She gave me her personal recommendation with toppings and she had a great attitude! Can't wait to make it back to the Circle City to see Kristen and her staff again!

Absolutely fantastic! My family and I very much enjoyed our lunch there. The food was very tasty, the service was great and the decor was beautiful. They also have a play area for children which my 5 year old was thrilled about. 
We ordered the Kafta and chicken thigh meals along with baklava and rice pudding. My son, 10 months old, couldn't get enough of the Kafta! 
Will definitely go back and recommend to others. Thank you

I literally watched the old fry guy scoop the burnt fries from the fryer using a strainer and slide them off in a full trash can using the edge of the trash can to get the tough ones. Then while the strainer was covered in old lettuce and crumbs from the trash he cleaned it off by dunking it back in the fryer. The manager told him not to do it in front of customers. It was super unsanitary and this place should be closed. Not to mention the manager and employees used the same gloves to count cash and use the computer as they did to build the burgers. Please if you have kids or people you care about do not go here!!!!!!!!

I came here on a Friday, I think. It was pretty crowded, but there was one table left for me and the two other people I was with.
Overall, i thought the food was good. Very, very large portions. My friend had a burger, I got the shrimp and grits and my boyfriend got the chicken and biscuits. Everything had kind of a sweetness to it, that I didn't really like that much. But overall, I thought it was all pretty good if you're in the mood for something big and heavy! 
I'd still like to go back to try a po boy. We also got the hush puppies, which were good, but not what I was expecting. I'm used to the basket of fried stuff, but these were on a plate with a kind of sweet and spicy sauce with peppers. It was really good, though! Just not what I was expecting. 
The waitress was good and the chef came out to see if everything was OK. Everyone in the restaurant seemed to be really enjoying themselves.

I am having lunch at Fred's Market Restaurant in Riverview FL. The food is fantastic and the service is always FIVE STAR. I highly recommend this place if your looking for down home Southern cooking. Most people order from the buffet but you can order from the menu and they have special prices for Seniors. My server today is Kalli Neal. Kalli  is a very lovely young lady with a beautiful smile and makes all her customers feel at home. When ever you are in town and looking for a good place that offers several selections of food at a reasonable price that you can bring your entire family then come to Fred's in Riverview.  It's located on US 301.  When you come make sure you ask for Kalli. If she is not here then ask for  any other server. They are all very good and will make you feel at home. Tell them that DAN THE CAB MAN sent you.

This place is a nightmare. I've been going there for years, but it seems like it's getting worse. I went there today and they purposefully weren't answering the drive-through. I went in, and there were people that had been waiting for 20 minutes or more for their food. Turns out the cook walked out on the other employees. I will say, the employees were handling it OK and doing their jobs. Whoever the owner is of this specific franchise store, needs to go to that store and figure this crap out.
You cannot go wrong eating at Terror Tacos! We both ordered five tacos and we devoured them in seconds. We left full and satisfied. You cannot go wrong eating here!

It's all about the pho.

We love coming to Dao's because it's just basic Tai food with good service. It's a very simple place that makes good food. No frills or meal that hits a best of list. Just good service and good food. 

We love stopping by on cold days and getting a bowl of the pho, its delicious and done well.

Meh. I mean the food is average to decent. I do like there egg drop soup and sesame chicken while my boyfriend always gets their sweet and sour chicken. They do deliver but they only accept cash for deliveries. My biggest complaint is the customer service. The lady who takes phone orders can be very off putting but it's not a deal breaker.

Ok tasting bratwurst, nice counter girl.  But disappointed when I saw my bill being charged more than what the menu board prices.  WTF ??!!!!!

Paid by credit card so I asked if they charge more than what is posted.  They couldn't give ma a straight answer. Guess most people are not bothered w/ the details and I'm a penny pinching bastard.  If they are going to charge you a plastic card fee, a business should say that.

Somewhere on a sign by the POS terminal 

Whatever's
Lots of vegetarian options. If you like your dishes really spicy, order 5 of 5 on the heat.

Went in for lunch. Food came out very quickly and the lunch special was reasonably priced, but we both agreed all the food except for the spring roll was not good. I had the basil chicken and my fiance had the pad thai, both were lacking flavor and my chicken was kinda chewy. My dish was mostly green beans. It's a nice spot and the service was really good so it's a shame the food is just not good.

Our 5 day stay in NOLA greeted us mostly with cold weather throughout most of the days.  The one day the sun warmed up the city, we made sure that we had reservations for Cafe Amelie for lunch.  I had the cochon au lait sandwich which was delicious.  The bartender took good care of us and mixed us some great cocktails to enjoy before our meal.  Whatever he suggests, just take it and get inebriated dame it! The courtyard is a wonderful respite from the bustle of the quarter and worth the visit for a long lunch.

We've been here several times and so far, we've never had a bad experience! This place is absolutely delicious! It's definitely not the best Chinese food I've ever had, but I'd say this is easily the best Asian restaurant anywhere in the area.
Our normal orders include orange beef, pork fried rice, spring rolls, wonton soup, pad thai and something from the sushi bar. All of the food is extremely flavorful and delicious. Our favorite sushi roll is the summer roll--I love the mango sauce on top! 
I also love the combination of Asian cuisines-- there's selections of Chinese, Japanese, Vietnamese and some Thai food on the menu, all of which are delicious. I definitely recommend.
Also, a great bonus--they deliver! :D
Love the hot dogs there. Great place to get together with some friends and have some of Dave's awesome custom dogs.

Food was very good from pho soup noodles to crispy noodles with a variety of meat and veggies plus shrimp and tofu, to vermicelli with lettuce, cucumber, veggies and bbq pork, shrimp and a fried egg roll. Still our old standby in downtown Reno. Prices were raised about $1 per entree but this small business needs to survive!

YO YO YO and a woooohooooo, This spot has the hook up for every diet. They cater to the halal, vegan and kosher set. THANK THE FOOD GODS!
The owners are friendly and personable as what I expected. The food is mad good and ya better know I already got on the horn and told ALL of my foodie friends about it.

There was only one ish about it that  rubbed my family and I the wrong way...And that was the young lady who took our order.

We came to this restaurant on a recommendation by a friend who spoke very highly of the place. Unfortunately we did not have the same experience: The waiter would ignore our orders, we would only receive half of them when they did come and we were lucky enough to get a special roll with hair woven into the rice. Since we like shooters so much we decided to try them after our meal and found out that we would only get half of the ones we ordered because the waiter thought we couldn't finish it despite the fact that we had left nothing on our plates not even a grain of rice.

Zento is an intimate spot, but can get a bit noisy sometimes due to close quarters. There's still space to enjoy your food, and enjoy you will! My friend ordered for the entire table - the appetizers (Zento salad, scallops, foie gras and cucumbers) were wonderful, and the various rolls (Zento roll, and others; $6-$10/roll) melted in our mouths. Don't forget to order the duck hand rolls, they're delectable. Prices aren't the cheapest, as you're paying for quality Japanese fare and their Old City rent probably!

The sushi chef is friendly and you can see him preparing away with a big smile on his face. Servers Jimmy and Chi were accommodating, and helped us secure a table for nine. As noted by another reviewer, and confirmed by my friend, their chef used to work at Morimoto. (No surprise, as every piece of sushi I tried was exquisite.)

Zento is tiny but packs a BIG punch.

This Cici's Pizza is alright, the food tastes okay, but it is laking the high variety of pizzas that can be found at other locations like the one in North Dale Mabry. It's still cheap and a good buy if you do not care so much about variety.

I came here twice with some former coworkers.  The first time here, I ordered some pieces of sushi.  It wasn't anything spectacular and the quality wasn't that great either.  

But the next time, my coworker recommended getting the shaved noodles.  It was the first time trying shaved noodles and it was pretty awesome.  The broth was flavorful, though a bit greasy.  The portion was good for at least two days of lunch.  They were a bit skimpy on the pork though.

I love Hedge Row, every time I go it never disappoints! My recommendations are the roasted beets and the half chicken, they also have really nice cocktails to enjoy.

I am rating as 4 stars just because the last time I came I had to wait 40 mins for my food but this is the only time it has happened to me

Online ordering wasn't working and said the place was closed. I'm used to ordering late here so I called; big mistake. Reached a very very rude guy who just said they were open until 10pm on Sundays; it was just after 9pm so I ask why the online ordering wasn't working. He didn't like that question and repeated they were open until 10pm and he didn't know anything about online ordering. I went ahead and ordered over the phone although the guy didn't seem to want to be bothered. I ordered a 12" thin crust with sausage, mushrooms, and onion. He said it'll be 30 minutes and he was good on that. I tipped the delivery guy and got ready to enjoy my pizza. It was not the this crust I ordered; I assume what i got was their regular crust. It was thick, doughy and generally awful. The ingredients did not stand up well to that thick crust and the crust itself way too chewy and had a lousy taste. It was too late to call and complain, not that it would have done any good. Their thin crust is okay and that is all I've ever ordered. I was starving and managed to eat 2 slices but it. Wasn't a pleasant experience. What a waste of money. The rest of this pie is not worth saving for leftovers; into the trash.
This is my new favorite restaurant. I believe they changed owners not too long ago. They no longer have sushi but the authentic Thai food is beyond awesome!
Small and quaint, excellent food. Definitely a "go again" on next visit. The ribs were tender and flavorful.

Once again this location failed at getting an order right. Stopped placing orders months ago due the quality of the products and service.  On this visit not only did they provide the wrong toppings on one of my pizzas someone failed to properly cut the veggies placing oversized chunks of bell peppers and onion my pizzas!

We wanted to support our local business's during this time and was extremely disappointed with the quality of the food. We ordered chicken steaks with sauce on the side, fries and breaded mushrooms. The chicken was over cooked and dry. The marinara sauce was salty and the fried food had a after taste. We called and let them know and the owner/cook was rude said 10% off next order! And the woman who answered the phone said " they aren't worried about bad reviews since they are the only person ones open! Maybe the good reviews come from the pizza but definitely do not order the sandwiches and don't expect good service.
Paco's looks like a Los Betos or a Filiberto's but it is so much better. I saw good reviews and was not disappointed. Fast, friendly service and delicious Mexican food.

It was 11 o'clock at night we had just gotten off of work and we went to the closest place that was open that late and we walk in and you can see clearly the cooks weren't wearing any mask they had mask on but they're under their chin we weren't going to eat there but we figured since it was so late we weren't going to find any other place close by so we decided to stay. the waitress said hang on she ran into the back and she yelled you guys need to put your mask on and as soon as she said that she comes back and it's like you gave us a table that is dirty so apparently it's not sanitized. she didn't like it it seemed,  she came over and sprayed the table with whatever and wiped the leftover food that was left on the table and then took her out order pad like oh well no big deal. Shortly after that here comes the cook mask underneath his chin still you can also see the floors had stuff dropped on them that hasn't been cleaned up so obviously cleanliness is not a part of IHOP. so we won't be eating there anymore

Stop by here by searching yelp for Mexican food. And it was Really good Mexican food!  Came here for breakfast and got breakfast burritos. The chorizo and egg was bomb!  Fresh salsa bar and chilis too!  The burrito was served quickly and very hot!  Fresh made chips come with your food too. Would def return to try other items when we are in town next.
Well up till now its been great. Order from there weekly but the last few times things missing from my order. Very disappointed

I have yet to find anything that remoteLy compares!! 
Pho Mien is by far the BEST pLace to fuLfiLL my random Asian food cravings whenever I'm here in Reno!

Sure, I wish this restaurant was 24 hours but I'll take whatever I can get. It's been a coupLe of  years since I've been here & they seemed to have changed a few things up. No harm, no fouL.
Everything is kind of seLf served. You are no Longer seated by a worker and if a menu is needed, you have to go up to the register area and request for one. 
Once you have decided what you wouLd Like to eat, go up to the counter and pLace your order.  A number wiLL be given and your food wiLL be brought out to the tabLe once it's ready.

Great variety of food and oh so very affordabLe. 
Whether you're interested in eating "famiLy styLe" or having individuaL entrees, this pLace does not disappoint. 
Soups are fLavorfuL and the rice/noodLe dishes offered are very fiLLing. SCORE!!!!

This is a small, nondescript place to get delicious fresh-to-order gyros, chicken fingers, sandwiches, and other better-than-most carnival-type food. Fries and crispy, prices are very fair, and portions are big. Soda is sold by the can and water only comes in a bottle, but a gyro combo is $7.28 and a ribeye steak with Greek salad and baked potato is $9.99. Don't let the offering of ribeyes, t-bones, or other flame-grilled items fool you--this place is in no way considered fancy. But the food is fresh, tasty, and very good.

Food is served in to-go containers even if you are dining in at one of their five tables. Not fancy but a great place to have weekend lunch with my kids without the distraction of lots of people and loud music found at so many family restaurants.
Great authentic Colombian food. Empanadas are on point...bandeja paisa delicious, all the soap are awesome. well worth the visit every time

Well, what a disappointment. Went Saturday night, the place was a zoo. Waited for a table for not long, but while waiting, the food was piling up to be picked up by the servers, 3 plates high. Not a good start.

We had the owner's daughter as our 'server' and she was absolutely horrible. Yelling about how she was supposed to be off 5 hours ago and obviously not built to handle stress. She should NOT be working with the public.

The dinners: the salad lettuce was warm, the soup tasted like out of a can, and the bread had been sitting out for a while so not very fresh. Ordered the Lamb Chop special but of course they were out of that but they offered the rack of lamb. Odd huh? It was not seasoned well and the potatoes were just a salty oily mess. Had the whole fish - was poorly done and little seasoning. The flaming cheese was tough - and poor quality.

Drinks - Greek wine was poor quality and the Greek beer (bottle) was warm.

Belly dancer was borderline stripper-ish rather than entertaining. 

This is a tourist trap and a poorly one at that.

LOVE this place!! Big portions, one salad makes two meals for me. Very good prices. And the tofu.....AMAZING!!! It was seasonal but the prickly pear lemonade was FANTASTIC! Will definitely be going back and soon.

The fried alligator was good for an appetizer.  I had the steak and lobster for dinner and it was good, but should have been a lot better for the price.  I wouldn't recommend this place.  There are better restaurants with better prices down the street.
Good food over priced in a Scientology area were no one wants to stay maybe the area will get better .she needs to have the city give her money for destroying the area like they did

The fact you place an online order and an hour later it's not ready. This place is overstaffed for the 5 tables they have full. The kitchen doesn't realize how loud their tacky comments are. This place needs new management and a sense of urgency.
Small place but friendly and attentive staff. Even though they were busy and we didn't have reservations, they squeezed us in. The food was great!  Looking forward to our next visit.

This really is not a refection of management or staff. All I wanna know if what in the hell was IHOP thinking putting the tiniest IHOP I've ever been too in a populated area like Mt. Juliet, especially when there is only 1 Waffle House and Cracker Barrel in the city? Seriously! I don't think anyone was excited as me seeing the sign that IHOP was coming 4 ms ago or so. 
Walking in, the small tiny area where you catch the bitter cold from patrons opening the outside doors to come in and the other set where customers open doors to leave is just enough room for 4 people to sit on each side. Isn't that area always kind of awkward? People waiting always stare at you with big longing stares as if they are asking, "Is it worth it? What took y'all so long? Why are we still sitting here? Why are these kids running around? Whose minding these kids????" Anyway, it's a Sunday at 10am so we are packed like sausages. It takes 10 minutes to get my name on the list because the hostess is so frazzled from taking names, seating people AND cleaning tables off and taking dirty dishes to the kitchen. I know I should leave. I know I should. The hostess is bussing tables and is apologizing with arms full of dirty dishes and she rushes past us saying, "sorry!" What is she sorry for? Sorry they don't have enough help? Sorry it's taking so long to get your name? Sorry iHOP lost their minds and chose this tiny building? Finally the hostess/busboy was able to take our name. 20 min wait. It's raining, so no room to stand outside. At some point, I became a picture on the wall, I think. We waited about 20 minutes before we were sat in a booth. One thing we noticed right away are how much smaller the booths are. Especially the 2 seater. I noticed the thicker bunch were unhappy immediately and asked to be seated elsewhere with more space. (I mean, come on. This is America.) I counted this occurred 5 times while we waited for our food. The problem with this is that they were immediately sat at a table for 4. Know what happens when that happens? Yep, the wait starts taking a turn and servers are double sat. I think a better suited way of handling this is telling patrons upfront, "A table for two? Ok. We have very small booths to seat two people, is this ok or would you rather wait for a larger table?" Then put them under the last table of 4 who are waiting instead of immediately finding a more comfortable table and double sitting servers. 
I ordered the mushroom and spinach omelette with diced tomatoes and hollandaise on top, hash browns, and a pancake on the side. My husband ordered a patty melt with fries and a side of onion rings. My son ordered a patty melt with fries. The server was nice. Servers and kitchen help are running everywhere. There was a server in the bathroom replacing toilet paper. They were out of paper towels to dry your hands. 
Our food came out after about 20 minutes. My omelette was covered so heavily in hollandaise, I couldn't tell it had any because it was a huge yellow gloppy pile that matched the egg. There were about 4 leaves of spinach in my spinach and mushroom omelette. Very little cheese. She said my hash browns would be out soon because they had to cook a fresh batch. My husband had a patty melt, no fries and a side of onion rings. Sigh. My son's was correct. I scraped off the slop from top of my omelette and was almost through by the time my under cooked hash browns arrived. They were hot on top and cold inside. I didn't waste my Keto diet on this. I also didn't waste it on my cold pancake that didn't even make the butter melt on top. I just put it all in a pile of frustration. My server made no comment. It's as if she knew and was not surprised. She didn't charge me for mine but she did charge me for my cold pancake that I did not eat. Ok. Whatever. Hey, my water was good. It was clear, cold, and tasted just like water. It even got refilled. 
I was totally prepared to be forgiving and patient with this visit today but the IHOP headquarters really missed the mark on this one. Making customers uncomfortable as soon as the walk in and have no where to go until seated is a bad start. Confused looking staff and servers makes for an uneasy outing. Double seating servers because you went with the tiniest booths for your tiny restaurant, is a no go. I won't be back. I'll settle for the grease and servers who discuss their drama with customers at Waffle House. Sigh. I had such high hopes. I waited 2 months for it to get to a good flow. Disappointed. I feel bad for the servers and staff because I don't feel like they are given a fair chance of having happy customers to begin with. We were seriously in everyone's way until we were seated because there is no where to go. Makes for a rough start.
Highly recommended. Cool little place, food was perfect and our server Ashia was very polite and attentive.
Great portion for my pizza craving. I did chorizo and all veggies. I would've like it to be pulled out of the oven a little sooner. The crust was a too toasted.

During my stay in Clearwater, I was on the search for a good lunch. I didn't want to go to any major chain restaurants so yelp helped me find a great spot for lunch!

I found Jakes Cantina as soon as I started looking for a place on yelp. It was the first restaurant that appeared for me. Most of the reviews are good but there was a few bad reviews that made me begin to question rather I should give it a try or not.

I decided to give it a give. I love quesadillas but I didn't see much reviews for it. I decided to try it, so I can let you guys know the quesadilla is a great option. 

Chicken quesadilla- added jalapeños, queso fresco and .50 cent extra to add black beans.

The chicken was very good and and seasoned. The quesadilla was a large portion as well.

Parking is a bit small. Since covid, the dine in is closed. But there are tables outside near by with umbrellas to sit and enjoy your food. 

ALSO, $1 SODA here.. 

Great choice for lunch or dinner! I'll be back.

We went for the lunch buffet and the flavors of everything was really good. However, the chicken breast dish was way over cooked. Very dry. I hated the cloth napkins. Indian food can be messy and they just weren't cutting it. The meat in red sauce was practically just bones and cartilage. The sauces were all great though and the bread was amazing. I just ate the bread with all the sauces and was content. Overall I wasn't overly impressed but I might try it again sometime if they work out the kinks.

Its all about the cheeseburgers! It's also all about nostalgia.  My Dad would have loved this place. The old cigarette machines, old signs, beat up booths, and all coolness of Nashville's Rotier's.  I recommend this place!  

Unfortunately, it has very limited hours.  
No breakfast (they should)
No dinner hours.
Just Lunch.

Creux-Creux (16$)// whole fried bullfrogs served with a green goddess sauce. Okay, I've had fried frog legs before but when these babies hit the table, I was a little shock! Just take a look at the picture I posted! Anyways, the frogs are fried in a light and tasty batter. The frog legs are very succulent. I'm not familiar with eating the 'body/ribcage' but that part did not have much meat. The green goddess sauce is amazing. It's tart, but not, just very refreshing. I recommend this dish but I felt that it was a little to heavy as an appertizer. & a little messy, plus difficult to eat. I had no problems using my hands and gnawing on the bones.. lol

Turtle Boudin Noir (14$)// turtle boudin served on top of pear, fingerling potato, house-cured bacon, and pickled mirliton. Turtle boudin had a very nice spicy (not too spicy) kick to it. The texture is a little pasty and dry, but not in a bad way. Fingerling potato brought a crispy texture to combat the boudin. I feel that this dish was very harmonized and enjoyed it a lot.

Warsaw Grouper (31$)// mustard green fettuccine tossed in a duck egg carbonara and topped with the crusted grouper. The grouper was cooked to perfection. I hate overcooked fish. This piece was moist and delicious! I wish the noodles were cooked al dente to add more texture to the dish. Don't get me wrong, pasta wasn't over cooked. I just prefer harder noodles. Overall, flavor was okay.  I think this is a 'play is safe' dish for the less adventurous diners.

Backstrap&Sweetbreads (29$)// lightly fried venison, seared sweetbreads, with squash gnocchi and pickled red onions. Again, the venison was cooked well. It was very lightly fried and tender. I'm almost positive I had a piece of gnocchi drench with truffle oil. Manfriend did not get that flavor, so, IDK! I'm a little over truffle oil at the moment.. Also, the gnocchi was a little gummy. I find that my standard for gnocchi is a ridiculously high. That being said, its unfair.. I prefer this dish over the Warsaw Grouper.

Fries (7$)// I know I said I was over truffle oil, but this truffle honey dipping sauce was delicious! Honestly, barely a hint of truffle. When the fries were delivered to the table, they looked very soggy. I hate soggy fries, but to my surprise, each and every fry was super crispy! I've never had fries like this before and they could be the next best thing for me..

Baked Chitarra (7$)// Cheesy Pasta. Yummy. I love cheese and I love pasta. So I love this.

Aged Banana Bread Pudding (10$)// It was okay. Definitely pre made and refridgerated.  If you don't like peanut butter, it can be over-bearing. I enjoy peanut butter. So, I liked it. Manfriend said fresh bananas could've made it better. I think the harden chantilly cream killed the dish for me.

This is the kind of meal you have with a beer. They do offer a handful of drafts. I had a toasted coconut something something and it went so well with my meal. I really like the glass they serve the draft in.. Nothing fancy, just a thin rimmed lowball glass (?).

I would come back. But beware, the meal was really heavy. So, maybe gym sometime before you dine and come hungry.

I loved this place. The atmosphere was so inviting. The food was amazing and fruit drink beverages were so refreshing. The jerk chicken is done just right. The Bajan Macaroni Pie was so creamy and spiced perfectly. Everything I tasted gave me a euphoric feeling. My friend got the cucumber mango salad, and it was light and fresh. We also tried the festival and that was good too. I'm definitely stopping in when I'm in Nashville again.

Hot pot is one of my favorite things to eat when it starts to get cold outside. I came here for lunch on a weekday (party of 2) and was quickly greeted and seated. This restaurant is unique compared to most other hot pot places because there's a conveyor belt in the center of the restaurant which allows you to grab any additional dishes that you would like for your table. These dishes aren't just for your hotpot but they also had a selection of different sushi rolls and sashimi dishes. For broth, we ordered the original and tomato. I really enjoyed the tomato broth although, would not recommend for anyone who doesn't like broths that are in the sweeter side. For a Thursday, it was fairly busy and there was only one waiter managing the tables. He was really nice but would say that he wasn't super attentive considering he had to check in on 10ish tables alone.

Overall I love this place. It's a go-to for out of town visitors because of the location & ambience. It's a lovely rooftop restaurant and bar with pretty twinkling lights next to the marina. They have heat lamps near the tables and little "fireplaces" in the lounge area for cooler nights. The food is good but a little more variety in the menu would be nice, especially where vegetarians and vegans are concerned. I do appreciate they they have some options though which is more than many restaurants can say. I love the tacos and the brussel sprouts and always get some guacamole and salsa with chips to start. Can't go wrong there. They have an interesting drink menu with a variety of cocktails to try. Service has always been great.
These guys rock! Saved my grits and the food is great! Eat this pizza you won't regret it;

Rolf and Daughters is amazing.  My husband planned a surprise dinner party for my 60th birthday and the restaurant helped make this a spectacular evening.  When we arrived they told us the table wasn't quite ready and to wait at the far end of the bar which was a plan so my children could come in and the manager captured my reaction on one of my children's phones.  Then when we were seated they had a printed menu just for us on the table and we proceeded to have a tasting of everything  on the menu.  The food was fantastic and by doing the tasting tried some things I may not have selected.  I can't thank the manager and staff enough for helping to make this evening one to remember and recommend the restaurant highly.  This will be a go to next time we are in Nashville.

Cute Italian place in the quarter. Found this by walking past and really wanted to try the pizza. Service was good and we ordered the Mona Lisa pizza right when we sat down. The cheese was delicious, the toppings could stand to be cooked longer and I don't even know if I tasted pizza sauce. The pizza was slightly above average but the thing that knocked it down is the dough being wet on the bottom of the entire pizza. Yea, not sure I should have even been dabbling in Italian here in NOLA anyway. I'm sure their entrees are much better.

This is a wonderful neighborhood restaurant.  We are able to walk there.  The service is excellent and the food delicious.  Steak frites were the best.  All three of us devoured the meals.  Drinks are fun and wonderful too!

This place is so good!! Got 2 volcanoes and 1 sirloin steak w eggs. Everything was amazing. Our server Ben was very attentive and nice. 10/10 definitely coming back! Also got a almond bear claw to go.

So far this place has not impressed too much. We set reservations for a certain time for outdoor seating and had to wait an additional 20 minutes before we were seated although there were plenty of outdoor seating.

See previous reviews: I'm obsessed with pho. 

And this one is pretty good.   The nice part about Pho 75 is that they only do one thing, its called pho and they do it well.  Both my guest and I had wonderfully slup-worthy concoctions, mine the chicken and hers the beef.  As others suggested, the food certainly passed the mustard.  I have to agree however that  the broth could have used a bit more flavor. . . 

So what's my gripe?  I'm sure people are reading this going, "Man Mike, you are kind of a hard ass.  If the food was was on point like you said, what gives with the three stars?"  Well, frankly the server needed to chill out. 

 I met up with a gal for a late lunch round about 2:00.  By 2:02 the waiter asked what we wanted.  I told him we just got there and needed another moment - After all, 120 seconds is barley enough time to get the ole, "Hey! Ya I know, its good to see you too" routine done and over with.  He sneered and left.  At 2:04 he was back, this time he asked if we had ever had pho before, I replied yes.  With that, he waited silently, pen in hand, for my guest and I to give him our orders . . . Alright, I guess we were in a rush. 

I'm not huge on service.  Normally; don't spill something over my shoulder and I'm happy,  but this go under my skin.  

Moral of the story, 4 star pho, 2 star service = Sorry Pho 75 but 3 stars is all ya get. . .

We have always ordered for delivery from this restaurant.  The previous two orders have left us with pizzas that are uncooked in the middle, and soft/non-crispy French fries and a personal wish we had not made the same mistake we made.
Please do not misunderstand--our first 3 orders were perfect and we thought we had found nirvana for ordering pizza.
We will probably try them  in the future, but the Tampa Bay Area offers many choices and it is our perception that Brooklyn Pizza is not as careful with delivery orders as they once were.
Good luck if you order from this restaurant.

Tried this restaurant for the first time last night. The restaurant was very clean. All of the employees were wearing masks.  We were greeted at the door and seated immediately.
Our server was excellent.
Once we ordered the food arrived in a very reasonable amount of time. 
I had the Miso Soup which was very good.
We shared an order of Spring Rolls.  The came out hot and crispy.
I then had the Sexy Lady. It was delicious. The portion was more than ample.
Wifey had a salad. Miso Soup and an assortment of Sashimi and spicy tuna rolls. She really enjoyed her meal as well. 
We will be back soon.
Exactly what you would expect from a Chinese restaurant. It's the typical Americanized version. They are consistent, but nothing that will provide that "WOW" factor

Delicious! I was a little skeptical looking at the outside of the building but like they say, never judge a book by its cover. Walked in about noon on a weekday, seated immediately. Warm chips, yummy salsa. We had the chimichanga meal, Nachos and chicken fingers and fries for the little. Everything was great, warm, fresh. The server was nice and always came to check on us.. we will definitely be back!
Great for lunch, coffee, dinner, dessert-- if I lived closer I'd spend a lot of time here. Excellent food!

My friends and I decided to have breakfast after having a blast at Hard Rock Casino.  
We wanted to go to Ihop, however there was not any around.  Actually this was the only place you could dine-in at the time in the area.  It was 3:30 in the morning on Sun....

When we got to the hostess stand, it seemed people were waiting just to be seated.  10 min after we got there,,,the line got even bigger but no one  was being helped.  
About after 15 min later, we were finally seated at a table...

Our watiress was very nice and she quickly took our order. However the food took about 1 hour to come out.  We were all having fun talking so we did not really mind waiting....but if we were starving that would have been a little different story.  

Since our waitress was nice and the food was good, I am giving it 3 stars.
I hope they work on their management and figure out that maybe  they do need more staffs for mid night shifts on weekends.

Great hidden away restaurant. I had the shrimp and grits and they were great. Wish I had some garlic bread to sop up the sauce. Complementary beignets for dessert!  The wait staff is laid back and friendly but attentive.

my wife , my son and I really enjoyed the fried chicken and the fried calamari here. They have this massive cup of grape fruit green tea, very refreshing. I enjoyed hat as well. Definitely coming back here again.

The food is interesting and well prepared, noise level a bit more than comfortable. I was expecting a casual neighborhood dining experience, portions are small, prices high, not someplace to go often, although every dish, save one, our party ordered was tasty. With one drink and wine, about $120/ per person.
Oh my god! This place was soooo good. I'll be back every Tuesday for the rest of my life!!!
The best burrito... period. There is simply nothing better than a steak burrito from Honest Tom's.

Today I made my way through the forth street road construction en route to Big Ed's Alley Inn for a roadside Bacon Burger craving and House Cut Fries. Upon entry into the dark, quiet little joint, I cozy'd up to the bar only to find my self at nearly neck level. The low rider bar stools must go! Had they been playing Santana, I might have felt more comfortable. Actually, had they been playing any type of music, I would have felt great. Pot scrubbing sounds from the dish pit was not the base I was looking for. It's ALL about the base.  Ok...I ordered a bacon cheese burger from the big smiling and gracious bartender. Hand Cut Fries are awesome, but the Denny's style bacon didn't cut it. Never downgrade your burger with skimpy meat candy. Go "Big" or go home.  All in all I had a pleasant experience. I love seeing owner operators work had to avoid working for the MAN! Keep it Big, Keep it Real.

We ordered the the original buttertop burger value basket . Which included medium fries and medium drink. For dessert I ordered the vanilla frozen custard. (Plain) staff was friendly. parking was easy.  Hamburger was very tasty and the frozen custard is a must. Hope this review helps. Enjoy!

"Vegetarian" apparently doesn't come with tofu or anything, it's just minus the meat. Food was half hot and half cold and very little flavor. Waiter tried to argue with me. Another associate here was quite nice, though, and the manager had it remade with tofu. This time it was great and she was extremely nice.

They have now started to make fruit smoothies to go along with the food. You can really tell that smoothies were made out of fresh fruit. I could see a woman cutting the fruit at the table!  I had a watermelon smoothie and it was delicious.

I've been meaning to write this review for awhile now, but the food here is absolutely amazing! Fairly priced and a lot of food. The bread they use is so good and fresh! We met the owner of the food truck who even gave us some water bottles on the house! I would definitely recommend this place to anyone!

I loved the food in this place...the mushroom and chicken pho were all amazing...the seating is tight, but I can deal with that.   What I CANNOT stand is that they don't have a phone.  What that means is that I cannot call to order anything to take home...even though they have takeout options.  Also on more than one occasion they are closed, somewhat inexplicably based on their posted internet hours...my husband says stock is like the hot chick you can't date...you try and try but you just give up after a while ...

I always have a positive experience at Chava's. I've had the De Guadalajara fried tacos as well as the ground beef tostadas. Both are great. The chips and salsa they serve you once you are seated are always fresh and delicious. The service has been consistently good every time I've dined here. Out of the many Mexican restaurants in Edwardsville, this is one I would definitely recommend.

This place makes,  uh "tipsy Joi" happy. To be clear, it's rare that I even want fast food because I like to take my time eating and I prefer to do so with table service... con vino. Then there are those times when you're in the Quarter and need to stuff your face immediately or you're not going to be feeling well. Behold: Carnival time. 

An entire vegan section on the menu in a fast food joint? Dreamy, indeed. I landed here after Barkus day this past weekend... three hours past because I, uh, lost track of time while at Bar Mon Cher down the block as one is apt to do. 

"No buns," read the sign on the door. But who needs a bun anyway? Not so into those but then again, I'm not exactly always in the mood for a hot dog. Wait, the door was locked! WTH? Are there really people out there loathing the bunless? Someone was kind enough to open the door. I actually pleaded with them... and promised, "I really don't need a bun." WHO NEEDS A BUN WHEN THERE ARE WAFFLE FRIES?! I ordered the Vegan Andouille dog with Creole mustard (a must). I'm looking back at the menu and I'm mad at myself for not adding on other stuff... lots of VA (vegan available) add-ons like mayo and cheese. I guess I was in a plain kinda way, because I didn't even think to get my waffle fries seasoned and instead, eyed my friends' fries quite enviously.

I love this place and I need to remember it in times of great tipsy hunger.

Amazing bruschetta.  Very nice waiters.  Atmosphere is very European.  But they have no AC.  Even though you're close to the ocean, the inside gets warm pretty quick.  Enjoyed the food.  Beautiful view of Santa Barbara mountains when sitting outside.

If there was a 0 star that would be my choice!!! My fiance and I walked in at 9:10 pm and there was a lady making orders for the drive thru. I asked if they had any more spare ribs and I NEVER received a hello or answer. She did however ask another coworker clocking out what time it was..? The girl answered her and she said " I'm locking the doors at 9:30"! My fiance and I looked at each other and turned to leave. No one said one word even with us walking away! Absolutely ridiculous! Someone should remind their employees that it clearly states CLOSES 10 PM! Plus EVERYTHING on the buffet looked disgusting and old. I would definitely recommend to go inside and check the food out before strolling through the drive thu! Our first visit as well as the last!

As Jessica mentioned, Lola's does have their own wine list. You can still bring your own bottle and I believe the corkage fee remains ridiculously cheap. 

We started with the Garlic Shrimp, Mussels, and Crabmeat and a few bottles of Sangria. There is a vinaigrette on the Mussels that is absolutely divine. The Shrimp were excellent and the Crabmeat was on top of an avocado with croutons--tasty. 

I took a leap of faith and did not order the seafood paella (but my friend did, yay!).  They were out of Kalamata olives for the spinach linguine and I am sure the dish would have been better with them. I also remained meatless. 

Also got to try a bit of the Duck Breast with fig sauce. It was expectedly a little sweet, but nicely done.  The Seafood Paella still stole the show, though. I think if you order a size larger than a single serving, the rice remains more moist and delicious. I have had few things in New Orleans that hit the spot better than this dish.
My favorite Thai lunch spot! Everything is wonderful and the server I always get is amazing!!! We come from work at least once a week!

The food is delicious. Ordered the California salad and my husband ordered the black rice and they were both extremely good.

The service sucked. I wouldn't go back.
What a fantastic sandwich place. My family and I stopped in on a recent trip to Philly and was blown away! We order the liverace, the roast pork, the hot dog. All amazing!! Will definitely be back!!

Just had an eclectic lunch at Franks which included several tastes of NO. Barbecue shrimp and  muffuletta were outstanding as was the ambiance of a family operated restaurant with their attendant squabbles. Kathy our waitress was best!  It's not fancy but was fun.

really big fan of the creativity going on in the restaurant. Burger was cooked to perfection, however they lose a star because i once overheard the owner talking to what seemed to be a family member saying that they accept cash only so they can make extra money on your service fee at the ATM inside... not a fan of your loophole antics to take my money. ITS 2018! buy a Square for heaven sake.

Right on the beach! We got lucky we found parking as soon as we drove into the lot. The bar area is small and very limited seating for happy hour but we got lucky again and found a table. Their happy hour prices are very affordable for being on the beach that made happy hour happier! Glass of Kalyra Sauvignon blanc was $6.50 and the Strawberry Jalapeño shrub cocktail was tasty. We ordered the calamari and the ahi poke about $10 each. Both were delicious and a good amount on each plate!

We read about Sate Kampar in the Washington Post and decided to give it a try last night. What an unforgettable experience!  Thank you to the very attentive server who suggested we try the salmon fish head curry with eggplant!   We felt so honored when the chef came out and explained all the fine points of this dish, considered a great delicacy and for good reason!  The variety of textures and tastes was amazing.  If you are a first timer with this dish you may have to overcome a certain squeamishness factor, but if you do, you'll be glad you did!   Every part is edible and delicious right down to slurping up the eyeball and sucking the rich curry sauce off the bones.  Even if you stick to just the meat, there is plenty there to keep you interested.  The refreshing kerabu salad goes very well with it and helps tone down some of the fiery power of this superlative dish.  We're from Maryland, but we hope to come again soon!  Prices were reasonable and everything else we tried was delicious.

I cannot comprehend how anyone could rate this restaurant anything above a 1-star.  We went for the sushi and were utterly disappointed.  Some of it was warm and overall not the highest quality.  For how overpriced everything on the menu is, we were expecting excellent quality sushi!  The service was terrible, waitress said hi and asked us what we wanted, then once the food arrived disappeared until we asked for the check.  There are so many places to get better sushi at awesome prices; avoid this place like the plague!

I found out about this place through the restuarant.com deal they offered. I had the Cheesesteak with onions and mushrooms. It was delicious. It took awhile for the food to come out although no one else was in the restaurant. The place is clean and has a fancy menu display.

VERY EXPENSIVE. Only one piece per order for sushi even though the menu clearly says two. They cannot tell you ingredients for many dishes which is bad for anyone with a food allergy. Very disappointing!

This place looks pretty for a pizza place that serves both pie and by-the-slice options.

However, I'm not sure why it took 10 minutes for them to heat up the one slice I ordered. It wasn't even warm when I got it. I thought it took so long because they were making it fresh to order, but turns out it was the same shriveled up slice I saw in the window. 

For over $3, I didn't expect my slice to carry such a scarcity of toppings. I don't need my pizza slices to overflow with a plethora of toppings, but taking a bite of this slice made me think of what pizza might taste like during droughts, famines, and wartime rationing.

There was barely any tomato sauce on it, and only a little bit of dried up cheese in addition to some chewy/dried bits of sausage. I guess the quality might be better if you order whole pies, but they shouldn't offer slice options if they are going to do a half-@$$ job of it.
I have been impressed that last 4-5 times I've visited. The burger is fresh and looks like a masterpiece! I've been excited to unwrap the burger lately!

Yo, let's be real. This place is awesome. It's inexpensive, delicious, and the workers and owner (woman who is always there?) are unbelievably friendly. We came back to back Saturdays and she remembered us as well, which was nice. Now, is the food the best Asian I've ever had? No. But is it pretty damn good for a low price? You betcha.

The tea is on point, the food is great, (especially for the price), and the service is speedy. I don't know what else you want in a good Asian restaurant, but I highly recommend this place, in the same way it was recommended to me.

Might be back for a 3rd Saturday in a row.

I have only had thai food a few times, but the King and I was very good. Our service was great-- he kept checking on us and make sure we were doing good. We ordered crab rangoons and thai ravioli for appetizers-- definitely recommend them both! We got 2 entrees-- green curry and spicy noodles (both medium spice and with chicken). The green curry was DELICIOUS. The spicy noodles were extremely hot, and not our favorite. But overall- great experience and will definitely be back!
Horrible, horrible, horrible. Took 2 hours for the delivery, more than half our lunch order was missing (of the 5 sandwiches we ordered we got 2!)   DO NOT WASTE YOUR TIME!

Jimmy John's sandwiches are good, but nothing spectacular, at least to me.  The bread tastes fresh, the meat is too and the sandwiches are decent, there's just nothing particularly noteworthy or special about these sandwiches taste-wise for me.
The service here however was lighting fast, very efficient and it's very easy and simple to order.  They have a friendly staff and the place is immaculate. They deliver too.  The service here will bring me back.   I like that they sell day old bread at a discount too.

3 stars is the absolute most I can grant to a pre-fab chain with average food and cattle-call service.

We were dragged here for my mother in law's birthday and it happened during "Lobster Fest".  For $30 I got the Lobster lover's plate with a very nicely done rock lobster tail, a tough, dry overcooked Main lobster tail and a small blob of baby shrimp and lobster micro-bits alfredo (I luckily was smart enough to get mine with no alfredo sauce).

Edible?  yes.
Good?  Some of it was good.

But I am still not a fan of this place.

Monday night dinner and I must say that I was super disappointed in my meal.  I had the Chicken Roulette  and the Gnocchi was gummy and had zero flavor.  The chicken was dry and over cooked but the sausage stuffing was really delicious.  I wish I could have just had a bowl of that. I was so hungry but just couldn't eat most of my dish....and I felt I wasted my money and I hate that.  As we waited for our food I looked around and noticed the shelves were really dusty.  I hope they don't start to let this place go downhill.  This was my 4th visit to Datz.  The first three times were for Sunday brunch and this time was for dinner....I was hoping for wow and just got blah.  I must say our waitress was amazing.  She was fun, quick and super attentive she made up for my mediocre meal.  3 stars for her.

Beautiful  place but the service is not good at all,I went there couple times and always the same service,they need serious worker to make the place better,.the food is great but i don"t want  go there again,I want to seat,eat and be happy

When I was 23 and new to St. Louis, I stumbled in drunk with some friends who were local.  I hesitated to order as I deciphered the menu through the thick smoke of cigarettes.

A middle age waitress at the cashier with heavy blue make up, smoking a cigarette, both took and made my order FOR me.  "nope.. NOPE.  You're getting a FUCKING slinger. you'r getting a slinger. I'm not hearing anything about it."  And so.. I was introduced to "the SLINGER."

Gritty establishment to say the least surrounded by car dealerships without the fancy lights and curbsides of suburban car dealers.  The grill was manned, I kid you not, by a guy in a wife-beater tank top, smoking a cigarette as he cooked the food, and thick mustache.  I was either drunk or this place was unbelievable.  Gritty is an understatement.  Awesome.

It was one of the best post-drunk meals I ever had.  My introduction to St. Louis was solidified and I emerge a new man.  The slinger probably grew hair on my chest.  My innocence taken. hah.

Perhaps they will never have anti-smoking indoor laws in St. Louis city.  It wouldn't be the same without them?

I was always planning on writing a more in depth update on Nu Yalk Pizza, but now I am told that they will not be there anymore because the city is widening the road.  Not sure if they are moving to a new location, but I hope so.

Tired, cold and hungry after a long work week my husband and I decided to finally try Rice. Driving all the way from Meridian we got right in on a Friday night (Datenight, no kids!)However, we were escorted past several empty tables located centrally in the dining room towards an extremely awkward table for four all the way in the back located right by both rest rooms, the back door, and facing the service station! boooooooo! It was extremely cold and I had to put on hubby's sweatshirt as I sat shivering... The bathroom was filthy and unmanaged. The paper towel dispenser was falling off the wall. 
Drunk folks kept staggering past us back towards the bathroom which was very unpleasant , one lady kept brushing up against hubby's arm as she swaggered passed, and an old cowboy and his wife??? Were making out on the bench wall and groping each other!!! Gross! The place "looked classy" but as an area supervisor of several restaurants, my expectations were way too high for this place~ oh! The food was amazing and the service was mediocre. Our second drink was terrible and when our waitress came back to check 15 mins later, it was too late for us.. Won't be back. Disappointed on Datenight...

This place doesn't fit in with the rest of St. Louis.  The decorations are way out there, well I guess for my tastes.  It's a unique place catering to vegeterians.  They do have a brunch.  I liked how it's such a friendly place; the owner will come talk to you.

The food was okay. We choose this place because there was a Yelp deal $20 for $30. The deal was incorrectly applied to the final cost of our meal. We only had $20 subtracted from the total cost of our meal instead of $30. So ultimately we did not save anything and paid full price in the end. The waitor did not correctly apply our Yelp deal. It was too late and not worth the hassle once we realized it. There was a nice ambiance to the place. The cheaper meals were really basic and you had to pay extra for things you wanted to add on.

I wish there was a way to give this place a negative star. This was an horrific meal. I had a craving for a "good" cheeseburger and fries and these guys couldn't even offer up mediocre. For me to drop $10 for a cheeseburger and fries it better be damn good. The burger patties were overcooked, dry and rather flavorless. The only cheese they have is the processed American cheese product. No thanks. When I asked for no mayo, I guess that was the go ahead to add 4 times the usual amount of mustard. Fairly nasty until I scraped it off. The fries were cooked a while ago and were room temperature and kind of oily limp. All in all a trip to In-N-Out would have been cheaper and better. The search continues for a good burger.....

This place is amazing. You should make reservations at least a day in advance it gets booked up pretty quick. We just stopped by and found that we should have called sooner. 

The hostess was very nice and told us we could wait till 9pm or eat at the bar. Tommy was really nice and let us eat our meal at the bar. The service was excellent. My ice tea and my husbands drinks were filled often. 

The explained the menu and gave you time to decided and relax.

The lobster dumplings were sooooooo good it was like melting in your mouth. I could have locked the bowl clean.

The soft shell crab in brown butter was crispy outside and moist inside.

The BBQ shrimp was just ok compared to the other appetizers.

The scalubit with halibut topped with scallop and with lobster rosoitto  was worth every penny and then some. I was in amazement of how the scallop was nicely chard golden brown and the Halliburton moist inside and the rice so cream like an angel made it.

Let's just say between the excellent service and dinner I am a fan for life!

Everyone should try this place once, because it's a novel idea. The soup and sandwiches are good, but at some point during your 3rd or 4th visit you'll say to yourself, "Ya know... all I'm really doing here is eating a grilled cheese sandwich and having tomato soup." And you'll wonder what's really so special about it. That's when you'll realize that this place is ok, but nothing to write home about.

We've been here twice. The food is always good. The Korean wings, Mac bites, and pretzel bites are our favorites. The burger is really good. The menus are different depending on the day. They have amazing drinks and desserts. There is an app to put your name on the wait list which is convenient instead of standing in the crowd. Right next door to a donut and ice cream shop: dough. The service has been fantastic each time we went. My only caveat: no diaper changing stations so change your baby before you get there.

I was a little skeptical about going out to brunch on Easter Sunday at 11:30, but out hostess told us it would take 30-45 minutes and it took half that amount of time. 20 minutes.  Devon was our waitress and she was very nice and polite. Her recommendations were spot on; short rib Benedict, Creme Brûlée french toast and the Kitchen sink were all delicious.  
The short ribs were tender and melted in your mouth,  Creme Brûlée French Toast had the fresh Creme on top and had a little crust and soft on the inside. The kitchen sink was big enough for two.  Fantastic. 
Best party, the manager cane over and asked if we ordered Blueberry French toast, we responded that we didn't but he could leave it if he'd like and we would tAke care of it. To our surprise, he did just that.  We were shocked, but that too was awesome!  I'm excited to go back and try more things on the menu!
One of  the last places where you can get AUTHENTIC Pizza Hut Pizza...HELLO!!!!  Supreme Personal Pan Pizza here I come!!!!!

Just tried this place for the first time. It was very good. Even my picky husband found something he liked. Was pretty busy at the lunch hour but it's a big place with plenty of seating. I had the tomato stack. It was awesome. I highly recommend it for a lighter bite. It's a combo of items I would never have thought of with Fried okra and beans. Sounds odd but surprisingly delicious!!!! The Mac bites as an appetizer were also very good. We will definitely be back. I can't wait to try some of the other interesting items I saw on the menu.
Awesome food at a great price. Loved my Arizona Burro and the wife enjoyed her Chicken quesadilla. Chips and guac were fantastic as well. Highly recommend

Came here for lunch on a weekday with a group of four. We each ordered a slice of pizza and a salad. The pizzas sit out and they just warm up a slice in the oven. There was artichoke, plain cheese, veggie, and plain tomato pizza. I am one of those weirdos who enjoy tomato pizza, so I was happy to see that option! The salads are quite large and tasty. The pizza, however, falls flat (literally- the crust has no rise to it), and it lacks in flavor. It felt like eating for function rather than for fun.

TL;DR: pass up on Slice and go for any of the other good food places near Rittenhouse!

Great decor.  We were really excited when they opened.  Unfortunately, it's been all down-hill from there.  The miso is near flavorless.  The sushi is mostly fried w/ cream cheese filling.  The service has never been good.  At least not in the 10 times I've been there.  Not a place I would recommend but if a friend wants to go... whatever.  Watanabe should definitely be better.

The location is little odd but definitely worth it if you're in the area.  I had the Cock-A-Doodle Moo and sweet potato fries.  Both were fantastic and really filling.  Think of Five Guys Burgers with a little more variety and creativity.  Damn good burgers and I should know.  I'M A MAN, DAMNIT!

Honestly, have order from them four times total. I've had their Banh Mi twice. Once at this location and once at the Ambler location. Both times there was not much sauce in the Banh Mi and the meat didn't have much flavor. Not something I will buy again honestly for the price. 

I also order Pho from here. Let me say it is not Pho. You pay over $6 for a pint size container that how two bits of under cooked rice noodles and scraps of beef. And sad to say the broth has no flavor. If you have read any of my other reviews then you know I don't like Main Street Pho in Manayunk, but I rather have the pho there than here. 

Only think I've tried from them that I really enjoyed is the seasoned fries.

I give them a 2.5 out of 5 really.

For inexpensive Chinese, less oily tasting and fresher vegetables than most.

Not sure I can go five stars for a restaurant that serves on plastic and doesn't have a bathroom, but if rating on value alone, probably close.  Offers $4.95 weekday lunch bowls.  The best of the four our party tried was General Tso's with steamed tofu substituted for chicken.  Their chicken, though, came with a crispy breading which wasn't mentioned on the menu.

Came here to join a group for a friend's birthday. I arrived later.. around 630 so everyone else had already eaten and drank. Our waitress was sweet and pleasant but did not provide very good service. My friend waited 20+ min for a beer and I had to remind her twice. We ordered one burger to split and it took over 30 min.  It seems that young inexperienced people work here. As we sat waiting and waiting and waiting... the waitress was talking and laughing on the side with a coworker. It's a very loud space. If if was half empty, it might be enjoyable. The burger was good. The drink I got had a good flavor but 11$ was a little steep. With a little more training, less people and better drink specials it could be a great venue. I'm not sure I would return other than to maybe play some ping pong
This is my favorite Mexican restaurant in the area. Great margaritas and great food, I clear my plate every time I go! :)

what happen you messed up my order i ordered a cheese steak hoagie and got a cheese steak also ordered a pork roll on a steak roll got a pork roll on a bagel would have come back but we live 7 miles away and got home to eat and saw this did not want to drive back with all the rain and traffic this has never happen before are you slacken tired sleepy or have someone new orders should be checked before they go out the door .

Let me just say, I heard really good things about this place. I was going in with pretty high expectations, even though the photos didn't really appeal to me. The rating for this restaurant on Yelp is really high and I trust my fellow Yelpers.  BUT, unfortunately, I left with a series of very salty plates that kind of ruined the experience for me.  I am not sure if I will come back again because this place is a little far off from where I normally roam.
 
Everything started off great. The restaurant is large and beautifully decorated. It has clean seats. The place is busy, so you can be assured the food turnover is good. The servers are all friendly. The restroom is clean and well maintained.... The menu was reasonably priced.

We ordered a bunch of appetizers. Of those, only one was good. The guacamole was delicious but needed pepper. The rest were almost inedible. The tuna that was recommended was undercooked and bland. The portion size was too big to be easily eaten.  The burger appetizer was really over cooked. It tasted like a dried out piece of hotdog that tasted like a taco.  

After that, we got the entrees pretty quickly. The entrees we got were also not as pictured but more importantly either completely flavorless or WAY over seasoned. The shrimp tacos that I got were really salty.  They shrimp appeared to be salted. Then, drizzled in a soup of extremely salty cheese. I could barely finish forcing myself to eat all of the shrimp inside. 

As a result, I felt really full but not in a happy way. I felt like I stuffed myself with salt and cheese. I also got to try a little bit of everyone else's food at my table and the other dishes were pretty bland as well.  I probably will not come again.  If you do not like salty food, then I would recommend you do not go here.  

There are a couple of Mexican places around the area that have better quality food, even though they may be higher priced. For example: you may want to try Tamarindo's (in Plymouth Meeting) or Magarita's (in Lansdale) instead.

This place is an absolute must visit if you are in the Franklin area. We eat tacos all the time and expected to like the food, but this place is truly original and surprising. We stopped in on a saturday for lunch and sampled the spicy pineapple margarita which was awesome. The chips and salsa was the perfect appetizer; the chips were warm, crispy, and the salsa fresh and delicious and unique! Next, we had "the puffy" taco and the fried avocado taco and the spicy caesar. Everything was amazing!!! Cannot wait to go back and try more tacos!

unreal. best sandwhiches in the city. not your traditional menu but if you feel like going out on a limb and trying something knew you will love this place. gustaio is my personal favorite but ive had countless incredible sandwhiches from paesanos. good service and great food

Great food! Chicken was crispy and stood up to the copious amounts of hot sauce I put on mine, mac and cheese had nice seasoning, and the green beans were flavorful. Sat out on the patio to enjoy the nice weather and got to listen to the birds singing too :) I find it hard to wrap my mind around this place though, I'm used to meat and three's being hole in the wall dingy places. It's too clean here! Lol Minus one star for the plates, they use styrofoam for dine in and take out. I wish more places in TN would either use real dishes or recycled materials. It's just a lot of waste for no reason.

this is the best chinese food in santa barbara hands down
everything here is good, but my favorites are the potstickers, chicken noodle soup, and kung pao beef.

Not too shabby of a place.   I took my wife here on Valentine's day and they gave her a rose (and gave me the check!  but I suppose that's fair)  :)

The food was good.  My wife's dish was fantastic.  Tip: ALWAYS ask the server what they recommend.  I always make the stupid mistake of ordering the same thing (chicken parm) which there's just only so many ways to make...  But some of the specials can be amazing.  That's where the chef gets to have fun.   

The decor is nothing to mention... so i won't.

The servers were all very nice and the price was quite reasonable.

It's by no means the best food I've ever had, but it was a good place.  
Give it a try!
Louis is the quintessential basque experience! Family style serving and hospitality. Don't forget the pecan punch!

Wow!  Our first time here and we both really enjoyed it. The meal was very good and the service was just as good. We both got the Mexican Skillet and both had to get a take home box. Great flavor and filling. And the prices are fair. Looking forward to trying their lunch and dinner also.
Best date place to eat sushi. Price was good food was even better. Come and enjoy this wonderful food called sushi! My girlfriend was very excited to come to this place, definitely worth the trip.
Great small local mexican restaurant. I was very happy with my fajita. Authentic. Priced well.

Our go to spot for delicious food and wonderful service! The staff here is delightful and our waiter tonight was Rodrigo and was so patient and kind with my family and friends. We come roughly once a week and always sit on the patio. Between the food, staff and atmosphere I could not recommend this place enough!

I have received multiple recommendations for this place.  I have seen it many times driving up 1st Ave, but I haven't tried it until now.  
Your first impression of it is that it looks sketchy.  And to be honest, it is sketchy looking on the inside and outside.  I think the location and the way the building looks was one of the reasons I hadn't been in earlier.  But the food by contrast, will make you forget your first impressions.  The food served here is a combination of Caribbean and Asian fusion.  So there are Chinese options and Puerto Rican options.  I thought it was really good.  I was impressed by how well the food went together. My friend and I both got the Mofongo combo.  (Mofongo is mashed plantains).  The combo comes with chicken broth and a choice of 1 entree.  The recommended entree is chicken enchilado, with varying degrees of spiciness.  I think I went with mild or medium.  But it was plenty spicy.  My fiance got the broccoli beef combo (b/c he doesn't like to try new things).  
The way you're supposed to eat the mofongo is basically dunk the whole thing in the chicken enchilado and eat it like that.  But I think I was eating it incorrectly.  I was eating it separately.  Oh well.  Next time for sure.  
This is a popular take out place.  While we were eating, at least 3 or 4 different couples came by and picked up.  I would love to return or get take-away from here.  10/10 would recommend if you haven't tried it.

Nikka Ramen offers authentic Japanese ramen served with rich tonkatsu pork broth and the traditional thin straight style ramen noodles. 

I tried the Tonkatsu Spicy "Aka" ramen and the Tonkatsu Kuro ramen and both were very good. The Aka really wasn't that spicy, more a subtle heat. The Kuro was nice with its deep complex garlic flavor. I came back for the Kuro several times! Just a tip, the chashu pork or chicken it comes with can be quite fatty depending on the cut and this is normal fare for this dish.
