But if you're like me and just prefer leaner meats, you can ask for a leaner cut.  

I did add a soft-boiled egg for an extra charge of $1.50. I was surprised the egg is not part of the base price. The quintessential Japanese ramen includes the soft boiled egg in my opinion. I was however very happy to see that green onions, mung bean sprouts, the kikurage mushroom, and a small sheet of nori is included without an additional charge.

In terms of service and decor, the restaurant has a sleek dark modern look. Ramen chefs can be seen working in a type of bar or open kitchen. It was nice seeing customers sitting at the bar casually speaking to the chefs. The servers are straightforward but still seem to be getting used to the environment. The owners however are excellent hosts. They are warm, cheerful, and polite, always greeting customers with a bright smile. The dish of complimentary Hi-chew candies at the register as you're leaving is a nice touch.
Tasty pizza and great location right in oaklyn on the white horse pike. (Rt 30) and they also have good prices if your looking for delicious pizza at a good price go here!
Really yummy and cheap!! I got a large pizza with 2 toppings for $7.99. The breadsticks were really good as well! We got our food to go.

I love this place for a quick down to earth meal. Their food is flavorful and there's always a place to sit and talk without having to yell at each other because of the loud music. The menu consist of Caribbean food with some fishes that are Asian. I had a small boil of chicken broth perfect for getting over the flu. I also ordered their side order of green plantains which they season with garlic salt and serve with a mayo sauce to dip. I gobbled it up.  This is not a fancy place. It's perfect to order lunch when you're short of time. The staff is fluent in Spanish. I didn't see any high chairs but it seemed fine for wheelchair access.

Overrated. This is not St. Elmo. The food isn't anything special; it's not nearly worth the price. The bartender was friendly and she did a good job. The bar was a nice area for singles to interact (friendly, not a meat market or gold digger feel).
lunch buffet from Monday to Saturday at 11:30am-2:30pm

Always good food and love supporting local.  Had some co-workers in from out of town and brought them here.  They loved it so much they are still talking about it and can't wait to come back.  Service matches the quality of the food making it an all around pleasurable experience.

Not a HUGE proponent of chain restaurants, but Houlihan's does something that makes me want to come back, if for nothing else, happy hour. 

Prices are exceptional for happy hour, and the choices are abundant. The drink prices cannot be beat, and our bartender was charismatic and attentive. 

The actual food on the main menu, I could take or leave. At places where the genres of food are so scattered, I feel that they all just end up mediocre. I have tried the jambalaya and stir-fry- both meals I could make better in my own kitchen for a fraction of the price.

I'm in love .... I've heard phenomenal things about the restaurant but it was hard to get a reservation. I picked my husband up from work downtown yesterday, it was a such beautiful day we decided to hang out and have dinner. On a humbug at 4:45pm my husband went to OpenTable and found that Steak 48 had 5pm reservations. Wow I wasn't expecting that. He said valet parking is $26. I REFUSE to pay $26 to park the car for a few hours. I said if it meant to be I'll find a meter nearby. IT WAS MEANT TO BE, I found a meter a block away from the restaurant. I called the restaurant to make the reservation then paid for parking . (If you don't have the meter up app to pay for parking please get it, it make's life so much easier) At this point my heart is telling me I'm in for a treat, and I was. 

We walked into the restaurant , everyone was so kind. Even the young lady who took my phone reservation came over and introduced herself. As we were escorted to the table the host again welcomed us to the restaurant . Every aspect of the restaurant is BEAUTIFUL .... the floors, walls, artwork, ceiling, decor, employees etc ... By the time I was seated I was giddy. 

Our waitress, Porscha , was beautiful , knowledgeable and attentive. Every suggestion she made was on point, including our drinks, I had a strawberry rhubarb something and my husband had an orange old fashion. My drink was so good, i had another 

Now lets talk about the food . For starters they placed piping hot freshly baked melt in your mouth rolls on the table. Delicious. I had to stop myself from eating more. For appetizers we couldn't decide if we wanted crispy shrimp deviled eggs (Porsche highly recommended it) or fried calamari and shrimp ... we got both and they both were DELICIOUS but I have to admit I dreamt about the deviled eggs last night. For the main course I had the crab cakes (I'm a Marylander so I can be quite snobby about Crabcakes ... they were delicious but a little loose for my liking) my husband had a perfectly prepared filet mignon. Our shared sides were mashed potatoes (chef how did you make those perfectly fluffy, perfectly seasoned mashed potatoes) and snap peas (so so so delicious) . We could not finish all of anything, so there was no room for dessert until Porscha placed two chocolate chip cookies on the table. Of course it was a buttery perfectly baked perfect cookie. 

There's so much more on the menu I want to ... no I HAVE to try so I'll be back really soon.

Oh and my parking was only $4.09 because you can stop the meter on the Meterup app and only pay for the parking you actually used

I gotta admit that the Rodizio isn't normally my thing.
While the IDEA of eating huge variety of meat sounds awesome, I find that normally I don't eat anything BUT meat and end up stuffed to the gills and feeling like death - my own fault of course!
However I was very very happy to find that in addition to every type of meat under the rainbow, there was also an excellent buffet/salad bar that had a wide variety of excellent additions to the meat including different types and styles of beans, rice, and salad mixtures.

If you can get past the paper napkins, bent forks, plastic beverage cups, then put this place on your go-to list next time you are near Philly.

used to be one of my favorite places but too many younger people working there now. they have poor customer service and aren't attentive when ordering. my order has been completely wrong the last few visits. 
the food is good but used to be better, i haven't seen the people i used to see around lately. i'll probably find a new place soon.

Update:  quite a few reviews talk about service which I agreed with; however, they seem to have updated the team and hustle is the name of the game which is awesome because the food is really good and reasonably priced.  Try this hidden gem!  (No, I don't know the owners, just excited a restaurant opened in the area).

Admittedly, we walked in 15 minutes before close, however you wouldn't know that because they only post their hours on the back entrance door (why, by the way, would you only post your hours on the door facing away from the first entrance anyone drives up to?) If we would have known this, we wouldn't have went in. And before someone tells me "you should have googled it." We were driving and decided to pop in for a quick bite and they close at 8 or 9 every other night. 

Nobody mentioned anything about closing time being 7 (you know, in the middle of dinner time) and the cashier even asked if it was for here or to-go, we said "here" at which she went quiet - but again, saying nothing about closing time. So my wife and I ordered, got our drinks, wiped down the table, and sat down. 

Ten minutes later, she brings out all of our food in a to-go bag, tells us "Thanks guys! Have a nice night!" And stands over the table. I asked what time they close and she said "Oh seven. Sorry!" And halfway ushers us to the door - which she apparently locked while we were in line so we had to unlock the door and walk out because she disappeared in the meantime. 

Look. I totally understand quitting time. And if the cashier would have said "hey, we are closing in 15 minutes so unfortunately this will have to be to-go" (though coming from a Chick-Fil-A background in the past, I would have been fired), I would have promptly went across the street or not ordered a soup and salad that I had to manage to eat in the car in a Target parking lot before it got cold because we didn't have a place to sit. 

So, if you're planning on Earl for dinner on a Sunday night, get there early. Though I won't be back because I left feeling frustrated and embarrassed.

I went here on three separate occasions- once on a date, and twice with friends. The food is excellent. The choices for vegetarian food are limited but the dishes available are AMAZING. They prepare everything in fresh every day and the walls are adorned with fresh basil which is plentiful in the cooking. If you are not vegetarian like myself, the chicken I hear is phenomenal, tasty, and well prepared. 

One of the best things about this restaurant is their wine tap list and service. They do not serve bread for appetizers Instead have several different rotating antipasti dishes. The entree portions are large and come with salad and a shot of espresso following the meal. The waiters and waitresses are very kind and attentive. The place certainly has the feel of a family owned, old world, Italian restaurant. I don't ever eat out at Italian restaurants because the pasta can get boring and bland ( think chains) but Oggi is certainly one of my favorite restaurants in Tampa.
Love the food. Bahn Mi was a nice crispy roll, picked vegetables as a twist on the usual fresh vegetables was good. Very pleasant service, nice decor.

Oh my, what a menu! The hardest choice for me was to either go custom made or try one of their menu items. I opted for the veggie malta melt and am pleased to report that I was not disappointed! The sandwich had just the right amount of toppings and the bread was toasted to perfection. The place is pricier than I would have expected but I did feel full and satisfied so I wouldn't balk at that. I plan to make this a go-to for my future visits to Santa Barbara!

wish I could give zero stars. went to try the new ch'king sandwich that people won't shut up about. ordered the spicy one and was given the non spicy deluxe sandwich which is not even remotely close to what I said. the correct thing that I ordered was even listed on the receipt which means that the people in the kitchen were not paying attention to what they were doing and clearly didn't care about messing up peoples orders. burger king sucks anyway. the sandwich was soggy and nasty. do not recommend.
They forgot to put the spring rolls in the order but the pho is all fresh ingredients and delicious !

Aggravating as it gets to simply order from them. Walk up window that they won't except orders from. Call in orders or door dash only. Dangerous parking lot. Expect vehicle damage from this parking lot. Won't be going back

One of the things I miss most about living in NY is the enormous selection of Chinese restaurants. Since we moved to PA, I've been to Philly Chinatown a few times and we found some really delicious food options. However, due to the commute time involved and the fact that we always have small kids in tow, its not practical to eat there with any frequency.

Then there's the predictable reality of suburban Chinese food. I don't mean to be dramatic, but the shell shock we've experienced from half assed Chinese food in our area has been breathtaking. Nonetheless, there's no way we're going to eliminate Chinese food from our eating out repertoire, so we pushed ahead, explored a bit more and ultimately came across China Carden. 

China Garden is a bit nicer than a typical Chinese restaurant. The decor is generic and the service is a bit unrefined, but overall, the setting was pleasant, the dining room was clean and the atmosphere was family friendly. The food was pretty good as well. 

For our meal, the wife and kids went with the chicken chow fun, the beef lo mein, a pair of egg rolls and the wonton soup. I went Japanese and got an order of the tuna tataki and a salmon roll. The Chinese food looked and smelled good and everyone said they really enjoyed their choices. I only tasted the chow fun which was nice, though a touch on the oily side. As for the sushi, I found it all to be surprisingly solid, though some of the maki pieces were poorly constructed and several fell apart as I dunked them in soy sauce and hoisted them up to my mouth. The fish was fresh for what it's worth, so I can't complain much. 

In the end, we liked China Garden. Sure, it's always better to head to the city for Chinese, but China Garden will certainly do for our day to day Chinese food needs. Three stars from me.

First time at Luciano's tonight for dinner. Portions are huge, service was friendly, food was average. House salad was way overdressed, and I got the Chicken Parmesan which was also over sauced, with way over cooked linguine. I'd rather have smaller portions of higher quality food rather than a trough of mushy noodles.
Excellent food!!  Great price!  May seem sketchy walking up to it, but anywhere medical professionals frequent must be doing something right!!!

This breakfast spot came highly recommended from a colleague who travels to the area often. It's set in a small strip mall just past a hectic intersection. But it's absolutely worth the trip!

I loved the selection of healthy alternatives and egg dishes. It took my coworker and I an extra ten minutes to debate our order. I tried the special (egg frittata) and he had the breakfast burrito. The portions were huge (but that didn't stop us from enjoying every bite!!

The coffee was also delicious. I know that should be standard, but I remember it being remarkably good here.

Their dishes are just so savory and delicious. I would highly recommend the Red Curry, especially the night I came in, there was a lil' rain outside so it was even tastier. The Pad Thai and Salad were good too with a hint of sweetness. The waiter and waitress were friendly, especially Lisa. Warm decor and lighting inside makes it more Thai authentic!

I love sitting next to people from all over the world and the food is still good. Just felt like we were just necessary evils to the staff having fun amongst themselves.  The older staff seemed to do the heavy lifting but you want to smack a few of the younger staff for goofing off and also sneering (mustache guy).  This used to be a favorite place for locals to take their visiting friends to have fun and great food, now just seems like you are sat and fed at light speed to get next person in...and as easy a possible so a party by the emps can continue on the sidines.

Conveniently located across the street from the Airbnb from where my fiancée and I were staying, we were told by our hosts that it was an absolute MUST that visit here while we're staying at their place. I can say that after eating their food, I had to agree.

Pricing: The pricing of the food was on par around other restaurants I saw in the area, in the $11-20 range depending on the item. The amount of food that you get for the price was definitely fair. This is a decent sized meal for someone with a hefty appetite, and maybe 2 meals for someone that doesn't eat as much. 

Food: The food was good. I got the Santa Fe Benedict while my fiancée got the Creme Brûlée French Toast. The benny came with plenty of meat, to the point where I was slightly struggling with finishing it, which was a good sign! The English muffin was replaced by a corn cake which was a great texture change for me. The French toast was cooked really well with a soft inside and a slight crispness to the outside. Along with the maple syrup and berries that came with it, there was plenty of sweetness to go around, but not overwhelming. 

Service: The service was okay. We only had our waiters refill our waters once during our meal, which was a bit of a bummer since the place we were staying at had no drinking water for us so I was trying to stay hydrated when we ate.
 
Overall: This was a great place to eat. I would come back here if I were ever in Philadelphia again. Just make sure to bring cash, or a card to use at their ATM.

I discovered Stock a few months back via a food delivery service. I had never been to the actual restaurant in Fishtown, so today for dinner my boyfriend and I decided to give it a try. The place is tight, so I wouldn't suggest anything over a party of 2. There is limited bar seating and a few small tables. We sat at the bar. 

For our appetizer we chose to try the prahok katee which consisted of Cambodian pork seasoned with little pieces of spicy heaven, and some green cabbage. All I kept thinking about when I ate it was how I'd love to add the meat as a topping on any and everything--delicious. 

Usually when I get delivery from here I order the chicken pho, so that's what I had in my mind to order today. I didn't anticipate it being such a hot summer day, and I also did not anticipate it being so incredibly hot in the restaurant, but whatever, I still wanted the chicken pho. The chicken is sooooo tender and delicious, and the bowl it came in was huge. When my hot steamy bowl of chicken pho came out, I realized I was about to get into a really sweaty situation. I managed to still finish my delicious pho--wiping the sweat off my face after every bite. My boyfriend shared a similar sweaty experience, having gotten the Thai pho. This pho was very similar to my chicken pho, but was super spicy. 

The wait staff at the bar where we were sitting were very attentive and kept us hydrated the entire time. I'm pretty sure everyone around us was either getting cold noodles or banh mi, so I will definitely have to try those next time.
12am and we just had the best customer service from Clinton! He was very professional and so nice.
Love the food the service was amazing wishing we had this restaurant back in Las Vegas
Decent prices and good fries. The burgers are ok. Nothing special. But I have only had one burger there. Definitely worth coming back to try more and find your favorite burger.

Brunch Done Right! My friend and I had the most pleasant experience at Anejo this Saturday. We thoroughly enjoyed their Bottomless Brunch (When they say bottomless, they mean it! No skimping around here!). For Starters, we had the Chips and Salsa Quatro...I had the ever so delicious Chicken Chilaquiles as an entree, and we finished off with some Toasted Coconut French Toast...OMG...For my Bottomless drink, I opted for the Margarita (You can change your drink at any time), they were on point, and never weak! The service was PHENOMENAL! Benny, the GM came to my rescue after I clumsily spilled salsa on my pants. The entire visit, he could be seen assisting staff and assuring that guests were enjoying themselves. Our waiter Justin was very attentive, knowledgeable, and oh so welcoming! We will be returning soon and spreading the good word of this marvelous place!

Peter's Diner is by far my favorite diner in the entire world/ the portions are HUGE and the prices are extremely fair. My entree came with house made breads (cinnamon rolls and sweet cheese bread), salad, and house-made bowl of soup. You are full before the meal even comes! For my entree, I got the chicken Alfredo sauté with crab meat. There is so much chicken and crab, it's insane! I get so much to take home and I always leave happy and satisfied. The food is so flavorful and amazing. I seriously can not recommend this place enough!!! We also got the shrimp scampi sauté. It came with so many shrimp! Love, love, love it here! If it were closer to me, I'd go all the time!

Went to Woodfire in Mt. Juliet tonight. This places is a prime example as to why you must use Yelp carefully. 
2 stars because it was loud and the manager didn't comp their dinner?!!? WTF!?
Tonight we had the Ribeye, Collard Pesto Chicken (pictured below) and the burger. Eveything was delicious, quality food. None of us felt the prices were too high. (This isn't 2 for 20 at chilis where your food is microwaved.) My parents and myself we
re all in agrement we would return; if you've ever sat through a dinner with the 3 of us you would know that's a compliment. We even had good service. 
The only fault I found was the chocolate custard was over cooked and began to curdle on the rim but the center was creamy as ever.
    We went at 5:30 and when we left it was getting loud which I'm sure the owners are working on...why not let them get a few bucks in the bank and work the kinks out before you throw a 1 or 2 star review out to a local business that 1. Mt. Juliet so desperately needs and 2. will affect this business for forever! 
  They did a ton of work on that building just to get open!  I had a great experience. Unless this place falls apart when they get busy I wouldn't  listen to yelp and make up your own mind.
All in all ...  A-

My Family and I came in Friday night and the place was packed! we expected some hiccups as this place has only been open for 3 weeks but the service was excellent. The waitress was super friendly and quick (a little stressed as well). our salad came out at the same time as the pizza which didnt bother us but she apologized and told us the kitchen staff is still training and trying their hardest.  The Food was amazing! we ordered a cherry apple salad that was better than expected and their Firenze pizza. The pizza was amazing, flavorful pesto but not too greasy with a perfectly fluffy crust. Good job Mofos and welcome to the neighborhood!

Stay away. We had a bad experience all the way through from the lobby to the food to the service. 
Granted they have been open for three weeks. We went early on a sat and it was not busy yet. Hostess had no clue what was going on. Waitress was nice but incompetent. Food was average burger stuff they tried to make fancy. Had to ask for water EVERYTIME my glass was empty (my pet peeve). 
I will never go back to this place. On top of all this they took a historic building and gave it a modern/Home Depot decor. I give it 6-9 months and it will be closed.

Tried this place on a whim because the original sushi place we planned to order from was closed. So glad I did. Coming from LA and all the good sushi there, it has been difficult to find a place in Nashville that I'm really happy with. 

We ordered the Brentwood roll as our wild card roll...it was delicious!!! Their spicy salmon and Philly rolls were close to the best of each I've ever had. 

We also got the soy sauce noodles which was so tasty! 

The only thing we weren't thrilled with was the tuna tataki. 

I have been thinking about this meal all day today and plan to repeat it tomorrow. 

Really really good.

We ate here on 3/29/16 and the food and the service were awesome!  The rich pork belly appetizer melted in my mouth.  My Tomahawk Pork Chop was cooked and seasoned perfectly.  My son's rib-eye, served on a sizzling platter, was wonderful.  My daughter's only complaint about her fish of the day served with gnocchi was that there were not sufficient gnocchi on her plate.  Each one was prepared ideally and she could not get enough of them.    My husband said the rich, amazing gumbo would made a very satisfying main course, but he had another course coming.  We highly recommend this place!

I just moved to town so I was excited about trying this place - I have not dined in, but I ordered Togo and was completely let down. I ordered edamame and that was the best of what I ordered, which says A lot. I ordered sashimi tuna and it was only one overpriced piece that was not fresh. I ordered a side of hibachi shrimp and it was 6.95 for maybe 8 max really small shrimp. Then I ordered a cucumber salmon roll that was weirdly soggy and the cucumber was not rolled right and falling apart. I would like to dine in and if my experience is different I will certainly change and edit my review.

Attempted drive through at 8:30 tonight and was told they are 10-15 minutes behind on order.  Only two cars ahead of me, WTF.  I backed out of the drive through and went to Mickey D's.  15 minutes for a cheese burger??!!  I see why the experts say BK will not survive the year.  Hello chapter 7.

I'm so glad that we decided to get take out from here. We've ordered from them twice. The first time we got several things but what stood out were the pretzels and fondue. I don't know how in the heck these people managed to make awesome cheese fondue to go but they did. The pretzels were also nice and soft and the rest of the food delicious. Second time we got pork belly nachos and they were awesome! Again perfect cheese sauce, meat, everything. We plan on ordering again and dining in once we feel comfortable to do so.  It is a bit pricy but well worth it. Best take out since We started ordering more regularly in March.

Absolutely amazing for brunch! I checked in on yelp before we arrived so we wouldnt have to wait long! We waited about 15 minutes before we were seated. I ordered the peacemaker with eggs cochon and chicken st charles and my friend ordered the bananas foster pain perdu. It was absolutely amazing. The chicken and cochon were soft and moist, and the bananas foster was delicious and not overly sweet. This brunch place is a must when in New Orleans !!

Some of the best Italian food we have found in Tucson. 
Portions are more then fair, their garlic knots are amazing !  The only thing that I would do different is a little more sauce on the pasta and the salad I wish was iceberg lettuce and not the fancy field greens. My husband and I find these very difficult to eat. It looks nice but give us cold crisp iceberg any day!

Wait staff was amazing food was hot and prepared perfectly.  Definitely will go there again. 

Olive Garden and Oreganos could take a lesson from this place.

I never had a problem ordering from here before but tonight wasn't the best. I ordered critters with a 6oz sirloin cooked medium to-go. The sirloin was way over well done and burnt on the outside. I was told 30 mins until ready, and I got there a little later than 30 mins which gave them plenty of time to fix this mistake. I paid almost $100 for dinner just to throw half of it in the trash.

Outstanding. Everything about this place is top notch. The decor is sophisticated and warm. My server was attentive, knowledgeable and gave some great tips on what to order. 

The broiled oysters were served perfectly and full of flavor. For my entree I had the signature scallobuit. It was one of the best things Ive eaten in  a long time. The accompanying lobster risotto was perfectly cooked. It complimented the fish perfectly. 

The Samoan ice cream tasted just like everyone's favorite Girl Scout cookie and was the perfect ending to an outstanding meal.
Best fried chicken in STL. Juicy and delicious. I don't like southern, as it's too salty and dry. You are not going be disappointed with the Original Sauce!

Really more like a 3.5.  I wish I had ordered what my sister ordered!  I think it was the kefta kabob combination, which came with to die for hummus.  Loved the smoky, charred meat flavor. We also got the falafels as an appetizer, but I wish they would have splurged on more than a thimbleful of tahini sauce.  I ordered the schwarma sandwich, which was flavored, strangely, a little too sweet for me, and not like the schwarmas I used to eat several times a week when I was broke and living in London in the 1980s.  And please, easy on the ice.  When I order a drink, my pet peeve is a glass that's filled 3/4 with ice.  Otherwise, a nice enough joint.
This was delicious! My husband and I got the grilled pork and grilled chicken bun and it was great! A big portion and so fresh tasting. We'll definitely be back

I took a few guests to dinner at this location and the atmosphere was very modern in the dinning area.  I will say the hostess had ZERO personality and was heavily focused on watching TV and lacks people skills but the waiter made up for it! He was nice, charismatic and able to be bartender and waiter!  Awesome! The food was excellent and I will return however I will sit in the bar.  The restaurant needs more A/C. It was very warm and one unit was not cutting it.  If this place invests in a second A/C it would move the rating to 5 star!  The crowd is very diverse from young to mature and I would definitely recommend giving this place a try!
My husband and I love going to Luciano's the service is great everybody there is super friendly and the food is out of this world wonderful!
This place is so good and they are so accommodating to our request. We will definitely come back here again.
Don't bother going here! They are rude and don't care about your business blew me off today because I asked to put tacos in a separate container. Told me to go somewhere else lol
Monkey bread madness!! Yummy stuff. Even better with bacon. Grits good. Bloody Mary so so. For those avoiding carbs, good luck! Hip vibe, especially sitting upstairs.

How did I go the last 30 years of my life without this cheesesteak!! Growing up in delco born and raised I have tried a lot of cheesesteaks and this is by far the best I ever had. I'm still in shock how amazing it was. From the roll to the fried onions it's just perfection. Please please make this place a must try if you are in the area you will not be disappointed.

Lastly cash only very old school spot no sides like fries just chips candy and soda. Nice little section to sit down if you want to eat in. Also sweetest owners so kind they make you feel like family from the second you walk in. Customer service 10 out of 10
Great food and huge portions. The service was excellent. The server who waited on me was very attentive and funny.

Great find in Clearwater Beach! Our server, Kyle, was happy to provide recommendations and the food was perfect and filling. Bringing home leftovers to enjoy again tomorrow  Check out this hot spot with a lively atmosphere and a draft beer list a mile long!

You'll not going to leave here Hungary OR unsatisfied! This place is amazing!!!! Brent...... is such an awesome server, try not to steal him for your own business! Wow!! He knows his stuff! 

Parking is easy, seating was easy, ordering was easy! This place has amazing flavors, super flexible with the bar and the food menu! 

I will be back again and again!! 

Great job Ijji noodle house!!! You just won 4 new regulars!

Last week, for my birthday
My friends and I came to Datz. 
I'm really disappointed to say
That our expectations kinda fell flatz. 

The crazy parking situation
Made me wanna punch someone's face. 
I circled the parking lots forever
Before I finally found a space. 

We didn't have to wait much for a table
Since we used their call ahead seating. 
We got the cool table in the corner. 
And before too long, we were eating. 

We shared an order of monkey bread 
With a side of bacon bits. 
For sugary bread, it was ok
But I wouldn't say it's the shit. 

For my entree, I went with a Bennie. 
The one with chicken and waffles. 
It was seriously lacking in flavor. 
But I ate it, it wasn't awful. 

The best thing about our experience 
Was the phenomenal service from Gabby
We can be a handful to deal with. 
She could've easily gotten a bit crabby

It just seemed like we weren't blown away
By any of the food that we ordered. 
I'd say "meh" to "just ok" 
Is where my star rating bordered.

'Surprisingly good tasting food served at your car window with prices that compete with fast food joints' is my quick summary.

I have driven by this local drive-in hundreds of times, but I had never stopped in before.  It sits across a steep "up and down" railroad crossing that you don't want to take very quickly and then is along a frontage road that isn't in the best condition.  Let's face it:  Mars Hill doesn't get much support from the city, so I'm usually surprised when I DON'T hit a pot hole.

Pulling in to their lot, you may want to pick a spot at the overhanging roof that allows you to read the menu.  The place looks in need of some maintenance, so it doesn't make a great first impression.  But what I noticed late in the lunch hour was that there were cars sitting there in the January cold with folks eating their meals.  And there was a steady stream of cars rotating in and out for their meals "to go".

In less than a minute of my arrival, a pleasant woman greeted me at my driver side window and asked what I would like.  I ordered the Tenderloin with Fries and an Oreo Shake (small).  The Tenderloin (I chose breaded) is small for Hoosier standards, but for just $.80 more, you get a second one ($3.50 for both!)  I passed on the second, but was impressed.

In just a few minutes, the waitress brought a small tray to hang on my window complete with napkins and condiments (salt, pepper, catsup, and yellow mustard) along with my meal.  I haven't had a dining experience like that since I was a kid!  

And though I've read a lot of reviews that refer to this as a "greasy spoon" or with a derogatory cliche, the food wasn't greasy or oily.  It may have been fried, but I've had far worse and paid higher prices.  This is simply good (and affordable) food that is served quickly.

And they take credit cards too!  

I didn't drive around the building, so it appeared to me that there wasn't any inside dining, but they do have picnic tables tucked away on the back of the lot that could be used in warmer months.

This was a nostalgic experience for me and I would gladly recommend stopping here for anyone who wants to remember what fast food was like before the drive-thru was invented.  It doesn't have roller skates or lots of neon here, but I did get a good meal and great service.

When I was finished dining, I simply put my basket and trash back on the tray on my window and the waitress (she covered every customer) immediately came over and removed it so I could get on my way.  

Review #3607
If you're looking for authentic Italian, this little place is definitely the spot! Live music, amazing pasta and Italian dishes and affordable wine.

After eating out a lot recently, I wanted something on the healthier side and this was one of the places that popped up on Yelp when I did a quick search. Located in the Paseo Nuevo shopping center so close to State St, it was a convenient stop!

Pickles & Swiss sandwich: 4/5. Gotta order the namesake, amiright? I added avocado to mine, and though the sandwich was great and had a decent amount of meat, cheese, sauce, and pickled vegetables, I literally laughed when I unwrapped the sub and saw how the avocado was slapped on the outside of the sandwich, with no attempt to layer it inside the baguette with the rest of the fillings. That interesting part aside, it was a good sandwich and I liked the aoili.

Spicy Chop & Toss salad: 5/5. Probably the best salad I've ever had. Would definitely recommend! Warning- if you're sensitive to spice, I would suggest getting a different salad because this thing is fireeee (taste-wise and spice level-wise, at least on the normal scale for a salad.) The only downside for this salad was that the chickpeas were bland, but mixing it makes it a bit better! Happy with the healthy food choices here :)
We are usually happy with our pizzas from this dominos. Today's order was awful. Pizzas were way under cooked and the border was 4-5 inches with no cheese or toppings. Like eating a large dough ball.

What a gem! Go here. We were on a day trip to Tucson and stopped in here on a whim for lunch. Perfect combination of:
1) Wonderful Mexican food -- barbacoa and pollo mole were phenomenal, beans were excellent, the prickly pear juice is a must try.
2) Great service -- super chill and friendly servers. As advertised, mandatory free hugs. The owner embraced us after lunch and wished us a wonderful day.
3) Ambience - the place just exudes music and joy. the notes on the wall about the projects funded by customer donations warmed my heart.
4) Value -- 2 big lunch entrees (more than we could eat) and a juice for $20 even. Wow.
5) Speed -- Could have been in and out of here in 15 minutes if we had wanted to, but we were having too much fun and lingered for an hour.

Me and my husband went to this restaurant, expecting a good dining experience and dinner. We got in, very quickly (was a Thursday night) and were seated right behind the "waiter hangout" area. The service was OK, the staff seemed to lack enthusiasm and passion for working there. 
We decided on the Couple's Night Special of the Evening. This started out with 2 glasses of champagne and a raspberry salad - which was completely overdressed and had the lettuce core and an onion peel in it (really?). We then received a glass of wine - which on the plus side was a good portion.
My husband received the osso bucco with mashed potatoes and green beans. It was prepared nicely and had good flavor. I ordered the red snapper, which was a big disappointment. It was terribly spicy - to the point I couldn't taste much of anything else, and on top of that was undercooked. I pushed that aside and thought I could eat the rice pilaf, which had to have been from a box - overpowering fake taste and lack of any creativity. 
I will have to say that we will not be back, it is unfortunate because it is close to us and a local place, but without the passion for service and the inspiration for cooking a nice dinner - a place like this would not be on our list of places to try for anyone.
We order mackerel sushi-was not fresh,the waiter tried to explain that it didn't unfroze completely..it wasn't okay with a food and the service was okish..(

If you love sandwiches, this is definitely your destination spot in Philadelphia.  Paesano's is really as good as it gets.  Not just in Philly, but anywhere!  

Be you one of those crazy vegetable preferring folk or just a plain old omnivore, they have options for every diet. Try the "Paesano" - it's well worth the...uh... really inexpensive cost and fast prep. time. 

Another thing to love is the service.  I've always found everyone working to be pleasant and funny.  Did I mention that this location is also conveniently located just 1/2 a block from a State Store...?

Yes, it's a great place to find yourself when you need a sandwich fix. Cash Only.
Pizza was excellent!  I only wish they had a dining room to sit down and eat in.  Mostly a takeout place.

We were a bit hesitant to try a poke place in TN since we are from California, but were pleasantly surprised! The place was clean and the service was great. 

We each got a build your own poke bowl. I got a small since I wasn't very hungry and my husband got a large. The portions were good for the price. The fish was fresh abs there were lots of topping and side options. The sauces were yummy too! I hope to try their sushi soon!

I would recommend this restaurant to someone looking for a solid poke spot!

I just tried this place for the first time even though they are 5 blocks from home. Wish I had gone sooner. The owner a very friendly young man has developed a rub and smoke that makes his meats unique. I had the spare ribs, very meaty, excellent flavor and tender. The bone is clean when done. The 2 sides I had were so good, the cheesy potatoes you could tell were homemade and I couldn't stop eating them. The green beans were cooked perfectly and had a great flavor. I know there are BBQ places all over but, for the price and quality, you need to give them a try. I'd give 6 stars if I could.

Okay beside the fact that it took me 2 weeks to get through on the phone after the move to the new location, this place was fantastic!  
Yes I was trying to make reservations for my wife's birthday and new they had moved but their phone kept ringing and ringing.  
Finally did get through and reservations were kept as promised.  
This location is right next to Pastaria in the heart of Clayton.  Parking is just above the restaurant in the garage and YES they do validate parking!  
When you first walk in you think you're in VEGAS not a sushi restaurant.  Very modern and chic decor.  The customer's walking in were definitely dressed the Vegas part. Thank god I brought a change of clothes after running the 80's/90's Fun Run in Forest park that evening.  

We ordered almost everything on the menu and somethings twice.  
My only 2 complaints were 1) the sushi menu was a little difficult to follow.  The names rarely help you with what is in the roll, 2) Some of the portions were a little small.  
That being said the quality of the food was excellent.  The roll that stands out the most of course is the flaming Oh My God roll.  The fire added just the right amount of smokiness to the sushi that was quite unique.  
Overall an excellent place to dine on wonderful sushi and people watch!  Definitely dress up if you plan on dining on a Friday or Saturday night, as the "pretty" people definitely come here to be seen.

This was supposed to be an Italian hoagie. Not sure who makes their sandwiches but apparently they don't know what an Italian hoagie is, this is a pile of tasteless junk on an equally tasteless roll. Unfortunately it wasn't possible to give them negative stars.

Pretty dang good pizza! My husband is obsessed with their mushroom truffle sauce! Dave Portnoy from Barstool has made me obsessed with New York style pizza and I have zero regrets. Perfect amount of cheese, flavor and that crunch! Yum! So delicious!

We stopped by this place on a Sunday afternoon. They were so nice! The patio has such a great feel and is covered so we ate outside even though it was raining. We ordered the hummus, a chicken plate, and falafel tacos. Everything was so good! Excellent service too. We will definitely be coming back!

I haven't been to this place in years. Had a bad experience awhile ago. Hubby and I decided to give it another try today.  We dined in . The food was super yummy. I had the chicken platter with onion rings and rice. Delish. The food wasn't even the best part. Our order was taken by Leticia. The most jolly, friendly cashier I think I've ever seen. She made me smile. She was just the sweetest. She made my not so great week a lot brighter!

I am very disappointed in the service and quality of the food that I ordered. I have been a customer for over 20 years and always order the same item. When I received my order tonight apparently per the owner their menu changed and the side salad that came with the chicken Dijon platter was not included. I had ordered this item less than a month ago and received a salad. The chicken tonight was very fatty and the coleslaw is terrible..also portion sizes have gotten smaller while menu prices increased. Will not be returning...lost a long time loyal customer because of your apathy.Was told by girl who answered phone that menu changed 5 months ago and when owner got on phone she changed it to 9 months pretty fishy if you ask me.
I eat here often. Like super often. Love Samuel and Christy and their food is always good. Go on Saturdays and get Birria Tacos! You won't regret it. And order a Michelada!
I really liked this place. I thought the food was good, great beer selection, decent kid options, and I liked the casual atmosphere!

8/10 - The chicken thigh was juicy on the inside and crispy on the outside, and the sauce wasn't too spicy; just the right amount of kick. The biscuit was soft and not too dry, it did a great job soaking up the chicken juices and sauces. The potatoes were... potatoes, nothing special. The brussels sprouts salad was surprisingly good, great crunch and acidity; paired very well with the Princess. Lastly, Nashville helped me realize my love for cheesy grits, it was delicious!! This was good, but there were stronger contenders for chicken sandwiches. I'd definitely come back and try other items on their menu!

IG @ hweedafoodie for more reviews!!

So I have to admit I was skeptical after reading the reviews.  A friend of ours from the Bay who recently moved to Reno made the recommendation.  AYCE?!  I'm too old to eat as much as I used to but can't resist sushi so hubs and I rolled on over.

I think we lucked out and caught the second lunch wave so our wait wasn't more than 10 minutes.  People complained the service was slow but it was fine and come on people it's PC (post covid) do you know how hard it is to get restaurant help?  Have a heart people!  

Anyways, we ordered a bunch of food and all I was stuffed silly.  It's true there is a good fish to rice portion (the nigiri) and even though some reviews complained the rolls were "small" but honestly, 10 pieces is plenty and I want to be able to order things and not just eat a bunch of rice!

I didn't try EVERYTHING but did repeat the salmon and unagi nigiris.  They were popper size and I love the freshness of the unagi nicely crisped and warm.  Baked mussels were good.  Definitely give them a try.  Loved the batter on the tempura.  Nice and light with a touch of sugar.  For dessert, fried banana with ice cream for sure!  Yum yum yum.  I used to love AYCE sushi in Davis but now I'll have to drive farther to Reno!

This is one of my most favorite spots with excellent food. The atmosphere is great and I have yet to be anything but satisfied when I've eaten here. I became a fan at the original location but I frequent this location more and I can't complain. Excellent food excellent service and authenticity all around!!

Heard about this places $20(ish) all you can eat sushi dinner, so my buddies and I had to check it out. This place is super small and all the tables are crowded next to each other. The menu has changed and has about half the stuff on it now. Only one menu was provided to our table and when I asked for another, I was told no... Thus me not being able to update the menu on here.

We ordered a bit of everything. Shrimp tampura, chicken teriyaki, regular rolls, shashimi, special rolls, gyoza. None of it was that good. The shrimp tampura was over fried, the chicken teriyaki was the most bland chicken I've ever eaten in my life, though it was drenched in some brown, flavorless sauce. All of the rolls were about 95% rice with the smallest piece of fish placed on top. The shashimi was the same. About the size of a goldfish cracker. The gyoza was the only decent hot item. We each had an ice cream to wash down the poor meal. It was the best thing we ate all night. 

I doubt I will be back anytime soon.

The place deserves a zero rating.  Let me first start off by saying I was absolutely starving when I arrived to New Orleans and was in hopes for some really great food.  I was referred to this place by a security gaurd on the street.  She swore by this place so, me being a foodie I wanted to try it.  I was in town on a bachelorette trip and had about seven other people to join me here.  Right before we placed our orders the waitress told us that we could not split our check and that we needed to get cash out of the atm out back.  My sister and myself were the only ones who did not have cash on us so, proceeded to the atm.  

We came back and placed our orders.  Our order took about 30 minutes to come.  In the midst of being so excited to be there we didn't complain about how long it took for our food to be delivered.  After, we got our plates the waitress came to check o us to see if we needed more water.  While enjoying my time with my friends and attempting to eat another bite of the craw-fish etoufee I noticed a roach in my food.  I started screaming and that roach began to crawl over the table.  I ask the waitress to come over to show her the roach and she became very upset with me and said that the roach was not there before.  She grabbed my plate and stormed off.  

My friends along with myself waited around 10 minutes for a  manager to come by and apologize.  We never received an apology however, the manager came over with the check and told us that if we didn't pay for the food he would call the police.  Instead of him calling the police,  I took it upon myself to call them before they could.  About 10 minutes later, the police arrived.  We explained our situation to three of the cops and the cops seems to be on the managers side saying that we needed to pay for our food.  We were very disturbed with how to cops treated us, even after showing him the picture of the roach on my plate. We even saw one of the cops high five the manager and stated "we will see you later on tonight."  

The cops made us pay for our food and told us that we were not in Georgia and that we were in Louisiana and that Louisiana laws were completely different.  

I really wish that we had seen all the terrible reviews of this place before coming here.  DO NOT GO HERE!!! THIS PLACE IS VERY UNSANITARY AND CORRUPT!!!!!!!!!!!! PLEASE READ THE REVIEWS AND FOLLOW THEM.

Unfortunately, this is the first review I'm posting that is unfavorable.  The door was locked upon arrival, as I walked away the owner Pie came out to greet us and explain the restaurant is not open so there's only outside dining.  After being seated my daughter and I requested water with lemon. There were no other patrons there yet it took a while to receive it.  We ordered an appetizer and sushi to enjoy along with the Yelp Elite Entree.  I enjoyed the stuffed jalapeños but the sushi was warm and not rolled tight.  We then waited an hour for the fish tacos!  They were a disappointment because they came out cold and dry!  Additionally, the menu should state its Tilapia.  I don't eat this type of fish, therefore I did not eat it.  My daughter ate a bite of hers and agreed it was not good at all.  The owner/manager was the only person taking care of everything.  Although there were only four tables she appeared overwhelmed.  Three different parties came and left due to the slow service.  I asked to use the restroom.  She stated sure but give me a few minutes.  When granted access i could see the restaurant is no where near completion, there's stuff everywhere piled up!  The bathroom had no a/c do it was stuffy and the trash was filled so clearly it needed attention.  I'm total were were there around 1/2 hours and left as soon as the tacos arrived. 

My recommendation is they should have waited to host the event when the restaurant was done and had ample staff.
This place is great. Everything from the food to the customer service. I love Jamaican food and I've tried pretty much everyone in Indy, this one is a close second.
Love the charbroiled Oysters ,buttery ,garlicy with great spice perfect with a Nola Blonde Ale . Friendly staff in sports themed surroundings

A year later Inca's is still doing their thing. Patio was crowded, inside not so much. Huge to-go and DoorDash business. 

They were horribly understaffed but the team did their best. Our whole chicken meal was perfect, packed well, and cut how we asked. 

Bravo.
I have to say, this place has amazing customer service. Their prices are incredibly reasonable and you get good portions. I will be going back when I'm in the area!
The employees were disgruntled and extremely rude. Our order took over 30 minutes to get out to us. Not a good place to go unless you have at least an hour and a half to eat.
Service was great and food was excellent. Definitely worth a visit, regardless of time of day or type of food.

Sat at the drive through for around 5 minutes with no answer. Drove up to the window and a girl was in the back and came up to the window to ask If I needed anything. I asked if I could order and she told me to drive back around. She put on her headset and trying to order was very very hard with a language barrier. My sandwich was made completely wrong. Not at all a good experience

I was in the downtown Indianapolis area on the circle and stopped to have lunch. This is my second trip to the shop.

Both times in the shop it has been very busy because it has been on a work day. I think it is all about getting people through the line quick. Which is a good thing for people who work downtown.

I had the pick a pair which is a half sandwich and half soup or half salad. I had the meatball sandwich and a farmhouse salad with Potbelly vinaigrette. I also had a short shake in Oreo. 

My sandwich was good and filling although a bit messy. The salad was in a good size bowl for the money. The shake was good and it had a little cookie on top of it!

If you are looking for a quick and tasty lunch this is a good place to go. If you are trying to park downtown on the circle or anywhere near it on a work day then good luck.

I've been on the hunt for the best Tomato Pie in the Tri-State area so when my Dad said he had a Groupon we decided to try Jersey's Tomato Pie and Bistro. I was so nervous coming here after reading the other reviews about the owner and the Groupon. But luckily I didn't have the same experience.

My Dad and I shared the Chopped Antipasto Salad. It was light and flavorful. And it's large enough to share with three people. The Tomato Pie had a thin crust but it wasn't crispy enough. If you tried to pick up a slice without the pizza server, the sauce would slide off. The sauce was good, it was on the sweeter side. I would definitely order their Tomato Pie again. Our server suggested the Brooklyn Pie. So that's next on my list of things to try. I can't wait to try their other entrees as well. The menu is extensive. Oh and it's BYOB!

The best Thai I have ever had...likely ever......and I am a food snob.

It looks like a very humble place on the side of the road, but the flavors were real.  Moreover, the owner made us feel welcome......she took time to speak with us.....made great recommendations....

Can't say enough about how we loved our experience.   You will too.

My third visit here (and first visit since the rats episode). The food is still very good. But the reason for the 2 stars is the service. Each time I go here it seems to be worse than the previous visit. 

The staff is pleasant enough - but they are clueless. How else can you describe a breakfast place that seats you, and then it takes 9 minutes for the server to come over to your table. Eventually she came over to take our order, meanwhile all we wanted during those 8 minutes was someone to offer us coffee. It was just after 8am, there were only two other tables (4 people total). How do you not train your staff to offer coffee to each table within the first minute of being seating for breakfast!!  It should be the law.

So she asks if we're ready to order and we politely tell her we'd like coffee and need more time to order since we were talking and catching up.

Anyway, the coffee is good - it's above average, but not exceptional. Throughout the 90 minutes we were here, she only offered a coffee refill one time without us asking - otherwise we had to ask 4 times for more coffee. 

I guess I'm spoiled by Hymes Deli - they walk around with coffee all the time, and you never have to ask for a refill or top off. And they have coffee on your table within 10 seconds of being seated, every time like clockwork!

Another issue is you can't order eggs without bacon or sausage. Yes, you can decline the meat when you order, but they won't reduce your bill. Very lame. I get that they're trying to get a higher average ticket - but they shouldn't make you pay for meat when you don't want it. It's stupid little things like this that business owners should pay attention to - instead of creating good will, they're creating bad will, which results with customers not returning.

The last issue is the single bathroom in between the two dining rooms. It was clean, however the door lock does not work properly. I had to play with it way too long to make it actually lock. Come on, just get a locksmith and fix it properly.

I think part of the problem is there's no manager around. The three times I've been here, it seems like it's just the hostess, servers and cooks - I've never seen anyone who looks like they're running the show. Ok, if you don't want to pay for a manager during slower weekday morning hours that's fine, but at least have a set of protocols that employees can follow and held accountable to.

After the rats and pizza in the window this past summer, I felt they would have no trouble overcoming what would normally put a lot of places out of business, due to the quality food and the huge following they've developed. However, after my last two visits with below average service - well all I can say is if they ignore the service issues, it will eventually hurt their business.

Ok I'm updating my previous review, I decided to give these guys a second chance as my wife wanted some fried chicken, and I didn't want to drive to the closest Church's! 
No bell this time, than goodness, no welcome to Popeyes being yelled across the room, so already a great start! The counter critter was polite and friendly, and helpful with the app deals which I had never used!
Huge difference in my meal this time around as well the chicken was hot fresh and delicious! The mashed potatoes were decent, the gravy though is bomb!
Dirty rice did not disappoint either, the biscuits are ok here I much prefer Church's biscuits. Overall this was a much better experience and as a result I will try them again when in the mood for a waistline expanding meal!

Quick stop for lunch with the family. The restaurant is nestled away in Lambertville area, a short distance from the busy main street, which is a bonus. Street parking was free and available. 

It seemed pretty quiet for Saturday, only a few patrons around the time we went. The ambiance is nice, very homely. Service was friendly.

As for the food ordered, mixed reviews... the samosa appetizer was a nice light pastry with lamb/beef filling, the meat was charcoal burnt, which is truly unfortunate.

The gyro sandwich with garlic sauce was tasty, pretty standard. The highlight of the meal was the lamb ouzi. The pastry wrap was light, could have been a little crisper, but not an issue. The rice and lamb was absolutely delicious, very aromatic and flavorful!

The baklava was sweet and yummy, we took it to go.

Overall, the food was reasonably priced, drinks may have been slightly above average. This place could have earned 4 stars if it wasn't for the disappointing appetizer.

My husband took me to Saffron recently for my birthday.  This was our second visit.  It had been two years since we had been there on a previous birthday of mine.  It had been far too long!

We again received excellent service and had wonderful food!  Mr. Hoque waited on us.  As before we had great conversation with him.  He is very good at making food and wine suggestions.  I am vegan and he helped me navigate the menu.  I had the eggplant dish which was very good.  We also tried a bread we had not had before that had mint in it.  Also delicious.  My husband had a lamb dish that looked to have spinach in it.  He seemed to enjoy it very much.  For an appetizer I had the potato pakora.  My better half had the meat samosa.  If memory serves, the wine that was suggested was a malbec wine and went perfectly with our dinner.  We had plenty of food left over to take home, but I admit I wish we had ordered more to take with us just because I missed the food so much once it was gone.  =)  

The food at Saffron is a little different than other Indian restaurants I have been to, but I have never been to two that were exactly alike.  The owner is from Bangladesh which makes the food more unique than the others in which I have dined.  Saffron is definitely one of my/our very favorite restaurants!  We will have to order extra food next time so that we can have our favorites as well as try some of the things on the menu we have never had before.  I would like to try some things from the Indo-Chinese section.  I would also love to try their lunch buffet sometime if I ever wake up early enough to go.  =)

Wonderful decor and atmosphere.  You hardly feel like you are in a strip mall.  And you will feel comfortable dressing up as well as going in casual clothes.  Just depends on your mood I guess.  Wonderful place as always!  Can't wait to return!

Friendly environment and are flexible with anything you want to do to change a meal like switching from fries to onion rings or adding things to your burger or even having them make one off the broiler

Fresh and delicious food, served fast. What more can you ask for? Margaritas? Well, ya. But until then try the Chicken Tenders, they are TENDER and flavorful. This place is in a great location, easy to get to and plenty of parking.

We met family here last evening for dinner.  The service is great!! We had our 2 little dogs and they brought fresh water right away.  The patio is so nice and comfortable.  
The guacamole was made at the table and was outstanding.  By far the best o have ever had!  I wAnt to know what magic ingredients are in there because it was delicious!  Can't wait to go back next year!

The owner of this restaurant is a horrible person!!!  He is employing a shady tow company to sit in the parking lot and put boots on cars.  I pulled in a space in less than 1 minute the tow truck driver blocked me in and then put a boot on my tire and required $95 cash to remove it!!!!  And the owner is ok with this....bad business and unethical!  I will NEVER eat at subway again.

This is a great pizza place that is also one of the few that deliver into Trenton. We ordered a tomato pie and it was delicious, all of us loved the super thin crust and the copious amounts of marinara sauce (it was very slightly sweet which blended it great with the cheese, which was also not too heavy). I think we have definitely found our new go-to pizza place. Also, another plus is that they delivered very fast, well within the timeframe they said they would!

Very happy with this location. Clean and organized. Our wait on Sunday morning for 8 was minimal. Gotta love the list on Yelp, which you can join in advance. 
The menu is a little different than the Warringron location, or maybe the menu was just revised recently. Loved the addition of salmon and bagel platter, which we ordered. They also have shakshuka (yum) and additional toast aside form just the avocado toast. 
I had the CrossFit omelette: egg whites, kale, mushrooms and it was absolutely perfect size.  Coffee is great and so was the service!
The best pizza on the west coast as far as I'm concerned.
Very solid food and fun rustic vibe! I had a great salad and a great experience. The wait staff was very attentive and helpful!
Great food for the price from Appetizers to Soups& Dinners accurate orders & great customer service. Best Chinese food in St Bernard Parish
The good is fresh and delicious. Do not come hungry. Food take a while to be cooked and brought to you but worth it.

Tried out a new to me spot with my wife and adult daughter while visiting Tampa.   Cask came highly recommended and didn't disappoint.
We all ended up ordering the Chicken and Waffle which came with a nicely prepared (not overdone) Waffle and 3 good-sized strips of nicely seasoned  fried chicken, along with some bacon jam (candied bacon!), a nice vanilla cream, honey butter, and maple syrup that had orange marmalade in it. I was afraid it would be too sweet but the combination worked quite well.
They had a good assortment of drinks and beers available and the service was good. The place was rockin' and was quite loud with a lot of happy people.  Definitely worth a stop!

First time here. Waited for 30 minutes when the place was less than 1/2 full for our food. Had to get our food to go to make it to make it to an event and not be late.  Ate on the drive and the food was great but the service was poor.  Doubt we will go back. Great product, terrible service.

what a wonderful experience!  Never had Tibetan food before and just WOW!  this knocked it out of the park.  My only regret, we didn't have more people with us so we could order more food.  Our server was so knowledgeable regarding and just as sweet too.  Great place to go with a group!  And, it is BYOB!

Over the past month I have visited here 6 times in my attempt to try a dish from nearly all West African countries including:
1. Mali
2. Benin
3. Senegal 
4. Togo
5. Guinea 
6. Ghana
7. Niger
8. Burkina Faso
9. Nigeria 
10. Sierra Leone
11. Côte D'Iviore 
12. Cameroon 

On day 1 I honestly was overwhelmed with how many possible options there were to choose from but I ended up getting the #3 Sauce d'arachide with Goat and Rice. This dish was pretty good, I'm not personally a fan of goat but I definitely respected the African roots. 
Day 2: I decided to try the infamous Bissap drink of Senegal along with Ginger Juice, Degue Yogurt, and a side order of Plaintains. Everything was really really good especially the Bissap which is made from Hibiscus and shocking has an amazing distinctive and unique taste.
Day 3 came around and I decided to try more traditional dishes including Kedjenou Sauce au Poulet with Rice, Tchep Djen, and Fufu. These dishes had very distinctive flavors some that I personally had a rough time with but in the end I'm glad I expanded by taste pallet. 
Day 4: I decided to try more basic and popular dishes around West Africa including Attassi with Fish and Okra Sauce with Rice. I enjoyed the fried fish but the Okra Sauce was a bit rough. I just don't like Okra but I'm sure for some this dish is amazing. 
Day 5: I went for lunch this day and again wanted to get something simple so I ordered the Egusi and 3 Beignets or Puff Puffs. The Egusi is alright again I'm not really a fan of cooked greens but definitely respect the culture. The Beignets were a pretty good though.
Day 6: I saved the best few dishes for last and ordered the infamously notable Afri-cue Whole Tilapia Fish with Plantains, Attieke, onions, and tomatoes. The Tilapia and Attieke were outstanding this had to be the best over dish combo from the entire menu.

All and all over the past month I got to enjoy, expand, and respect the various similar cultures of West Africa. Definitely an amazing place to stop in while roaming or staying in Tucson!
Food here is fantastic and uber healthy! I love the almond crust pizza, and the desserts are insanely delicious!

I found this place from Uber eats craving Indian. My husband and I now get take out every other week from here. They always hit the spot. The garlic naan is my favorite to slop up the tikki masala and madras. They were very accommodating with providing vegan options too. It's a great small business and family owned place. You can tell they work really hard!
The wait time is horrible we were in the window after we had paid and waited like 25 minutes just to get our food!!! Not recommend

We was in town doing some shopping, looked on yelp as we do for places to go eat. This place popped up, info on the site showed they was open.. awesome so we Waited about 20 mins before they opened back up(4:00) to be told at the door they was not open for dine in. Seriously, enough is enough, everybody else for the most part is open. Waste of time waiting around just to be told they aren't open for dine in and I'm sure as hell not getting it to go to eat in the truck. Lost our business. 
2 stars for the simple fact that they was open.... but wasn't.

It's a hidden little spot in a strip mall on Moana, near the intersection of Kietzke (close to the gas station)  some of the best restaurants are.  Parking is ample, and it's a small restaurant with about 15 tables.  They are Covid compliant, with masks, and seating spacing.  When you go to a restaurant, and all you see are locals and every table is filled, you know it's a good place.  It was clean, but hot as hell in there on Sunday (they should have left the door open)  They do a booming take-out business, with some people taking 10-20 meals out at a time!  

The menu is expansive, with beautiful pictures, and many items that looked D'licious :) Service was okay - I give a pass because they were busy, but we were seated without a menu for a bit, and others were seated after us and got menus right away.  Other than that, our orders were taken timely, and we were offered refills of water, etc.  

I ordered the meatlovers skillet - it is on a sizzling cast iron skillet, and it came with a side of biscuits and gravy, which unfortunately must have sat in the window waiting for the other items to be ready because they were cold, so I didn't eat them.  My skillet however was amazing!  Crisp potatoes, bacon, sausage, ham, all sorts of pork goodness, cheese, onions, bell peppers.  My friend ordered the spinach omelette and she said it was great.  It came with pancakes (?) kinda weird but okay.  She substituted hashbrowns, they were crispy, but to be honest, it was just too much food to eat for one person.
Bill for 2 was $23.00 - quite a bargain for such a big breakfast.  

Would love to come back for lunch sometime.
I live in the area and frequently eat here. Always fresh and flavorful food with great service. No rushing and BYOB to boot!

Cozy and friendly atmosphere, and good food. The server was polite and on top of good suggestions. The table bread is great. The plating and taste was very enjoyable of the entrees we ordered. I cannot compare it to fine dining, as it is not fine dining. However, it is an enjoyable mid-level restaurant with good service. The ambiance is decent (not a fan of their chairs), unless they are busy - then it gets a bit tight space wise and noisy. They only take reservations for large parties, as I usually say - be patient while waiting. Parking can be tight. Overall a decent establishment.

Its improving... slowly, but improving nonetheless.  Food is fairly good... the last few trips the food has been better even compared to a few months ago when orders were always taken incorrectly or served wrong, at least that problem has been solved.  Service is quite slow still.  It took about 10 minutes to get my $5 box.  Staff members were polite but they really do need to work on the speed of service.  From the looks of it, prioirty went to the drive through customers.

I enjoy Landry for many reasons. First, it has an upscale atmosphere, without enormous prices. Second, the food is very good and they have great specials. Then, of course, there is the view of the lake.

Why, Robbie, Why?!

Just to preface this review, I am a huge fan of Sweetie Pie's.  As proof, here is my prior review of the one in The Grove:

http://www.yelp.com/biz/sweetie-pies-at-the-mangrove-saint-louis-2#hrid:_FtFh3r4Xbu1jzPIzb0v4A

This weekend I celebrated my birthday, so I did some of my favorite things.  One of these things was eat at Sweetie Pie's.  However, knowing that it was Sunday and that The Grove location would be undoubtedly overflowing with people, I made an audible and tried the Upper Crust location.

The reviews were light and average, so I figured that the wait wouldn't be nearly as long.  I was wrong.  

We arrived at about noon, and the line was pouring out the door.  Apparently, the Kansas chapter of the NAACP made a special stop at Upper Crust that morning.  This is not a joke.  There were literally fifty or more people in yellow Kansas NAACP shirts sitting and eating as we finally made our way into the building.  If this didn't happen, perhaps our wait would have been cut into half.  However, the time in line was nearly an hour.

As we made to the front, I noticed that the chicken wings were slightly off.  Someone brought a fresh batch before I ordered, and I could tell that they were pretty skimpy.  Not taking a chance on another course, I went with my gut and ordered the staple.

Finally, we were seated.  I dug into my mac 'n' cheese, the highlight of the meal.  They basically gave me a dollop of mashed potatoes, but the grand disappointment was the chicken.  When I bit into that first wing, I could find no meat.  Each wing followed with the same issue:  little to no meat.  At first, I thought maybe they had overcooked the chicken, but I'm not sure that was the problem.  I finished my meal, but with no great joy.  This has never happened at The Grove location.

I just hope that it was a bad day at Upper Crust and that they were overwhelmed by the large group of people visiting on top of the normal lunch rush.  However, I don't know if I'll take another chance on Upper Crust when Man Grove is still batting 1.000.

Thai Station has become my go-to Thai place.

It is never crowded, service is good, the people are friendly and food comes out pretty fast.

It is always clean, and the decor is pretty contemporary. They add things here and there to celebrate different holidays. And there are always fresh flowers on the tables, a little detail that I'm a sucker for.

The food is fresh and tasty! Their heat levels are pretty standard. I usually do medium and can handle it just fine. The drunken noodles are my go-to menu item, although I've ventured out and tried the orange chicken which was so much food!

They also have some alcoholic beverages. And they'll deliver through DoorDash.

Prices are around $10-$15 per item.

I ordered the Big Judd burger to-go. It was the biggest burger I have ever seen or tasted. It is about the size of a frizbee 7-8 inches in diameter!  I especially loved the tater tots with thousand island dipping sauce, not because it was so delicious, but a change from French fries was great.

WHERE'S THE BEEF?  They actually have the worlds record for slowest fast food restaurant.  I'm sure of it.  In addition, they literally ration meat. My beef chalupa supreme must have been mistaken for a vegetarian chalupa.  No I did not order a head of lettuce with a minute ounce of meat hidden in lettuce and undetectable.

Horrible wait time for literally almost no meat in your food

20 min drive thru never again.  Plus they mysteriously held on to my credit card which made me feel uneasy.
